### BiohackersTO — 2025-10-29T18:26:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-out-of-dodge-club.30922/post-361824>

This post is about how to get your money out of Canadian banks and into bitcoin.

Uncle Jack has said you can't do it anymore. While I agree the doors are closing, they are not quite shut yet.

There are at least three Canadian companies that sell you Bitcoin and send it directly to your self-custody wallet. None of these companies will do business with you if you don’t provide a self-custody wallet address; they do not hold your bitcoin, ever. I will cover Bitcoin Well the most, because that’s the one I have experience with, both as a customer and also through helping a high net worth boomer get started with Bitcoin and with Bitcoin Well Infinite (purchases of $50,000 CAD or more).

Bitcoin Well is a publicly traded company on the Toronto Stock Exchange. I sat in on a call with Reilly Coombs from Bitcoin Well Infinite today. He works directly with clients to help them wire transfer their Canadian dollars to buy bitcoin (they also have services for Americans in the US with US banks). You get direct one-on-one support with him or one of his colleagues for payments and troubleshooting anything the bank requests. In the last year he has held that position, he has not seen a single one of his clients get debanked from sending a wire transfer to Bitcoin Well.

None of that guarantees you won’t get debanked. I have used Bitcoin Well’s regular service for small purchases of bitcoin and while I have not yet been de-banked, Toronto Dominion (TD) bank quietly stopped my payments to Bitcoin Well and removed the option to send money to them, without telling me and also blocked my account for a period of time, last year. I know a Bitcoin entrepreneur based in Canada, who was recently debanked by Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) while simultaneously getting invited to an RBC round table for “crypto entrepreneurs.”

Canadian banks do actively debank founders of Bitcoin companies.

Adam O’Brien, founder of Bitcoin Well is unbanked and the Canadian banks debanked his wife, who does not work in the business. I met Adam at the Canadian Bitcoin Conference last month. You can read more about him here: [https://bitcoinnews.com/interviews/adam-obrien-bitcoin-well-bypass-banking/](https://bitcoinnews.com/interviews/adam-obrien-bitcoin-well-bypass-banking/) He’s a very friendly and optimistic guy, but he also was on a panel titled “The Problem with Treasury Companies,” which concluded (among other things) that a 6102 type confiscation event is a certainty.

When I ask around in Ontario Bitcoiners’ chat group (400 members), it’s hit and miss. Some people have zero problems buying bitcoin from Bitcoin Well or other similar Canadian services, while others get hassled by their banks. Generally the big five Canadian banks are the worst for this. The discount services (like PC financial) seem to give less trouble. People have reported that Credit Unions are not giving as much trouble. One pattern that emerged is trying to access your banking while abroad. People are getting de-banked for that, even in the absence of buying bitcoin. So if like me, you are headed for El Salvador and you are not yet de-banked, it’s probably wise to arrange things so you don’t have to use any credit cards or banking directly in El Salvador (or any other country outside of Canada).

If you want to book a call with Bitcoin Well Infinite, you may do so here: [https://bitcoinwell.com/otc](https://bitcoinwell.com/otc) Click on “Speak to our Team”
If you want to thank me, you may include my name in the Referral box (and I'd be grateful for it): Cathia Badiere
That box is however, optional.

Bitcoin Well competitors that offer the same services (purchasing bitcoin that goes directly to your self-custody wallet) include Bull Bitcoin and Beaver Bitcoin.

If accessing customer service in French is important for you, Bull Bitcoin is strong on that count; they started in Montreal as the Bitcoin Embassy trading desk. They’ve been around for a while. I don’t think they are allowed to serve US citizens, but I could be wrong, so check if that’s something you need. They recently expanded operations to both France and Mexico. I don't know how they manage to open shop in overtly communist jurisdictions, but I guess that's where they started, so they learned a thing or two. [https://www.bullbitcoin.com/](https://www.bullbitcoin.com/)

Beaver Bitcoin is the youngest of these companies. I met the founder, his dad and one of his employees at the Canadian Bitcoin Conference. Very nice Bitcoiners. I haven’t done business with them yet. [https://www.beaverbitcoin.com/](https://www.beaverbitcoin.com/)

I hope this post is helpful to any Canadian who is trying to get out of dodge with their hard-earned money.

As for me, I’m still living the perpetual punch in the mouth the last five years have delivered to me personally. I am still getting back on my feet financially but will be spending the winter in El Salvador to optimize what I can for my health, while I figure out where I can provide value in the Bitcoin space.

If anyone is going to the Adopting Bitcoin conference, Bitcoin Historico or Bitcoin Economia in Berlin, I’d love to meet up with some mitochondriacs there. Let me know.

### BiohackersTO — 2025-11-06T15:04:48+0200

I'm referring to citizenship. Citizenship is a contract and it is where the jurisdiction originates.
Here's how you "opt out" of U.S. citizenship, but the process is universal.

### [8 U.S. Code § 1481 - Loss of nationality by native-born or naturalized citizen; voluntary action; burden of proof; presumptions](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/8/1481)

 www.law.cornell.edu

> [Adamo said:](/goto/post?id=367987)

The reason why they have power over you is because of your citizenship contract.
The only way out of a State/Nation/Country (Synonyms) legal system is by following the options laid out in the above link.

Essentially you have three options:
!. Stay a citizen right where you are and be subject to whatever the State wants to throw at you.
2\. Naturalize as a citizen in a different State and be subject to whatever they want to throw at you. Could be worse, could be better, either way you are their subject.
3\. Form up your own State under the right of self-determination and make your own rules. Not easy to get other people on board with this as "the Matrix has you". The right of self-determination is how every State/Nation/Country has been formed throughout history.

### Jacks — 2025-05-17T17:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/microseism.30925/post-352735>

### "Microseisms in Global Seismology​

In [seismology](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Seismology), a **microseism** is defined as a faint [earth tremor](https://wiki.seg.org/w/index.php?title=Earth_tremor&action=edit&redlink=1) caused by natural phenomena.[\[1\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-1)[\[2\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-2) The term is most commonly used to refer to the dominant background seismic noise signal on Earth, which are mostly composed of [Rayleigh waves](https://wiki.seg.org/w/index.php?title=Rayleigh_waves&action=edit&redlink=1) and caused by water waves in the oceans and lakes.[\[3\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-LonguetHiggins-3)[\[4\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-Hasselmann-4)[\[5\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-Kedar-5)[\[6\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-Ardhuin-6) Thus a microseism is a small and long-continuing [oscillation](https://wiki.seg.org/w/index.php?title=Oscillation&action=edit&redlink=1) of the ground.[\[7\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-7)"

### [Loading…](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic)

wiki.seg.org

Usually detected on broadband seismometers - I've observed them.

### Jacks — 2025-05-18T02:30:00+0300

# Thread: Question about menstrual bleeding and evolution
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-menstrual-bleeding-and-evolution.30927/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiohackersTO — 2025-05-17T18:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-menstrual-bleeding-and-evolution.30927/post-352740>

For some time, I have wondered what on earth would be the evolutionary advantage for female mammals to shed blood regularly?

Culturally, we are taught that pain, crankiness, fatigue are a normal part of our periods. But this occurs to me as BS. There are no evolutionary advantages to this. There is a book I enjoyed, written by a woman who teaches Fertility Awareness Method and how to read the signs and symptoms in our cycle to better understand what is going on with our overall health. It's called "The Fifth Vital Sign" and considers tracking fertility signs and symptoms in women's bodies, as a fifth vital sign that has much to teach us. I believe this book is directionally accurate, but possibly inaccurate in some details. Generally, tracking signs and symptoms through the Fertility Awareness Method has been one of my favourite biohacks.

So I see pain and fatigue and crankiness as symptoms I am doing something wrong, living in a problematic environment.

What about blood loss through menstruation? In the past, I read something that suggested that only domesticated mammals actually bleed as part of the oestrus cycle. For wild mammals, they have a cycle, but blood loss is not a part of that. So I came to see menstrual bleeding as another symptom arising from a terrible environment (light, water, magnetism and beyond).

Today I queried Perplexity AI about this: Other than humans, which mammals bleed as a part of the oestrus cycle?
In summary, outside of humans, true menstrual bleeding is rare and limited to certain primates, bats, elephant shrews, and the spiny mouse. Other mammals like dogs and cows may bleed during their estrous cycle, but this is not menstruation.

| Mammal Group | Type of Bleeding | Mechanism |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Humans, some primates | Menstruation | Shedding of uterine lining |
| Some bats | Menstruation | Shedding of uterine lining |
| Elephant shrews | Menstruation | Shedding of uterine lining |
| Spiny mouse | Menstruation | Shedding of uterine lining |
| Dogs, cows, some others | Estrous cycle bleeding | Leakage from vaginal blood vessels |

**My questions are:**
How accurate is the AI's response?
If it is accurate, is bleeding in human females a combination of menstruation AND estrous cycle bleeding, as a result of leakage from vaginal blood vessels?
And is this estrous cycle bleeding from leakage from vaginal blood vessels the result of the terrible environments we live in?
And back to my original question: if menstrual bleeding occurs in some mammals, even in optimal environments, what is its evolutionary advantage?
Leakage from vaginal blood vessels doesn't sound like it explains excess bleeding associated with diseases like endometriosis and fibroids. I assume such bleeding comes from these growths... unless in addition to leaky gut and leaky brains, we also have leaky vaginas...

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-17T19:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-menstrual-bleeding-and-evolution.30927/post-352747>

The leptin-melanocortin pathway is a central regulator of mammalian energy balance, controlling food intake, metabolism, hormone production, fertility, and body weight. Leptin, produced by adipose tissue, signals energy availability to the hypothalamus, where it activates the melanocortin system, particularly via melanocortin-4 receptors (MC4R). This pathway, conserved across ALL mammals, evolved to ensure survival in fluctuating environments by balancing energy storage and expenditure. Epigenetic and genetic disruptions in this pathway lead to severe obesity, underscoring its role in maintaining photo-bioelectric harmony.

In the context of reproduction, the leptin-melanocortin pathway acts as an energy gatekeeper or energy capacitor, ensuring that pregnancy, a massive energy investment, only occurs when energy reserves are sufficient. Leptin levels rise with fat stores, signaling to the hypothalamus that the body can support a fetus. Just as a human is born obese to finish brain development postnatally, leptin is critical in human fertility. In female mammals, this pathway links energy status to reproductive cycles, with menstruation serving as a photo-bioelectric reset mechanism when conception does not occur. But why shed blood? Why not reabsorb the endometrium, as most mammals do in their estrous cycles?

Think about the blogs and all the things about iron and oxygen from the GOE.

The GOE predates mammalian evolution but sculpted what systems they should have. Why? The GOE introduced oxygen as a metabolic game-changer, driving the evolution of heme proteins, oxygen-binding molecules like hemoglobin and myoglobin, which enabled efficient energy production and oxygen transport.

With their iron-porphyrin cores, Heme proteins evolved to scavenge and metabolize oxygen, protecting cells from its corrosive effects while harnessing it for oxidative phosphorylation. This innovation was critical for multicellular life, particularly for mammals, whose high metabolic rates demanded robust oxygen delivery systems.

In menstruation, heme proteins in blood (e.g., hemoglobin) play a pivotal role. Menstrual blood loss involves shedding heme-rich endometrial tissue, which is laden with iron and oxygen-binding capacity. While seemingly wasteful, this process may have evolved as a photo-bioelectric adaptation to manage oxygen and iron homeostasis, leveraging the GOE’s legacy of heme protein functionality. This idea may not stimulate you to understand what menopause really is..........

Let’s explore how this ties into the evolutionary advantage of menstruation.

Menstruation as a Photo-Bioelectric Adaptation for chronic winning in a chaotic world.

Menstruation ensures that energy is allocated efficiently in female mammals, particularly in species like primates with high reproductive costs. The leptin-melanocortin pathway monitors energy availability, and in humans, where spontaneous decidualization occurs, the endometrium prepares for pregnancy each cycle regardless of conception. If energy reserves are insufficient, signaled by low leptin and melanin levels, the pathway inhibits pregnancy, triggering menstruation to reset the system. Shedding the endometrium, rather than reabsorbing it, prevents the accumulation of metabolic byproducts (e.g., ROS, lipid peroxidation products) that could disrupt mitochondrial stability and increase entropy, aligning with the thread’s emphasis on minimizing cellular chaos.

From a photo-bioelectric perspective, the endometrium’s preparation involves light-driven processes. I always comment that sunlight (UV, IRA, NIR) activates VDR and CCO, stabilizing mtDNA and reducing ROS (the first evolutionary bomb in the GOE). In the endometrium, similar light-driven mechanisms likely regulate cellular turnover, with UPEs (200–800 nm) coordinating gene expression via wave function collapse, as discussed in the thread (third bomb of GOE evolution). Menstruation clears out this tissue when pregnancy doesn’t occur, preventing the buildup of light-stressed cells that could impair future reproductive success. This reset mechanism ensures that each cycle starts with a clean slate, optimizing energy allocation for high-cost pregnancies in primates and humans.

Heme Proteins, Iron Homeostasis, and Quantum Coherence: Menstrual blood loss, rich in heme proteins like hemoglobin, serves a biophysical purpose tied to the GOE’s legacy. Heme proteins evolved to manage oxygen’s corrosive effects, and in menstruation, their shedding may protect against oxidative stress in the reproductive tract. The endometrium, a highly vascular tissue, accumulates iron and oxygen during the buildup. If reabsorbed (as in estrous cycles), this iron could fuel Fenton reactions, generating ROS that damage mtDNA and increase entropy—a risk highlighted in the thread (third bomb, IV Vitamin C fueling Fenton reactions).

By shedding this tissue, menstruation expels excess iron, reduces oxidative stress, and maintains mitochondrial stability in the uterus, a critical organ for reproductive fitness.

Quantum mechanically, heme proteins in menstrual blood may act as quantum transducers. My notes in today's X thread below teach you that collagen’s triple helix entangles photons and supports proton tunneling (seventh bomb of the GOE). Similarly, heme’s iron-porphyrin ring can entangle electrons and photons, absorbing UV light to emit UPEs that signal cellular turnover. This quantum signaling may coordinate the shedding process during menstruation, ensuring coherence in the endometrial reset. While energetically costly, the loss of heme-rich blood aligns with the thread’s emphasis on light-driven coherence, removing light-stressed, ROS-laden tissue to protect the quantum harmony of the reproductive system.

**CITES**

### BiohackersTO — 2025-05-21T22:05:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-menstrual-bleeding-and-evolution.30927/post-353076>

I only have high school level biology, chemistry and physics and that was a long time ago now. But at a very preliminary level of understanding for me, something about all those who were saying that the body is eliminating "toxins" through menstruation, there is (possibly) some half-truth in there. When people said that to me, it sounded preposterous. Why would mother nature use the same organ as a nursery and a toxic dump? Makes no sense. but when you say it, it's not like that. The uterus is not a toxic dump but can detect when it is full of the wrong building blocks or the building blocks in the wrong forms and also dump it all out.

So years of excessive menstrual bleeding is the result of my not understanding how harmful my electro-magnetic environment was for me and every month, my body worked hard to minimize the damage.

When I searched "Jack Kruse" and "How do I determine if I am leptin resistant?" I got the leptin series and this one addresses that question.

### [The Leptin Rx: FAQs](https://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/)

Readers Summary What questions do you have about The Leptin Rx? What questions would you like to see added to the FAQs? Post your experience with The Leptin Reset in the comments What should I do before I start The Leptin Reset? Before you start, take a picture of yourself from all angles...

 jackkruse.com

But I'm not really chronically overweight or chronically underweight. I'm optimal weight and I obsessively eat paleo because when I don't, things go bad with pain or hives or other issues, but not weight gain. So it's easier for me to lose weight than gain weight, but really, I have to **fast** to lose weight. So I don't think by this test that I'm leptin resistant.

Also mentioned in the article above, you might be leptin resistant if you ever had an eating disorder. I could only ever be considered as having had an eating disorder if my obsessive adherence to the paleo diet is interpreted as such. I avoid non-paleo foods because eating them gives me physical pain or other unpleasant symptoms like hives. But that doesn't occur to me as an eating disorder. It occurs to me as avoiding physical pain and hives.

In many ways, members of my family and I tend toward similar symptoms (but not as severe) as Jordan Peterson and his family: mostly skinny people who get auto-immune (or auto-immune-like issues).

Often the same strategies that get fat people thin, have also helped relieve my symptoms. Nearly all of my symptoms disappear when I spend time outside, getting a good tan. Cold baths help a lot and going paleo and lower-carb generally works better than eating a normie diet.

I once heard you say on a podcast that the electrical grid in Europe is different and tends people more toward autoimmune issues while the electrical grid in the USA tends people toward obesity. I have only lived one year of my life in Europe, and that was just over 20 years ago. The rest of my life was lived in Canada and the USA (as far as I know, the same electrical grid).

What is it that makes some families in North America tend toward autoimmunity instead of obesity? And is a tendency toward autoimmunity more likely in people who tend to be thin (but not necessarily underweight)? And is there one or another part of the leptin reset we should really focus on? Possibly I am mildly leptin resistant. For people like me, is it that our leptin is mostly okay but our melanin is fucked?

# Thread: WHY WOMEN BLEED AND WHY THEY DO NOT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-women-bleed-and-why-they-do-not.30928/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-17T19:37:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-women-bleed-and-why-they-do-not.30928/post-352748>

Check out my latest article: The Biophysics of "WHY WOMEN BLEED?" [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/why-women-bleed-jack-kruse-7aeie](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/why-women-bleed-jack-kruse-7aeie)

My Decentralized Thesis and the Garden of Eden: A Parallel of Human Design and Modern Disruption

Imagine the Garden of Eden (GOE) as the blueprint for humanity’s optimal state, perfectly attuned to Nature’s rhythms, where sunlight, circadian alignment, and cellular harmony sustained life as God or evolution intended. In this divine or evolutionary design, 20% of hemoglobin (Hb) delivers oxygen from the heart to the brain, fueling cognition, consciousness, and our unique mammalian identity. But what happens when that 20% is preserved in volume yet corrupted in function, with Hb oxidized to metHb (in the +3 state), unable to carry oxygen? Can we still embody the fullness of humanity as planned? This question mirrors the decentralized thesis we’ve been exploring: a vision of systems, biological, social, or technological, operating in harmony with Nature’s principles, free from centralized distortions. Like a flawed centralized protocol, modern life disrupts this design, with profound consequences.

The Fall from Eden: Modern Life’s Assault on Biology

Just as the GOE represents humanity’s symbiosis with Nature, our decentralized thesis champions systems that align with natural laws—autonomous, resilient, and adaptive. Today’s world, however, imposes a “centralized” override: artificial light, non-native EMF (nnEMF), and 24/7 tech. This disconnects us from daylight and inundates us with nightlight. This mirrors the epigenetic fall from Eden, where each generation strays further from the original blueprint. Chronic diseases, cancer, neurodegeneration, endometriosis, and metabolic collapse emerge not as random failures but as predictable outcomes of this mismatch, accelerating transgenerationally as tech and light abuse intensify. The decentralized thesis warns that centralized systems (like modern tech-driven lifestyles) erode resilience, creating fragility where Nature intended antifragility.

### BiohackersTO — 2025-07-29T01:15:29+0300

Very interesting.
I took a fertility awareness method course and found it helpful for tracking what was going on in my body. They like to track basal body temp, vaginal mucus and cervical position. I never mastered that last one, but the other two are very clear to me and most methods do not use all three indicators.

But one indicator none of them seem to track is blood loss. But I do track blood loss because having had endometriosis and fibroids I cannot understand the volumes of blood loss I have experienced as anything but pathological.

If I understand correctly, my excessive blood loss has been my body's way of dumping +3 hemoglobin that can no longer carry oxygen.

Regarding the reply above about altitude... I have always felt very good at altitude - especially during menses. Going up mountains has reduced my period pain noticeably, and I've never experienced altitude sickness. But for [@Mightygumshoe](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/40094/) , it caused loss of period. Maybe Kilimanjaro is too much altitude. I've never been a mountain climber. My experiences are going up the Rockies (site seeing) and the Pyrenees and visiting higher altitude cities like Bogota & Medellin.

Does going up in altitude help reverse the +3 state of hemoglobin somehow? I don't have any data on my blood loss at altitude versus sea level, but I remember severe pain disappearing when going up mountains during menses.

Would C02 tolerance exercises (like Patrick McKeown teaches) be at all helpful wrt +3 hemoglobin?

PS I'm already walking barefoot on the grass and seeing every sunrise.

As such, free iron is stored as ferritin, and as it rises it acts as an antenna in you and transforms energy into non coherent UPEs = heat in you which drives heat higher = where hot flashes come from.

**1\. The Loss of the Monthly "Scrub"of mass**
For decades, the female body uses the menstrual cycle to offload **excess iron** and **deuterated metabolic water**.

**The Post-Menopause Trap:** When periods stop, the "exit valve" closes. Iron begins to accumulate in the tissues.

**Melanin Failure:** If **melanin** (the body’s primary broadband absorber and chelator) is depleted or "jammed" by glyphosate or nnEMF, it can no longer "caged" this free iron.

**2\. Ferritin as a "Dirty Antenna" like biology had to deal with in the GOE. Point made clear in the blogs.**

**The Antenna Effect:** Ferritin is a nanocage with a mineral core of hydrous ferric oxide. In an environment saturated with **nnEMF**, these ferritin cages act as **paramagnetic antennas**.

**Energy Transformation:** They capture ambient electromagnetic frequencies and, because the system lacks the coherent **220nm UPE signals** (due to the "seed oil" D-PUFA interference), they transform that energy into **non-coherent infrared (heat)**.

**3\. The "Hot Flash" as Mitochondrial Exhaust**

This is where the "Hot Flash" originates. It is not a "brain glitch"; it is a **localized thermal dump**.

**Heteroplasmy & Heat:** As mitochondria age and **heteroplasmy** (mutated mtDNA) increases, they become less efficient at making "cold" ATP and more prone to "leaking" heat.

**The Flash:** When the "ferritin antennas" pick up nnEMF, they trigger a sudden discharge of this non-coherent thermal energy. The body attempts to dump this heat via vasodilation and sweating from the eccrine glands talked about in CPC #79 ---> the classic hot flash. Some women get a lot og GI symptoms in menopause -----> exocrine mass dump = CPC#79 too.

**4\. The Matrix Collapse**

This rising iron/ferritin level further poisons the **TCA cycle**, leading to more deuterium incorporation into the **collagen matrix**. This creates a feedback loop:

1.  **High Iron** = More nnEMF absorption.
2.  **More Heat** = Destruction of the **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water from CCO**
3.  **Low EZ Water** = Protons can't flow, leading to more saggy collagen, saggy breasts, fat, **ligament tears** and skin aging, (the "exhaust problem").

I'm getting sunrise sun while barefoot on the beach. I'm super grateful to be here. My ferritin and hemoglobin and vitamin D were all super low when they were tested back in December. I spent my first month in El Salvador going to the various conferences, so my blood tests reflect that I was indoors and not out on the beach at sunrise. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-plants-for-emf-sheilding.30930/post-352790>

The best way to reduce nnEMF in your home is to turn off the source - WiFi/bluetooth, all “smart” devices etc. and go hard wire ethernet. The 60Hz (50Hz) from the wires in the walls, floor and ceiling is, for most people, the #1 source of EMF in the home. Turn off bedroom circuits at night and unplug every appliance (coffee pot, toaster, light etc) when not in use. IF the source of RF is from the outside, shielding can help BUT the more you shield IF you still use a cell phone the more it will power up to make the connection to the nearest tower. Your exposure from the phone in your hand is far greater that the cell tower down the road.

All plants/trees block higher 5G frequencies which is why they are cutting down the trees along the roads.

# Thread: Cheapest/easiest way to block emf from outlet?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fouadfo1 — 2025-05-18T06:54:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352800>

Child sleeps next to an outlet. There is nothing plugged in. Is this an issue? Is there cheap way to block any harmful emf without shutting down the electrical?

Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-18T09:16:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352807>

> [fouadfo1 said:](/goto/post?id=352800)
>
> Child sleeps next to an outlet. There is nothing plugged in. Is this an issue? Is there cheap way to block any harmful emf without shutting down the electrical?
>
> Thanks

There is no EMF per se from an outlet. The EMF - electric field - (and maybe magnetic fields) comes from the electricity in the wires in the walls. You can shield the walls OR just turn off the circuit at the breaker at night. Measure and you will know. More info and videos at [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Mat.I — 2025-05-18T09:38:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352808>

> [fouadfo1 said:](/goto/post?id=352800)
>
> Child sleeps next to an outlet. There is nothing plugged in. Is this an issue? Is there cheap way to block any harmful emf without shutting down the electrical?
>
> Thanks

If you don't want to turn off the whole circuit at the breaker and subject to where the outlet is on the circuit you can just install a double throw switch at the outlet before (or a few before) the particular outlet - this will kill everything from that point to end of line..

### fouadfo1 — 2025-05-19T01:05:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352853>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=352808)
>
> If you don't want to turn off the whole circuit at the breaker and subject to where the outlet is on the circuit you can just install a double throw switch at the outlet before (or a few before) the particular outlet - this will kill everything from that point to end of line..

Thank you!

### fouadfo1 — 2025-05-19T01:05:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352854>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=352808)

Thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-19T01:11:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352856>

Turn off the power. Costs nothing.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-19T01:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352858>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=352856)
>
> Turn off the power. Costs nothing.

Sometimes the power cannot be turned off & left off due to mixed circuits and essential equipment (such as fridges/freezers and other electronics). As the only other alternative being splitting and back feeding parts to a new CB.. A double throw switch (i.e. dual pole A + N) costs a couple of dollars literally.. Thus subject to where the EOL is you can retain the functionality of the circuit without loosing the whole circuit..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-19T02:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352867>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=352858)
>
> Sometimes the power cannot be turned off & left off due to mixed circuits and essential equipment (such as fridges/freezers and other electronics). As the only other alternative being splitting and back feeding parts to a new CB.. A double throw switch (i.e. dual pole A + N) costs a couple of dollars literally.. Thus subject to where the EOL is you can retain the functionality of the circuit without loosing the whole circuit..

Fridge/freezers do not defrost over night If turned off.
Wired smoke alarms can be replaced with battery ones.
Medical equipment can be powered from a circuit still on with an extension cord.
If a security system is “essential electronics” . . . Time to move.

Measure the bed room, identify offending circuits, turn them off. Depending on wiring layout a double throw switch can be an option but in many situations it is not. To go from centralized (high nnEMF) living to decentralized (low nnEMF) requires changes and adjustments AND every situation/home is different.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-19T08:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352879>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352867)
>
> Fridge/freezers do not defrost over night If turned off.

Noted. Read the post I said left off meaning not turning back on as power is typically not needed in bedrooms unless you are doing something in the day.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352867)
>
> Wired smoke alarms can be replaced with battery ones.

Not wise. As redundancy is gone.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352867)
>
> Medical equipment can be powered from a circuit still on with an extension cord.

Noted.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352867)
>
> If a security system is “essential electronics” . . . Time to move.

I do not have a security system.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352867)
>
> Measure the bed room, identify offending circuits, turn them off. Depending on wiring layout a double throw switch can be an option but in many situations it is not.

I have used double throws in many situations and the majority will not offer these as they want to sell products to people or only offer particular options that involve more labour. I also have them in parts of our house including the light circuits. Obviously, this will be subject to where the line in is vs the end of line. It just happened to work well in my house and others.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352867)
>
> To go from centralized (high nnEMF) living to decentralized (low nnEMF) requires changes and adjustments AND every situation/home is different.

Agreed.
.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-19T09:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352880>

Likewise with kill switches - people sell premanufactured units, when you can have something that does the same job with less moving parts for a fraction of the price by using off the shelf products that any competent electrician should be able to install.. Very basic stuff, however it becomes stressful & complicated for some people as they are not getting the full story.

### fouadfo1 — 2025-05-19T21:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352928>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=352856)
>
> Turn off the power. Costs nothing.

I live in an area where I need the cameras on unfortunately otherwise there will be a mostly peaceful disturbance LOL. Love you doc

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-20T00:05:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352941>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=352880)
>
> Likewise with kill switches - people sell premanufactured units, when you can have something that does the same job with less moving parts for a fraction of the price by using off the shelf products that any competent electrician should be able to install.. Very basic stuff, however it becomes stressful & complicated for some people as they are not getting the full story.

Sure IF you have one (or two) circuits and the wiring layout allows for it, a double throw switch CAN work.
Most homes I have measured need 4-8 circuits turned off to get the BV reading down below 100mV.
If you have 4-8 circuits to cut it usually needs to be done at the breaker box.
AND most people will not comply with turning off circuits IF the circuit breaker is any distance from their bedroom, especially if it is out side. So a remote controlled cut off switch is best for most people.
Having bought all the parts, built the unit at $100 - $200 per hour, good luck finding a competent electrician to build you one that is UL for less than our unit especially if you want a built in whole house “DE” filter - at least in the US.
We have many electricians that used to build their own units buy ours because it is a better product and cheeper for the client.
Things maybe different in Australia but here in the US and Canada to comply with building code a device added to a home electrical system needs to be third party certified (UL etc) Or it could void your home owners insurance.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-20T03:16:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352965>

With respect, you're conflating two very different things:
UL certification for consumer products, and
Code-compliant installations using UL-listed components.

If I’m installing a contactor, relay, timer, or remote switch into a residential panel - using UL-listed parts, wired to NEC standards, and ideally done by a licensed electrician - that’s **not a “system” that requires its own UL certification**. That’s just normal electrical work.

This is exactly the same setup used every day to control pool pumps, HVAC systems, irrigation circuits, and lighting loads. No one calls those setups “non-compliant” because they don’t have a UL sticker as a package.

The only time UL system certification is required is if you’re manufacturing a complete plug-and-play consumer device. Sure - if you're selling boxes, you need UL or ETL listing for liability and code compliance in that context.

But suggesting that a custom install of UL-listed components is inherently non-compliant is incorrect. You're basically saying timers, contactors, or remotely switched circuits are illegal unless prepackaged, which is obviously nonsense.

The real reason many sparkies or integrators push prebuilt units? Because it’s easier to install and mark up - not because it’s the only safe or legal option.

Let’s not gatekeep basic electrical control work that’s well within the competence of any licensed electrician. If someone can install a time clock on a hot water system - which is done in some cases to reduce energy use or match off-peak tariffs - then they can easily install a contactor and a few relays to remotely cut a bedroom circuit. Electricians routinely install contactor-based setups in pool pump controls, irrigation timers, HVAC thermostats, whole-house fans, bathroom exhausts with humidity sensors, dusk-to-dawn outdoor lighting, solar priority switches, garage door controllers, and even emergency stop systems in workshops - all of which use the same logic: a low-voltage control signal (timer, sensor, or switch) operating a contactor to switch higher-voltage loads. These are not exotic systems; they’re standard NEC-compliant installs using UL-listed parts - and applying them to a bedroom or whole house cutoff is simply another valid, practical use case.

### S Hudson — 2025-05-20T14:39:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-easiest-way-to-block-emf-from-outlet.30933/post-352992>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352807)
>
> There is no EMF per se from an outlet. The EMF - electric field - (and maybe magnetic fields) comes from the electricity in the wires in the walls. You can shield the walls OR just turn off the circuit at the breaker at night. Measure and you will know. More info and videos at [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

Is this sort of thing enough to shield, or would you need to test to confirm? [https://www.amazon.co.uk/dp/B08RDRYBH2](https://www.amazon.co.uk/dp/B08RDRYBH2)

# Thread: Hello from the Windy City

### Thomas Ó hÍceadha — 2025-05-18T07:15:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-windy-city.30934/post-352801>

Hello All - My name is Tom Hickey and I'm very glad to be here with you. A little bit about me: I grew up on the southwest side of Chicago and after a brief stint away, I returned and now live in the area with my wife and two young boys.

My paternal grandparents are first generation Irish immigrants and my maternal line is from Poland/Slovakia.

Of the may stories and lesson's my Irish grandparents have shared over the years, the most interesting may be that my Hickey family lineage can be traced back to a line of celtic physicians reportedly known for brain surgery and the study of medicine.

Through Dr. Jack's work I've woken up to the reality that nature does not make any mistakes. And the deeper I get into this, the more and more I feel that perhaps my ancestors knew that this would be really important stuff for me to know and have now entangled me with this community for a reason. One way or another I am determined to find out.

Looking forward to meeting and engaging with you all. May you be blessed in your journey and wherever it may lead, now and for all time.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-05-18T07:23:57+0300

### Thomas Ó hÍceadha — 2025-05-18T07:43:18+0300

Thanks Rohen, I really appreciate your note. I do wish it was otherwise, but it's an EMF hell hole between the two airports and the 5G rollout. # Thread: Less Sleep since I Started Biohacking
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 56
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eelefter99 — 2025-05-18T16:40:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-352819>

I’m sleeping less. And less.

Problem
Wake every morning between 3 - 4ish.
Last night I woke at 1:30 and maybe slept an hour before seeing the sun rise at 5:30.

I used to fall asleep when I hit the pillow. My wife was envious. Now: 15 or so minutes pass no matter how sleepy I was before going to bed.
I used to wake up at 5ish.

In January, my sleep really changed. I’m worried enough about it to write this post.

Who Am I?
I’m 54.
Male.
White.
5’8”
160lbs. (I weigh myself every morning.)

Fitz 2 skin type.
I have built up a tan since the UVB has been available this year. I used to run from the sun and use sunscreen. (That all changed last August.)

No diseases that I know of.
Annual physical labs all within Centralized Western Medicine range.

Born: Long Island, NY.
Reside: Woodstock, NY. (10 years)
Career: Theater director and Professor (retired).

Always in bed between 10 and 10:30.
Asleep 15 or so minutes later (even if I was just dozing on the couch).

What Happened in January?
I was about 3 months into CT: 1x/wk.
55 - 63 degrees
10 - 15 minutes.

I had also stopped taking DHEA cream in Jan. (Started in Nov. @ 25 mgs/day.) I stopped because I suddenly started waking up at 3:45 every morning like clockwork. And thought I’d overdone it or didn’t need it in the first place. I started supplementing because I’m 54 and based on what I read on Jack’s DHEA blog - everyone over 50 should supplement — decided to experiment on the conservative side.)

A few weeks later I had some targeted labs done.

Labs (Feb)
DHEA-S: 176
Free Testosterone: 9
Total Testosterone: 441
Omega 6/3 ratio: 7:1 (this is down from 16:1 in August — before I heard Jack and started eating fish and supplementing with Cod Liver Oil)

Cape Cod
I was there last month and I slept much better. The first night I was sleepy and went to bed at 9:30. Woke up at 6am. Every night after that for a week I slept from 10 - 6ish.

Woostock
Since I returned to Woodstock, I am not sleeping well after waking in the middle of the night. Before waking, I sleep very well. Five hours, uninterrupted.

EMFs
My house is luckily down a long driveway that takes it away from neighbors and the street. That said, we have plenty of nnEMFs of our own creation.

WIFI router is far away from bedroom. Erick Hill EMF meter tells me there are no RF, MF in bedroom, but the walls are hot with EF. However, when I measure from where I sleep (where my pillow and body lie) my readings are 0 - 10 v/m.

Light
I see the sunrise every morning. For sunsets, I take a walk or stand outside on a covered deck.
I use Dminder to leverage UVB exposure between 11 and 2pm. Usually the skies are 100% clear and I am 100% naked. Current D estimate: 70ng/ml.
No sunglasses or tinted glasses.
Block blue light in the evening. Red light bulbs in house. Wood stove for heat in winter.

Myopia: -1.25 in each eye with a touch of astigmatism and the “very start” of a cataract.
Stopped wearing glasses (except to drive — and even then I wear them on top of my head if I can).
Have an eye chart hanging 10 ft from a wall and use it a few times a week to exercise and test my vision. I’m hoping to see an improvement at my annual exam.

Magnetism (Grounding)
I ground everyday outside on bluestone for 1 - 2 hours while watching the sun rise. I’ll also read from my red-lit iPad during this time. My theory is that the sunlight will wash out the iPad light — which again is tinted entirely red.

I also used a grounding pad and sleep mat under my sheet for several months only to remove them very recently — after reading much about the subject on this forum. I wrote to Dr. Chevalier at the Earthing Institute and got his input about how grounding indoors works and he refuted this and that, just as the folks here with refute what he says — so instead of going round and round I decided to pull all the grounding mats and use only the earth and the abundant bluestone on my property.

Water
This past winter, I embraced the cold more than usual. I grew up on Long Island and lived 25 years as an adult in NYC. My winters were already plenty cold. But from Oct - March I did a 55 - 63 degree cold bath 1x/week.

I drink about a gallon a day. Well water. I test it regularly and it’s excellent.

Fitness
I swim at the local university pool — about 2500 yards 3x/week. The water there is about 78 degrees and I believe the pool is grounded. After, I’d take a cold(ish) shower for 5 or so minutes. I run 3-4 miles 2-3x/week. (No cold shower afterwards.)

Food
My wife and I cook our own meals. Shop local produce and chicken/beef. Fish is harder to come by in Woodstock, NY. We eat Wild Planet tuna regularly.
Used to be vegetarian (10 years). Reintroduced meat 4 years ago -- we became live-in caregivers for my in-laws and we had only enough energy/time to cook on set of meals. So...we embraced meat again. Chicken and/or beef only 1x/week.

Yogurt. Tofu. Eggs. Bread. Dark Chocolate. Stir fry. Salad. Burrito if we're feeling wild. Nothing to excess.

Drugs
No booze. No weed. No meds of any kind.

Stress
Retired a year ago in January. No more fluorescent lights in the classroom/office. Use IRIS on my laptop/monitor for several hours each morning/afternoon.

My parents are toxic so I don’t engage with them. My brothers side with my parents — grandkids are in play. I have no children. But my family has shrunk and that makes me feel alone. But I know I better off with them. (Sorry and sad to say it.)

My wife’s mother died 2 years ago. Parkinson’s. We took care of her for years.
My wife’s father was in an accident 4 years ago. We took care of him (with his wife) in his home for 2+ years. My wife does it now.
Since MIL died, I’ve dialed back my involvement because he doesn’t need me as much and because I needed my life back.

My wife and I own our home. We have no debt. No kids.

We take care of FILs 2nd home on Cape Cod because he simply cannot anymore. (Spinal cord injury.)

Tech/Work
Watch TV from 7 - 9:30ish. With blue blockers on.

I create music: recording, mixing, mastering on my laptop (with IRIS) but use wired headphones on a regular basis.

I spent my career as a theater director and teacher. Lots of blue light. Lots of working from 6 - 10p. But I stopped that lifestyle 10 years ago. And like I said: my sleep was better before I started tinkering with my quantum health.

Question
_What’s going on with my sleep?_

No apnea. Some snoring but I don’t wake myself up and I breathe fine. Sometimes sinuses are stuffy at night after I lie down. But the 5 or so hours I sleep are uninterrupted.

Is it just that I’ve become a very efficient sleeper? I have lots of energy but I am also foggy and my eyes are tired. I’m definitely not purring like a Ferrari.

Thanks.

### eelefter99 — 2025-05-19T23:12:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-352936>

Thank you for guiding me to those blogs. It helps to think of insomnia as a mental disorder.

Reading your Redox Rx was also helpful. My recent DHA was 3.3% — not high but much better than it was 9 months ago (1.2%).
I have a TENS unit but have to learn more about how it can tell me about my Redox potential.

My Takeaway
I’ve overdosed on blue light and nnEMF for too long. My body developed ways to compensate and to make the most of what I was giving it: bad light and nnEMF interference.

My instinct is that my body was doing its best, coping — which felt “normal” to me. When I recently started giving it what its been wanting for decades (lots and lots of full spectrum light and grounding) I threw a wrench in the already gummy works and the current result is Bad Sleep.

My Plan
Reduce mass in my cells which are heavy with deuterium-rich water.

How?

Blue Light Protocol

-   No screens indoors.
-   No screens during outdoor morning sunrise time.
-   Always use IRIS (set at 2400K/100%), even outside — like I am right now on a covered deck in my yard.
-   No studio time for a while. Consider new restrictions on studio time in the future. I have some ideas already, including targeted recording sessions of up to two hours on alternating days. (Or some version of that, depending on the results of my current plan.)
-   Blueblockers on earlier than usual — before official sunset. I’m in a valley and the sun “sets” behind a mountain. That’s when I’ll block the blue from the TV and kitchen light — if they’re on.
-   Red lights come on at this time, too.
-   Blueblocked TV until 8pm only. Then it goes off and we use red light only, or with candles. Use this time for reading or cards or listening to music — via my speakers, not wired headphones.
-   Limit wired headphone use during the day to 1 hour.
-   No laptop/headphones after 4pm.
-   No bluetooth headphones ever.
-   Stay outside all day — as weather permits.
-   Leave windows open and use screen-doors. We have lots of skylights and windows so don’t use electric lights during the day. Lots of blue light coming through the skylights, I know, but we’ll temper it with natural light from our open windows and sliding-screen doors.

EMFs
Get body voltage meter and verify exactly what’s active in the house, especially the bedroom. I don’t know what action to take if it comes up dirty but I’ll research that and happily take guidance/advice (especially from Jack and Anthony).

How to Get More Net Negative Charge?

-   Grounding — but I’m doing that already.
-   AM sunlight, especially during Sunrise and UVA window — but I’m doing that already.
-   CT. I could add sessions of that multiple times a week in addition to swimming.
-   Get in the ocean when I’m near it. (7-10 days per month)
-   Get near ionized air = the ocean. ???
-   Embrace rainstorms = ionized air. ???
-   Use RLT panel in evening (sunset times) to boost mitochondrial health? (I have a full body one for use in winter when red and NIR light is hard to come by.)

Basically — get out of blue light, stay in the sun (especially AM and PM), get bare feet on the earth, minimize nnEMFs, eat more seafood, CT (even though it’s spring/summer).

Questions

-   Does using the computer outside mitigate blue light exposure? (I have IRIS, yes, but I’m curious.)
-   How does intensity of light play a part? Color does, yes — blue in isolation is no bueno but what about red (iPad filter) if the intensity is still high? Or if watching TV through blue blockers — what about the intensity of the light?
-   Does living near the ocean (not on the beach but near it, where you can still smell the ocean) play a part in optimal health, and/or Redox Potential?

Thank you in advance for reading and guiding me. It's very exciting to learn about something I've taken for granted my whole life. Light. Health. I'm learning a lot about myself and what's important to me.

#1 - turn off the WiFi router
IF you can’t and it needs to be on, be as far from it as possible and/or put a shield between you and it.
I do not cover routers, just shield the side of it you are on.

Lots of power lines in ES and I measured 8mG (VERY not good) walking down the street under them.
If the place you are staying in is close to the road with power lines get a room as far from them as possible.

Dirty electricity “DE” is BAD in ES. BUT - if you turn off the circuit the “DE” (on that circuit) goes to zero. Taking a few plug in filters for other circuits that need to be on would not hurt.

If you have the option to turn the breaker off to your room without affecting other rooms, turn it off. In most ”Mito” places this is not a problem.
Take meters . . Measure . . . And you will be equipped and empowered to reduce your exposure.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-26T00:25:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-353328>

There is a common misunderstanding in regard to the 60Hz AC electric field from the wires in the walls and from everything plugged into them and dirty electricity (“DE”) which is an artifact/harmonic of that electric field and a VERY small % of it.

The ”DE” people have done a great job in making people think in the world of EMF “DE” is THE problem. It is part of the problem BUT a VERY small part. They make lots of money selling “DE” filters (<5% of the problem) and leave the “elephant in the room” the 60Hz AC electric field untouched.

The odds are the Air Con (AC) is on its own circuit and you should be able to cut off that circuit in the panel. The fridge is most likely on the kitchen circuit and the outlets in the kitchen will work if the fridge is on (Not always but usually) for charging while at the beach. If you have meters you should be able to find and fix most problems.

### eelefter99 — 2025-05-27T13:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-353408>

OP here with some updates.

Several days ago at about 4 AM I had been lying awake for a couple of hours and was desperate. In the spirit of experimentation, I remembered what I learned from the blogs, I needed more net negative charge and I thought of the grounding mat that I had abandoned in fear a couple of months ago. I asked myself “what are you afraid of?” The thing is not gonna kill me. And I need the negative charge. So I gave it a try.

I was asleep in 10 minutes. Been using it every night since then and my sleep has been normalized. I sleep about seven hours. Problem solved? It’s not that easy.

It’s been rainy here, so I haven’t been getting my normal amounts of afternoon sun.

Yesterday, however, the skies were clear and I got about 8,000 IUs of vitamin D in a half hour. This morning I woke up at 3:45am.

I’m sitting here outside right now. Clear skies — going to be a lovely day. Looking back at my sleep journal… which I have been keeping for the past couple of months…. I noticed that on days where I get intense afternoon sun, my sleep is shorter.

Can anyone help me to understand how increased sunlight exposure would lead to decreased sleep? I noticed I get sleepy around 8:30 at night and I have to fight it to stay awake till 10. On days where I don’t get intense sun, I don’t get sleepy until 10ish.

Going to experiment today with cutting down the amount of intense, direct sunlight, I get. I use dminder and will err on the side of caution, instead of staying out the maximum possible duration.

One final thing I’ve noticed: since I’ve used the grounding mat under my sheet, I have very heavy eyes the next day. They look fine, but they feel heavy. More so than when I only get five hours of sleep. So I sleep more, but my eyes feel tired.

Fascinating, no?

-   Sleep relies on darkness to increase melatonin production, which absorbs UV light and modulates mTOR signaling. Poor sleep (e.g., due to blue light exposure or irregular cycles) reduces melatonin, disrupting the mtDNA-UPE-sunlight loop.
-   This increases neural noise (higher entropy) by impairing UPE-mediated signaling. In SCAN, this should manifest as reduced motor-cognitive integration, e.g., impaired planning or coordination.
-   Ketosis and Mitochondrial Stress:
    -   Ketosis shifts metabolism toward beta-oxidation, increasing mitochondrial workload (higher OXPHOS). This boosts UPE emission (via ROS, excited states) but also increases mtDNA heteroplasmy if mitochondria are stressed (e.g., from poor sleep).
    -   In my thesis, high heteroplasmy triggers apoptosis to remove defective mitochondria, but disrupted light-dark cycles impair this QA process (e.g., by reducing melatonin’s protective effects), leading to systemic effects like cancer. So this is how ketosis and cancer can be linked. It is counterintuitive but quite logical via quantum efficiency loss of the mechanism in cells.
-   Macroscopic Quantum Effect:
    -   The quantum effect lies in the UPE-mtDNA loop: poor sleep and ketosis amplify UPE emission (due to mitochondrial stress) but disrupt their coherence (due to circadian misalignment). This creates a measurable, macroscopic outcome, e.g., systemic inflammation, cognitive decline, or motor deficits (observable in SCAN’s functions).
    -   For example, increased UPE emission without proper light-dark anchoring might lead to “quantum noise,” manifesting as brain fog, fatigue, or motor errors and a phenomena we should be able to measure with EEG (neural coherence) or biomarkers (e.g., oxidative stress).
    -   This effect is macroscopic because it scales from quantum-level disruptions (UPE signaling) to observable physiological changes, supporting my view of the brain as a quantum antenna.

### eelefter99 — 2025-06-15T13:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-354661>

Here’s an update. Had the opportunity to be near the ocean for two weeks. In an effort to get some more free electrons and maximize my cellular battery, I got in the ocean every morning at 9:30 for anywhere from 15 to 20 minutes. First day was five minutes, second day was 10 minutes, third day was 20. Did this for seven straight days. And no, this is not my first time with cold water. I’ve been doing cold water immersion’s in my bathtub at 55 to 60° since November. My sleep started to become a real issue in January anyway my recent ocean time did not improve my sleep. But it did land me in the ER.

Heart palpitations. I’ve had them on and off since I started biohacking. They started in January. A flutter in my chest. After exercising, I would get it and it would calm down. I chalked it up to my lack of sleep.

Three nights ago after dinner, at 6:30, it started up and I could not get it to stop. It got worse, pounding. I lied down to try to sleep, but it did not stop. I tried Valsalva techniques, cold water on the face for the vagus nerve, breathing, nothing worked. It was not subtle. And it was very frequently skipping a beat. Pounding. So I drove down to the ER.

Did a couple of EKGs on me. Captured the palpitation. Tell me my blood work was all fine, but when I looked, I noticed I had a low red blood count, low alkaline phosphate, low MCV and MCH, Low hemoglobin. Nothing huge, but lower than the desired range. Also put me on 12.5 mg of Metoprolol. I’m meeting with a cardiologist next week.

I should also mention that I stopped using the computer indoors. I use it very rarely at all now. Same goes for the phone. Again I don’t use it much. Dictating outside right now.

And yes, I woke up again at 3:15. So no matter how many emfs I limit, how many electrons I collect, or how much sun I get… my sleep is still fucked.

And now my heart, maybe too.

I’m afraid my palpitations will never go away and it will be something I’ll have to live with. Last summer this time before I heard about light or grounding or any of this… before the Rick Ruben podcast… I was sleeping until five, 5:30, my heart was a non-issue, and my health was not a concern.

You could argue that it would’ve been a concern in time and maybe all these protocols just accelerated things. You could argue that these issues would’ve come up regardless. You could also argue that tinkering with my health cost me some health.

I still believe in light. I still believe in grounding. I still believe in cold. Correlation is not causation. Maybe it’s not the ocean exposure that landed me in the ER. Maybe my sleep problems and heart palpitations would’ve come along anyway. But… Something is going on. And it was not going on before.

### eelefter99 — 2025-07-23T17:53:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-356856>

Update. I've started eating fish. Daily. Sardines mostly. About one month ago. The idea being to get some DHA in me so that my brain and heart can get on the same page. Also: more grounding on my deck via a copper rod in the soil and a mat. And...15mgs of DHEA each morning. Recent bloodwork showed my levels at 206 (DHEA-S). I could take more but I don't want to tax my heart and so I'm going slow. Will test again to see the results.

Took echo stress test and verified that my heart is in great shape. No plaque. Nothing wrong with the muscle. Cardiologist says it's PACs and that I'm good to go back to running / swimming. Says this is just something I've got to live with now. Put me on Metoprolol @ 50mgs and sent me on my way.

Since I've been on sardines, DHEA, and grounding more...the PAC (fluttering, skipping) feelings have gotten quieter. Used to be I could feel them in my throat and bouncing in my chest. Now, I can barely feel them but when I put my fingers to my neck, I can feel my pulse skipping beats. My solution is: don't put my fingers to my neck.

Is the improvement due to the Metoprolol? I don't think so. Why? I've been on 50mgs since this happened on June 12 and I've had palpitations so bad that I've had to send a pal I had over for dinner home. Had to leave an outdoor concert in the park earlier than I wanted. Had to just not stand up cause that'd trigger pounding and bouncing in my chest. All while on this drug.

My goal is to get off the Metoprolol and surprise my docs when these PACs stop altogether. But...I'm not there yet. I've dialed back the Metoprolol to 25mgs. Was feeling confident (and still am, in spite of...) today's run produced a racing heart and lots of skipped beats. Had to bail after one mile. (Was only aiming for 2.5). It was hot out this morning and I was going too fast too soon. That's what I think anyway. Heart's back to normal now.

My sleep has been improving too. I'm getting back to sleep after waking in the dark hours....I've stopped looking at the clock.

And...I tried hypnotherapy to address my anxiety, depression, and overall usual gloom that used to define my outlook on everything. My grandmother called the two of us "Doctor Doom and Nurse Dismal." She's gone now. But, since my hypno-session, so is Doctor Doom. I'm thinking about learning to be a hypnotherapist myself so that I can help others deal with whatever needs clearing out.

Anyway...that's where I am now. Still working on this heart thing and this sleep thing but I've doubled down on fish and I'm lucky it turns out that I like sardines.

More anon...till then, take care.

### eelefter99 — 2025-08-22T13:12:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-358613>

Premature ventricular contractions (PVCs) are irregular heartbeats that occur when the electrical signal to start a heartbeat comes from the lower chambers of the heart instead of the upper chambers. A PAC, or premature atrial contraction, is an extra heartbeat that occurs in the upper chambers of the heart, temporarily disrupting its normal rhythm. Both are common and usually not dangerous, but if they happen frequently, they may indicate an underlying heart condition.

Exercise that results in tachycardia (a condition where the heart beats faster than normal) means that the heart is beating much faster than expected or safe: in an adult male over 50 -- anything that stays over 170 after exercise (in my case, running) ends is "exercise-induced tachycardia."

### eelefter99 — 2026-01-18T17:54:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-since-i-started-biohacking.30937/post-365534>

I thought I'd update, in case this is of use to anyone.

My sleep has transformed. I don't wake up and stay up anymore. If I wake to pee, I go back to sleep. I pee once per night -- but more and more, don't need to pee at all. Bed by 10pm and wake between 6 and 7am.

What did I do differently?

I studied hypnosis. I practice it daily on myself and have been doing so since August.

The reason I haven't written in nearly 6 months is that I wanted to test my work before putting anything in writing.

I've helped myself with chronic pain (lower back, elbows, knee). I've addressed my past anger and anxiety issues. I cleared my sinuses back in October with one 5 minute session and I now breath clearly and freely. I was allergic to dust and fall leaves (mold). No more. I was allergic to cats. No more. I can either make a headache go away completely or take it way back to something that doesn't have my attention anymore -- before I know it, it's gone. If my back feels like it's tightening, I know what to do and within minutes, it's no longer an issue. But the biggest thing, besides not being so anxious and so angry anymore (stress and anger happen, yes, but they don't go nearly as deep and last a fraction of the time they used to last)...the biggest thing is my sleep. Read the above thread to see how I used to be. All of that's gone.

For other people (my wife, friends, strangers I met at holiday gatherings):
\- my wife's chronic knee pain, headaches (gone)
\- my friend had chronic anxiety over meetings at work -- no more
\- another had a cyst in her eye that her doc wanted to remove surgically (it was benign but bothersome to her) -- now it's gone (within 2 days of treatment)
\- her son had a ganglion on his wrist (he's a jazz pianist) -- that's reduced dramatically and expected to be gone (I only treated him a few days ago)
\- a woman at a party had chronic shoulder pain -- gone
\- a guy at the pool I swim at also had chronic shoulder pain, and back pain and arthritic thumb pain -- gone
\- a woman who I study hypnosis with was experiencing "extreme sadness" in her early months of her 2nd pregnancy -- now she tells me she's feels free and that her life is "transformed" and "awesome"
\- a musician friend couldn't pick up the guitar anymore without feeling "pressure to come up with something great" -- now he's back to having fun playing and writing like he hasn't in years

I could go on but you get the point.

Just consider this: your experience isn't something fixed. You can do something about your pain, your problems, your stuckness. You can move in the direction of wellness and well being and health and productivity. Your mind is powerful. Your thoughts are powerful. Just as powerful as your biology. Maybe your thoughts influence your biology. Maybe you can do something now you didn't think was possible before.

Explore these ideas.

If you want to reach out and connect with me, I'd be happy to hear about where you're at and what you want to have happen. If I feel that I can help you move in a positive direction...even help you to get free of your pain and teach you ways you can do it for yourself, I'm happy to do that.

It's not just hypnosis. There are simple things you can learn in a few minutes that will make a positive impact on your life.

As someone who uses only red light after sundown and grounds and practices cold thermogenesis, eats a lot more fish (esp. sardines) than ever before...in short: I practice what Jack preaches -- and -- I also practice a deeper understanding of my mind and how it influences all of this.

I hope this helps someone.

And yes....all of this works perfectly over Zoom.

And no...I don't have a website or anything like that. I'm just a guy with some powerful new tools. I've used them successfully for myself and others and the results stick. I'm still learning but I'm confident that this stuff works. It's simple, painless and continues to unfold in positive ways over time. Why not try to share it with others who are in pain?

I work with those who I share something with -- a life, a way of life, core values. My family. My friends. People I meet out in the world who I see how I can help very quickly -- and without their needing to "believe" any of this, because it works regardless of whether or not you believe it. It's based on neuroplasticity. Science. So believe whatever you want. If something works and helps -- isn't that the point? I'm only writing this because I was in a bad place and now I'm in a much better place. I understand how I got here. And I share a lot with a lot of you.

Take care and I hope you feel better...however you get there.

Ed

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-19T01:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-supplementation-risks.30939/post-352855>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Beware the hidden risks of creatine supplementation. Major brands rarely disclose levels of deuterium, dicyandiamide (DCDA), or triazines, toxic residues from manufacturing that slip through even third-party testing. Deuterium, a heavy hydrogen isotope, disrupts cellular energy. DCDA and triazines? Potentially carcinogenic byproducts that no one’s quantifying. Certificates of Analysis (COAs) claiming “purity” often dodge these contaminants, leaving you gambling with your health.

Dr. A. Benzi, a pharmacologist and early creatine skeptic, nailed it: “Isolating one molecule and mega-dosing it ignores the symphonic interplay of nutrients in whole foods.” Synthetic creatine isn’t nature’s version. This is a complex topic with limited direct data, but the physics of the chemicals often found in creating due to its manufacturing suggests nnEMF, especially 2.4–5 GHz (Wi-Fi) and 24–100 GHz (5G), should amplify DCDA and triazine toxicity by enhancing reactivity, spin perturbations, and ROS. This would have massive effects on UPEs are a result.

Triazines’ UV absorption (200–300 nm) exacerbates these factors. DCDA and triazines, especially when coupled with 3G to 5G nnEMF excitation, should be expected to disrupt mitochondrial proton hopping by mimicking deuterium’s kinetic isotope effect. nnEMF’s spin perturbations may randomize hydrogen spin states (ortho/para), slowing the Grotthuss mechanism and increasing ROS, as I’ve linked to bipolar disorder and neurological issues. Decentralized medicine looks at issues and topics that are problematic for mitochondria, whereas allopathic and functional medicine do not even tell people to expect a problem.

nnEMF’s dielectric effects on DCDA and triazines could alter tissue refractive indices, impairing biophoton propagation. A 2017 Journal of Biophotonics study (DOI: 10.1002/jbio.201600226) confirms EMF alters optical properties. So if you are a tech abuser, stay away from these products. Employ the precaution principle.

# Thread: Tara's optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-optimal-journal.30945/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tarascribbins — 2025-05-19T21:50:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-optimal-journal.30945/post-352929>

I am so grateful to be learning about decentralization, and savagery. let the games begin.

I am currently a 43 year old mother of 5 amazing kids. 4 boys, and the baby is a girl. (no c-sections) ages 9-1, I live in Wichita, KS, and I have had two rounds of the pfizer jab. I take 30mg of NP Thyroid.

Here's my story:

I was 41 years old and about 13-14 weeks post partum around the end of November. I had resumed Brazilian jiu jitsu and went in to roll. I was fighting a larger man, and a i was pushed down to the ground. The world started spinning. I thought i was dying. I had my husband take me to the ER, but ultimately left because the wait was so long. I went and had an IV done, thinking I was dehydrated from breastfeeding. I was still not feeling well, crashing into walls and feeling as if the floor was moving. I went back to the ER that night. Vertigo. I still felt off and was later diagnosed with post concussive disorder. I sought upper cervical care, and went to see Dr. Mickey Collins at UPMC for my vestibular concussion. I was finally starting to feel better, following his protocol but then it gets weirder.

I started getting sinus infections, styes on my eyelids, dry eye disease, meibomian gland dysfunction, and the worst allergies i have ever had in my life. I ended up having 7 sinus infections, and 7 rounds of antibiotics. I ended up having sinus surgery. The infections persisted. I then started getting ulcers, heartburn, kidney stones, migraines, and the vertigo continued. I pretty much thought I was just going to die. I even had to go to infectious disease for my sinus infection after a swab indicated enterobacter. Finally in October there was a breakthrough! My right breast implant had capsular contracted. I explanted and started feeling better about 2 months later. My neighbor told my husband about the Danny Jones podcast, and I listened....and my life changed. I immediately listened to every podcast I could from our dear Uncle Jack. In November 2024 I had a surface Melanoma removed. I have a lovely scar on my back .
I have made significant progress feeling almost like my old self again. I still struggle with year round allergies (Wichita is the allergy capital of the USA), and dry eye and MGD. I make poor quality oil apparently. I no longer drink coffee, and the heartburn is gone.
I watch the sunrise and hit the goldern hour of sunset daily. I embrace the darkness, wear blue blockers, monitor my tech use, switched out my light bulbs, ground and eat all the seafood i can. I drink DDW daily. I am so tan the moms at school are asking me about it. I had my breasts reconstructed with fat recently, so I am nervous to do too much with ice baths just yet. Can I leave the girls out, or will the fat transfer fail? How do i beat these new seasonal allergies? I take ivermectin for the jabs a few times a week. I have only been on NP Thyroid for a year. I was taking 60 mg but it was giving me SVT. I would like to get off it. Should I still see the dermatologist? What can i read, listen to beat the allergies, and continue improving. I just finished the 2012 webinars. I started the twist 25 DHEA. Should I book a 30 or 60 consult? I am ready to be optimal. I will take all the advice. I am grateful to be feeling good again.

LFG.
Tara


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: This Hit Me Hard — Schools Are Building for Autism Now
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-hit-me-hard-%E2%80%94-schools-are-building-for-autism-now.30946/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-05-19T22:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-hit-me-hard-%E2%80%94-schools-are-building-for-autism-now.30946/post-352931>

Was listening to Jack Kruse talk about how autism rates are exploding while I’m bidding a new middle/high school. He’s breaking down how it’s not genetics — it’s the environment we’ve built.

Blue light. No sunlight. Screens everywhere. Kids stuck indoors all day under fake light, eating garbage, staring at iPads.

Then I look up from the plans I’m bidding…

And I see THIS.

-   “SC AUTISTIC GRADES 5-6 CLASSROOM”
-   “SC AUTISTIC GRADES 7-8”
-   “SC AUTISM CLASSROOM”

Like it’s just a normal part of the blueprint now.

What used to be rare is now expected. Built into the floorplan.

This isn’t progress. It’s a silent admission that we’ve messed up our environment so bad that we’re designing schools for sick kids as the new normal.

It’s not just sad — it’s insane how blind we’ve been.

Once you start waking up to this stuff… you can’t _unsee_ it. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. # Thread: Grounding Blankets / Tents for Camping

### Mr.AJ — 2025-05-20T01:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-blankets-tents-for-camping.30950/post-352952>

Can anyone recommend a tent or blanket setup that will keep you grounded while sleeping. Lots of rocks and scorpions where I'm at so sleeping on the ground is not very pleasant. And AFAIK polyester/ fake fabrics are mostly ungrounding you.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-20T02:19:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-blankets-tents-for-camping.30950/post-352959>

Most tents these days have a built in plastic floor to keep you dry and the critters out. The ONLY time I would recommend a grounding pad is in a tent outside and use a SHORT ground wire WITHOUT a resistor built in and a ground spike right outside the tent.

### MattB99 — 2026-03-21T20:38:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-blankets-tents-for-camping.30950/post-369327>

I have wondered this too. I would think a floorless tent with animals skins or wool blankets for a mattress/blanket would be best. I would like to test this out soon.
