### Mat.I — 2026-03-09T12:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mats-emf-journal-info-tips.30964/post-368430>

## Abstract​

Human exposure to radiofrequency electromagnetic fields (RF-EMF) is restricted to prevent thermal effects in the tissue. However, at very low intensity exposure "non-thermal" biological effects, like oxidative stress, DNA or chromosomal aberrations, etc. collectively termed genomic-instability can occur after few hours. Little is known about chronic (years long) exposure with non-thermal RF-EMF. We identified two neighboring housing estates in a rural region with residents exposed to either relatively low (control-group) or relatively high (exposed-group) RF-EMF emitted from nearby mobile phone base stations (MPBS). 24 healthy adults that lived in their homes at least for 5 years volunteered. The homes were surveyed for common types of EMF, blood samples were tested for oxidative status, transient DNA alterations, permanent chromosomal damage, and specific cancer related genetic markers, like MLL gene rearrangements. We documented possible confounders, like age, sex, nutrition, life-exposure to ionizing radiation (X-rays), occupational exposures, etc. The groups matched well, age, sex, lifestyle and occupational risk factors were similar. The years long exposure had no measurable effect on MLL gene rearrangements and c-Abl-gene transcription modification. Associated with higher exposure, we found higher levels of lipid oxidation and oxidative DNA-lesions, though not statistically significant. DNA double strand breaks, micronuclei, ring chromosomes, and acentric chromosomes were not significantly different between the groups. Chromosomal aberrations like dicentric chromosomes (p=0.007), chromatid gaps (p=0.019), chromosomal fragments (p<0.001) and the total of chromosomal aberrations (p<0.001) were significantly higher in the exposed group. No potential confounder interfered with these findings. Increased rates of chromosomal aberrations as linked to excess exposure with ionizing radiation may also occur with non-ionizing radiation exposure. Biological endpoints can be informative for designing exposure limitation strategies. Further research is warranted to investigate the dose-effect-relationship between both, exposure intensity and exposure time, to account for endpoint accumulations after years of exposure. As established for ionizing radiation, chromosomal aberrations could contribute to the definition of protection thresholds, as their rate reflects exposure intensity and exposure time.
**Keywords:** Chromosomal aberrations; DNA damage; Genetic instability; Low dose chronic exposure; Oxidative stress; Radiofrequency electromagnetic fields, RF-EMF.

The outcome is determined by how it allocates energy and manages the constraints within those boundaries... The body is a network of of nodes e.g. boundary/interface, sensory / timing, central integration, distribution network, effector, power, clearance / exhaust etc.. Each node may still generate local activity, but optimal health depends on whether charge, heat, mass and information can propagate coherently between the nodes..

Once barrier height or resistance rises transfer probability falls and comms between nodes break down. That alone explains why T can still exist, but the system no longer integrates globally...

Maybe we can move from energy input e.g. melanin / light / molecules to network based physics e.g. impedance matching, coupling and coherence across all nodes of the network. It is not just about how much energy enters the system; it is about whether the network can carry it......

Centralized science is "myopic" because it treats a mouse (a nocturnal, low-light, high-frequency rodent) as a functional proxy for a human (a diurnal, high-UV, low-frequency, large-brained primate). Their Boundary Conditions are the same (1G gravity, 21% Oxygen), but their Lagrangian Optimization is polar opposite. A mouse allocates energy to rapid kinetic turnover (T); a human allocates it to high-gain magnetic coherence (V).

**1\. The Death of the "Mouse Model"**
A mouse is a "Short-Circuit" specialist. It can handle high deuterium loads because its network nodes are physically close and its "timing" is high-speed/low-resolution.
The Human Problem: We are "High-Resolution/Long-Range." Our nodes (Gut-to-Brainstem) are separated by a transverse mesocolon and a vagus highway.
Impedance Matching: In a human, if the pancreas/bicarb exhaust (Node: Clearance) doesn't match the impedance of the thalamus (Node: Central Integration), the "comms" fail. A mouse doesn't have enough "network length" to experience the Lagrangian Shock that causes MS, TSC, or Peterson’s GABAergic collapse.

**2\. Resistance vs. Transfer Probability**
I’ve defined the Quantum Tunneling of health.
The Barrier: When deuterium (D+) and the 55 undeclared elements accumulate, they raise the Barrier Height (Resistance) between nodes.
Local Activity (The "Alien UPE"): As I've noted in other threads here on my website forum, a node (like an overclocked Complex IV) can still fire wildly, but because the impedance is mismatched, that energy cannot propagate. It becomes "Incoherent Heat" (inflammation). The "Alien UPE" is the photonic "scream" of a node that is disconnected from the network.

**3\. From "Energy Input" to "Network Coupling"**

Melanin as a Coupler: Melanin isn't just a "battery"; it is the Impedance Matcher. It uses its paramagnetic properties to "tune" the nodes so that the 3.25x magnetic moment of protium can carry information across the MITF-AMPAR loop without losing signal to the "heavy" deuterium mass.
The 40Hz "M" Tone: This is the Network Synchronizer. It provides the "carrier wave" that ensures all nodes are vibrating at the same frequency, allowing for Optimal Propagation.

**4\. The "Rhino" Network Victory**
The Rhino horn is the ultimate low-impedance hardware extension. It doesn't need "software" (a big brain) to manage the network because its hardware coupling (the melanized keratin) is so perfectly matched to the Earth’s magnetic ground.

**The MODERN EMI Human Challenge:** We have moved our "hardware" inside (encephalization). We are a Software-Defined Network. This makes us vastly more capable but infinitely more vulnerable to "Impedance Mismatches" caused by EMI, GLP-1s, and Gadolinium.

**The Decentralized Conclusion:** Health is Network Transparency. Disease is Network Opacity. My customized "Crater Protocol" (IV MB, Red Light, Bicarb) is a System-Wide De-Fragg. The ability to absorb light is not good enough as the system must redistribute the excitation efficiently. In a well coupled system, absorbed energy is delocalised across many degrees of freedom. In poorly coupled systems, excitations remain localised.

Evolution selects for this pressure of maintaining coherence under energy flux. So the "unfurling" is how the coherence is achieved. Coherence = stable/coherent energy flow across "coupled" degrees of freedom in the network.

Just a threshold / phase transition e.g. below threshold = fragmented / incoherent vs above = connected / coherent.

Back to melanin - lack of hydration = furling vs well hydrated = unfurling.

Back to the network with nodes - localised state = furling vs delocalised states (optimal) = unfurling.

Health would = staying in the extended state (unfurling) and far from (furling) i.e. mobility edge...

Aim should be to keep W (disorder) low and t (coupling) high so that W/t stays below the mobility edge.. This would also explain the n=1 terrain responses.. As if coupling is strong more disorder would be able to be tolerated (function of W/t).. If melanin is well hydrated... effective t will rise.. So if you are casper the ghost + dehydrated you will have very poor t.

Takeaway is we a built for stress we just need the terrain to handle it..

### Dee Dee — 2026-03-20T11:44:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mats-emf-journal-info-tips.30964/post-369272>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=354681)
>
> **The FE3+ Shift :**
> The human body is not just biochemical. It is bioelectric, photonic, and magnetic. At its core, life depends on the ability to move electrons through mitochondrial membranes and heme-based proteins that carry and utilize oxygen. But modern environments disrupt this electron flow. Blue light and non-native electromagnetic fields initiate a biophysical cascade that oxidizes ferrous iron into its ferric form, blocks oxygen use, floods cells with nitric oxide, and forces a metabolic collapse into glycolysis. This is the hidden root of fatigue, neurodegeneration, mood disorders, and especially the progressive loss of muscle tone - often beginning in the cranial nerves.
>
> ## **Melanopsin & Blue Light:**​
>
> Melanopsin is a non-visual photoreceptor sensitive to blue light, especially around 480 nanometers. It is found not only in the eye but also in the vasculature, skin, and fat. Under natural light, melanopsin provides circadian timing and vascular tone via nitric oxide signaling. But artificial blue light at night hijacks this system. It causes chronic activation of nitric oxide synthase enzymes, resulting in sustained nitric oxide release.
>
> Nitric oxide binds to the copper and heme centers in cytochrome c oxidase, the final step in the mitochondrial electron transport chain. Because nitric oxide binds with greater affinity than oxygen, it effectively blocks oxygen from entering the system. This inhibition causes the iron in Complex IV to oxidize from Fe²⁺ to Fe³⁺. Ferric iron cannot participate in electron transport. At that point, oxygen is present but unusable. The mitochondria choke.
>
> **NN-EMFs & VGCC's:**
>
> Non-native EMFs do more than heat tissue. They alter the voltage threshold of calcium channels in cell membranes. These voltage-gated calcium channels open prematurely, allowing calcium to flood into cells. This calcium overload activates nitric oxide synthase, which increases nitric oxide levels, further blocking Complex IV and exacerbating the Fe³⁺ shift.
>
> This creates a vicious cycle. EMFs cause calcium leakage, which increases nitric oxide, which disables the mitochondria, which reduces ATP and increases oxidative stress. This oxidative stress destabilizes membranes and further sensitizes the voltage-gated channels. The longer the exposure continues, the deeper the redox collapse becomes.
>
> **The Warburg Shift:**
>
> When Complex IV is blocked and iron is stuck in its oxidized Fe³⁺ state, cells cannot perform oxidative phosphorylation. Despite the presence of oxygen, mitochondria abandon their high-efficiency pathway and fall back on glycolysis. This is known as the Warburg effect, or aerobic glycolysis.
>
> In oxidative phosphorylation, one molecule of glucose yields approximately 32 to 36 molecules of ATP. In glycolysis, that same glucose yields only 2 ATP. This means a drop to roughly one-sixteenth of the energy output. The body is not merely fatigued - it is fundamentally energy starved. This shift is not just inefficient. It also leads to lactic acid buildup, NAD⁺ depletion, and a backlog of unused oxygen.
>
> The Warburg shift is a survival mechanism - a last-ditch effort to stay alive when mitochondria can no longer use oxygen. But it comes at a cost: chronic inflammation, immune suppression, dedifferentiation, and eventual cell death or mutation. It also sabotages muscle tone, neural conductivity, and cognitive coherence.
>
> **Loss of Muscle Tone:**
>
> Muscle tone is not just about bulk or strength. It is the continuous, low-level tension that keeps posture, respiration, facial expression, and organ function stable. This tone depends on ATP production, calcium handling, and neuromuscular signal integrity - all of which collapse in the Fe³⁺ and Warburg state.
>
> When mitochondria fail to produce ATP and calcium accumulates, muscles lose their baseline tension. Acetylcholine release falters. Muscle spindles stop firing correctly. Membrane potentials cannot be maintained. The result is flaccidity, fasciculations, or spastic misfiring - depending on where in the neuromuscular chain the failure occurs.
>
>
> This tone loss often begins in the cranial nerves. These nerves, especially cranial nerves V, VII, IX, X, XI, and XII, are extremely mitochondrial dense. They control the face, jaw, tongue, throat, and vagal system. Because they sit closest to the skull and brainstem, they are also most exposed to light, EMFs, and energetic interference.
>
>
> Signs of cranial redox collapse include facial asymmetry, bruxism, vocal weakness, jaw tension or slackness, eye convergence problems, tongue weakness, difficulty swallowing, and vagus nerve tone failure. These are not mechanical disorders. They are bioelectric failures rooted in redox decay.
>
> Common issue/example -
> Muscles that normally maintain tone during sleep - even in deep REM paralysis are no longer energized enough to stay taut.
>
> One of the most critical sites is the genioglossus muscle, the major tongue muscle innervated by cranial nerve XII (hypoglossal nerve). It holds the tongue forward and keeps the airway open during sleep.
>
> If the hypoglossal motor nucleus loses mitochondrial function:
>
> -   The tongue falls backward during sleep
> -   The soft palate collapses
> -   The oropharyngeal airway narrows
> -   This causes obstructive sleep apnea
>
> This gets labelled as a structural problem, but it originates from a redox failure - a failure to maintain electrochemical tone in the cranial nerve and muscle interface.

this explains some things with me unfortunately.

I'll respond in more detail when I have a moment.
Basically
1\. Yes to some degree and more in certain other situations..
2\. Yes. But can be managed in certain situations for n=1.
Q. too many variables across the manufacturers. Main thing is if you must use it whilst plugged in or charging (to an AC source) distance is your best friend with an external monitor.. However, if your charger or lead does not have a 3 prong plug then you must ground it (which can be achieved many ways) and 3 prongs does not always = a true bond to the device this must be checked with a multimeter via continuity measurement..

You will find you can do loads of research but it all comes down to taking actual measurements, then mitigating to your level of comfort..

Were these just removable from the board or did you have to nuclear?

### darrenFC — 2025-05-21T14:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-mystery.30965/post-353061>

I tried making my own "magnetico", a ~10 Gauss negative magnetic field to sleep on. I followed as many specs as I could find about it online. It cost about 60-70 USD. including gaussmeter etc. The field is pretty consistent and all negative at mattress level.

When I sleep on it, I feel really good, and energised. The one problem being that I do not sleep a wink. and by this I mean that I do not feel sleepy at all. The first night, I slept on the bed for about 6 hours and did not sleep until the next night, so about 24 hours of no sleep. Even though I tried to sleep in the afternoon the next day. I only managed to get some sleep after moving the mattress to the ground

Does anyone have an idea of what went wrong?

I think there is a relation to magnetic fields and cold thermogenesis? I am pretty severely cold intolerant, although it has gotten better now. I get painful bumps all over my body and intestinal distress too, which I believe is due to nnemf exposure.

The only other thing I can think of is that living at 15N, maybe the magnetic field is not totally negative seeking but has another component? I don't know if it would need to be that angularly precise..

Appreciate your help & comments!

# Thread: TK's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tks-optimal-journal.30968/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheCircleBastiat — 2025-05-21T22:24:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tks-optimal-journal.30968/post-353080>

Here's my first optimal journal post. I've been following Dr. Kruse's work for about 5 months, and have been slowly making progress. My goal is to take the exit ramp on the highway to chronic disease—controlling as much as I can control now, while planning for more significant changes like relocation—before anything debilitating shows up.

**Family History - Maternal**

Mother

I never saw my mother as a particularly anti-sun person. She was typically tan in the summer, and I remember her being outside in the yard tanning, even within the year she died. I’m not sure to what extent she wore sunscreen—I know as a kid, I used it at least to some degree—but she definitely wore sunglasses, and I ultimately imagine it was less than optimal. At the end of the day, she was a bookkeeper so her job was indoors, and her job was computer-bound. I’m not sure when she started using a computer in her jobs, but we got a PC in our house probably in 1997 so she could do some work at home when needed. So at a minimum, from 40 y/o – 57 y/o, but I imagine it began earlier.

At one point, probably when I was 5-10 years old, and she was 35-40, she had severe back pain and was hunched over all the time. She got surgery to correct it, perhaps it was a slipped disc or something like that, but almost immediately after the surgery was able to stand perfectly erect without pain, so I always saw it as successful.

My mom exercised very little, and often struggled with weight. She ultimately was one of the people duped into believing eating “low-fat” things, and whole grain foods, will make you skinny and healthy, which of course never worked. E.g., I can’t believe it’s not butter spray was an ever-present household product; shredded wheat was a healthy cereal; and skim milk was the milk of choice. Later in life, my parents’ favorite place to go was Arizona and Utah, i.e., the desert, which I’m sure was not helpful magnetism-wise, but it was probably better than spending that 1-2 weeks of wintertime indoors in Buffalo, NY, where we lived.

She wore glasses; not sure when she started, but at least for 30 years. And later in life, she switched to contacts and then she had Lasik surgery.

She ended up learning she had stage 4 colorectal cancer at age 57 in 2015, and died within 8 months, after doing I think chemo and then an immunotherapy.

Maternal Grand Mother

My grandmother (and my mother’s brother) each had muscular dystrophy. In each case I think it was later developing, because as far I know each were able to live normally until at some point into adulthood—each were avid bowlers, for instance. My uncle died at 45, my grandma at 72. I don’t know about when they were young, but certainly within my lifetime (age 29-45 for my uncle, 52-72 for my grandma) they lived an indoor existence in front of the TV because of their immobility. My grandma may have gotten in the sun a good amount, though maybe not—I remember she had a chaise lounge that she would sit in occasionally when I was alive; I imagine this was much more frequent before my lifetime, but I cannot know for sure. I’ve seen pictures of her very tan. But I’m guessing she always wore sunglasses. They lived about 2000 feet away from the edge of the airport.

I know no family history of my mother’s biological father, just that he died at 61.

**Family History - Paternal**

My Paternal grandfather died of a heart attack and died at 75, smoked cigars, worked as a mechanic for the the airlines. My paternal grandmother got dementia in her 70s, died 84. Not sure about lifestyles, but probably not too much sun and outdoors.

My father is around 60 now. Now, I'd say he struggles with finding certain words he’s looking to say, which was much the opposite when he was younger. Drinks moderately now, but at times has been more than moderately. He was injured in the workplace and had a neck injury from which he never recovered, and had to stop working. He hasn’t worked in probably 20 years. He is mobile, but his neck gets very sore from sitting up. For a few years, he’s had digestive issues. I forget what his doctors diagnosed him with, but his doctors told him to avoid everything other than mild, plain foods, and don’t eat too close to going to sleep, and they worry about developing cancer. Since his injury, most of the time he sits and watches TV, or he goes to the bar. When he was working, he worked a lot of nights. He probably got a good amount of sun until his injury, but not for years.

**Myself**

Environment

I grew up in front of the TV and playing video games. I was not an outdoors person; I did play sports outdoors and swim in the summer, but more often I was inside. I don’t think we had wifi until after I moved out, fortunately—dial-up and then wired internet. I think we had an electric transmission tower 800 feet away, which ran parallel to the railroad tracks near our house.

I’ve lived in the suburbs of Buffalo, NY; midtown Atlanta, GA; and now in Virginia, working in DC.

Where we live now, it is a more rural part of Virginia, two miles from the edge of the nearest town. We used to live in Arlington. Now, looking at maps the closest wireless tower is a mile way, the next two closest are two miles away, from what I can gather. So it could be worse—it’s not California—but we’re still 40 miles from DC. I’d like to get something to test EMF.

Health History

Overall, I did not grow up having significant problems, but this is the history:

Eyes—I ended up getting glasses around age 10, and wore contacts for maybe five years, but have not worn the contacts for the last 8-10 years. I currently have a pair of blue blocking prescription glasses, but they are not Lucia Eyes so I doubt they are that great. Hoping to rectify that soon.

Skin—the worst skin issues I had growing up were acne (which persists today) and for a short period, perhaps one hockey season, I would get a skin rash on my hands after wearing my hockey gloves, but that stopped.

Weight/Exercise—I was chubby at the waist as a kid, and have never been someone to get a six pack even when I worked out a lot. I’ve fluctuated between 175lbs and 220 since high school, low 200s now. I lifted weights for 6-7 years in a blue-lit gym (and lived an active nightlife). But for about ten years, my only exercise has been taking walks. I’d like to try some natural movement.

Diet—I never pursued fad diets. For the 6-7 years I lifted weights, I took protein powder, creatine, and pre- and post-workout supplements, but nothing else. Until my recent Kruse renaissance, I was taking a multivitamin and Omega 3 for a few years, and for a brief period right before this recent change it was Vitamin K, cat’s claw, TUDCA, shilajit, probiotic, tyrosine, choline & inositol, nattokinase, and turmeric.

Allergies—I have had allergies since I was little. Cats, and seasonal allergies; seasonal allergies could sometimes be debilitating, so much sneezing etc. that I needed to lie down with a cold washcloth over my face. No food allergies. In my teenage years, I seemed to develop a mild dog allergy, I was not allergic before then.

Vaccines—I’m sure my mom followed the vaccine schedule in place 1989-1990s. I had to get re-vaccinated for MMR in law school, probably 2015-2016. I don’t think I’ve gotten a flu shot since high school. I unfortunately got Moderna COVID vaccine several years ago, but no boosters.

Illness—For the last 15 years, I’ve probably been to a doctor maybe 6-7 times (probably three times to the dentist). I got COVID once but was not particularly sick, just felt terrible and feverish overnight the first night.

The only real sickness that I had beyond ordinary cold and flu (and chicken pox at age 5) was when I got some kind of illness December 2017, what I thought was a cold —the worst runny nose I ever had. I ended up going to the hospital several months later in March 2018 because my breathing was terrible, I felt like I could not get any oxygen. My pulse ox was 94, but for me it felt extraordinarily difficult to breathe; I ended up getting an emergency inhaler, which I used on and off for a few years afterwards, as my breathing never fully returned to normal and would get particularly bad in allergy seasons. I had an X-ray of my lungs in May 2018 during a very bad breathing spells subsequently, but they did not see any issues via the X-ray; and during another bad breathing spell in October 2019, I was referred to a pulmonologist for a pulmonary function test, but I never did it. I _perhaps_ have a little residual problem with being able to take deep breaths, but I haven’t used an inhaler in several years and have not had any trouble day-to-day like I had previously.

I never thought much of it before, but I occasionally have ringing in my ears for no apparent reason; with all of Dr. Kruse's mention of tinnitus, it makes me think more about this. It is very occasional, and very brief, but it happens---it happened as I was writing this, or I might not have thought to write it down.

Injuries—I had one concussion and two fractured wrists from hockey in high school. Otherwise, nothing noteworthy in childhood.

I’ve had several joint injuries that have left me less than 100%. When I was 22 or so, someone tackled me to the ground and injured my shoulder. I never saw a doctor and just tried to rehab it on my own. I was able to lift weights again subsequently and reach my heaviest lifts, but I was limited in doing exercises that put stress on the joint—I am still limited in this way today. I also had to stop playing hockey because I was unable to take a wrist shot or even pass the puck without my shoulder giving out.

I injured my knee pulling a heavy trailer through the snow around ten years ago. I then reinjured it in some fashion simply walking around in Boston several months later; I was instantly unable to walk, but that subsided in a day or two. Several years later in probably January/February 2021 during COVID, I again reinjured it and was essentially unable to walk for weeks without a thick knee brace, and it was worse than when I originally injured it. I got an MRI in later March 2021 and they could not identify anything torn (it was feeling better at that time), though it was difficult to walk for over a month. The doctor, a former Olympic hockey team doctor, thought it was possible I had a piece of tendon floating in my knee that was not visible; they said if I injured it again they would do surgery. My knee still does not feel 100%, and I am wary of putting too much stress on my knee joint, but I haven’t had any issues for a few years.

DNA Testing
mtDNA haplotype is H11a20.

I ran my autosomal DNA I had tested a while back through genetic genie’s variation panel, and got the following results. The only one that resonated with me was colon cancer for the maternal reason mentioned above. None of the others resonated with me, as far as any current manifestation.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Journey So Far**

To date, the steps I’ve taken have been to see the sunrise. Every day I go into the office, I stop at a park and see the sun in shorts and no t-shirt for 15-20 minutes (more when I can), with my bare feet in the grass. I just received Kiniki shorts, so I will be using those from now on. Those in-office days, I also sit outside around lunch time for 30-45 minutes and read print outs of Kruse blogs and books. And when I get home, I get some sun and have my feet in the grass as much as I can, but it varies from 30 minutes to 2 hours.

Weekends or work from home days, I try to get 15-20 minutes of morning sun when I take food and water to our chickens, but it is typically not right at the sunrise. I’ll also spend time outside throughout the day, which I am trying to make pretty much all day now. I built myself a stand-up desk, so I've been spending the whole work day at it, weather permitting (but I can sit on the porch on rainy days, too).

I started limiting wi-fi use, having it automatically turn off at night via a timer—I hope to go fully wired soon. I got another iphone 11 to limit flicker, and use wired ethernet for it. I never was someone who used them 24-7, but I have used both wired and wireless headphones. Now I don't.

The most resistant to change is in the office. Lots of wifi, blue light, etc. I adjusted the monitors and laptop to limit blue light and always have Bluetooth turned off. Next step is to ground those electronics, since they are only two pronged. I brought three lamps to work and am using two incandescent and one NIRA bulb, no overhead lights. But work is probably the hardest thing to modify to limit exposure. Would like to get a body voltage meter. I also wonder how much exposure I have on the long drive to work. Somewhat near term, I’d like to trade in the car and get a convertible, but I open the windows every drive currently.

While less important, I’ve cut out seed oils entirely and most carbs. Meat, seafood, raw A2 cheese/milk, egg yolks, coconut oil, butter, tallow, honey, macadamia nuts, 85% dark chocolate, and limited fruit and vegetables. That’s about it. I’m trying to keep to things local and in season, as much as possible. Honey is raw and local, organ meat and other beef is from a farm down the road, raw A2 milk and cheese is from a farm a half hour away. I’ll be trying to stick to the farmer’s market for fruits this summer to keep it local and in season. Will be growing more things in the backyard this year too. I’d like to work to limit coffee intake; currently, I have one-two per day.

I only drink reverse-osmosis filtered water. Would like to work in some DDW, if needed. I haven’t drank alcohol more than 4 or 5 drinks a year in 13 years, and have never smoked or done drugs. I don’t use fluorinated toothpaste or mouthwash, though until a few years ago I’m sure toothpaste was always fluorinated.

All in all, it seems my biggest risk after not getting enough sun is almost certainly blue light/nnEMF from my job, then wifi in the home.

I am looking to find a doctor in Northern Virginia to get the kind of testing done to monitor myself and keep away chronic disease. I am going to a doctor that my wife went to and she said did a lot of testing, so hopefully that works out—though I have no plans to follow their centralized advice, as they advertise on their website following Johns Hopkins chronic disease management. And ultimately once I'm doing the right thing, I should hopefully be able to cut out the testing.

### TheCircleBastiat — 2025-06-16T19:14:50+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-21T17:17:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tks-optimal-journal.30968/post-356735>

New blog out on LASIK: [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-62-130349379](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-62-130349379)

Why is putting an excimer laser in the hands of a refractive eye surgeon like giving a serial killer a universal hotel room key?

Over 3.8 billion years, sunrise’s role in TCA/urea activation evolved with light, temperature and oxygen variations to optimize energy and repair, with thanatotranscriptomic genes adapting to these variables and these biological cycles. LASIK demoloshes this stiochiometry in nanoseconds.

Daytime TCA activity stores fat reserves, reducing sleep’s metabolic burden and enhancing repair (e.g., mitophagy).
Circadian Sync: Sunrise aligns melatonin suppression with TCA/urea cycle activation, ensuring deep sleep occurs at the end of a day that kills you slowly. It works this way so your colonies of mitochondria can recover every night to give you more time on Earth. If this fails you can die rapidly in ways you cannot fathom.

This blog tells you how the thanatotranscriptomic genes evolved and what their stiochiometry is. These genes were critical in evolving wakefulness from sleep. Enhancing AM light exposure (e.g., UV-mimicking therapies) amplifies TCA/urea benefits, complementing DDW production and ideal oxygen utilization to create precision in UPE output to extend life day after day. Temperature control is critically important to this thanatotranscriptomic method. This evidence is still seen in melatonin action today. It is maximally active in the brain after a 3-4 degree drop in temperature of the CSF. LASIK destroys this relationship.

### TheCircleBastiat — 2025-11-05T21:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tks-optimal-journal.30968/post-362219>

**Every day**: Sunrise 10-45 minutes outside, just shorts, barefoot. (I'm pretty religious on this for months, but in May probably less so). Only weakness in this is that I probably can't seek the sun directly for the first half hour about half the week, but I am definitely outside every sunrise. Beyond this:
\-4 days/wk (at office) - 2x 10-15 minutes outside (~10 am and 2pm), 30m-1hr outside at lunch (between 12-1pm). Now that it's winter, on these days sunset will occur while I'm driving home. Other times of year, on these days I'd get at least 30 minutes outside after work, though not necessarily at sunset.
\-1 day/wk (work from home) - probably average 3-4 hours outside, a good portion during UVA/UVB time, just shorts and on the grass. Not sure how this will work once it's fully winter, but still doing this currently---I'll probably buy a propane heater and try to continue working outside. At new year, I'll be doing up a home-made D setup ([https://optimizeyourbiology.com/diy-vitamin-d-sun-lamp](https://optimizeyourbiology.com/diy-vitamin-d-sun-lamp)) along with some red lights, but I did not do that last winter. Sometimes I'll be out at sunset, sometimes not.
\-2 days/wk (weekend) \- 2-6 hours outside, but varies a lot. Sometimes out at sunset, sometimes not.
I follow Leptin Rx, but not religiously. I try to stay seasonal, but not perfectly; I prioritize seafood, but I don't get it everyday (though I'm shifting to try and get it for breakfast at least 5 times per week); I rarely eat anything (but not _never_) that's not seafood, meat, cheese, raw A2 milk, kefir, eggs from my chickens, yogurt, honey, fruit, beef bone broth, coffee, avocados (i.e., pretty much eat no vegetables or grains); and haven't cooked with anything that's not coconut oil, butter, or tallow in probably 9 months. 95% of the time I'm not eating within 3 hours of sleep, though I could be better about going to sleep earlier. Wear blue blockers after sunset, and just got a pair of Lucia eyes daytime glasses for days.

Before starting to get sun, I'd wake up 3-4 times in the night, always to go to the bathroom. Less so now, but I have a 1 month old and three other kids, so that's most of of the times I'm up now. But as a total guess, I'd say I wake up 1-2x. I definitely _did_ snore, not sure if I still do.

My next Drs appt is December, which I doubt will be great for vitamin D, BP, and lipids---I definitely am more conisstent on things than I was in May, but I guess we'll see how much seasonal impacts will affect it.

### ToothDriller — 2025-11-06T02:19:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tks-optimal-journal.30968/post-362227>

Tell me about your sleep. Do you snore, do you have trouble falling asleep, wake up frequently (with young kids this is hard).

# Thread: Seeking Insights on Retinal Detachment in 13-Month-Old – Red Light Therapy, AM Sunlight, and Next Steps
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-insights-on-retinal-detachment-in-13-month-old-%E2%80%93-red-light-therapy-am-sunlight-and-next-steps.30969/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Asante94 — 2025-05-22T01:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-insights-on-retinal-detachment-in-13-month-old-%E2%80%93-red-light-therapy-am-sunlight-and-next-steps.30969/post-353090>

Hi Dr.Kruse

I’m reaching out for guidance on supporting my 13-month-old son’s recovery from a retinal detachment in his left eye. We’ve been diligently optimizing his environment and exploring several approaches for healing.

Background:

\- My son was diagnosed with a retinal detachment at ~5 months, likely a birth injury per the ophthalmologist’s speculation.

\- Injury is localized to the left eye; no other abnormalities found via MRI, CT, or bloodwork (ruling out retinoblastoma or rare diseases).

\- Initial ultrasound showed old blood in the left eye from birth (not yet reabsorbed), contributing to poor vision.

\- Post-diagnosis, we noticed swelling, tearing, and cloudiness in the pupil. These persisted for weeks but improved significantly after red light therapy (RLT) started at 6 months. By 7 months, swelling and tearing resolved, though some light pupil discoloration remains (seems to be breaking up).

\- Retinal detachment surgery was deemed not viable based on ultrasound results, per a retina specialist.

What We’ve Done:

\- Morning AM Light: Consistent exposure to natural sunlight in the morning to optimize circadian rhythm.

\- DHA Supplementation: Using cod liver oil to support eye and brain health.

\- Blue Light Reduction: Eliminated artificial lights at night and minimized blue light exposure during the day.

\- Red Light Therapy: Applied RLT for 1 month, which coincided with reduced swelling and tearing.

Our questions:

1\. Red Light Therapy Potential: Could continued or adjusted RLT further support healing of the retinal detachment or improve eye health? Any specific protocols (wavelengths, duration, etc.) we should consider?

2\. Sunlight Exposure: Will consistent morning AM light and midday sun exposure help with recovery or long-term eye health? Any risks to be aware of for a young child?

3\. Surgery in the future: If surgery isn’t viable now, could it be an option later as he grows? If not, what alternative approaches might support his vision or eye health long-term?

Your insights are greatly appreciated Dr.Kruse we became gold members to help our little boy.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-22T17:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-insights-on-retinal-detachment-in-13-month-old-%E2%80%93-red-light-therapy-am-sunlight-and-next-steps.30969/post-353123>

"likely a birth injury per the ophthalmologist’s speculation."

Nope.

Transgeneration epigenetic injury more likely.

# Thread: Leptin Rx when Underweight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-when-underweight.30970/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiohackersTO — 2025-05-22T17:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-when-underweight.30970/post-353126>

I'm a beginner and a dumbass. The Leptin Rx has often occurred to me personally as not relevant, because I have never been overweight. I thought the Leptin Rx was for those who are overweight. I tend the other way. The foods that would make me fat, give me bad pain, so avoiding them is a no-brainer.

I used to love fasting 1 to 3 days per month, but then I stopped putting the weight back on once I started eating again. Friends started to comment that I was too skinny and then I saw in one picture, I started to look anorexic  So I discontinued fasting. Today, I still do time-restricted eating, but I rarely fast because I would prefer to fatten up, not lose more weight. I am not underweight now but it won't take much for me to get there. I am 5'6" and about 130lbs. I can still wear the clothes I wore as a teenager, but in my 40s, that is not a life goal.

Clearly, I don't get enough AM sun and I'm working on that, as well as increasing my tan the rest of the day.

What's a good way to view the Leptin Rx for people who tend to be underweight, not overweight and people who tend toward autoimmune symptoms? No one in my family is obese. Many have autoimmune like symptoms and some have diagnoses. Every time I read about Leptin problems and weight gain, I think great! Sign me up!

Please help point me to the right thinking about the Leptin Rx for someone who is underweight (or close to it) and deals with growths like endometriosis and fibroids and autoimmune like symptoms.

### Jacks — 2025-05-22T18:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-when-underweight.30970/post-353130>

Suggest you read the Leptin blogs including The Leptin Rx: FAQS on jackkruse.com . You can search for the appropriate ones. Read the answer to "How do I determine if I am leptin resistant" question.

### Ron P — 2025-05-23T23:24:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nj.30981/post-353235>

Hello all - I've followed Dr. Jack's work for about a year now but only just became a member last week. My wife and I grew up in south NJ and have lived here the majority of our lives. We lived close to and worked in NYC for 15 years before moving to the suburbs.

I've been a follower of the health scene for many years, mostly influencers, primal/people people, and chiropractors aka functional medicine providers, etc., have spent time and countless dollars working with a few of them (mainly in the past year for my son) so I thought my wife and I were healthy (no meds and fit...). We had our first child in 2021 and he was diagnosed with autism in 2023 so I've spent the last year and a half revisiting everything I thought I knew about health and it turns out I didn't know anything.

Our son is doing better now, thanks to Dr. Jack's work, but he still has a long road ahead. I've also turned my sights on the health of myself and my wife, as we have a lot of mitochondrial sins to atone for.

Looking forward to chatting with and learning from everyone on the Forum. Glad to be here with like minded individuals and happy to chat anytime.

\- Ron

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-09T15:47:14+0300

All the laws of reality were given to us by nature. Humans didn’t create these laws, but by understanding them, we can use them to foster our own evolution and achieve our goals. For example, our ability to fly or to send cell phone signals around the world came from understanding and applying the existing rules of reality—the physical laws or principles that govern the natural world. Excessive phone use will fuel our extinction.
I’ve found it both interesting and valuable to observe the laws we humans share with the rest of nature and those we break, which differentiate us from other animals that cannot circumvent nature’s laws and create diseases due to entropy gain. If you live behind a facade for too long, there will come a time when you can not remove it without removing your soul. When I see this in a patient, I move on rapidly because nothing good will come from the encounter in decentralized medicine.
My perspective has had a profound impact on how I interact with people and patients, as well as my approach to life.

# Thread: Bone broth makes gut / stool worse
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30984/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2025-05-24T11:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30984/post-353254>

Hello,

I started to make my own bone broth from grass fed cows - 20-24 hrs cooked. When its cool it is gelly so high kollagen content.
The problem is, when I drink it (1 cup 250ml) I will fell very full afterwards. I get bloating and bad gas and my stool changes to more soft consistency (not good). Sorry for the description. Sometimes I even feel a little stomach sick. When I quit consuming it after 1-2 days it gets normal again, no gas, bloating and stool gets perfect.

I want to support my gut with it, but what can I do? Whats your idea, what it could be?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Hi from Cubanada!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-cubanada.30987/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### navig8r — 2025-05-25T00:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-cubanada.30987/post-353287>

Hey Folks.

A couple of "Kruse missles" landed in my back yard recently and rocked my world so I thought I'd learn more.
Glad to be here!

Special thanks to the biohackerTO couple for the enthusiastic referral during a recent hike together.

Jack, Ive heard you on a few podcasts off and on over the years. And i have adopted various therapies to great sucess. Cold emersion, sunlight, grounding, reducing emf exposure (if thats really possible at all anymore?) Ive lost alot of weight through trial and error. But i am always pushing boundaries. I will be Sphinxing the morning sun as a new experiment - athough this morning was a writeoff.

I digged into some of your recent extended podcasts and was impressed by your no holds barred candid honest educated opinions.

At the beginning of covid, I too became incensed by centralized health. I mean, I distrusted it for at least a decade, but now i despise it with a vengence. So much so that i requested a meeting with my Attorny General just as "vaccines" were being bandied about in the media after the first lock down in 2020. Anyway, nothing seemed to get the government to change its policy.

Imho, every government worker that didnt stand up to stop this atrocity deseves to be injured by the covid vaccine. Divine justice as work. Karma is a bitch y'all.

Im curious if anyone has read this paper:

[https://www.scivisionpub.com/pdfs/covid19-and-illegal-us-bioweapons-activity-an-insiders-revelations-2432.pdf](https://www.scivisionpub.com/pdfs/covid19-and-illegal-us-bioweapons-activity-an-insiders-revelations-2432.pdf)

On a personal note. In 2018 i was hit by a car as a pedestrian. I spun out and was unconscious for appr. 20 sec. I regained consciousness and shook it off. No one called for help and i didnt go to the hospital. There eventually was a minor settlement but issues persist.

After 4/5 months of general knumbness , i started getting issues with anxiety and depression.

I discovered, by accident, cold showers really helped with elievating symptoms. But occasionally anxiety still gets triggered and i have to hit the ice again to find a happy space. It can be debilitating.

Im still dealing with proprioception issues. Back Pain issues and coordination issues. Like i have to activitly "think" about my body whereas before it naturally moved. Maybe im just getting old, - maybe i need some professional help.

Any help would be appreciated.

# Thread: New Member - advice on where to start???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-advice-on-where-to-start.30994/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kddb801 — 2025-05-26T13:04:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-advice-on-where-to-start.30994/post-353351>

Hi everyone! New member here and looking forward to learning as much as I can!

I somehow stumbled across Dr Jack’s podcast with Danny Jones back in December and since then have been trying to absorb as much as I can and make lifestyle changes. Prior to hearing the podcast, I couldn’t even define mitochondria and wore sunglasses every time I left my house!

Looking for advice on where someone like me - who is completely new to circadian rhythm, mitochondrial health, decentralized medicine, etc even starts?

Are there any particular blogs or podcasts you recommend to start with for a beginner like me? Any advice from [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) himself???

I plan to post a more detailed health journal- but for now, I’ll share a little bit about me at a high level….I’m turning 38 this Saturday, live in St Louis, and I’ve been working from home ever since COVID started in 2020- sitting at a computer all day. Up until recently, I’ve spent very little time out in the sun - spending most of my days in the house working. I’ve struggled with anxiety over the last 12 years and diagnosed with ADHD.

I’m tired 24/7! At first I thought it was normal to be this tired from my stressful job and having 2 young kids (12 and 21months), but have realized it’s not. I’ve also had extreme brain fog over the last few years and have difficulty remembering little things.

Over the last 6-9 months I’ve had several thyroid nodules appear and they continue to get larger and larger. I have an appointment scheduled with an ENT in 2 weeks.

Another thing I’ve noticed over the last few years is I’m thirsty all the time. It’s like I can never drink enough water. I didn’t think too much of it until recently. Is there something else going on that constantly has me chugging water throughout the day??

Any advice is appreciated - I’m sure there are many others out there who were just like me at some point- overwhelmed by all the information and figuring out where to start. Thanks so much!

### Mat.I — 2025-05-26T13:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-advice-on-where-to-start.30994/post-353352>

Welcome. My advice would.be hire a building biologist to check your environment for levels of nn-emf and try to mitigate to no concern levels as per the SBM guidelines.. If you have wifi - get rid of it and go hardwired.. Also set up your workstation outside. I have been WFH since 2020. Easy to do and working no longer feels like working.

All the best
Mat

### Mat.I — 2025-05-26T13:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-advice-on-where-to-start.30994/post-353353>

And.. never miss a sunrise, ground, eat like a great white shark (as jack says).. when the sun goes down stay dark and use minimal light (candles or low lux red lights).. Connect back to the earth..

Any suggestions of decent areas would be much appreciated. We were going to get a short term rental house that I was going to test for nn-emf prior to signing any agreement.

>
> Any suggestions of decent areas would be much appreciated. We were going to get a short term rental house that I was going to test for nn-emf prior to signing any agreement.
>

Am considering doing similar. Agnes water sounds like a good spot. Do you think the difference in latitude between Agnes and Daintree would make much of a difference?

### Mat.I — 2025-05-28T03:10:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353442>

> [jaedyn said:](/goto/post?id=353407)
>
> Am considering doing similar. Agnes water sounds like a good spot. Do you think the difference in latitude between Agnes and Daintree would make much of a difference?

Anges water is ~24S and daintree / port douglas area is ~ 16S. You would get stronger UV at the lower latitude, however around Daintree cloud cover may be an issue due to the rainforest.. The only thing is - you should avoid any of the tourist areas as density and tech (nn-emf) would be a lot higher in those areas.. The Daintree area (in forest zones) would have a decent terrain for grounding/telluric earth currents..

I also stumbled on the Atherton Table Lands (slightly inland) youngest area in Aus in terms of extinct Volcanoes. Magma wouldn't be intact or active, however, I assume there would be benefits from the solidified lava (rich in magnetite / hematite and conductive minerals etc)..

I still have a bit of researching and weighing up to do..

Anges Water looks good in in general and has pop density of ~46.3 ppl per km2
Daintree is ~ 14.76ppl per km2
The table lands is ~ 0.4ppl per km2
Port Douglas is ~ 194.7ppl per km2

I just looked up the population and area to get those figures..

Property at Anges Water looks quite reasonable in terms of cost, however, not many rentals available. Unless you look at AirBNB but then you will likely get hit with the premiums..

The Daintree area (in forest zones) would have a decent terrain for grounding/telluric earth currents..
>

>

> Anges Water looks good in in general and has pop density of ~46.3 ppl per km2
> Daintree is ~ 14.76ppl per km2

>

> Property at Anges Water looks quite reasonable in terms of cost, however, not many rentals available. Unless you look at AirBNB but then you will likely get hit with the premiums..

If we were to move there i would consider Atherton Tablelands, I have friends in the Malanda and its great in there, very low population density, lots of farmers ie access to excellent meat, chicken, organic veggies, raw milk and cheeses etc, good small communities who thrived and bucked the trend during Covid years.

Also would consider Agnes Waters and would not go anywhere near Port Douglas or Palm Cove - too heavily populated with tourists when we were there.

I have heard good things about Mossman - inland from Port Douglas as a good little area but never visited, might be worth looking into as well.

Interestingly we were in Cairns a few years ago and even though its a 5G city, population close to 180k people, the city is very small itself and lots of land surrounding it with lots of vegetation so imo i would not rule it out but rather if access to travel/food is a factor i would look for something outside the city, well away from the airport on acreage and you would obviously need to test to see if it worked for you.

Spoke to friends last night and the geo engineering in Qld specifically Sunshine & Gold Coasts is off the charts - lots of reasons to stay away from there.

Good luck with the move.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-30T05:14:24+0300

Our rough references points were around Port Douglas/Cairns (in terms of the latitude) but knew that we would have to go slightly up or inland to avoid the high pop density & nn-emf around the tourist hubs..

I will also check out Mossman - thanks.

Re: the geo engineering - I have heard the same especially around the areas you have mentioned.. It is getting quite bad.

We are in a decent place with low pop density at the moment south of Syd where the majority of the population are in the older demographic, it was a good place for people to retire, more young families have been coming in over the last few years and is becoming a bit more diverse. Semi rural area ~ 800m elevation with the ability to source great local produce straight from farms and local seafood that comes up from further down on the coast. There is quite a solid homeschool community here too that we are a part of.

The aim was to leave our place in the Winter for my wife to get stronger UV for a few months of the year and we were open to a permanent move if we liked the area. The thing I like about where we live is the majority of people (especially in the homeschool community) are good people who are awake. Coming from Sydney, it took me a year or so to adjust to people who truly enjoy others company and wanting to talk for the right reasons. Everyone is very friendly here. So hoping in the long term we can find something similar at a lower latitude for the benefit of my Wife.

Thanks again.

### Pete Collins — 2025-05-30T05:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353561>

> [Laudy Cincotta said:](/goto/post?id=353529)
>
> If we were to move there i would consider Atherton Tablelands, I have friends in the Malanda and its great in there, very low population density, lots of farmers ie access to excellent meat, chicken, organic veggies, raw milk and cheeses etc, good small communities who thrived and bucked the trend during Covid years.
>
> Also would consider Agnes Waters and would not go anywhere near Port Douglas or Palm Cove - too heavily populated with tourists when we were there.
>
> I have heard good things about Mossman - inland from Port Douglas as a good little area but never visited, might be worth looking into as well.
>
> Interestingly we were in Cairns a few years ago and even though its a 5G city, population close to 180k people, the city is very small itself and lots of land surrounding it with lots of vegetation so imo i would not rule it out but rather if access to travel/food is a factor i would look for something outside the city, well away from the airport on acreage and you would obviously need to test to see if it worked for you.
>
> Spoke to friends last night and the geo engineering in Qld specifically Sunshine & Gold Coasts is off the charts - lots of reasons to stay away from there.
>
> Good luck with the move.

So hoping in the long term we can find something similar at a lower latitude for the benefit of my Wife.
>
> Thanks again.

It all sounds good Mat.

I will tell you my friends in Malanda home schooled too, lots of home schoolers in the area so another plus for you.

One thing i thought of to mention is climate.

They have 100% humidity at various times of the year and big issues with mould so this needs to be factored in for you if you haven't already got a plan.

If you haven't looked into mould then ideally renting a house with split system air conditioners or whole house air con if possible with a dry function will act as a dehumidifier and keep the humidity under control so you can control mould growth. Relative humidity needs to be ideally around 55-60% to do this with a low dust load.

Hygrometers are pretty cheap at Jaycar and they will help in rooms to give RH readings to know when to run dehumidifiers.

If you do find an ideal house but it doesn't have these options Aus Climate are a good price and good units so you might need to invest in some small freestanding dehumidifiers to run to control the humidity manually.

All the best, let us know where you end up.

# Thread: GATE / 90s public school gifted programs an offshoot of MKUltra and DARPA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gate-90s-public-school-gifted-programs-an-offshoot-of-mkultra-and-darpa.30998/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### doctorwu85 — 2025-05-27T17:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gate-90s-public-school-gifted-programs-an-offshoot-of-mkultra-and-darpa.30998/post-353416>

Fascinating videos here about the gifted and talented elementary school programs of the 1990s and their connection to Operation Paperclip, MKUltra, and DARPA. The reported techniques and aims of these programs lines up with all of Uncle Jack's information about these programs over the last 80 years. I thought this was the appropriate place to post given the other content on this subforum, but feel free to move or close if it's not the right place.

I remember being in one of these programs, where once a week a handful of us would go to a separate classroom and do different activities from other students. I really enjoyed it because we got to play chess, work with computers, and do interesting creative projects. But the video is correct that I don't have a lot of very specific episodic memories of what happened during that time. From what I remember, I was spared some of the more sinister experiences described in the video (being brought to isolated areas, strange field trips), but a lot of it lines up:

\- "Hearing tests" where you had to listen to different tones and conversations with interference in the background
\- Emphasis on technology (we learned HTML as 5th graders and made our own webpages in the mid-90s)
\- Playing lots of Oregon Trail and chess
\- Flash card games with shapes
\- Talking to local businesses (for us it was asking for sponsorships for a fundraiser)
\- Being recruited later on by exclusive groups -- I got a mailing in the late 2000s for some sort of "exclusive professional club" that met in secret on some sort of private grounds. I never responded.
\- Feeling of heightened self-awareness and always being different than others
\- Talent for pattern recognition and intuition, at times verging on uncanny ability to predict things

The "drinking a bright liquid out of a dixie cup" seems vaguely familiar but I can't place where or when that happened.

Curious if anyone else here was part of these programs. It never occurred to me, but makes total sense that this was a government program to identify future assets and potentially unlock MKUltra-type mental abilities of pre-pubescent kids that would be considered to be paranormal by mainstream science. Marshmallow experiment on steroids.

### KFDags — 2025-10-07T19:57:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gate-90s-public-school-gifted-programs-an-offshoot-of-mkultra-and-darpa.30998/post-360591>

Hello,
Well, this is unsettling for a random Tuesday. I do recall having to take these hearing tests in elementary school, possibly twice? Don’t recall drinking any liquid though, but I did belong to the “Crusades” club in school. Never knew or could figure out the actual purpose of Crusades; it was my ticket out of class so I wasn’t about to question it. I don’t recall doing anything nefarious there. We watched movies, played games, ate snacks, and basically just hung out, feeling superior in comparison to the people left in regular class.
I remember you said you were originally from RI; did this take place in RI for you also?

# Thread: Contacts cause sunburns
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contacts-cause-sunburns.31003/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Base_Strength — 2025-05-28T17:46:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contacts-cause-sunburns.31003/post-353465>

Hi everyone,

I’m 54, and for years, I’ve noticed my skin doesn’t tan like it used to. Growing up, summer photos showed me with a deep, bronze tan, but as an adult, my skin leans more reddish than golden, even though I’m outside a lot. I walk for an hour in the morning and midday without a shirt, soaking up sunlight, yet the tan never came.

For the past 15 years, I’ve worn contact lenses with UV protection. Recently, I went down the rabbit hole learning about how sunglasses and UV-blocking lenses can disrupt the body’s ability to sense UV light through the eyes and therefore reduce melanin production. I wondered if my contacts were the culprit.

So, I tried an experiment: I stopped wearing my contacts while walking outside. Within one day, my skin started turning that golden-brown I remember from childhood!

It’s incredible how quickly my body responded once my eyes could fully sense the sun’s UV rays. It’s like my skin got the signal to produce melanin properly again.
I’m in awe of how intelligent our bodies are when we align with natural sunlight.

Has anyone else noticed changes in their tanning after ditching contacts?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Practitioners?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/practitioners.31005/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### VivaLaValerieBeth — 2025-05-28T19:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/practitioners.31005/post-353477>

Please help me- I'm looking for a practitioner who truly understands the truth that Uncle Jack teaches. Like the Sun, Uncle Jack is TINA but I'm not sure I'd be able to even afford a consult with him. I don't know who to trust. I can understand Uncle Jack's anger and frustration. Centralized medicine is corrupt and criminal. It's hurt me and my loved ones- but now that I know better, I'm determined to do better.

The list of practitioners on his website are few. I've looked at the list of practitioners on the Quantum Biology Collective, and their list also seems limited. I tried writing to Dr Petra Davelaar, but from my understanding, it sounds like you have to join a community and then you can have access to her during Q and A's. I need concrete advise. I am overwhelmed and scared. I have a young family. I need to be healthy for them and to protect them from the harms I endured by centralized medicine. They've already been harmed themselves because of my lack of knowledge. I love them more than anything.

Who am I?

43 yo Female
Married x 14+ years with four children ages 12b,10g, 8b and 5b.
Stay at home Mom (former RN), we care for and support my Mother-in-Law financially
Skin Type: FP Type 2? (Fair, blonde hair that's become darker with age, green eyes)
Maternal Ancestry: Northern European (Mainly German)
Residence: I've primarily resided in Austin, Texas most of my life- I currently live in a suburb of Austin named Leander
Current Weight: 150 lbs
Height: 5'5"
Fam Hx:
Maternal GGM- Slight dementia- death at age 101
Maternal GM- Melanoma, Ovarian CA- death at age 84)
Maternal GF- Prostate CA, Liver CA?- death at age 76 (the CA was all over his body when found- unknown site of origin, he was a geologist who often worked in the mines)
Mother- Breast CA dx age 62, weight loss resistance, mild depression
Paternal GGM- Healthy, death age 104
Paternal GM- Lung CA d/t hx smoking, stomach CA?- death at age mid 70-early 80s
Paternal GF- Heart disease- death at age 70s
Father- Appendiceal CA dx age 66

1981- Born in Oct via vaginal birth, NOT breastfed, my mother always commented that I needed a lot of down-time as a young child, I was quite active in ballet and swim team growing up.

Childhood asthma, frequent ear infections, sinus infections and strep throat as a child, antibiotic usage multiple times a year, 6 dental caries at age 6 with fillings and placed on supplemental topical fluoride, ate a SAD.

1996- Age 15, sophomore in HS on swim team, wonderful family/home life, first diagnosed with "depression" and "anxiety" due to frequent crying for reasons I couldn't understand, started on birth control (only took 6 mo d/t weight gain) and eventually placed on Paxil, low energy, family history of depression on maternal side of family, cystic acne.

Continued low energy with major depressive episodes, exhibiting symptoms of OCD, frequent changes in psychiatric medications, placed on stimulants to help with the lack of energy, lived in Tucson, AZ from 2001-2008.

2011- Married, initial pregnancy resulted in a miscarriage extremely early on, multiple nodules noted on my thyroid, labs were "normal" so no intervention done, given oral progesterone at certain times of my cycle to help with PMS.

2012- First child born in Sept via c-sect at 39 weeks gest, baby was "sunny side up" and head got stuck, baby unable to breastfeed so I pumped x 1 year.

2014- Second child born in Sept via c-sect at 39 weeks and 3 days gest, was induced but suffered uterine rupture, baby also had trouble feeding so I pumped x 1 year.

2016- Third child born in Dec via c-sect at 36 weeks and 1 day gest, baby could not breast feed so again I pumped x 1 mo, increasing fatigue led to increasing depression.

2020- Fourth child born in April via c-sect at 32 weeks and 4 days gest, still no luck breastfeeding (I was told all my kids had very small mouths) so I pumped x 1 year.

2020- Increasing depressive symptoms, admitted to psychiatric hospital x 1 week, changes in psychiatric medications, slightly elevated liver enzymes on labs, constantly craving carbs/sugars.

2021- Admitted to psychiatric hospital for a second time, started ECT therapy, began new antipsychotic medications including Seroquel (gained 40 lbs in 3 months), sadly in June I did received the Pfizer jab (I had questioned to jab prior to this time and was not going to receive it but I believe the plethora of drugs I was given and the ECT weakened my sensibilities- that's my excuse anyhow- I fortunately had the strength to protect my children from being jabbed), liver labs normal.

2022- CT of abd for persistent right lower abd pain noted a fatty liver only (they think the pain is from a ligament though a colonoscopy later showed some external compression of part of my bowel on the right side???), minimal alcohol consumption since age 21- only drank socially, NEVER smoked, stopped Seroquel but weight remained elevated at almost 200 lbs, still very low energy causing me to be increasingy depressed, large 4 cm nodule noted on my right thyroid- FNA revealed atypical cells and genetic testing showed nodule likely cancerous- rt thyroidectomy performed in Aug, large nodule benign but 1.5 mm nodule on isthmus positive for papillary thyroid CA, not other treatment given, slightly elevated liver labs again.

2023- Increasing frustrated with episodes of severe depression and lack of energy, increasingly angry and lacking patience, took myself off all my psychiatric medications (tapered) in Nov, liver labs normal, hysterectomy Feb d/t heavy bleeding and painful periods.

2024- Started seeing a functional med doc in May, dx with gluten sensitivity- started a mostly carnivore diet but entire family went gluten free, stopped drinking alcohol completely, {my daughter had strep throat in April and was given abx at the time- explosion of eczema on her face, neck and inner arms/elbows with symptoms of PANDAS (symptoms have decreased slowly over the past year but are occasionally still present)}, I was able to lose approx 40 lbs being on a gluten free diet/mostly carnivore, depression was no worse after discontinuing psychiatric meds, lightly elevated liver labs again.

2025- Feb: FNA done on 4 mm nodule on left lobe near isthmus of thyroid, nodule slowly increasing over past few years although no change in size noted since Sept 2024, cells atypical and genetic testing showed mutation consistent with Papillary thyroid CA, DISCOVERED UNCLE JACK'S WISDOM!!!!

2025- April: CT of neck, chest and abd done- lymph nodes in neck clear, chest CT neg but CT abd showed small indeterminate lesions on my liver, noted intermittent right upper abd pain

2025- May: MRI findings... Three distinct liver lesions are demonstrated, all of which demonstrate arterial hyperenhancement and become less conspicuous on delayed phase imaging. There is associated mild T2 hyperintensity. At least one of the lesions (series 12, image 45) contains fat. Findings likely represent benign lesions of hepatocellular origin, favor adenoma. \*Segment 8 (series 27, image 17), 1.0 cm. \*Segment 5 (series 27, image 47), 1.5 cm. \*Segment 6 (series 27, image 44), 0.9 cm.

For the past few weeks I've been seeing the sun rise every morning while grounding and trying to follow Uncle Jack's Leptin Rx with occasional implementation of his CT protocol. It's been difficult with 4 young children but I'm doing to absolute best I can. I've switched most of my lights to incandescent red lights and doing my best to decrease my exposure to EMFs. It's been difficult to eat locally much. I do try to go to the farmers market and I get what I can afford. Still mostly carnivore diet with some cruciferous vegetables. Still somewhat craving carbs- particularly at high stress moments. I have been drinking DDW as my budget will allow. I've gotten my skin and the game and have a great tan now! I started taking methylene blue but became concerned when I saw Uncle Jack's article on how it can be dangerous if abused and you do not get enough sun. Which is why I really need someone to help walk me through everything and knows the nuances like Uncle Jack. I'd like to try to save the other half of my thyroid and eliminate my tumor if possible. I don't want to be sold a crap load of supplements or any other crap my functional medicine doctor is trying to sell me. He has helped some I will admit, but when I tried to ask him recently to look into Dr Kruse and his teachings, he kind of refused and brushed me off, so I'm done with him.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-12T07:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/practitioners.31005/post-354541>

Disclaimer - I'm not a Doctor..

You have signs of redox collapse and your labs confirm you are blue light / nnemf toxic. The issues you mention are also tied to transgenerational epigenetics of light induced stress.. A few basic examples -

Glutamate - severe ecotoxicity - nnemfs open VGCC's >Ca2 influx = massive glutamate release. Blue light amplifies NMDA receptor activity.
GABA -/glutamine imbalance - melatonin from AM sunlight converts glutamate > GABA. This imbalance reflects circadian disruption.
Dopamine - without sun DA can't functionally signal correctly..
PEA - spikes when under stress but is normally degraded by MAO-B as the sun regulates this via the retinal opsins.
Serotonin - melatonin shunting is blocked as you are stuck in serotonin phase - Tryptophan > serotonin > melatonin needs morning sun/UV + nightime darkness.
5-HIAA - light polution pushes this pathway. Sunlight resets pineal mito clock to switch to melatonin.
Tryptophan - melatonin precursor depletion .. Chronic cortisol + light mismatch depletes tryptophan and causes circadian chaos
Histamine - mast cell activation - nnems > mast cell degranulation > histamine release > neuroinflammation. Blue light enhances mast cell sensitivity.
BPA - xeonoestrogen overload - nnemfs increase mem permeability allow BPA deeper cell access.
CYP1B1 > 2-OH-E1 - estrogen detox fail . CYPB1 is light and nnemf induced > makes ROS generating 16-OH-E1 (tumor terrain)
The positive intestinal permeability panel - nnemf + blue light disrupt the tight junctions > leaky gut > immune system issues
DHEA - adrenal compensation - when redox is low the body uses DHEA to prop up the terrain.
Estradiol - nnemfs amplify estrogen effects via membrane disruption and receptor overactivation.
Cortisol - nnemfs / poor light environment flatten cortisol oscillation.. No light cue = no amplitude..

**My recommendation:**

Leave no stone unturned in your current environment where you spend your time e.g. home and work.. Hire the best building biologist you can find and afford as I believe you are working and/or living in Chernobyl. Do not entertain half arse fixes such as turning wifi off at night or killing circuits at night you need proper mitigation to eliminate the excess noise in your system.. Also be very strict with not missing a sunrise and staying dark at night. Also get off any tech and chuck any dumb devices aka smart devices in the bin...

### TheCircleBastiat — 2025-05-29T15:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-coffee.31010/post-353524>

Does anyone have thoughts on this [new "coffee"](https://rarebird.coffee/pages/px-science-explained?replo-experiment=test-11&replo-variation=LP_Educational&replo-experiment-id=43bcc8a4-aeeb-4a82-b019-ed92ae73b8c8), which apparently replaces caffeine with paraxanthine? I hadn't heard of it until today. Will this avoid the effects mentioned in [Jack's blog](https://www.patreon.com/posts/cpc-17-will-be-17225978)? My read out of the Patreon blog was that caffeine delays and reduces melatonin and is an adenosine antagonist, so it negatively impacts the signal to go to sleep and the processes that happen during sleep that reduce our heteroplasmy. And, there's the issue of deuterium. On its face, the new "coffee" avoids caffeine, so it would on its face reduce at least some of those negative impacts; but I also can't help but think this is a bioengineered, quasi supplementation marketed product that won't achieve what is necessary to drink "coffee" again in a 5G world. What is the biophysics, rather than the reductionist biochemistry? Thoughts?

### MTA321 — 2025-05-29T18:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-coffee.31010/post-353528>

I think it will change nothing and even potentially be more disrupting. Px is still an adenosine antagonist. The coffee still contains deuterium and has been processed via decaffeination which could further disrupt things. The Px they use is synthetically made. This synthetic process could incorporate more deuterium and have negative biophysical effects when isotopes are replaced with ones of heavier mass which changes spin states and fidelity of signal.

Bypassing theobromine and theophylline production from caffeine could further disrupt secondary pathways, such as vasodilation (theobromine) or bronchodilation (theophylline).
