### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-09T17:14:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this-very-eager-to-find-healing.31085/post-354362>

**Exploring Post-Antibiotic Chronic Fatigue Syndrome and Associated Symptoms: A Photo-Bioelectric Approach to Diagnosis and Management**

_Abstract_

This case study examines a 39-year-old individual from Istanbul, Turkey, presenting with a complex array of symptoms following three consecutive urinary tract infections (UTIs) treated with antibiotics. Symptoms include post-nasal drip, mild sore throat, extreme headaches, fatigue, swollen submandibular glands, joint aches, scalp sores, and vitamin D deficiency (26–36 ng/mL), with laboratory findings of positive ANA, low C3, and high eosinophil cationic protein (ECP) at 46.8 µg/L. This person is a vocalist. She does not tell us, but she likely works at night all the time under ALAN. This is inferred from her occupation, which she left out; it is likely her biggest risk. She is effectively a shift worker and likely rarely sees sunrise, and she lives at a higher latitude.

Within a decentralized photo-bioelectric framework, we hypothesize that antibiotic-induced mitochondrial dysfunction and disrupted ultraweak photon emission (UPE) coherence underlie these symptoms, potentially linked to chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) or a post-infectious state. Dietary interventions (e.g., AIP, carnivore) and Dr. Jack Kruse’s teachings on light, water, and magnetism are considered. Recommended labs and lifestyle adjustments aim to identify the root cause and restore health.

_Introduction_
The patient, a 39-year-old female vocalist from Istanbul, Turkey, developed a cascade of symptoms following three UTIs treated with antibiotics approximately one year ago. Initial symptoms, post-nasal drip and mild sore throat, persisted despite multiple antibiotic courses and consultations with ENT specialists, infectious disease specialists, rheumatologists, and endocrinologists. Subsequent symptoms include extreme headaches, fatigue, swollen submandibular glands, joint aches, scalp sores, and painful back/neck pimples, occurring in flare-like patterns. Labs reveal a positive ANA, low C3 (indicative of complement system dysfunction), high ECP (46.8 µg/L, suggesting eosinophilic activity), and vitamin D deficiency (initially 26 ng/mL, increasing to 36 ng/mL in November). MRI confirmed sinusitis, treated unsuccessfully with antibiotics, nasal spray, and a proton pump inhibitor. Dietary trials (AIP, carnivore, keto) and extended water fasting provided variable relief, but flares persist. Exposure to the decentralized teachings on light, water, and magnetism has prompted further exploration. This narrative integrates these findings into a photo-bioelectric framework to hypothesize a root cause and suggest diagnostic and therapeutic strategies.

**Theoretical Framework**

_Photo-Bioelectric Loop: BROKEN_

Sunlight drives UPE production (100–300 nm) via the mitochondrial semiconductor (melanin-mtDNA loop), collapsing microtubule wave functions to maintain cellular fidelity and consciousness (Hameroff & Penrose, 1994). This is likely the source of the cognitive haze. The reader should read all of the demyelination series and the first two consciousness blogs to get the full impact. Yesterday's Q&A would have been a compelling listen for the artist, but she is new, and she is not a GOLD member. Big error.
UPEs at 220 nm activate the leptin-melanocortin pathway, crucial for energy and immune regulation (Van Wijk, 2014). Disruptions (e.g., antibiotics, nnEMF) reduce UPE coherence, impairing mitochondrial function. It destroys the IMM and lowers water production from CCO decreasing the heat sink so the UPE needed between 100-300nm light are never made so the chance of symptom and disease resolution will NEVER persist.

_Arc Welding Analogy_
Microtubules form the weld seam, UPEs the arc, and structured water (e.g., EZ water in cells) the shielding gas. Antibiotic-induced mitochondrial damage introduces defects, disrupting this process (Kruse, 2025).

_Environmental and Mitochondrial Stress_

Antibiotics damage the microbiome and mitochondrial DNA, increasing ROS/RNS and reducing UPE fidelity, potentially triggering CFS or post-infectious sequelae (Naviaux, 2014). Vitamin D deficiency (optimal >60 ng/mL) exacerbates this by impairing VDR-mediated mitochondrial protection (Kazemi et al., 2013). This was extensively covered in the DM series of blogs 40-55. Working at night = blue light and nnEMF toxicity, all reducing the heat sink via dehydration, so it guarantees no proper UPEs will be made to repair this defect via Becker's current. It also means the integument and Neural crest cells are dysfunctional = no magnetic or electric function on your human motherboard.

**Case Presentation**

_Medical History_

-   Age/Sex: 39, female.
-   Location: Istanbul, Turkey (latitude ~41°N, limited winter sunlight). Nice place I hear Dr. Kruse will be there this week.
-   Symptom Onset: Post-three UTIs (treated with antibiotics) ~1 year ago.
-   Initial Symptoms: Post-nasal drip, mild sore throat (3 weeks).
-   Progressive Symptoms: Extreme headaches, fatigue, swollen submandibular glands, right knee/hand aches, scalp/back/neck sores, flare-like patterns.
-   Dietary History: Avoids processed foods, past keto/carnivore phases, current AIP with carnivore trials and 3-day water fasts.
-   Medical Consultations: Multiple ENT, infectious disease, rheumatology, endocrinology visits; no diagnosis, referred to therapist/psychiatrist.
-   Labs: Positive ANA, normal ENA, low C3, high ECP (46.8 µg/L), vitamin D 26 ng/mL (initial), 36 ng/mL (November).
-   Imaging: MRI showed sinusitis, untreated effectively.

_Current Status_

Two weeks into implementing Dr. Kruse’s teachings (e.g., sunlight, grounding), the patient experienced improvement until a recent flare-up (headache, swollen glands, mucus, mild fatigue), no labs since December.

_Results_

**Hypothesized Root Cause**

The symptom onset post-antibiotics suggests mitochondrial damage, reducing UPE coherence and impairing MT dynamics. This may mimic CFS, with positive ANA, low C3, and high ECP indicating significant immune dysregulation (e.g., eosinophilic inflammation, complement deficiency). This is especially worrying so close to a summer solstice. This points the finger at blue light and nnEMF exposure causing a rising heteroplasmy at a young age. Vitamin D deficiency (26–36 ng/mL) significantly worsens mitochondrial function because the brake a human needs on ETC is missing in action. This makes dietary changes superfluous in this case. Moreover, while Istanbul’s limited sunlight (41°N) and potential nnEMF exposure exacerbate the issue, flares may reflect circadian disruption or environmental triggers. This case tells me the patient is headed to CFS/ME, an autoimmune condition, sleep disruption, gut dysbiosis, GERD, demyelination, microtubule functioning issues, hypothyroidism (Hashimotos), and her cancer risks are now elevated due to circadian mismatches and MT dysfunction at the centrosome = aneuploidy.

_Phenotypic Manifestations_

Neurological: Headaches, fatigue (possible “brain fog”). = Microtubule and myelin issues are clearly present. WMV is likely already decreased. OCT and ERG likely would show these electrical changes. I would expect EEG, too.

Immune: Swollen glands, high ECP, low C3, positive ANA (autoimmune tendency). T reg cells cannot become NK cells = which means the cancer risk is higher.

Musculoskeletal: Joint aches, scalp/back sores = lack of autophagy and apoptosis due to altered melatonin production from mtDNA = working on old engines.

Respiratory: Persistent post-nasal drip, mild sore throat = Clear indication of blue toxicity. Why? All skin barriers are protected by Vitamin A biology. Her blue toxicity is releasing massive Vitamin A from her opsins, and this is what will destroy her sleep and eventually all her cytochromes and CYP. Her hormones are likely to crash very, very soon.

Risks for developing Anemia of Chronic disease very high due to mtDNA pathology. Sleep apnea likely. Sexual libido will drop like a rock and sexual dysfunction is coming. Cataracts, Glaucoma, and myopia risks are high.

_Response to Interventions_

AIP and carnivore diets have been shown to reduce inflammation because they make more water but she clearly cannot use TCA and urea cycle well. Hence, they are half-truths for her until she restores it with AM sunrise sunlight, with carnivore and fasting initially improving energy levels, possibly by minimizing antigenic load and enhancing mitochondrial efficiency. However, inconsistent relief suggests an unresolved mitochondrial or UPE-related defect.

_Discussion

Antibiotic-Induced Mitochondrial Dysfunction_

Antibiotics may damage mitochondrial DNA, increasing ROS/RNS and reducing UPE fidelity, a mechanism linked to CFS post-infection (Naviaux, 2014). This aligns with the patient’s symptom onset and immune markers (low C3, high ECP), suggesting a post-infectious state rather than a psychiatric condition, challenging the therapist referral. She is not crazy, her doctors are impotent to understand her demyelination, and MT function is tied to why her brain is turning into MARS from Earth. Her job is killing her.

_Vitamin D and Light Deficiency_

Vitamin D levels (26–36 ng/mL) are suboptimal (<60 ng/mL), impairing VDR function and mitochondrial protection (Kazemi et al., 2013). Istanbul’s latitude limits UVB exposure (the optimal latitude for haplotype is unknown, but her job disrupts this relationship), reducing UPE production (1.76 × 10^17 photons daily at 13°N vs. lower at 41°N), which contributes to fatigue, flares, and all associated symptoms. She is browning out.

_Decentralized Teachings & Lessons_

Nature emphasizes on sunlight (POMC), grounding (sweat glands on feet and hands), and minimizing nnEMF supports restoring UPE coherence. Initial improvement reflects this, but recent flares suggest incomplete implementation (e.g., inconsistent light exposure, nnEMF sources like Wi-Fi).
Your job is likely the number one issue. You must dress like a Muslim woman at night and wear blue blockers during your gigs.

Recommended Labs
To connect dots and identify the root cause:

-   Comprehensive Blood Analysis: Assess inflammation (CRP, ESR), thyroid (TSH, free T3/T4), nutrient status (B12, magnesium, zinc), and iron.
-   Mitochondrial Function Tests: Lactate/pyruvate ratio, CoQ10, NADH levels to evaluate energy production.
-   Immune Panel: ANA titer, complement (C3, C4), ECP, ANA subtypes, EBV/CMV PCR to rule out chronic infections.
-   Vitamin D Metabolism: 25(OH)D, 1,25(OH)2D, VDR expression (if available) to assess deficiency impact.
-   Gut Health: Stool analysis for leaky gut, dysbiosis (post-antibiotic effect).

Therapeutic Recommendations
Lifestyle Adjustments

Sunlight Exposure: 30–120 minutes daily AM UV/blue light (200–500 nm) at 41°N. You can initially supplement with 10,000–20,000 IU vitamin D3 orally (target 50–70 ng/mL). But you need to be off of this by the Solstice.

Grounding: Daily barefoot contact with the earth to enhance DC currents—minimum 2 hours.

Minimize nnEMF: Reduce Wi-Fi, use blue-light-blocking glasses at night. Your job is your toxin.

Hydration: Drink deuterium-depleted water (DDW, ~100 ppm) to optimize mitochondrial function. It will help, but won't solve the job problem.
Dietary Continuation

Maintain AIP/carnivore phases, monitoring flares with a food/symptom journal. Add omega-3-rich foods to offset blue light toxicity (e.g., fatty fish for omega-3s, organ meats for CoQ10). Getting a M sunrise will make the diet provide a better heat sink to make UPEs in 100-300nm.

_Monitoring and Follow-Up_

Repeat labs after 2–3 months to assess UPE-related markers (e.g., vitamin D, mitochondrial function). Consult a practitioner of decentralized medicine who is familiar with photo-bioelectric principles. If you do not improve, this means your job and location are going to have to change until you fix the issues with VDR and CCO on the IMM.

_SUMMARY_

The patient’s symptoms likely stem from antibiotic-induced mitochondrial dysfunction, reducing UPE coherence and triggering a CFS-like state with immune dysregulation. Vitamin D deficiency and Istanbul’s limited sunlight exacerbate this, while dietary interventions offer partial relief. Recommended labs and lifestyle adjustments, as per Decentralized medicine teachings, aim to restore UPE fidelity, potentially resolving flares and identifying the root cause. This case highlights the need to challenge conventional dismissals and explore photo-bioelectric mechanisms in post-antibiotic syndromes. You have numerous warnings that many aspects of your health are at a critical point right now, if you do not adapt soon.

I have been waking up and grounding since I came across the knowledge you bring, but as you suspected, and according to your recommendations, it's been insufficient, I will adjust. I've been out there about an hour at a time grounding. There is an old historic park walking distance from my house where there are 100 year old trees, I only assume it's the safest place I can be, considering. I don't know my haplotype either but I am half American and half Turkish ( mother Turkish ) My father was of scottish / irish czech decent, my mother greek, persian, kurdish. I was born in Istanbul but grew up in Kuwait, 17 years. I was in the sun all the time. I love the sun, I've never been afraid of it, I tan very easily, I never use sunscreen. I have blue eyes. I moved to Istanbul in 2003, spent a brief 3 years living in LA for school, then came back to Istanbul and have been here ever since. I do go back to Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania every now and then. I don't know if this is relevant, I am just eager. I had mono when I was 17 but my first scary health incident was right before the pandemic in January 2020, I had optic neuritis which resolved within a month but set off other symptoms, tingling in extremities, headaches, spinning head etc which turned doctors on to MS but I did not meet the criteria and my health returned to normal in around 6-8 months. I did not get vaccinated during the pandemic. My husband and I endured trauma regarding fertility in 2023, we became pregnant through IVF with twins that were born at 7 months by emergency c section. One of the baby's placentas had separated and caused an infection. We lost both of them and I was in the ICU for 3 days with threatening sepsis, another load of antibiotics. Very, very sad. I healed for a year. My vitamin D level was actually 76 and B was good too at my check up in Nov 2023, but my doctor said you can stop taking D it's high enough and thats what I did. UTI's started exactly at the start of 2024. 3 rounds of antibiotics later the mucus drip starts, When i made it to the first ENT in June my D had plummeted to 26, Then the cascade of symptoms, but for the past 3 months post nasal drip, swollen glands, and headache remain. Although I am in another more manageable phase at the moment. I have been trying to implement everything I am learning, and now I have homework to do.. I will study your reply and look into labs. Thank you for your time.

### sarahnile — 2025-06-28T12:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this-very-eager-to-find-healing.31085/post-355402>

Attached are some of the results that have come in so far, my symptoms are headache, swollen submandibular glands and post nasal drip, this comes and goes but post nasal drip always stays and I never know why I feel good or bad on any given day. no pattern that I can tell. Since starting seeing the sunrise, I'm on day #14, I've had two good days in a row started some time after day 12. the flaring seems to ease up and worsen then ease up back and forth during the day now instead of an all day thing. I had a week of 20,000 vitamin D and stopped on the solstice as per Dr. Kruse's recommendation, the test was given on the 24th. I guess I have to test again in some time.. ?

### maria@modernhuman — 2025-06-28T19:58:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this-very-eager-to-find-healing.31085/post-355426>

Sarah, thank you for sharing your story-- I cannot imagine the trials and tribulations. I cannot imagine processing the emotions of the IVF twin pregnancy loss while at the same time fighting for your life in the ICU. Your hunger for health and recovery is a testament to your courage, strength, and important value as a human being.

A brief note in case it is helpful (information purposes, not medical advice): in my experience as a provider, the lactate/pyruvate ratio is obtained using pyruvic acid levels, not pyruvate kinase levels. And the sample needs to be drawn on the same day, meaning a lactate from 1 month ago is not to be used with a pyruvic acid level drawn today. If you and Jack are doing something off-road with pyruvate kinase levels, by all means kindly disregard my note.

Thank you for your kind words. I was actually not aware of the difference, I gave the list of tests that Dr. Kruse suggested I have checked to a private laboratory and they prepared them. I only found out after I had given them that pyruvate kinase is a different test altogether. I did not know however that they must be done on the same day, thank you for that bit of information. I will have to go in again and ask for the right ones now unfortunately. Dr. Kruse and I haven't actually spoken, he was just kind enough to give me some detailed feedback on the things that I spoke of and some tests that should be looked at. Hopefully I can get some input once all the tests are in.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-30T03:03:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this-very-eager-to-find-healing.31085/post-355507>

Firstly, not medical advice (I'm an engineer).

I had a quick look at the labs out of curiosity and noticed your 1,25 (OH) 2D (aka active vit D) is quite high. I believe a back door pathway may be at play here (given your symptoms). As normally the kidneys convert 25(OH)D (aka storage vit D) to 1,25 under tight regulation (feedback loop PTH, Ca & phosphate levels etc). The enzyme responsible for this is CYP27B1, however, this enzyme is also expressed by immune cells (meaning these cells can locally convert 25 > 1,25 ) that is inside the tissues and independent of kidney function. Basically, a tissue level redox decision.

Essentially immune cells are redox sensors and when they sense trouble they increase intra cell NADPH and redox signals to upregulate CYP27B1.. This aims to supress excessive immune activation (like a safety brake to prevent collateral tissue damage. So in certain contexts (assuming no issues in feedback loops at kidney level or renal pathology) 1,25 can be a sign that the immune cells are under stress. Even with your 20,000 IU dosage, it looks like it could be immune driven as the immune cells could have flipped the switch as you have a loaded capacitor (being the 25 (OH)D).

There are other markers that suggest possible low grade immune issues, however the point that I was making that 1,25 may be a proxy for this in certain circumstances..

This leads me to my next point - if you want to improve your health you must measure and mitigate your environment for nn-emf. You are best having a competent Building Biologist do this. As chronic VGCC activation (from EMF) can prime the immune system into a low-grade inflammatory state. There is a spectrum here, however this will shift you towards a FE3+ state. Noting UIBC is on the low end so this could suggest lower capacity to bind free iron (not absolute, however a suggestive marker whilst considering all of the above).

All the best.

Thank you for your input, what's been extremely strange is my symptoms have completely shifted into new symptoms, achy hands and arms, headache and the occassional "twinge" in a random ankle or knee joint. i still have a slight lingering post nasal drip but gland swelling has stopped and it seems like it's turned into a totally different state of being for the past 3-4 days. also it flares throughout the day as if it's oscillating. I am past the two week mark of consecutive sunrises and it feels like whatever was hanging around has been tossed up in the air and it's all kind of settling down in different places than before, there is definately an upheaval of some sorts going on, I will test again in a month or so regardless.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Opening Baseline Entry (June 2025)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-06-10T02:26:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354386>

I’m becoming obsessed with learning everything I can. My problem is I can’t concentrate—at least not the way I’d like. I tried to do some YouTube fun videos on my channel called “Fun Friggen Facts,” which was a fun way to learn new stuff. But now I feel overly obsessed and gullible—I’m believing all kinds of shit I hear.

I see how many here have opened up and are not judged, and members have good support. I’m a hermit and don’t go out much because I always seemed to fall into the wrong crowd and got myself in trouble with a drink. Now I’m in a new mindset and with a sick mother and daughter, I need some happiness. But I can’t be happy when I physically don’t feel well—physically and emotionally.

I feel weaker this last year and my balance is not so good. I was always strong and flexible—another secondary shitty part of a desk job. I want to come and build and share my skills in El Salvador. I haven’t even been able to make any goals, but I want to start now. A goal to meet some of you and see the country, and when I get my shit together, to set up a time to get that punch in the face from Jack—he’s my kind of guy and I’m sure I will enjoy it, as I have these last few months here.

Thanks for listening and not judging my vulnerable, dirty laundry below.

**Maternal & Family History**

-   My **mother** is alive, breast cancer survivor, currently has bone cancer and receives monthly treatments.
-   My **father** was an alcoholic and died of Alzheimer’s at 67.
-   **Paternal grandfather** lived to 98 and died of old age.
-   **Paternal grandmother** is deceased.
-   **Maternal grandmother** died in her 70s in a nursing home with Alzheimer’s.
-   **Maternal grandfather** died in his 60s from emphysema.
-   Alcohol abuse runs on both sides of the family.
-   Skin tags are present on me.

**My Medical & Life History**

-   Diagnosed with **Type 2 diabetes** (HbA1c 7.2%, insulin 36 uIU/mL, low SHBG 15 nmol/L).
-   Had **bad eczema as a kid**, cleared up by teenage years.
-   In my early 20s, I had **colitis** that ended up with a **total colectomy**—now have a **permanent ostomy bag** for life.
-   Seasonal allergies—stopped allergy testing and haven’t taken any meds since last winter.
-   **Raynaud’s and chilblains**—toes and fingers turn blue in the cold or water, numb and tingle. Itchy skin in cold weather.
-   High BP, but my cardiologist said my heart is strong, so I’ve chosen no meds.
-   **Polyp removed** in the **sphincter muscle of my throat**—since then I have constant throat clearing, reflux, mucus, and phlegm, never feels quite right.
-   Dental work regret: titanium **implant** on lower left side, lost a lot of my jaw to infection when it was done. Next to it is a **root canal** with a cap. Work was done within the past 5 years. I was on amoxicillin, didn’t know I had an infection and started on them late. I have pain under my jaw below the area where the work was done. Dentist and second opinion say the pain is from the strap on my CPAP. I had an X-ray, MRI read by my ENT doc who said nothing there, and a 3D image also at the dentist. I wish I’d never done this dental work now that I know about dead materials in the mouth and nerve connectivity.
-   **Adenoids and tonsils removed 20 years ago.**
-   Diagnosed with **obstructive sleep apnea**, using a **CPAP** every night but I hate it. When I try to sleep without it, I choke and wake myself up.
-   **Tinnitus**—high-pitched ringing in my ears constantly.
-   **Sound environment**: I’ve always needed to sleep with a **sound machine**—I hate silence because every little noise wakes me. Now it’s worse; every single noise sends me through the roof. My mind races at night and can’t shut off—it’s like my head is on overdrive and every noise (birds, water running, leaf blowers, etc.) is stacked on top of the tinnitus. The sound machine helps me **focus on one noise**—it competes with everything else going bump in the night so my mind can focus on just that.
-   **Neuropathy-like tingling** in my feet when I sleep, and numbness in my fingers in the cold.
-   Past year has been **depressing**—I’ve lost my drive and interest in so many things.
-   Stopped drinking in December 2024—drank in moderation since my teenage years. Minimal drug use as a kid, but had some good experiences with hallucinogens.
-   **Diagnosed with ADHD** in my 30s, took Adderall for 23 years—it really helped keep me on track. Worked with Dr. Ned Hallowell’s office in Boston to get diagnosed and treated. Stopped Adderall recently because I was worried about the long-term exposure.
-   Feel like I’ve **missed my calling** in life. I had several successful construction businesses I sold, but lost one to a contractor going bad and had to take a job.
-   Now I work from home as an estimator for a large construction company that furnishes and installs architectural millwork.
-   I’ve been in the carpenters union, am a master carpenter, and have another 10 years to go before retirement.
-   I work with **two laptops**—one for work (connected to four external monitors so I can look at blueprints and multiple documents, pages, and software) and one personal laptop for podcasts and research.
-   Very heavy **tech load** and **EMF exposure**. I have an **EMF kit** and I’m trying to **run grounded Ethernet CAT 6 cables** to reduce WiFi exposure and set a timer to shut WiFi off at night.
-   My mind is stuck in rabbit holes and I lose track of time and space—like I’m paralyzed and can’t see the big picture.
-   My father and mother were alcoholics.
-   My son (26) is in good health, was diagnosed with Asperger’s as a child (which I disagreed with)—this caused a lot of stress and was part of the breakup of my marriage. My ex-wife had a prescription drug problem, moved and abandoned me and my daughter (31) in her eyes for the benefit of my son, moving him to multiple schools. He turned out OK and is now a great dad.
-   My **daughter (31)** still lives with me. She was diagnosed with **MS last year**. She’s an influencer with over a million followers doing makeup videos—she’s even more tech-heavy than I am, and I can’t get her to listen yet. So I’m blazing this path myself, hoping she will understand what I’m learning and trying to pass on.
-   I feel **vibrations in my body** sometimes when I’m in bed, like I’m on a vibrating bed.
-   **Current situation**: I feel like I’m **losing my mind**—I’ve lost time and memory and find it difficult to recall a lot of my past. It feels like I’m slipping into what I thought was depression but now feel it’s all part of the **EMF bombardment**. I can’t focus or get out of my own way.
-   I’m trying to get a **passport** and plan to visit **El Salvador**—I’ve always loved the beach, but can’t stand the humidity. That’s why I liked **New Mexico** so much, but now I see that’s a dead desert, not a healing environment.
-   I always loved the sun and barefoot as a kid and always joked that being in the sun charges my batteries, reading and listening here I guess it was true. It’s been a tough year but I’m doing good at getting up and hitting the lawn in my bare feet, have to whack down some trees as the leaves are blocking but I understand I’m still getting the goodness even in the shade and rain, ticks and mosquitoes suck! Shooting for half hour or until I’m skeeved out by bugs. I try to sun for half hour at lunch when it’s sunny, never sunscreen and I stopped sunglasses after what I’ve learned here, doing the blueblockers, looking to change all my LEDs back to incandescents. I love the sun, my mood can become magic as quick as the clouds go in front of the sun.
-   I’m scared of cold thermogenesis as it burns, but I always loved the winters here in NH and skied, ice fished—it was a favorite. Now just undoing the latch to feed the chickens or carrying something from the freezer is painfully cold. I’ve tried a few cold showers, funny when I was in my 30’s I had a place on a pond and I would sit outside in the hot tub for hours and had an opening in the ice where I did cold plunges, but that was over 20 years ago, I’m afraid my heart will stop now. I keep the heat off on my room thermostat in winter and get residual from the floor heat rising downstairs. Hate having AC on, but the light blockage I have been doing with blackout shades over the years is at a new level—taping LEDs on smoke detector, fan, and CPAP. I bought a red flashlight for when I have to pee and only turn it on when I have to. I don’t know what to do about electrical field of ceiling fan and oscillating tower fan, I will take that up with Anthony at EMFsafe when I do my follow-up.
-   I don’t have any gut issues now—my large intestine is removed. I do a lot of home canning and some fermenting, keep bees and have honey and no issues. I do eat some Siggi’s yogurt, try to stay away from carbs, now learning about eating in my zone which is going to be tough but I’m taking small steps.
-   I love being in the water. I had a pool here at the house I would try to extend in the season but it was 22’ long and let go a few years back and is a garden now—a beautiful garden I must say where I grew tomatoes and made 10 gallons of fresh salsa. This year there is nothing as my drive and mental capacity—I feel like I’m losing my mind and it’s barren. I have been doing the barefoot grounding in the AM, I saw a video of Anthony’s today who mentioned to do at night as well hugging a tree—going to try tonight as I watched a video on his channel today. I love the outdoors, just hate the bugs and humidity as I sweat like a pig.
-   Divorce was tough—raising a daughter sick with colitis, bleeding out my ass every day, driving 2½-3 hours to Boston each way, drinking and going to bed before dark around 7:00 to wake up and do again at 3am the next day—it took a toll. Lost my business which was hard—always worked for myself and it was hard to and is hard for me to work for someone, but they pay me well and I make them money and can now work from home (at a cost as I feel I’m killing myself with tech). Finding this site has been a blessing but it’s making me more depressed and I feel I’m trapped—too late to bitcoin game, stuck in NH. Thought quitting drinking and eating better would make me feel a little better by now, but no, no withdrawals or issues like that, just a sense of hopelessness and regret and not enough time and my fear that I’m getting Alzheimer’s. I was able to type this out because I took an Adderall today—it really helps me focus and helps my mood. I’m not taking any antidepressants, tried and hated that and hated speaking to a professional. Now hopefully I can feel welcomed and I am inspired and feel like a crybaby in comparison to some of the stuff I read and hear. I hope to not burn out and get better before winter as they are tough. I had a plan to purchase a snowbird place that I could get cheap and fix up in NM, but now I am depressed—that’s not a good idea. I was looking forward to work after work on fixing and working with the tools again in repairing a property.

* * *

**Summary & Big Picture**
This is everything: my health, my mind, my environment, my family, and my fears. I’m starting here. My goals are to feel better physically and emotionally, to reclaim some joy, and to bring my skills somewhere they can matter—like El Salvador, where I hope to build and share with the tribe one day.

### Mark1969 — 2025-06-10T02:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354387>

Might as well open the rest of my book: Paid for "Funtional Medicine" and i now understand from being here how i got fucked looking for a medical answer when its just Light, water and magnatisum...
**Key Lab Findings & Health Overview:**

✅ **Blood Sugar / Insulin Resistance**

-   HbA1c: 7.2% (Elevated – risk for Type 2 Diabetes)
-   Fasting glucose: Elevated
-   Insulin: 36 uIU/mL (High)
-   Low SHBG (15 nmol/L – linked to metabolic imbalance)
    **Action Plan:** Low-carb, high-protein diet, intermittent fasting (12–14 hours, if safe), strength training + cardio 3–4x/week. Goal: Reduce HbA1c below 6.5% in 3–6 months.

✅ **Inflammation & Heart Health**

-   High-sensitivity CRP: 3.2 mg/L (Elevated – low-grade inflammation)
-   LDL Particle Number: Elevated
-   Small, dense LDL (Pattern B – higher cardiovascular risk)
    **Action Plan:** Increase omega-3s, eat more fiber, avoid processed seed oils, daily exercise. Goal: Reduce CRP below 1.0 mg/L.

✅ **Brain & Longevity**

-   ApoE 3/4 genotype (higher Alzheimer’s risk)
-   MTHFR DNA variant (possible impact on folate metabolism)
    **Action Plan:** Mediterranean-style diet, antioxidants (turmeric, berries), good sleep & stress reduction.

✅ **Micronutrients & Energy**

-   Iron saturation: Low (despite normal ferritin)
-   Copper: Slightly low
-   Omega-3 levels: Below optimal
-   Magnesium: Low end of optimal
    **Action Plan:** Boost intake of iron, copper-rich foods (shellfish, nuts), omega-3s (fatty fish, fish oil), magnesium-rich foods (avocado, pumpkin seeds).

✅ **Thyroid & Hormonal Balance**

-   Thyroglobulin Antibodies: Elevated (possible autoimmune thyroid issue)
-   Estradiol: Slightly low
    **Action Plan:** Selenium-rich foods, reduce gluten & dairy, avoid excess iodine. Goal: Keep thyroid stable and inflammation down.

✅ **Environmental Toxins**

-   PFAS (PFHxS, PFOA, PFOS): Elevated
    **Action Plan:** Filter drinking water, avoid non-stick cookware, reduce plastic use, sweat more (exercise/sauna).

✅ **Weight & Body Composition**

-   Biological age: 3.6 years younger than actual age – great resilience!
-   Current weight: \[Add your current weight\]
-   Goal: Reverse insulin resistance, reduce inflammation, boost energy.

* * *

**Current Medications & Supplements**

-   **Supplements:** Omega-3, Berberine, ALA, Biotin, Multivitamin, Magnesium, Vitamin D3, Probiotics (per action plan).

**PS:** I saw some dude the other day on Jack’s old buddy Dave Ass-Bury’s page selling some frequency crap. Help me not go down another rabbit hole with that. I first came across the whole frequency thing at this rich woman’s house I worked on outside of Boston. T**hey had a pipe organ in the house**, and I’m sure the way this thing was hidden and how it sounded that the guy, who worked for T-Mobile and probably is part of the tech frying my brain, learned something. Maybe some good can come out of it, but these fuckers have to make a dollar on everything. I heard this thing fired up one time and you could feel the healing power, very rich and powerful family had this home.

I have these bouts of hiccups with a sense of being unable to swallow and a choking sensation like something is stuck in my throat. They happen out of the blue, but I thought it might be worth noting in my journal.

I decided to drop all these daily supplements, and hope I'm doing the right thing! So just on my perscribed meds as of today, sucks becouse i still am not into seafood excepts for shrimp, i was a beliver in the fish oil. An old indian man told me one time to take cod liver oil, we will see.

> -   **Raynaud’s and chilblains**—toes and fingers turn blue in the cold or water, numb and tingle. Itchy skin in cold weather.
> -   High BP, but my cardiologist said my heart is strong, so I’ve chosen no meds.
> -   **Polyp removed** in the **sphincter muscle of my throat**—since then I have constant throat clearing, reflux, mucus, and phlegm, never feels quite right.
> -   Dental work regret: titanium **implant** on lower left side, lost a lot of my jaw to infection when it was done. Next to it is a **root canal** with a cap. Work was done within the past 5 years. I was on amoxicillin, didn’t know I had an infection and started on them late. I have pain under my jaw below the area where the work was done. Dentist and second opinion say the pain is from the strap on my CPAP. I had an X-ray, MRI read by my ENT doc who said nothing there, and a 3D image also at the dentist. I wish I’d never done this dental work now that I know about dead materials in the mouth and nerve connectivity.
> -   **Adenoids and tonsils removed 20 years ago.**
> -   Diagnosed with **obstructive sleep apnea**, using a **CPAP** every night but I hate it. When I try to sleep without it, I choke and wake myself up.
> -   **Tinnitus**—high-pitched ringing in my ears constantly.
> -   **Sound environment**: I’ve always needed to sleep with a **sound machine**—I hate silence because every little noise wakes me. Now it’s worse; every single noise sends me through the roof. My mind races at night and can’t shut off—it’s like my head is on overdrive and every noise (birds, water running, leaf blowers, etc.) is stacked on top of the tinnitus. The sound machine helps me **focus on one noise**—it competes with everything else going bump in the night so my mind can focus on just that.
> -   **Neuropathy-like tingling** in my feet when I sleep, and numbness in my fingers in the cold.
> -   Past year has been **depressing**—I’ve lost my drive and interest in so many things.
> -   Stopped drinking in December 2024—drank in moderation since my teenage years. Minimal drug use as a kid, but had some good experiences with hallucinogens.
> -   **Diagnosed with ADHD** in my 30s, took Adderall for 23 years—it really helped keep me on track. Worked with Dr. Ned Hallowell’s office in Boston to get diagnosed and treated. Stopped Adderall recently because I was worried about the long-term exposure.
> -   Feel like I’ve **missed my calling** in life. I had several successful construction businesses I sold, but lost one to a contractor going bad and had to take a job.
> -   Now I work from home as an estimator for a large construction company that furnishes and installs architectural millwork.
> -   I’ve been in the carpenters union, am a master carpenter, and have another 10 years to go before retirement.
> -   I work with **two laptops**—one for work (connected to four external monitors so I can look at blueprints and multiple documents, pages, and software) and one personal laptop for podcasts and research.
> -   Very heavy **tech load** and **EMF exposure**. I have an **EMF kit** and I’m trying to **run grounded Ethernet CAT 6 cables** to reduce WiFi exposure and set a timer to shut WiFi off at night.
> -   My mind is stuck in rabbit holes and I lose track of time and space—like I’m paralyzed and can’t see the big picture.
> -   My father and mother were alcoholics.
> -   My son (26) is in good health, was diagnosed with Asperger’s as a child (which I disagreed with)—this caused a lot of stress and was part of the breakup of my marriage. My ex-wife had a prescription drug problem, moved and abandoned me and my daughter (31) in her eyes for the benefit of my son, moving him to multiple schools. He turned out OK and is now a great dad.
> -   My **daughter (31)** still lives with me. She was diagnosed with **MS last year**. She’s an influencer with over a million followers doing makeup videos—she’s even more tech-heavy than I am, and I can’t get her to listen yet. So I’m blazing this path myself, hoping she will understand what I’m learning and trying to pass on.
> -   I feel **vibrations in my body** sometimes when I’m in bed, like I’m on a vibrating bed.
> -   **Current situation**: I feel like I’m **losing my mind**—I’ve lost time and memory and find it difficult to recall a lot of my past. It feels like I’m slipping into what I thought was depression but now feel it’s all part of the **EMF bombardment**. I can’t focus or get out of my own way.
> -   I’m trying to get a **passport** and plan to visit **El Salvador**—I’ve always loved the beach, but can’t stand the humidity. That’s why I liked **New Mexico** so much, but now I see that’s a dead desert, not a healing environment.
> -   I always loved the sun and barefoot as a kid and always joked that being in the sun charges my batteries, reading and listening here I guess it was true. It’s been a tough year but I’m doing good at getting up and hitting the lawn in my bare feet, have to whack down some trees as the leaves are blocking but I understand I’m still getting the goodness even in the shade and rain, ticks and mosquitoes suck! Shooting for half hour or until I’m skeeved out by bugs. I try to sun for half hour at lunch when it’s sunny, never sunscreen and I stopped sunglasses after what I’ve learned here, doing the blueblockers, looking to change all my LEDs back to incandescents. I love the sun, my mood can become magic as quick as the clouds go in front of the sun.
> -   I’m scared of cold thermogenesis as it burns, but I always loved the winters here in NH and skied, ice fished—it was a favorite. Now just undoing the latch to feed the chickens or carrying something from the freezer is painfully cold. I’ve tried a few cold showers, funny when I was in my 30’s I had a place on a pond and I would sit outside in the hot tub for hours and had an opening in the ice where I did cold plunges, but that was over 20 years ago, I’m afraid my heart will stop now. I keep the heat off on my room thermostat in winter and get residual from the floor heat rising downstairs. Hate having AC on, but the light blockage I have been doing with blackout shades over the years is at a new level—taping LEDs on smoke detector, fan, and CPAP. I bought a red flashlight for when I have to pee and only turn it on when I have to. I don’t know what to do about electrical field of ceiling fan and oscillating tower fan, I will take that up with Anthony at EMFsafe when I do my follow-up.
> -   I don’t have any gut issues now—my large intestine is removed. I do a lot of home canning and some fermenting, keep bees and have honey and no issues. I do eat some Siggi’s yogurt, try to stay away from carbs, now learning about eating in my zone which is going to be tough but I’m taking small steps.
> -   I love being in the water. I had a pool here at the house I would try to extend in the season but it was 22’ long and let go a few years back and is a garden now—a beautiful garden I must say where I grew tomatoes and made 10 gallons of fresh salsa. This year there is nothing as my drive and mental capacity—I feel like I’m losing my mind and it’s barren. I have been doing the barefoot grounding in the AM, I saw a video of Anthony’s today who mentioned to do at night as well hugging a tree—going to try tonight as I watched a video on his channel today. I love the outdoors, just hate the bugs and humidity as I sweat like a pig.
> -   Divorce was tough—raising a daughter sick with colitis, bleeding out my ass every day, driving 2½-3 hours to Boston each way, drinking and going to bed before dark around 7:00 to wake up and do again at 3am the next day—it took a toll. Lost my business which was hard—always worked for myself and it was hard to and is hard for me to work for someone, but they pay me well and I make them money and can now work from home (at a cost as I feel I’m killing myself with tech). Finding this site has been a blessing but it’s making me more depressed and I feel I’m trapped—too late to bitcoin game, stuck in NH. Thought quitting drinking and eating better would make me feel a little better by now, but no, no withdrawals or issues like that, just a sense of hopelessness and regret and not enough time and my fear that I’m getting Alzheimer’s. I was able to type this out because I took an Adderall today—it really helps me focus and helps my mood. I’m not taking any antidepressants, tried and hated that and hated speaking to a professional. Now hopefully I can feel welcomed and I am inspired and feel like a crybaby in comparison to some of the stuff I read and hear. I hope to not burn out and get better before winter as they are tough. I had a plan to purchase a snowbird place that I could get cheap and fix up in NM, but now I am depressed—that’s not a good idea. I was looking forward to work after work on fixing and working with the tools again in repairing a property.
>
> * * *
>
> **Summary & Big Picture**
> This is everything: my health, my mind, my environment, my family, and my fears. I’m starting here. My goals are to feel better physically and emotionally, to reclaim some joy, and to bring my skills somewhere they can matter—like El Salvador, where I hope to build and share with the tribe one day.

Great job on your journal! Reading this I am truly amazed at how well you’ve done in your life given the challenges you’ve had. I’m sorry you’re feeling depressed or not motivated lately. Possibly this is due to stopping adderal and alcohol. I’ve gone through periods like this where I stop all the crutches and feel very monotone. I don’t like humidity either but I have adapted to El Salvador pretty well. It might be harder to be here in the summer months as it is rainy season. More humid and more mosquitoes. I leave in mid-July and come back in December. There are people here who are kind and have get togethers that might be helpful for you. It made a difference to me to be around other mitos who get it.

### Mark1969 — 2025-06-18T13:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354800>

Thanks so much for the encouragement—it really means a lot. I was actually in the process of exploring a multifamily property in New Mexico with the idea of snowbirding since I work remotely and really thrive in warm, dry environments for part of the year. But since diving deeper into this community and Jack’s work, I’m realizing that while the desert has some appeal, it might not be the best long-term move in terms of light and magnetism.

El Salvador definitely sounds intriguing, especially hearing how you’ve adapted well there. My challenge is that I’ve never traveled internationally and don’t yet have a passport, so I feel like I’m researching from the outside with no real frame of reference. The other big hurdle is that it’s just not as straightforward to search for property there like it is in the States. We’re so spoiled with tools like Zillow or the MLS—down there, it feels like a bit of a black box trying to get a real sense of pricing, ownership, and potential opportunities.

I’ve got a background in construction, so my hope was to find something I could improve through sweat equity. But ES seems like it could be a tougher place to make that work without boots on the ground.

Also, I’ll admit that if I don’t have a clear goal or endpoint, I can get stuck. My ADHD and OCD can be either a superpower or a trap—I’ll go down rabbit holes with research (whether here on the forum, YouTube, Reddit, etc.) and end up overwhelmed without a sense of resolution. That’s been a bit of my experience trying to wrap my head around what life and logistics in El Salvador would really look like.

It’s encouraging to hear that there are kind people and community down there. Having others who "get it" around makes a big difference. That might be something I need to lean into more, regardless of where I land.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-18T13:18:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354802>

New Mexico is a desert.

### Mark1969 — 2025-06-18T14:46:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354811>

**Follow-up Reflection: Is Misokinesia Part of This?**

As I continue to sit with the insights from Jack and others, [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads...ple-—-is-this-neural-noise.31132/#post-354805](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypersensitivity-to-background-sounds-wind-chimes-example-%E2%80%94-is-this-neural-noise.31132/#post-354805) something unexpected came up that feels worth sharing.

When I was searching the term _misophonia_, I came across a related term — **misokinesia** — which refers to an intense discomfort triggered by _visual movement_, like fidgeting, repetitive gestures, or body language.

Reading about it instantly brought back a vivid memory from high school — this was over 30 years ago, long before AirPods or constant tech were part of my life. I always struggled to focus in classrooms where there was a sign language interpreter. While they were essential for deaf students, my brain locked onto the motion and couldn’t disengage. I would completely miss what the teacher was saying. It wasn’t intentional — it was like the movement had urgency and my brain prioritized it over everything else.

At the time, I was told I just needed to “suck it up.” Teachers and even my mother made me feel ashamed, like I was being insensitive or selfish. But looking back, I realize: **it wasn’t about the interpreter—it was about how my brain filtered (or didn’t filter) sensory input.** I wasn’t being mean; **I had an undiagnosed sensory gating issue.** I now see that I may have been the one with the disability — not in morality or attitude, but in neurobiology.

So my question to the group (and to Jack, if he sees this):
**Is misokinesia connected to misophonia through the same root cause — degraded melanin, circadian mismatch, or mitochondrial disorganization?**
Or is this just a tangent I shouldn’t chase?

I don’t want to get lost down a rabbit hole of labels, but the resonance with my past is strong. And I wonder if others have experienced similar visual triggers alongside auditory ones.

Thanks again for helping me see this part of myself more clearly.

### Woodstock — 2025-06-18T15:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354818>

My ADHD and OCD can be either a superpower or a trap—I’ll go down rabbit holes with research (whether here on the forum, YouTube, Reddit, etc.) and end up overwhelmed without a sense of resolution. That’s been a bit of my experience trying to wrap my head around what life and logistics in El Salvador would really look like.
>

It’s interesting that we have some things in common as I was trying to listen to a podcast and heard a high pitched background noise…maybe wind chimes…and had to turn it off. And then reading your journal, I was keeping bees but since I was down here for the critical point in the season I gave bees to someone in between trips. I also can go down rabbit holes easily…or I used to. Not so much anymore. I think you would improve down here like I have. What is your Vitamin D? I am insulin resistant and will get type 2 if I don’t get it going the other way soon. I think I’m going in the right direction now. But I am going to be back in Colorado for 4 months. Not a desert but it is so dry there! I used to love the desert but as much as humidity is irritating with all the sweating etc, I think my body does better in it. So maybe you just need a lower latitude and more sunshine. Also I love the ocean and being in the saltwater. It has healed a lot of my skin and scalp issues. I do have some gut issues. I was starting to get EoE I think where it feels like you have a lump in your throat. I’m not sure of the diagnosis because I avoid centralized medicine but I had some symptoms and I am on the verge of sleep apnea too. Dr Kruse says CPAP machines are horrible for you but I think it’s not so easy to get off of one. You really need to change your environment! There is an EMF expert here and electrician who can go and measure a potential rental for you to get down here and then look for a place to buy and fix up. Olle and Hugo are the best. Very skilled and great people who have been great friends and. Neighbors as I rented a home very close by in Atami. All new mitos are accepted here with open arms and helpfulness. I would start with a rental in Atami and figure the rest out when you get here. There are less people here in rainy season. But the community is growing and getting stronger. Maybe you could come down for a month or two and look for that place to buy and get your melanin production up and all of this will not seem so overwhelming. Regarding the passport, you can get one in 3 weeks if you mail it in or 6 weeks online. You can even take your own picture for it if you follow their guidelines. I don’t speak any Spanish. But there are drivers here who speak English and we can give you contact info for one who can get you from the airport. You can get by with the google translate app. If I can do it you can do it.

Did your bees make it through the winter? I lost both of my colonies in insulated horizontal Layens hives—bears got them the year before. This year I only managed one hive, and prices keep climbing. I’m also pretty skeptical of all the mRNA-vax stuff they’re pushing. I tried catching a swarm but struck out. I’ve heard the global bee population has taken a major hit—no bees means no food.

* * *

**Reality Check & Fact:**

Indeed, honey bee losses have been alarming. In the U.S. alone, commercial beekeepers reported losing an average of **62% of their colonies between June 2024 and February 2025** [theguardian.com+5honeybeehealthcoalition.org+5canr.msu.edu+5](https://honeybeehealthcoalition.org/survey-reveals-over-1-1-million-honey-bee-colonies-lost-raising-alarm-for-pollination-and-agriculture/?utm_source=chatgpt.com). Remember, even normal winter losses hover around 30–40% annually—but this recent crash is well **above** that range [psu.edu](https://www.psu.edu/impact/story/protecting-pollinators/?utm_source=chatgpt.com).

Globally, managed honey bee colony loss has averaged around **30% per year**, with some regions reporting spikes up to 40% [people.com+14nature.com+14cals.ncsu.edu+14](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-59513-6?utm_source=chatgpt.com). Wild bees are also declining—about **25% fewer species** recorded since the early 2000s [sciencedirect.com+1psu.edu+1](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2590332220306515?utm_source=chatgpt.com).

### Mark1969 — 2025-06-20T14:32:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354914>

**Follow-up Reflection: Misokinesia, Melanin, and Now... Roosters?**

As if my wind chime hypersensitivity wasn’t enough, let me paint you a picture of what happened today.

I’m trying to think deeply — to process all this information about sensory overload, misophonia, misokinesia, and melanin — when suddenly, **both of my roosters** (yes, I have two) decide to plant themselves _right below my office window_. And I kid you not: they start **crowing in perfect tandem**. Not just random squawking — I mean synchronized, rhythmic, pulsating sound assaults every few minutes.

At this point, I’m ready to go full _Elmer Fudd_ on them — hunting cap, shotgun, the whole unhinged cartoon arc. And yes, I know that’s not PC, but when your sensory system is this wrecked, you hit that primal point where all bets are off. I’m not saying I’d actually do it — but the rage? It’s real.

**But here’s the kicker**: What are the odds they’d _both_ do this, together, right as I’m meditating on misophonia and quantum entanglement?

Jack talks about **melanin as a quantum material** — it processes energy and information at the subatomic level, acting like a biological antenna. If my sensory filters are broken, if my melanin is degraded, maybe I’m not just _sensing_ noise more intensely — maybe I’m becoming entangled with the chaos around me. **Nature mirrors back what’s going wrong inside.** Maybe those roosters weren’t just annoying. Maybe they were quantum messengers.

So now I’ve got:

-   **Auditory hijacks** (wind chimes, roosters),
-   **Visual hijacks** (interpreters in class),
-   A **brain that can’t gate inputs**,
-   And now a new word: _misokinesia_ — which feels like the missing piece of the puzzle I never knew I was holding.

If Jack or anyone else can speak to whether misokinesia fits into the melanin/mitochondrial/quantum cascade — I’d love the insight. I don’t want to chase empty labels, but damn, this is starting to make eerie, uncanny sense.

Thanks for letting me be raw. Sometimes quantum biology is a whisper. Sometimes it's a rooster symphony and a mental cartoon character screaming into the void.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-20T20:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354921>

### Woodstock — 2025-06-21T17:33:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-baseline-entry-june-2025.31087/post-354961>

My bees DID make it through the winter. I have a beekeeping mentor who helped me understand how to best control the varroa mite which I am sure helped. I was 4 winters in and this was the first time my bees survived which is common as a new beekeeper. I also have chickens but no roosters as I am in town and the noise is not allowed. I only raise them for the eggs. Maybe someday I will have a real homestead but now its looking more likely I will end up on a tropical island somewhere and live on coconuts and fish that I can spear out of the ocean lol!

# Thread: A greeting from Singapore
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greeting-from-singapore.31090/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Szen — 2025-06-10T17:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greeting-from-singapore.31090/post-354429>

Here goes nothing.
Hi, I go by Szen here. Chinese male, will be 32 this year, always resided in Singapore. I was born with transposed heart arteries.

If you keep telling an eager child: "You don't know anything, I'll do it."
The child gets the message: "Someone who knows better will take care of it. I'll only get in the way and be told off."
Amplify that with the "virtue" of meritocracy, and you get a child who never develops self-worth.
He will either seek external validation by trying to hoard the highest scores, or by withdrawing into "anything and everything all of the time". Or both.

At any rate, I'm here to pull myself out of the well that I'd become too afraid and almost too weak to climb out of.
I don't really understand anything right now. What I do know is that I cannot stay in a place where people are free-range chicken in a factory farm.
Certainly not when I can still feel the "side effect" on the left of my ribcage from boosters before my eyes were open to the TV-like static in my vision and ringing in my right ear.

I'm grateful that Uncle Jack has built and maintained a wonderful lighthouse here. I got the message that I should not think of my attempt at error correction as palliative.
The mark of a true greatness is not the ability to use great things, but to turn average into great.

I shall quietly study so that I can ask the right questions.

### tomstaplez — 2025-06-10T18:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruses-citadel.31091/post-354435>

Uncle Jack when you inevitably talk more about your home can you please address the following... Im keen on building a redneck version of my own.

1) savages acknowledge the sod roof. But is it because of the water in the living roof? Simply the greater mass of it?
2) is it all DC? Or strategically placed AC.
3) traditional plumbing? Copper? Savages are aware of your low D well water, but is it filtered or structured any further?
4) glazing, all savages uber glass jealous.
5) floor I'm sure is grounded, extra steel or copper in your slab?
6) bedroom, Mattress type? Grounded? Head facing north? Heck are you a side sleeper?
7) do you own a freezer? Do you even have preserved food methods at all?
8) no Air conditioning, but fans and/or natural air flow?
9) like cotton and linen on our bodies, do you avoid certain materials for your interior? Furniture, rugs, window coverings ect.
10) indoor plants? recommendations?
11) if some heat was mandatory, would you advise against in-floor hydronic heating combined with wood burning stove?
Thank you.

# Thread: Ladies ....Weight gain and bloating when beginning the Leptin RX?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-weight-gain-and-bloating-when-beginning-the-leptin-rx.31092/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gailhodg — 2025-06-10T20:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-weight-gain-and-bloating-when-beginning-the-leptin-rx.31092/post-354443>

**Leptin Rx – 5 Weeks In: Bloating, Gas & Weight Gain?**

I've been following the Leptin Rx for about 5 weeks now. Sleep has been all over the place — it started off great, then turned into full-on insomnia, and is now slowly improving again. My energy was low at first, but I’m just beginning to feel a shift and a bit more vitality.

However, my main concern right now is bloating. I feel like I’ve gained a dress size, and I can’t stop farting and belching . Is this a normal part of the process? I know Jack often says women don’t tend to lose weight at the start, but is it common to gain a bit too?

My primary reason for doing the Leptin Rx is to heal my body — weight loss is secondary — but I’d really prefer not to gain! I’m 54 and have just entered menopause, which I imagine may be contributing.

Any insights or similar experiences would be really appreciated!

### JHackett — 2025-07-23T08:05:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-weight-gain-and-bloating-when-beginning-the-leptin-rx.31092/post-356832>

have you gone through a root cause analysis to try to find any offending agent? I have had clients start eating tons of chickpeas, had others who were unknowingly sensitive to high levels of whey protein, others still very sensitive to low quality eggs.

### gailhodg — 2025-07-23T08:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-weight-gain-and-bloating-when-beginning-the-leptin-rx.31092/post-356836>

I’m good now. I think it was adjusting to more protein early in the morning which my body wasn’t used to. I’m eating less protein early on now and feel much better

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-23T16:14:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-weight-gain-and-bloating-when-beginning-the-leptin-rx.31092/post-356849>

Sounds like your environment needs a serious testing.

> Currently it's 300k to get residency but in roughly a year that amount is going up to 500k.
> I know of 3 french immersion schools could be more.

As I understand the qualified Investor visa currently involves a min upfront investment of $300K in real-estate that cannot be sold for at least 5 years if you wish to keep your residency. You are free to sell the property after 5 years and can reclaim your capital (plus any profits)..

There is also the friendly nations visa that is slightly cheaper upfront ($200K) with a lower retention period of 3 years, however it only gives you provisional status (2 years) before applying for permanent residency..

### Kirsten F — 2025-07-17T17:33:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-question-about-panama.31093/post-356486>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=356479)
>
> As I understand the qualified Investor visa currently involves a min upfront investment of $300K in real-estate that cannot be sold for at least 5 years if you wish to keep your residency. You are free to sell the property after 5 years and can reclaim your capital (plus any profits)..
>
> There is also the friendly nations visa that is slightly cheaper upfront ($200K) with a lower retention period of 3 years, however it only gives you provisional status (2 years) before applying for permanent residency..

To add to that, in Panama you are eligible for a passport after 5 years of permanent residency. However, very few passports, if any, have been processed in recent years. I am told that the new administration will take a different approach by clearing the backlog and granting the citizenships, but we will have to see if that materializes.

### Patrick Newhouse — 2025-08-05T17:39:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-question-about-panama.31093/post-357627>

I have never been to Ecuador, but I went through the Paraguay Residency process and it was a breeze!! Paraguay has a lot going for it, but the main benefits are a very easy residency process which gives you access to the Mercosur Countries, which is similar to the EU just in South America. This gives you access to pretty much the entire South American Continent with the Cedula you receive. Also, I was there during the Covid scamdemic and I can personally vouch for the fact that almost no one in Paraguay adhered to any of the guidelines. For instance, when I went into the immigration office there was a big sign that you had to wear your "slave muzzle of compliance", but not a single person, either visiting or working, was wearing the face diaper, and that was a room of probably 300 plus people!!! It was actually really nice to see!!

The only warning I will give is if you are thinking about doing it, do it soon because these processes never get easier once the Government knows there is an increased demand for travel documents.

# Thread: Planning/Chasing Family Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planning-chasing-family-health.31099/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ron P — 2025-06-12T04:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planning-chasing-family-health.31099/post-354536>

Hello all - it's been a whirlwind last three years and glad to have found a tribe of like-minded individuals. I'm 40yo, my wife is 38 and our son is 3.5. My wife and I grew up in southern NJ and currently live in (central jersey / jersey shore).

Pre-covid, my wife and I lived in Hoboken and worked in NYC for 9 years - so we have plenty of light and nnEMF sins to make up for. Right around covid, we moved south, away from the city, but still in a densely populated area. We have always been healthy, fit and followed the latest in health influence research, supplements, etc., thinking we had everything under control.

My wife got pregnant pretty easily and the pregnancy was pleasant, then came the birth. My wife's labor started the day prior and slowly progressed until we went to the hospital around noon. She received an epidural in the early afternoon and suddenly dilated. The nurses asked her to push but she was too numb to do so. After the numbness subsided, she pushed for a few hours but my son was stuck and his heart rate was dropping so they performed a c-section around 2AM. My son was having trouble breathing so they intubated him and when I asked the surgeon if he was going to be ok she said "we'll see". He was slowly taken off the breathing assistance over the next 24 hours and did not need further assistance but remained in the NICU for 5 days. The NICU doctor said they noticed an additional heart beat that normally resolves itself naturally, which is why they kept him in NICU for an extra 3 days, and thankfully the heart issue did go away. During the c-section, my wife lost a ton of blood and it took about two hours to close her up. I was 80% sure that I was going to be leaving the hospital alone that day... My wife ended up recovering slowly and remained in the hospital for the day time as my son (5 days) but needed a blood transfusion due to the blood lost during surgery.

A week later, my wife developed postpartum preeclampsia and was admitted to the hospital and given an IV drip. Her blood pressure stabilized over the following 24 hours and she has not had blood pressure issues since. However, a few months later during a routine cervical exam her doctor noticed some odd cells and she was referred to Memorial Sloan Kettering for a consultation. The cells turned out to be early stage cancer and she proceeded to have two leap procedures done. It's been two years now and the doctors have not noticed any additional irregular cells so hopefully that's behind us.

Regarding my son, at 16 months he, my wife and I all got Covid (winter 2022). He had a moderate fever but no other symptoms (we didn't test him but did test ourselves so assumed he had Covid as well). After that, his speech regressed then stopped completely and his mood shifted for the worse. That following December (2022) he was diagnosed with Autism at 27 months. Since then, I've been speaking with every functional medicine doctor I can find and have spent 6 figures (mostly supplements) trying to assist him in his development so he can become the best version of himself. His speech restarted in July of 2023 (around that time I found Dr. Jack and started implementing light, water and magnetism) and he's been making great progress since. So happy to have found Uncle Jack and truly grateful for the progress my son has made.

Some stats

Me:
Paternal grandfather: worked in marketing in NYC, was also in the Navy. Died in his late 60s of heart attack.
Paternal grandmother: worked various job and homemaker, recently died at 101 of breast cancer and other issues
Material grandfather: owned a convenience store in Hoboken, died in hist early 70s from Alzheimer's
Material grandmother: owned a convenience store in Hoboken, died in hist early 70s from Alzheimer's
All four grandparents lived whole lives in Hoboken
Dad: was a math teacher then became a dentist, still practicing. Has testicular cancer a few years ago but in remission and has not returned. Currently 79
Mom: is a homemaker, currently 75. Had arrythmia issue a few years ago, no issues since.
Sister: is an acupuncturist, currently 46.

Wife:
Paternal grandfather: died in his early 70s, not sure how.
Paternal grandmother: died in her 60s from breast cancer.
Maternal grandfather: died in hid mid 70s from pancreatic cancer.
Maternal grandmother: is still alive, is a homemaker, currently 82. In very good health.
Mom: worked on military bases since she was 18. Currently 63.
Dad: works/worked his hole life at a local horse racing track. has a stoke at age 40 but recovered. Currently 70
Neither my wife's mom nor my wife were breastfed as babies, my wife was also unable to breastfeed our son.

Son:
Growing like a weed, has always been 90%+ in height
Had lots of TV time between 6 months and 12 months. Now very minimal and we use the Daylight whenever possible (plane flights).
Loves the sun, swimming, running and playing outdoors. Loves planets, colors, numbers and puzzles.
Usually tans easily, obviously never use sunblock, but has not developed a full tan this year yet (or maybe his melanin is now properly being sucked inside).
Apart from the speech delay, which is getting better daily, the only other autistic symptom is minor stimming. Last year he loved to watch things out of the corners of his eyes but over this winter that went away and now he likes to clench his jaw and look at things (books, stickers, etc.) out of the bottom of his eyes near his chin. This occurs maybe a dozen times per day, not constantly.
My sister in law lives with us, which has become a disaster lately since she is the least Savage person on earth, and she has a nice dog. My son previously liked the dog but currently cannot stand the dog and is constantly telling the dog to "go away".
He is very social and loves to play with other people and be touched. He never had an aversion to touch or sounds.
He is a good eater, will eat basically anything, but does have a preference for crunchy things. He favorite snacks are freeze dried fruits and the lamb Carnivore Snax.
Stupidly, I didn't listen to my gut and we gave him two rounds of three shots, beginning at 6 months then the last dose was at 15 months and he has not had a shot since.
My wife did get the Rhogam shot while pregnant but none of us got the C19 jab.

Was early into bitcoin (MtGox days) but stupidly sold my coins in 2014 to buy something worthless. Got back into Bitcoin over the last two years so glad to see it's recent run. I work in fiat finance but mostly from home so try to work outside as much as possible. My wife works from home part-time so thankfully very minimal NYC exposure for us.

Next steps - find a suitable place to move to. NJ is going downhill quickly and they are building a Netflix sound stage a few miles away from our house so the EMF will skyrocket (it's already ungodly). My wife's mother and father live in Florida near Venice so that is what we're currently looking. Understand this is a C option but it's our best option at the moment.

Will continue to post progress regarding our light, water and magnetism journey. Wishing everyone well.

### Annemcnulty — 2025-06-15T04:06:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planning-chasing-family-health.31099/post-354652>

Just a note to send you solidarity and support. We have a 10 year old with mild ASD (plus PANS/PANDAS) and live in Canada. We are moving to Florida next year no matter what it takes (still trying to figure out where - my parents live in Fort Pierce). I have also spent tens of thousands of dollars on supplements. This is a painful journey but I do believe the kids who get better have tenacious parents who will do whatever it takes.

### Ron P — 2025-06-15T22:28:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planning-chasing-family-health.31099/post-354676>

Agreed and wishing you the best. Having a sick kid is awful but we brought them into this world so we have to fight 24/7 for their health.

# Thread: 2015 Webinar on Light — Conflicted by LED vs. Incandescent/Halogen Guidance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2015-webinar-on-light-%E2%80%94-conflicted-by-led-vs-incandescent-halogen-guidance.31101/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-06-12T14:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2015-webinar-on-light-%E2%80%94-conflicted-by-led-vs-incandescent-halogen-guidance.31101/post-354548>

Hey Tribe,

I’m new here and currently playing catchup with the older webinars to better understand the foundation of this journey. Right now I’m watching the **May 2015 “Let There Be Light”** with Ruben Salinas, and I’m feeling some internal conflict as I try to reconcile it with what I currently understand about light biology.

At one point in the webinar, it actually _seems_ like the stance is that **LED light is okay** — or at least not as harmful as I’ve since come to believe. I even heard comments that **halogen is not a good light**, which directly conflicts with my current efforts to **remove all LEDs from my home** and switch over to **incandescent and halogen sources** for circadian safety and mitochondrial protection.

I’m trying to make sense of this:

-   Has Jack’s view on LEDs changed since then?
-   Was this webinar meant to be more introductory before some of the later, harder truths about LED, flicker, and blue light were emphasized?
-   Or am I misunderstanding the nuance in the conversation?

I know we can’t “go back and change” older content, but I also don’t want to gloss over something important if I’m missing a key concept. At the same time, I don’t want to overanalyze a position that may have since evolved (as all good science does).

Curious how others navigate this — especially if you've gone back through older content with newer awareness. Do you rewatch and look for context, or trust that newer material reflects the more current model and just keep moving forward?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-12T18:24:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2015-webinar-on-light-%E2%80%94-conflicted-by-led-vs-incandescent-halogen-guidance.31101/post-354562>

In 2015 people were asking about what light the answer is always the sun and always will be the sun. People hate that answer. So they got the B.C, and D answer. All ALAN carries risk.

# Thread: Hello from Istanbul !

### sarahnile — 2025-06-12T14:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-istanbul.31102/post-354549>

Hello
My name is Sarah, Im half Turkish and half American living in Istanbul, Turkey. I am an extremely new baby at this. I have just emerged and am attempting to submerge myself in all the knowledge I can absorb. It's literally been a week

I stumbled upon Dr. Kruse possibly a few months ago but did not really start consuming his content until late May. I am blown away and intrigued and also angry and frustrated that it took me so long to reach this information. I have so much to learn. There is a lot I would change from my past if I could which is maddening but I repeat to myself "when you don't know better you can't do better"

I am now stressing over the amount of reading I must do and how I will do that without the technology. I have many questions, I am a true newb. I'm hopeful that this will be my way out of the last year of my life that has been riddled with my own health problems.

The time I have wasted with conventional medicine bothers me, but I must move forward to see the LIGHT.

I wish myself and all of you a pleasant jourmey, Oh, and is there anyone else out there from Turkey ???
