# Thread: My Optimal Ride
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-ride.31134/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sarahnile — 2025-06-18T10:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-ride.31134/post-354792>

My name is Sarah, I’m 39, American / Turkish living in Istanbul. I was born in istanbul but my parents moved us to Kuwait when I was 6 weeks old and I grew up there, 17 years. I moved back to Istanbul and started working professionally in music, jazz trio, gigs, weddings, etc. but mostly enjoy production and writing. I am a studio vocalist now, I work during the day although I have always been what they call a “night owl” I grew up in the sun, lots of sports and outside, my dad worked at our school and was my volleyball and basketball coach, we were always active and almost never sick. I got mono at 17, had an ectopic pregnancy at 27, optic neuritis at 34, another ectopic pregnancy at 36, I was pregnant at 37 with twins conceived through IVF but unfortunately they were born at 7 months and did not survive.

Since January of 2024 I have had ongoing health problems involving back to back UTI’S multiple rounds of antibiotics ( total 6 ) 3 for the UTI’S two for a suspected sinus problem that started after the UTI treatment, and 1 preventative round for an HSG scan. I had what started as a mild post nasal drip and mild sore throat but then turned into a myriad of symptoms involving skin, joints, and headache and fatigue, i have better phases and worse phases but the mucus never completely goes away. Skin has healed completely I believe due to diet, I’ve also done a couple water fasts, one was great, the other not so much. I now have a thick mucus in the back of my throat and headaches daily.

I’ve been searching for healing since last year after basically being left high and dry by doctors, I’ve been to multiple branches, they have each thrown theories and medicine at me and said you’re fine according to me why don’t you see this other branch. I stopped seeing doctors in February of this year. I implemented diets and some supplementation but haven’t had anything stick, I get to points where I see an upturn and say I’m healing now and then another flare appears and I’m back researching for answers.

I came across Dr. Kruse very recently. I must confess I thought our circadian rhythm was achieved just by waking up and going to bed early, I had no idea it’s so much more. I at first thought I was implementing correctly just by getting more sun but as I continued to consume Kruse’s content I realized I need to do much, much, MUCH, more. He was kind enough to reply to a labs post I made on my day one of registering. I later on started to navigate the website and am picking up on how members use it. I am following his prescription to me. I confess that I wasn’t seeing the sunrise, only waking up early and getting out more, ( which on it’s own provided some relief )

I am now on day 6 of consecutively witnessing the sunrise at waking, I’m also eating epi-paleo and AIP, grounding in the am 1.5-2 hours, watching the sunset, sleeping by 9pm, and trying to work up CT performance (very slowly) I wear blue light blocking glasses at home if I’m inside looking at a screen at night which I have considerably stopped. I got my period on the 6th day of sunrise watching. My period was 8 days late this month and I am usually always regular. I turn 40 at the end of August and my mother has always said she started menopause at 40 and it’s at the back of my mind. I normally have bowel movements in the day time around 3-4pm but for the past 2 days it’s in the morning right after sunrise. We’ll see if that sticks. I have unfortunately otherwise felt terrible regarding the other symptoms I’m trying to heal. I’ve had daily all day headaches and thick mucus over production, I’m wondering if there is a detoxification equivalent to this whole process?

Dr. Kruse gave me some labs to get done and 10,000-20,000 vitamin D until the solstice, I am currently shopping around where to get the tests done and will test on Monday after the solstice. I still don’t know what the root cause of my symptoms are. It’s extremely frustrating.

I will post more for updates, in the meantime I’m open to any input or advice anyone has for me,

Take care to you all!

Sarah

### Kirsten F — 2025-06-18T16:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-ride.31134/post-354831>

Hi Sarah, I hope you see continuous improvements as you continue with the new lifestyle. I didn't see any mention of nnEMF, so I would add that testing your environment and working on your RF device use from both ends (not just cell towers and wifi routers, but also the cell and wifi and bluetooth enabled on your devices) will be beneficial. Good luck!

### Carly18 — 2025-06-18T17:07:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-ride.31134/post-354835>

> [sarahnile said:](/goto/post?id=354792)
>
> My name is Sarah, I’m 39, American / Turkish living in Istanbul. I was born in istanbul but my parents moved us to Kuwait when I was 6 weeks old and I grew up there, 17 years. I moved back to Istanbul and started working professionally in music, jazz trio, gigs, weddings, etc. but mostly enjoy production and writing. I am a studio vocalist now, I work during the day although I have always been what they call a “night owl” I grew up in the sun, lots of sports and outside, my dad worked at our school and was my volleyball and basketball coach, we were always active and almost never sick. I got mono at 17, had an ectopic pregnancy at 27, optic neuritis at 34, another ectopic pregnancy at 36, I was pregnant at 37 with twins conceived through IVF but unfortunately they were born at 7 months and did not survive.
>
> Since January of 2024 I have had ongoing health problems involving back to back UTI’S multiple rounds of antibiotics ( total 6 ) 3 for the UTI’S two for a suspected sinus problem that started after the UTI treatment, and 1 preventative round for an HSG scan. I had what started as a mild post nasal drip and mild sore throat but then turned into a myriad of symptoms involving skin, joints, and headache and fatigue, i have better phases and worse phases but the mucus never completely goes away. Skin has healed completely I believe due to diet, I’ve also done a couple water fasts, one was great, the other not so much. I now have a thick mucus in the back of my throat and headaches daily.
>
> I’ve been searching for healing since last year after basically being left high and dry by doctors, I’ve been to multiple branches, they have each thrown theories and medicine at me and said you’re fine according to me why don’t you see this other branch. I stopped seeing doctors in February of this year. I implemented diets and some supplementation but haven’t had anything stick, I get to points where I see an upturn and say I’m healing now and then another flare appears and I’m back researching for answers.
>
> I came across Dr. Kruse very recently. I must confess I thought our circadian rhythm was achieved just by waking up and going to bed early, I had no idea it’s so much more. I at first thought I was implementing correctly just by getting more sun but as I continued to consume Kruse’s content I realized I need to do much, much, MUCH, more. He was kind enough to reply to a labs post I made on my day one of registering. I later on started to navigate the website and am picking up on how members use it. I am following his prescription to me. I confess that I wasn’t seeing the sunrise, only waking up early and getting out more, ( which on it’s own provided some relief )
>
> I am now on day 6 of consecutively witnessing the sunrise at waking, I’m also eating epi-paleo and AIP, grounding in the am 1.5-2 hours, watching the sunset, sleeping by 9pm, and trying to work up CT performance (very slowly) I wear blue light blocking glasses at home if I’m inside looking at a screen at night which I have considerably stopped. I got my period on the 6th day of sunrise watching. My period was 8 days late this month and I am usually always regular. I turn 40 at the end of August and my mother has always said she started menopause at 40 and it’s at the back of my mind. I normally have bowel movements in the day time around 3-4pm but for the past 2 days it’s in the morning right after sunrise. We’ll see if that sticks. I have unfortunately otherwise felt terrible regarding the other symptoms I’m trying to heal. I’ve had daily all day headaches and thick mucus over production, I’m wondering if there is a detoxification equivalent to this whole process?
>
> Dr. Kruse gave me some labs to get done and 10,000-20,000 vitamin D until the solstice, I am currently shopping around where to get the tests done and will test on Monday after the solstice. I still don’t know what the root cause of my symptoms are. It’s extremely frustrating.
>
> I will post more for updates, in the meantime I’m open to any input or advice anyone has for me,
>
> Take care to you all!
>
> Sarah

It took me almost 2 months of sunrise viewing to see some significant results. Wishing you all the best dear and keep consistent. Small happy little consistent changes or maybe even a big one in getting to a more equatorial climate for some time.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melkersson-rosenthal-syndrome-any-suggestions-besides-light-water-and-magnetism.31142/post-354879>

### [Melkersson-Rosenthal Syndrome: Symptoms & Treatment](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/23115-melkersson-rosenthal-syndrome-mrs)

Melkersson-Rosenthal syndrome is a rare disorder that affects facial nerves. It causes episodes of facial swelling, facial paralysis and tongue furrowing.

 my.clevelandclinic.org

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-19T19:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melkersson-rosenthal-syndrome-any-suggestions-besides-light-water-and-magnetism.31142/post-354886>

Mitochondrial and Charge Influence: The facial nerve paralysis and swelling in MRS reflect a disrupted mitochondrial activity, possibly due to altered cytochrome c oxidase (CCO) efficiency influenced by haplotypes, as we discussed. This leads to changes in proton gradients and the creation of water, impacting local charge environments. A larger EZ from DDW, with its net negative charge, might modulate inflammation or nerve function, offering a decentralized therapeutic angle outside conventional treatments like steroids.

LNPs and Environmental Triggers: If MRS flare-ups are triggered by environmental factors (e.g., infections or allergens, as speculated in some cases), LNPs with varying charges (e.g., neutral in certain regions) could interact with cellular potentials, exacerbating or mitigating symptoms.

Systemic Granulomatous Link: The connection to Crohn’s and sarcoidosis suggests a systemic charge or metabolic imbalance. DDW’s potential to alter cellular charge and improve the Zeta potential should influence granuloma formation, aligning with my idea that quantized electromagnetic processes (e.g., UPEs) are driving this disease process.

In my decentralized thesis, MRS could serve as a case study where centralized medicine overlooks charge and water dynamics. In contrast, a decentralized model, leveraging personalized mitochondrial and EZ water interventions, addresses the root causes rather than the symptoms. The rarity and variability of MRS support a need for individualized, non-uniform approaches.

# Thread: Mood Swings & Mitochondria—Why Cloud Cover Wrecks Me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mood-swings-mitochondria%E2%80%94why-cloud-cover-wrecks-me.31144/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-06-19T19:57:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mood-swings-mitochondria%E2%80%94why-cloud-cover-wrecks-me.31144/post-354888>

Can anyone explain—through a quantum biology lens—why my mood shifts so dramatically just from a cloud passing over the sun? It’s like my body downshifts the moment light dims.

This morning, I was outside barefoot trying to get some sunrise through the clouds and trees—but instead of feeling uplifted, I felt flat and down. Late morning, as the sun finally broke through, I felt a noticeable lift. Sitting outside in the strong sunlight, barefoot, eating lunch, everything felt good... until a cloud passed. I felt my mood and energy dip instantly again.

I’ve tried mantras like "My mood is not determined by the weather"—but they don’t touch this. It's not psychological. It feels mitochondrial.

Does anyone else experience this? Any hacks or “tribe tricks” for when the sky is gray?

### Mat.I — 2025-06-20T14:13:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mood-swings-mitochondria%E2%80%94why-cloud-cover-wrecks-me.31144/post-354913>

Your brain is a quantum-coherent light processor. The sun is its oscillator. Clouds scramble the feed. Effect would be redox dependent e.g. low redox = mood instability from energy loss and high redox = mood attunment from coherence loss.

# Thread: EMF Notes - Suggestions Sought!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TB14 — 2025-06-19T23:09:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354899>

Hi All,

Relative newbie here, wanted to post info about my EMF measurements and mitigation procedures to date at my homes in the Washington, D.C. and Ocala, Florida areas, plus seek some advice and float some ideas. Hoping this can potentially be useful to others and also would be very grateful for any suggestions.

**Magnetic Fields**

_Magnetic Fields in D.C. House_: AC magnetic fields are thankfully lower than average suburban homes in the areas of the D.C. house where I spend the most time, about 0.1-0.2 mG in my bedroom and 0.2-0.4 in most of the rest of the house (as measured by the TriField TF2). I had a plumber put in a PEX insert near where my water main enters my house to cut off what seemed like maybe current coming in on it (potentially from neighbors' grounding systems that are connected to their pipes, which were then conductive all the way to my pipes until putting in the non-conductive PEX insert), though haven’t noticed much difference in magnetic field readings since doing that. I also had an EMF specialist and electrician fix wiring errors at my electric panel, though again without measured reductions in magnetic fields.
There does seem to still be some current on the ground system; any suggestions on where this might be coming from would be awesome, because I and the EMF specialist I’m working with are both kinda stumped at the moment. Have considered the metal gas line, copper A/C refrigerant line, cast iron drain pipe, cable internet/old TV/phone lines, electric panel, and ground rod being too close to the A/C compressor, utility meters, and/or electric service lines outside as possible sources, and it could just be an amalgamation of things, but curious if anyone with current on their ground was able to discover the source and/or resolve it in some way I haven’t thought of yet.

_Magnetic Fields in Florida House:_ Unfortunately AC magnetic fields are higher than I’d like in the Ocala house and coming from the buried utility power lines, which I believe is generally treated as all but impossible to meaningfully address. They get as high as ~1.5-2 mG around mid-day in the summer in the rooms where I spend the most time. At night, however, even in summer, they aren’t nearly as bad, though still not ideal, mostly ranging from 0.3 to 0.6 mG. I measured in winter too and don't think it was nearly as bad even mid-day, but didn't write down my measurements or the times of day at which I took them, so will have to plan to do that again later this year. The TriField TF2 gives pretty much identical readings for weighted and unweighted magnetic fields at the Florida place, which I think is at least a good sign that the magnetic fields here aren't particularly dirty. There doesn't appear to be current on the water main. Haven’t had an EMF specialist out to the place in Ocala yet to check for wiring errors.
Thinking the solution to the powerline mid-day in summer high magnetic field issue is probably just to limit time at the Florida place in summer and use it as a winter sun place, and maybe find something to do out of the house on the summer afternoons that I do spend here. My understanding is there's really nothing you can do about magnetic fields from power lines except leave the house, but if anyone has any experience or even just a notion that contradicts that, I would of course love to hear about it. Not sure if anyone has ever tried or thought about putting MuMetal or other magnetic field blocking material around the circumference of buried power lines outside a property - I don't know if that would be "allowed" under utility terms & conditions or local easement/zoning ordinances, I guess, but in general the area around your power line is your property, since title extends to air above and ground below; plus you wouldn't be interfering with the service or damaging the utility's equipment; and realistically who would ever find out you even did it (especially if the line is buried) unless the power company came to service the part of the line in front of your house, and how often does that happen? I imagine that could be prohibitively expensive for most, and there could be some other reason I'm unaware of why it wouldn't work even if you had the money, but still interesting to think about, especially when the alternative is "move".

**Electric Fields**

_Electric Fields in D.C. House_: Body voltage measurements ("BVMs") laying in my bed in the D.C. house with the circuits off and using an outdoor ground stake as ground reference range from 0.06 to 0.1 V. With the relevant circuits on and nothing plugged in in the room, they’re about 2.2 V. I use an EMFSafeSwitch to remotely cut the circuits before I use the bed. I actually have no idea where the residual 0.06-0.1V is even coming from (maybe the ground wire?), but if anyone has any ideas or suggestions how to get rid of it, I would love to know.

_Electric Fields in Florida House_: BVMs laying in my bed in Ocala with the circuits off and using an outdoor ground stake as ground reference are roughly 0.1 V. As in the D.C. house, with the relevant circuits on and nothing plugged in in the room, they’re about 2.2 V. I don’t have an EMFSafeSwitch in Florida because I don’t need to use the circuit that most impacts BVMs for anything, so I just keep it off. As with the D.C. house, I have no idea where the residual 0.1V is coming from (maybe the ground wire?), but if anyone has any ideas or suggestions how to get rid of it, I would love to know.

**Dirty Electricity**

_Dirty Electricity in D.C. House_: DE is quite bad in the D.C. house, as measured by both plug-in meters and by the EMF specialist with a Picoscope 2408. Seems to be coming in from the outside, as the levels measured actually increase as circuits are cut within the house (I guess since the DE isn't getting spread out as much to the circuits other than the one from which the measurement is taken). I don’t currently use any DE filters as I don’t want to risk making the situation worse than it already is and all of the filters I’ve seen on the market appear to have certain frequency ranges of DE that they actually amplify rather than mitigate. I’ve been told DE filters are a developing area of technology and that new and potentially better solutions could be available soon - could be cool to hear about this from anyone who’s more keyed in on it.

_Dirty Electricity in Florida House_: The Ocala house seems to have about half the levels of DE as the DC house (per the Alpha Labs EMI100 plug-in meter), which still isn’t great, but seemingly significantly better. I need to evaluate DE at the Florida house more thoroughly, but I also suspect it’s possible DE could be coming from inside rather than outside (too many goofy modern electronic appliances that came with the house) and thus that levels could potentially be lowered further without the use of a filter.

**RF**
_RF in D.C. House_: Big issue in the D.C. house. High-intensity pulses, probably from radar at Reagan Airport and military bases, are pretty much constant, every five seconds or so. Inside the house, on the second floor, the pulses are typically about 4,000-6,000 microwatts/m^2 (as measured by the Safe and Sound Pro II). They’re about 1,000-2,000 microwatts/m^2 in the semi-subterranean basement, but that’s still obviously way too high. In parts of my back yard, which I unfortunately largely avoid for this reason, the radar pulses can get over 100,000 microwatts/m^2. I sleep within a Swiss Shield Naturell bed canopy with floor sheet that seems to live up to its claim of ~99% RF intensity reduction and brings the measured peak intensity of the pulses down to consistently under 100 microwatts/m^2, usually somewhere in the ~20-50 microwatts/m^2 range. I have no Wi-Fi router in the house and only use Ethernet. I also opted out of my power company SmartMeter, which was sending out strong pulses once a minute, but that didn't seem to make much of a difference in the measurements within the house.
I’m considering painting the entire exterior of my house with Y-Shield or something similar (then grounding it and covering it in some kind of finish to maintain it) and/or lining the floor of my attic with some other RF-reflective material (probably not just solid aluminum foil, to avoid trapping moisture). If anyone has any experience with anything like that or suggestions, like materials used, techniques, maintenance, efficacy, I’d love to hear about it.

_RF in Florida House_: Fortunately the RF situation seems to be much better in Ocala. Levels where I spend most of my time during the day seem to peak at less than 100 microwatts/m^2. In my bedroom the peak is consistently below 20 microwatts/m^2. Interestingly the building materials used for the Ocala house (I think probably thick concrete and/or possibly old lead paint) seem to play a significant role in keeping RF out, as the levels in the back yard and in the lanai (sunroom with windows for walls) are significantly higher (in some areas over 2,000 microwatts/m^2) than inside most of the home. The high RF levels out back combined with the high magnetic field levels from the power line out front however unfortunately mean there’s currently no area of the property that's both super-low RF and relatively low AC magnetic field where I can get sun, which I would like to change if I can. I keep the Wi-Fi feature of the router in the Florida house turned off and only use Ethernet. The house came with a number of smart appliances but fortunately I’ve succeeded in removing the WiFi chip from the stove/oven, turning the microwave’s Wi-Fi feature off, exchanging the washer + dryer for non-smart versions, and finding a TV from which I was easily able to remove the Wi-Fi chip without losing the ability to stream directly from Ethernet or otherwise impairing functionality (2023 Sony XL77; it’s also touted as one of the very few TVs that are flicker-free at all brightness levels by TV evaluation sites, which I haven’t independently confirmed, but could be another selling point for it if true).
I think one of the main sources of RF in Ocala could be my power company SmartMeter, but I haven’t opted out yet, which I’m thinking I probably should do. I might do some window shielding in the Florida house to see how close I can actually get to undetectable levels in certain areas, so if anyone has any window film recommendations or tips, I’d love to hear them.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-20T08:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354906>

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=354899)
>
> here does seem to still be some current on the ground system; any suggestions on where this might be coming from would be awesome, because I and the EMF specialist I’m working with are both kinda stumped at the moment. Have considered the metal gas line, copper A/C refrigerant line, cast iron drain pipe, cable internet/old TV/phone lines, electric panel, and ground rod being too close to the A/C compressor, utility meters, and/or electric service lines outside as possible sources, and it could just be an amalgamation of things, but curious if anyone with current on their ground was able to discover the source and/or resolve it in some way I haven’t thought of yet.

Quite straight forward to go through a process of elimination on site to narrow down the cause.. What were the current readings with main switch on vs off?

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=354899)
>
> _Electric Fields in D.C. House_: Body voltage measurements ("BVMs") laying in my bed in the D.C. house with the circuits off and using an outdoor ground stake as ground reference range from 0.06 to 0.1 V. With the relevant circuits on and nothing plugged in in the room, they’re about 2.2 V. I use an EMFSafeSwitch to remotely cut the circuits before I use the bed. I actually have no idea where the residual 0.06-0.1V is even coming from (maybe the ground wire?), but if anyone has any ideas or suggestions how to get rid of it, I would love to know.

Body Voltage **alone** is not good to rely upon.. It has many flaws to say the least. Very basic examples
\- it measures voltage (not field strength) i.e. pressure differential between you and the reference point.. By product of capacitive coupling (not direct field measurement)
\- Static accumulation skews readings
\- Low BV does not always = low electric field
\- You can get very low BV whilst still immersed in EF e.g. lying on a foam mattress (very poor conductivity + a dielectric insulator) = body is like a balloon floating in an electric field..

Physics - if capacitance is low (e.g. body floating on foam) then the induced charge is also low (low BV). Coupling governs how much BV appears, however the biological effects are driven by the presence and characteristics of the field it self (not just whether you collect measurable charge)...

BVM only measurements are like testing the depth of the swimming pool with your toe only..
BVM is not wrong, it is just incomplete if that is all you are relying upon.

BV is not an EMF meter. It's a bio-coupling indicator. It tells you how much your body is absorbing, not how strong the field is or how damaging it might be. So it is useful, but only when:

-   You understand its limits
-   You aren’t grounded during testing
-   You use it for comparisons, not absolute field strength

Measure with a decent 3D axis meter or a decent single axis and understand how to measure each axis and calculate the sum e.g. E total = square root of Ex2 + Ey2 + Ez2 (root mean square)

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=354899)
>
> _Dirty Electricity in D.C. House_: DE is quite bad in the D.C. house, as measured by both plug-in meters and by the EMF specialist with a Picoscope 2408. Seems to be coming in from the outside, as the levels measured actually increase as circuits are cut within the house (I guess since the DE isn't getting spread out as much to the circuits other than the one from which the measurement is taken). I don’t currently use any DE filters as I don’t want to risk making the situation worse than it already is and all of the filters I’ve seen on the market appear to have certain frequency ranges of DE that they actually amplify rather than mitigate. I’ve been told DE filters are a developing area of technology and that new and potentially better solutions could be available soon - could be cool to hear about this from anyone who’s more keyed in on it.

I assume the BB mapped, isolated and identified the dominant frequencies on each line for this already..

Cutting the circuits reduces the total sink, hence the concertation.

You will need a whole house "decent" filter within the attenuation range and energy demand.. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) - could help you out with this..
Just make sure you have identified the dominant harmonic bands and that the selected filter/s will take care of these (quite straight forward) then install and re-measure..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-21T02:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354932>

Body voltage does not give the whole picture but it is a useful tool to see if a water pipe, gas pipe, Ethernet cord etc is grounded or not. Hook up the BV meter (with shoes on) then touch the pipe or ethernet cord. If the reading goes down it is grounded. If the reading goes up it is carrying a field (maybe even current). Again, not the whole picture but useful in tracking things down.

Did an inspection on a house. Turned off the whole house and still had 2700mV BV in a child’s room. WHY?
Turns out the Sonos sound system had an automatic 120V power back up. When the main power went off it kicked in and the speaker wire and speaker, in the child’s flood, was putting out a 2700mV field under the child’s bed.

I’ve seen stuff like this on water pipes, ethernet, phone lines etc. all found with a BV meter.

### TB14 — 2025-07-25T21:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-356968>

> [PIAZ said:](/goto/post?id=356932)
>
> We wanted a meter to measure surroundings and ourselves - but this sounds too advanced for simpletons!

My first meter was the TriField TF2, which I think is a great intro meter. There is also the Cornet, but I can only speak to the TriField in terms of starter meters. The TF2 does a bit of almost everything at a relatively low price and I still find myself using it on occasion even after purchasing many other much more expensive meters. The TF2 measures:

(1) **AC magnetic fields**
\- 3-axis
\* This makes it easier and quicker to get an idea of exposure from all directions at a given point
\- Frequency Range: 40 Hz - 100 kHz
\* This is a good range; it has the same upper bound, for example, as the much more expensive Gigahertz Solutions ME3851A, and actually a higher upper bound than the Alpha Labs UHS2 (which tops out at 75 kHz)
\- Intensity Range: 0.1 mG - 100.0 mG
\* Comparable or superior to much more expensive meters along this metric as well

(2) **AC electric fields**
\- 1-axis
\* Harder and more time-consuming to get an idea of exposure from all directions at a given point than with a 3-axis meter, but even most more expensive AC electric field meters are 1-axis; plus electric fields are the hardest of the big three to measure in general anyway because the person holding the meter interferes with the measurements; but this can still sometimes be helpful for broad exposure measurement, keeping the limitations in mind. Single-axis AC electric field meters can also be helpful for tracing (live, unshielded) wires in walls.
\- Frequency Range: 40 Hz - 100 kHz
\* This is a good range; it has the same upper bound, for example, as the much more expensive Gigahertz Solutions ME3851A
\- Intensity Range: 1 V/m - 1,000 V/m
\* Comparable or superior to much more expensive meters along this metric as well

(3) **Radiofrequency**
\- Frequency range: 20 MHz - 6 GHz
\* The lower-end of the RF frequency range that the TF2 can measure is one reason I still use it, as my generally superior RF meter, the Safe and Sound Pro II, has a higher lower-end of its frequency range, 200 MHz; meaning, if the producer specifications are accurate, that the TF2 can pick up RF between 20MHz and 200MHz that the Safe and Sound Pro II would miss.
\- Inability to Detect Radar Pulses: Just thought I should note from experience that the Safe and Sound Pro II is capable of picking up high-intensity fast radar pulses that the TF2 misses, which is one major reason (among others) why multiple meters are necessary if more-comprehensive analysis is the goal.

(4) **Dirty Magnetic Fields**
\- The weighted magnetic fields measurement on the TF2 uses a formula under which from frequencies 60 Hz to 500 Hz, sensitivity increases proportionally to frequency, while at frequencies above 1 kHz, sensitivity decreases proportionally to frequency. The implication here would appear to be that magnetic fields with frequencies between 500 Hz and 1,000 Hz (1 kHz) are more harmful to human health than frequencies above or below these levels. I'm not sure of the accuracy of that notion, but comparing unweighted and weighted magnetic field measurements can help you get some idea of the dirtiness of a particular source of current (current produces magnetic fields). For example, I've found that incandescent lightbulbs have roughly equal standard magnetic field and weighted magnetic field measurements, while compact fluorescent lightbulbs ("CFLs"), in addition to generating higher levels of magnetic fields generally than incandescents, also have a bigger gap between the standard magnetic field measurement and weighted magnetic field measurement, indicating the presence of dirty current.

(5) Dirty Electric Fields
\- The weighted electric fields measurement on the TF2 uses a formula under which from frequencies 60 Hz to 500 Hz, sensitivity increases proportionally to frequency, while at frequencies above 1 kHz, sensitivity decreases proportionally to frequency. The implication here would appear to be that electric fields with frequencies between 500 Hz and 1,000 Hz (1 kHz) are more harmful to human health than frequencies above or below these levels, and while I'm not sure of the accuracy of that notion, comparing unweighted and weighted electric field measurements can help you get some idea of the dirtiness of a particular source of voltage (voltage produces electric fields).

### TB14 — 2025-07-26T23:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-357014>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=356982)
>
> You can also use a portable oscilloscope.

Any reccs for a relatively affordable oscilloscope set-up for measuring DE? My understanding is you need the following:

(i) The Oscilloscope itself;
(ii) An attenuator of 10x or greater to read the line voltage of 120 VAC (since the oscilloscopes are only generally capable of handling a max of +/- 20 V);
(iii) A 60Hz filter;
(iv) A differential probe with which to measure voltage (some appear to have the attenuator built in);
(v) A current clamp to measure current

Anything I'm missing?

Thinking about the following set-up:

\- **Picoscope 2208B**:
Bandwith: 100MHz
Sample rate: 1 GS/s
Memory: 128 ms
Channels: 2
Is there any reason why 4 channels would be much superior to 2? In theory it seems like it could potentially be useful in order to simultaneously monitor some combination of both hot and neutral, and/or both phases, and/or both voltage and current, but is there anything you can really determine with 4 channels that you couldn't catch with 2? There's a model of Picoscope (the 2408B) that seems nearly identical to the 2208B except that it has 4 channels instead of 2, but it's over $500 more expensive.

\- **Graham-Stetzer Ubiquitous Filter**:
Filters out: 60Hz

\- **Hantek HT-8100 (Voltage Differential Probe)**:
Attenuation: Yes, 50:1 or 500:1, which I think should be over the 10:1 necessary for the Picoscope to be able to handle the 120VAC
Am I correct that because of this attenuation by the voltage differential probe, I wouldn't need a separate attenuator for the Picoscope (as long as I was measuring voltage using this probe, not current)?
Bandwidth: 100MHz
Max Waveform Capture Rate: 30,000 wfm/s

\- **Fluke i1000s (Current Clamp)**
Maximum Voltage: 600 VAC
Usable Frequency: 5 Hz - 100 kHz
This is a more limited range than the voltage differential probe and oscilloscope - perhaps more research needed into the availability of current clamps with a higher maximum of its frequency range
Am I correct that because this clamp doesn't mention attenuation, an attenuator would be necessary when taking measurements using this clamp?

\- **Hantek HT-201 (Attenuator)**
Attenuation: 10:1 - should be sufficient for getting 120VAC down to below 20VAC
Bandwidth: 10 MHz
10MHz is beneath the bandwidth of the Picoscope 2208B (oscilloscope) and the Hantek HT-8100 (voltage differential probe), but I don't think it would be necessary when measuring those, only when using the current clamp, which has a max usable frequency of 100 kHz, so I don't think the 10 MHz bandwidth of the HT-201 should pose any limitations on the set-up since it would only be used with the current clamp with an even lower max frequency detected - does that seem correct?

>
> The easiest way to reduce RF-EMF exposure + reduce environmental stress… Put a faraday bag/beanie and aluminum foil over your Wi-Fi router. 20-30 fold reduction in emission on the spot

Hopefully, no one connects to the network & has wifi off on all devices (while shielded) due to the adaptive power control. As they can be worse off dynamically vs static conditions.. Your 20-30 fold (under static conditions) reduction can actually result in a net increase of total RF exposure (with greater packet re-transmissions & power ramp up at router + local device) if not managed correctly. For that reason IMO this is not a good idea. Just get rid of the wifi all together.. It is not needed and there is no reason for it period.. Not to mention the fire risk (void your house insurance policy) as this would be further driven by the adaptive power control ramp up (to full transmit power to bridge the low signal).

### TB14 — 2025-08-14T04:45:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-358201>

Ethernet-only at both places as well. Have been thinking about how best to block the electric fields coming in from on the Ethernet a bit recently. What I currently have that I'm not entirely satisfied is a powerbank-powered fiber optic buffer - the Ethernet from the router runs to a Ethernet-to-fiber-optic converter, then there's a short run (like three inches) of fiber optic cable, then a fiber-optic-to-Ethernet converter that's powered by a DC powerbank, and from there the Ethernet cable runs to the computer. The fiber optic cable disrupts the flow of AC electricity from the grid powering the modem, router, and first Ethernet-to-fiber-optic converter because it's made of glass (communicates using IR at 850nm) rather than copper like Ethernet cables. I have to change out the powerbank every morning before getting online. I know some people just ground their Ethernet but my DC house's grounding system is dirty (another area of recent focus), and I have to wonder how full-proof grounding it is even if I were to ground to the outside. I think totally interrupting the AC coming to the laptop by using the fiber optic buffer is likely better in theory.

The problem I'm now grappling with on that though is that although the battery pack is DC, it has a SMPS in it, specifically a boost converter to take the 3.7V and ramp it up to a steady 5V for the sensitive USB electronics (iPhone, etc.). I'm pretty sure the way it chops up the current is effectively creating AC. There is a measurable impact of plugging in the Ethernet at the laptop - AC electric fields go up from about 7V-8V when not plugged in to Ethernet to about 10V-11V when it is plugged in. That could also be from some internal component of the fiber optic to Ethernet buffer or I guess even from something my Mac is doing when it gets the Ethernet plugged in. But my top guess right now is it's the SMPS in the powerbank. My understanding of the market is that all the powerbanks these days have these boost converters in them. The next thing I could think of was just a 4.8V alkaline battery pack but I did the math and with four AA batteries drawing as much power as my fiber-optic-to-Ethernet converter is, four AA batteries would last me about six hours, which is not gonna cut it. More research to do here.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-14T08:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-358211>

Many basic ways to address this. Do a quick check first - ground your laptop to a clean earth (designated peg outside) then measure this again with ethernet plugged in.. Just get a spare USB cable confirm continuity with shield to connector (when stripped) and make your own cable to ground. Then post the measurements pre and post. I assume you are not using your laptop plugged in? plus I assume you cannot use your AC ground.

# Thread: Btc question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/btc-question.31147/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### optimal2024 — 2025-06-20T16:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/btc-question.31147/post-354916>

Hi

I have tried to look online but with no joy.

I am looking to invest a small amount of BTC in MSTY, via hanetf however the brokers they offer do not accept BTC.

I am struggling to find out whether converting the btc into gbp and investing in MSTY what the tax implications are?

If anyone could point me in the right direction it would be much appreciated.

### doctorwu85 — 2025-06-20T19:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/btc-question.31147/post-354918>

This depends on your local tax laws and N=1 tax situation, so nobody is going to be able to provide you with a perfect answer.

In the United States, you would typically use an exchange to sell your bitcoin for USD. This would trigger a tax event where, assuming the BTC has increased in value since you bought it, you would be required to pay capital gains taxes on the difference between the purchase and sale price. What percentage tax depends on how long you've held it, your tax bracket, etc.

In the US, the monthly distributions from MSTY are also taxed as normal income and not as capital gains, so they are taxed at a higher rate. All of this could be different in the UK. You should probably investigate how capital gains taxes work where you are.

I'm sure other physical therapists have a lot to add.

Healthy Disc = Hydrated Gel + Glial Charge + DC Current Maintains Structure

Degenerating Disc = Dehydrated + M1 Glia + No Current > Collapses.

The disc is an electro-gel antenna, not a bag of meat. You must tune the terrain before adding any signal, or you risk accelerating the degeneration you’re trying to reverse.

PRP can/will be damaging in shifted terrain.

Restore the DC pico amp Current and shift back to M2..

# Thread: Paige's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaigeK — 2025-06-21T02:28:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-354931>

Hello,
I'm a married 59yo female, Northern European (mostly German) ancestry.
Blood Type B-
mtDNA Haplogroup U, Subclass U2e1
Paternal German family lived in Volga River Valley in Russia for almost 150 years 1765-1900.

Birth Mom was generally healthy except a smoker and died at 67 with lung issues with untreated Lyme disease. She was born in Baltimore and lived near DC in Germantown, MD until age 7 then moved to Evergreen, CO.
Maternal grandmother is from MD, VA, WV, was healthy until the 6+ jabs, now 93 with blood clots, heart issues but lives independently and drives herself to have margaritas with her friends once a week.
Maternal great-grandmother died of bladder cancer in her 90's, lived in MD, VA, WV.

I have brown hair (now half silver), very light blue eyes and skin that tans and freckles, have developed white, brown and red spots over the years.
Born in Denver, CO. Raised in western burbs of Denver uncomfortably close to Rocky Flats where they made nuclear triggers for bombs and became a superfund site and is supposedly clean now. Currently live in Evergreen, CO at 8,000ft elevation. EMF specialist came a few days ago and found 5G blasting us, strong AC magnetic field from overhead power lines at 100' from our back door, I was sleeping with my head where power comes in to the house and a smart meter was just installed and my neighbors have solar that is affecting us. I was schooled in the devastating effects of wifi and our many Sonos speakers and devices. All are off now as is wifi. I need get out of here, I just don't know where to.

I was adopted as a newborn. I had severe allergies as a small child to this day. Allergy shots- ages 5-25.
As a child I had untreated ear infections and multiple treated cases of strep throat.
Tonsils/adenoids removed- age 12
Joined the Air Force - Age 18, (got all the jabs required in the 1980's & 90's) worked as an aircraft mechanic on KC-135, A-7, F-16 aircraft. (Many, many bad chemical/EMF exposures daily in those 9 years, 2 more years in admin, got out at age 29)(worked at Buckley SFB around the corner from the giant satellite dishes for 9 years)
Pelvic Inflammatory infection- age 19
Birth control pills- age 20-30
Migraines started- age 20
Kidney Infection- age 22
Vaginal birth of daughter, she had mild jaundice otherwise healthy- age 23
Cesarean birth of son, major birth defect (gastro-schesis, hypertonic), a lot of emotional trauma for me and a major infection followed
Concussion/closed head injury auto accident- age 28
Got out of the military - age 29
Breast implants inserted- age 29
Tubal ligation- age 30
Complete hysterectomy due to endometriosis - age 31
Started taking Premarin - age 31-38, then bio-identical bi-est to present.
Hypo-thyroid (healed this once but reoccurred and is current), Adrenal Fatigue age 31 (mostly healed now), started taking levothyroxine, DHEA, pregnenelone, natural cortisol supplements
Somewhere around here we got Wifi.
Breast Implants removed - age 43
Celiac diagnosed, Melasma, bunion/bunionette surgery right foot - age 43
Subtotal Colectomy w/ ileo-rectal anastomoses, sigmoidocele, cyctocele, rectocele repairs (due to colonic inertia, serious infection followed as well as severe anemia)- age 44
Upper GI bleed (esophageal tear) while climbing a strenuous 14k foot mountain- age 45
Rt shoulder rotator cuff repair- age 47
Abdominal scar revision surgery- age 47
Nasal terbinate reduction/septoplasty- age 49
Intraocular Lens Implants to correct vision- age 50 (blocks 90% of UV)
Lyme disease/ numerous co-infections, chronic pain (all original infections cleared) - age 51
Sjogrens/RA diagnosed (ANA still positive but RA now negative)- age 52
Took hydrocortisone for a year.
CVID immunodeficiency w/complement disorder diagnosed (IgG now borderline at 500, IgA low at 9)- age 52
Received crap biologics IVIG infusions plus Ruconest- ages 52-55 (the last year w/chest port, I quit before jabs became widespread in fall of 2020)
Non-length dependent small fiber neuropathy, mast cell activation, severe environmental chemical senstivities - age 52
Hashimoto's - age 53 (now in remission)
Osteoporosis - age 53 (has not progressed)
Aseptic Meningitis/sepis/GI bleed- age 53
Stiff Person Syndrome misdiagnosed- age 53 (it was wrong but they gave me rx of 6mg Xanax and Baclofen and I dumbly took it for 1 year, weaned off at age 54)
Stopped all infusions and prescriptions except for thyroid, bi-est, progesterone, testosterone, singulair- age 54
Numerous and various overlapping Lyme type infections, viruses, bacteria, parasites, one covid infection (no jabs)- age 55-59 (present)
I may have chronic pancreatitis since I need 10 Creon/day to digest food. I don’t drink much alcohol, so most likely autoimmune caused or aftermath of colectomy.
Son committed suicide, daughter became estranged - age 58
I'm finally supposedly free of infections for the first time since my son died 2 years ago. So I've begun my recovery! I want more now.
Recent brain MRI for recurrent migraines showed: Sequela of chronic microvascular ischemia.
My feet and ankles have been painful for 20 years (about the same time we got the first WiFi)
Now I have a neuroma in one foot.
I have a lot of floaters in my vision (maybe dead Lyme spirochetes, the eye dr thought I wouldn’t heal there were so many in my eyes).

Current Rx- Oxycodone, Creon, Levothyroxine, Hydroxycloroquin, Montelucast, Amitriptyline, Sumatriptan. compounded: Bi-estrogen, Progesterone, testosterone.

OTC- Pregnenolone, DHEA, B-Active, Vit C, D3, MK-7, iron, copper, osteo supplement, probiotics, beta plus (gall bladder support), cal/mag butyrate, manganese, xyzal, HistDAO, Quercetin, NAC, L-Lysine, Pharma GABA, Nasocort

I want to get off everything possible without crashing further.

I’m furious I let them break me with the colectomy and ocular lens implants.. and the other things (infusions and drug). Hopefully it’s not too late to heal some of the damage.

I am making plans to go to ES soon by myself for as long as it takes to heal.
Husband plans to sell house and move south. Thinking of TX but need more input. I don't want to jump out of the frying pan and into the fire. I have a feeling that I will want to stay in ES.

The Melasma on my face drives me crazy. I hope one day my mitochondria and system will heal and stop this. What is the melasma telling me? Will learn as much as I can.

### PaigeK — 2025-10-23T02:10:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-361451>

I went to El Salvador and spent 6.5 weeks in Shalpa with India (she is a gem!). During that time my body pain went down dramatically and my thinking became clearer again (thank God). I had planned to spend 6 months there but had to go home after I got a parasite and the medication I needed was nowhere to be found. I was miserable with tremendous itchy skin. I must also report that I got 3 total infections (mycoplasma pneumonia, trematodes and gut candida) which is not a surprise with my history. I've been able to actually absorb some of Jack's lessons lately (hooray!) and have been in the sun everyday since I have been home in the Colorado mountains. I'm thankful for the higher UV at 8,000 feet elevation while I'm still here. We have resolved to move (hubs refuses to leave US) and are currently considering far eastern Oklahoma in the Ozarks at 36º N on the Grand Lake O' the Cherokees. The closest military base is 70 miles (thoughts anyone?) I'm looking forward to no magnetic fields and mitigated other nnEMFs.

I see myself returning to ES annually most likely.

I had labs drawn and while I was considering the results, Jack's blog [https://optimalklubs.com/decentralized-medicine-76-why-are-anemia-and-autoimmunity-bedfellows/](https://optimalklubs.com/decentralized-medicine-76-why-are-anemia-and-autoimmunity-bedfellows/) came up and took a stab at reading it since I've had chronic anemia and autoimmunity. Wow, it seemed like he wrote it just for me.

The lab showed that I have slightly low mchc (dehydration, hypoxia and heme issues right?) with high calcium and albumin.
My immunoglobulins actually went up a tic (hooray, maybe from my time in ES?) IgG up from 597 to 602, IgA up from 69 to 72. Ok not fantastic, but they went UP, I'll take that little win.
My SARS CoV spike is 50 (I am not jabbed but got it once) is this good?
Thyroid levels look ok on my current dose and hashimotos is still negative (hashi was positive twice in the past 10 years).
However reverse T3 is within range but high at 21.5 (leptin resistance or ok?)
Vit D 25-Hydroxy is high at 112. I haven't taken oral vit D since August so this is what my skin is making?
Calcitrol (1,25 di-OH VitD) is 66.1. Is this difference showing my liver is not converting well?
ANA is still positive, barely at 1:80 but has changed patterns from speckled to homogenous, it that good or meh?

> Calcitrol (1,25 di-OH VitD) is 66.1. Is this difference showing my liver is not converting well?
> ANA is still positive, barely at 1:80 but has changed patterns from speckled to homogenous, it that good or meh?

We all have different medical histories (and systems) so this may not apply to your situation.
I have traveled to MANY third world countries and have always brushed my teeth and bathed in the local water and eaten local food in some VERY off the Main Street locations and several “on the street” locations and only had food poisoning (upset system) once. Ironically it was from a very traditional burger place in Australia.
Maybe I am lucky and have a good system BUT my personal rule when away from home is NEVER eat on an empty stomach . . .
I believe a glass of wine (or any alcohol) before eating out kills many of the things that you may eat that could cause problems. I’m guessing most microbes/parasites do not like alcohol. That’s my excuse and I’m sticking to it . . .

One of the joys of life is experiencing different cultures, customs and FOODS when traveling . . . as is being well. May I suggest an ounce (or several) of prevention is worth a pound of cure.

Ironically it was from a very traditional burger place in Australia.
> Maybe I am lucky and have a good system BUT my personal rule when away from home is NEVER eat on an empty stomach . . .
> I believe a glass of wine (or any alcohol) before eating out kills many of the things that you may eat that could cause problems. I’m guessing most microbes/parasites do not like alcohol. That’s my excuse and I’m sticking to it . . .
>
> One of the joys of life is experiencing different cultures, customs and FOODS when traveling . . . as is being well. May I suggest an ounce (or several) of prevention is worth a pound of cure.

Love it Anthony! Sadly, my gut is super "special" as is my immune system .. and not in a good way! Gut wise- no colon and chronically low salivary IgA makes for a fragile system. Immune wise- I don't even know where to start. But I'm not sweating it anymore and will keep rolling with the flow, tackling each issue as they come. Thank you so much for your comment, keep them coming!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Aussie Josh's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussie-joshs-optimal-journal.31151/>

### jvine — 2025-06-21T06:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussie-joshs-optimal-journal.31151/post-354938>

G'Day Im Josh from Newcastle, Australia.

Im 39 and after a fairly long journey of ill health, I am currently being investigated for histamine related reactive hypoglyceamia. This actually reared its ugly head in January of 2021 but we had no idea what was happening to me. After many dead end searches for answers I was able to realise that I was having low blood sugar and this was mediated by high histamine levels that were affecting my brain and also my sleep. This is also something that my mother was diagnosed with when she was 40 and her mother had also. I believe that catching Covid may have been the trigger to switch this program on in my DNA but not sure??

At the moment the best advice that I have been given is to eat small meals regularly & eat low GI. I have been following Jacks work for a couple of years now and so I catch the sunrise at our local beach every morning, catch as much sun as I can each day, I ground as often as possible, blue blockers at night and avoid EMF as much as possible, we drink RO water (was on Icelandic for a while but got quite expensive). The histamine seems the be the main problem and I would love some ideas around how to optimise my situation.

Im about to get some blood testing done soon so any ideas around this too would be awesome.

Cheers From Aus

Josh

# Thread: Getting spouse on board..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sarahnile — 2025-06-21T15:10:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-354952>

Hello everyone, I'm new here  Consuming more and more information and implementing in baby steps, I'm literally on my 7th consecutive sunrise. I have had unexplained symptoms for a year now and have been jumping down rabbit holes ever since. I've changed lightbulbs in the house, specifically the floor with our bedroom so that my night routine can be done in minimal to no light but I am married so obviously the bedroom, bathroom etc are all shared quarters. Each room has an option for regular incandescent light but I've got the red on when I'm winding down to sleep.

My husband (who contrary to me has actually always been an early riser and early sleeper) is in a little bit of a sleep-in phase these days, I think it's been a month or two. He came to the conclusion a while back that he must wait to sleep till midnight (or somewhere around there) otherwise he wakes up to a bowel movement at 4am, so he's been keeping himself up till that time looking at a screen of some sort till he passes out or comes to bed. I, admittedly used to be the one to get to bed late but we've switched now.

He had a pretty visceral reaction to the red light in our bedroom, bathroom, and closet while I was getting ready for bed last night. He walks up and turns a light on and gets upset if I ask him to turn it back off. He finds the lighting patronizing as if I'm dictating to him how he should live. I sleep with an eye mask but last night he looked at his phone for about an hour next to me in bed, I covered myself up but of course I'm pissed and then too hot under the covers to fall asleep so it's a cluster fuck  He said this morning "10 days ago you were fine with light at night and now you're not, it's troubling." also that he supports my waking up early in the morning but the night time lighting is really irking him, and he misses me at nights

Has anyone had similar problems like this? Any pointers? I sent him a podcast a few days ago to check out, I'm not sure if he listened, it specifically had information on weed smoking which he does sometimes, also alcohol once in a while, and there's a video game he loves. He works during the week and goes to the gym often, he plays tennis when he can but complains about not being able to lose some last bit of pudge on his waistline. He's been open enough to try carnivore in the past in the name of weight loss but then said taking out all carbs and the lack of variety made him sad, also that he never felt full or satisfied. He doesnt really get "sick" necessarily often but he does have days where he says he's "exhausted" and then he's horizontal all day. This month on the other hand he caught a cold twice and he's trying to recover from the 2nd one as I write. He had 4 days of an exhibition for work in closed doors under blue light and very crowded, he said he didn't really eat well either so I'm guessing his body gave out. He chose to recover playing his computer game, I was successful in getting him outside a couple of times and I caught him catching fresh air on his own as well.

All of this is screaming low dopamine but he may come around with time. I am not willing to go back after what I've been exposed to so far and only wish to learn more. It's surprising to me honestly how he hasn't said wow, is that true? and dove in with me.

Is there any content out there for the resistant? Or a specific approach someone has been successful with? I don't need him to start grounding with me but I would like to be able to have a seemless night time routine and get him out of the room if he's not done with his device and it's after sunset.

i think the solution all of us would say is you tried. if it was me id leave her immediatley. let him spread his wings how he sees fit.let him down gently and say " i loved you but this isnt gonna work , your weighing me down and i was meant to fly liek a eagle" my wings have been clioped my whole life, no more! ! this stops now!
we all have wings to spread but some of ours are bigger and prettier than others and that irritates them to the point of them hitting our feathers with their heavy greasy feathers that were meant to bring both birds down.
as one of my great teachers use t o say
" you can put your hand out to help once twice but if put out again it will be chopped off and you will be a one hand man that wont be able to wipe himsl3ef which is no man now"

and you may want to buy a new front door lock also

your welcome

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-21T22:45:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-354970>

I have done many in home assessments where the spouse was “not convinced” about EMF.
I have a magnet I can spin that throws out a field that my gauss meter registers going through my head.
I use the “E-pen” to show the electric field going through two or three people holding hands.
I explain the 120V AC field in their bedroom is 60Hz and to get to sleep the brain has to get to Alpha 8Hz - 12Hz.
On multiple occasions the guy then takes my meters and goes around the house taking measurements.
I have shown the RF level 4ft from a running microwave oven is the same 4ft from the WiFi router.
Bluetooth headsets are 100x higher than background RF.
That gets their attention.
Most guys love gadgets. Once they can see the nnEMF, most get on board or at least are not so negative.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-22T03:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-354981>

Most people seem like they don’t want to change, but in reality it’s often not about desire, it’s about dopaminergic capacity. Low dopamine means low agency. And agency is what gives us the ability to initiate change, imagine a better future, and take action toward it.

When dopamine signaling is disrupted through poor redox, no morning light, excess blue light, chronic inflammation, trauma, or mitochondrial dysfunction, people literally can’t feel the pull of change. The motivational circuitry goes offline. They default to the path of least resistance, even if that path is painful.

So it's not that they don’t want to, it’s that they neurologically can’t. Their system is stuck in survival mode.

Change your own environment: light, magnetism, redox. Even if they don’t join in, their shared field does shift.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-22T20:26:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-355029>

> [sarahnile said:](/goto/post?id=355020)
>
> I haven't started with testing my house for EMF yet, I need to learn a little more of what I need so that I don't go spending money on things I don't know how to use, it's kind of daunting, and scared of what I might find.. But thank you for this, this makes a lot of sense.

The answer to what you need depends on who you ask . . . And how much you want to (can) spend.
RF - the Safe & Sound Pro II is a great meter ($400)
Magnetic - the Alpa Labs UHS2 (3 axis) Gauss meter is good ($400)
Electric fields - the TriField for “spot” readings is ok ($180)

My preference for in home electric fields is the Body Voltage (BV) method. Others disagree. BVK $165

The Cornet Quad 5G Pro measures spot electric fields, magnetic fields, radio frequency (RF) 5G calibrated AND light flicker ($220)
The Cornet Quad meter is a great place to get started at a reasonable price.

We offer the EMFSafe Starter Kit - BVK, Cornet Quad, extra ground cord, with 30 min consulting for $445 with 10% off Kruse discount and free shipping.
We help you get set up, get the readings, understand them and come up with a mitigation plan. [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Mat.I — 2025-06-23T06:15:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-355061>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=355029)
>
> My preference for in home electric fields is the Body Voltage (BV) method. Others disagree.

Body Voltage doesn’t directly measure the electric field strength - what it shows is the voltage your body accumulates through capacitive coupling with the surrounding electric field. So yes, it’s an indirect measure, and the result depends heavily on how well you're coupled (e.g., mattress, posture, nearby conductors). It's not disagreement - it's just understanding how the physics of capacitive systems work. If using the BV method, always have a decent EF meter to compare and check all 3 axis (x, y, & z) to cross check and understand what is happening in each spatial plane..

### moon77 — 2025-06-21T17:16:20+0300

### S Hudson — 2025-06-21T20:05:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-gargle-with-a-bit-of-peroxide.31153/post-354964>

It depends why you are doing it.
If it is to remove plaque, don't bother. Only manual brushing can budge that.
Plaque is a biofilm that forms in layers. no mouthwash can penetrate established biofilm.

### digitald3 — 2025-06-21T20:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-gargle-with-a-bit-of-peroxide.31153/post-354968>

Good oral health requires a solid metabolism. Which means managing light diet and food diet. And because in a modern world managing the light diet is really hard. It’s wise to use a tooth brush and floss to disrupt the plaque. Toothpaste and rinses are superfluous for general health daily. There are rinses, like chlorine dioxide that will penetrate the biofilm but Introduce chlorine to the system. Peridex/chlorhexieine can be helpful if acute infections are present. Peridex has been shown to kill fibroblasts in culture so it has its potential downsides too. It also stains teeth.

Fluoride, nano hydroxyappetite, hydrogen peroxide et al are all adjuncts to the real story. Which is too much blue light and not enough sun. Blue light is the sugar story. As the a light diet full of blue often correlates to a high carbohydrate diet. None of the magic agents above will not completely solve a bad environment and diet.

### moon77 — 2025-06-22T15:18:27+0300

mat do you think dmso is stronger and more useful than methylene blue?
for someone trying to assist or correct their health

### JenniferM — 2025-06-22T19:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dmso.31155/post-355026>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=354978)
>
> ### [Therapeutic DMSO Combinations Revolutionize Medicine](https://open.substack.com/pub/amidwesterndoctor/p/therapeutic-dmso-combinations-revolutionize?utm_source=share&utm_medium=android&r=22nca8)
>
> How DMSO being mixed with numerous common medications creates a myriad of remarkable therapeutic possibilities
>
>  open.substack.com

Thank you for the information you have presented in your papers. I am proof DMSO works. I researched it when bloodwork was showing something was brewing in my lungs. I mixed it with Frankincense, Myrrh and ginger swiping across my upper chest. 6 months later CT picked up 1 ground glass nodule and 3 mets (4 total so they thought.) I reviewed the scans and began using the mix right over the 4 lung mets. CEA was high at 12.x so plan with 1st lung surgeon consult was kitchen sink chemo "and see what happens", next step would be SBRT, then next step would be take my middle right lung because a met was on the bronchial tube (to hard to get) and wedge resections. I said No Thanks! I contacted a surgeon in Germany whose expertise in Rolles Lung Laser surgery (lung sparing procedure) to get his opinion. He said he could get all 4 mets. When I had surgery they were impressed that the mets were 75% dead with only a minor amount of cancer cells remaining in 3 mets. The fourth met turned out to be a macrophage M2.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-22T21:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355036>

Not a Lawyer but -
Naked in public is against the law in most places (except a LGBTQ+ parade).
Naked in private is everyone’s right. At least in America, where do you live?
Take photos of where you were.
Show, if you can, you were not making a “public” display, but you were on private property.
Were you making a public display?
Were you visible from any of your neighbor’s windows?
It is also my understanding (in most places) you have the right to face your accuser.
File charges of spying and invasion of privacy against them.

What a wild read. Hey man. You will get past this. I don't know your specific circumstances as far as the arrest went or how public your environment where you live is, but unfortunately sometimes people are ridiculous and you may have to navigate around the rules or risk stupid things like this happening. I am guessing this occurred in a area more open to the public and not in a fenced yard etc.

If health is a priority and you do not care for the UV penetrating shorts, you have to be creative. Hell, make some type of privacy shield whether it be cardboard or otherwise and create a barrier that still lets you get sun but can block some prying eyes.

Best of luck.

### doctorwu85 — 2025-06-23T18:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355100>

I cannot emphasize this enough:

DO NOT talk to law enforcement without a lawyer present.

They will say the meeting with the shrink is for your benefit, all the while trying to manipulate you into giving them evidence to use against you. Just don't do it. They are not doing something to help you. This is classic cop misdirection. They are professionally trained to build a case against you, and not to do anything that's "beneficial to you and your case". Wake up and smell the coffee.

Make sure you have representation there. It sounds like you have some serious judgment issues, but I wish you the best. You are probably fucked either way, but you are certainly fucked if you go talking to these guys without a lawyer to advise you.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355126>

This is not legal advice, but first and foremost, the advice above about not talking to the police is generally good. More specifically, one of the big risks of doing so is accidentally and unnecessarily admitting to an element of the crime, even if not admitting to the whole thing. For example, if the crime is indecent exposure, the accused might tell the police "I didn't think anybody could see me! I would never have sat naked on my deck if I knew my neighbors were home!" Although such a statement might be good enough for exoneration if the accused was taken at their word on every element of it, at trial, it could be used as evidence beyond a reasonable doubt that they were, in fact, naked on their porch, which is doing the prosecution's work for them. This follows from the fact that if the defendant wasn't naked on their porch, there would be no logical reason for them to say that they were. So that admission might be considered as rock-solid proof. The portion of the statement claiming lack of intent, by contrast, might be given essentially no weight, because regardless of the defendant's true intent, they would have had a clear motive (attempting to avoid punishment) to claim that the exposure was accidental. So such a statement would likely be unwise even though if it were taken as true in its entirety it would result in a finding of innocence, because the only work it's actually likely to do in practice is towards a finding of guilt.

Also, I have no experience at all with this particular issue, but the fact that somebody is on their own property seems very unlikely to me to be a complete defense. The vast majority of criminal law as applied to everyday people is based in common sense. That's certainly not to say I think all of the criminal laws are correct, but just ask yourself "would a normie think this should be illegal?" and the answer has probably a 95+% correlation with whether it in fact is. If somebody is changing in their bedroom and they accidentally leave their window cracked, and this is the first time it happens, then no, that's almost definitely not illegal. If somebody is regularly deliberately calling attention to themselves while totally nude on their front porch five feet away from a busy intersection at rush hour, then yes, that's almost definitely illegal.

Ultimately almost all crimes have an element of mens rea, which is basically state of mind with respect to the undesirable outcome at issue, that has to be proven along with the other elements. I would guess that the mens rea is likely usually the deciding factor as to whether a crime has been committed in most cases where the prosecution is indeed able to prove beyond reasonable doubt that somebody was involuntarily visually exposed to another person's nude body. I don't know what the mens rea is for indecent exposure in any particular jurisdiction, and I should note that some crimes with sexual undertones (especially if involving minors) are among the extremely rare exceptions to the general rule of even the existence of a mens rea element (_i.e._, for a small number of crimes, intent doesn't matter - if you committed the act, you're guilty), but I would guess that anything a defendant can do to show that they actually took steps to try to avoid being seen would likely be helpful to their case, while any obvious steps that weren't taken might make a conviction more likely. For example, was the defendant on the side of their property with the least or the most public visibility when the incident took place? If so, that may be relevant, as it's arguably unlikely to be a coincidence that they chose that particular side. Was this the first time the defendant was provably made aware that they could be seen from the position they chose by somebody else - no prior warnings/requests, calls to the police, etc. - or the twentieth time? Also likely relevant. Putting up barriers that leave no doubt as to whether they were deliberately assembled for the sole purpose of making a good faith effort to avoid being seen would likely be highly relevant.

### TB14 — 2025-06-24T20:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355178>

The Bill of Rights, including the Fourth Amendment, only applies to government action.

In general, if someone was actually going out of their way to view someone else naked, I would think that would very likely work heavily against not only one but two elements of the crime of indecent exposure: (i) the involuntary nature of the exposure with respect to the alleged victim, and (ii) the mens rea requirement with respect to the accused.

If someone accused of indecent exposure were to try to flip the tables on the accuser by accusing them of invasion of privacy, I find it difficult to predict how that might go. I'm very confident that laws against invasion of privacy do exist - the common sense test tells me that Peeping Toms are committing a crime - but how grey areas in the application of that law would likely be resolved isn't as obvious to me except in cases without much nuance. For example, tons of people have security cameras on the outside of their property now. If the camera is set up in a relatively normal way (_i.e._, for general surveillance purposes, not targeting a neighbor specifically) and happens to incidentally catch a neighbor sunbathing nude because they were doing it outside, even if that neighbor was on their own property while so doing, then the common sense test tells me that the filming neighbor has committed no crime. If the camera is set up specifically so that somebody can look into their neighbor's bedroom window to watch them change and clearly has no other purpose, then the common sense test tells me that the filming neighbor has committed a crime.

It's much less clear to me whether a crime would have been committed if the neighbor was in fact targeting the sunbather specifically, while the sunbather was on their own property, and while the sunbather was nude; but only because the neighbor believed that the nude sunbather was committing a crime by being nude under the circumstances in which the sunbather was filmed and the neighbor wanted to record evidence of the commission of that believed crime to present to law enforcement. There's a lot of nuance here and a lot of competing interests - it feels like a fact pattern on a bar exam or a case from a law school casebook. You have competing property rights (your right to privacy on your property versus their right to film from their property), whether to encourage or discourage vigilantism in law enforcement activities (including surveillance) and under what circumstances, the quasi-sexual element (due to the nudity) that has repeatedly overriden other long-standing and otherwise highly prioritized principles in other areas of criminal law in recent times, somewhat of a privacy versus expression angle if filming is viewed as a form of expression, and more. I don't really have a good guess as to which way this would go. The right person to ask would probably be a criminal attorney in the applicable jurisdiction.

Another possibility besides trying to cause criminal charges to be brought against the filming neighbor might be pursuing civil action. The sunbathing neighbor could potentially file suit for invasion of privacy or a similar tort. Again, I don't know what the applicable law would be here in any particular jurisdiction, and there would likely be the same competing interests as noted in the paragraph above that would go into determining that law, but two potential benefits for the sunbather of going the civil route might be that (i) the sunbather wouldn't be reliant on law enforcement to bring charges (the sunbather could file the suit themself or with the assistance of an attorney, which if properly done I believe at least all but guarantees at least that it's considered at some level on its legal merits by someone with legal training), and (ii) the burden of proof would be less than the beyond reasonable doubt standard that's necessary to secure a criminal conviction. The latter could be relevant if, for example, it was impossible to prove beyond a reasonable doubt that the filming neighbor was intentionally targeting the sunbather with their camera (rather than incidentally catching the sunbather on a more general surveillance camera), but it could be established that it was much more likely than not that they were.
