Best practice is to do it all air gapped with the SD card, that way it is never connected to the net.. Also sparrow is a good wallet desktop to run for the setup. Also, cold card website tutorials are very easy to follow. And for good digital hygiene learn how to verify the keys/ signatures prior to downloading software (tutorial should still be on sparrow site) . Should also be tutorials on YT on this. It is pretty straightforward once you do it a few times..

# Thread: Decentralized Medicine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine.31222/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiohackersTO — 2025-06-30T18:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine.31222/post-355537>

Would it be helpful to have a category for posts called "Decentralized Medicine"?

Maybe the entire forum is about decentralized medicine, but the term is so broad that I would love to have a place to gather together basic definitions (so we are all on the same page on what we mean by decentralized medicine) and then branch out into the topic of decentralizing the administration of medicine (for example, patient self-custody of their own medical records...)

Here is what I understand underpins the term "decentralized medicine"

It goes back to nature being decentralized. Nature is decentralized because we are all free to make different choices of strategies for survival. No one bails us out from our own dumb choices. But when we are all decentralized and we all live with the choices we have made, we can observe which choices get the best success at survival and longevity, and if we are lucky, we can course-correct, based on observing how other strategies work.

The parts of decentralized medicine that interest me lately include the decentralization of medical research, with protection from censorship and the decentralization of patient medical records. The savages among patients of decentralized medicine might self-custody their own medical records (like bitcoiners self custody their own coin).

Here is a podcast where some folks are creating these solutions on Nostr.

The self-custody of medical records has become quite relevant in an age where health regulatory bodies enter offices like a fucking mafia, grab all the medical records they want and then mine data from the records to find ways to professionally crucify practitioners whose practices are deemed a threat to big harma and are then slandered as a threat to patient health or public health. I heard through the grapevine that a compounding pharmacist I used during the scamdemic to obtain ivermectin was raided in such a fashion, in order to find ways to professionally crucify an MD or two who did not tow the line. Back then, I was using a Nurse Practitioner as a prescriber and it seemed the nurses regulatory body was not deployed to destroy nurse practitioners who prescribed ivermectin, as was the case for MDs. I have heard that now the regulators for nurses and naturopaths are harassing clinics and professionals.

As a patient, I do not want my records to be accessed by any regulator without my permission. The law is not about to protect us on this. Certainly not in Canada, but possibly not in the USA either. There is no will to do that here. But what if the technology can do that? What if savages self-custody their own medical records and the only way to access them is through patient permission?

Then the regulator would have to individually request patient records from each patient in order to have access to data to mine to harass medical professionals. And savages will ask, what do you want them for? Or will simply tell them to fuck off.

On the subject of censorship-proof publication, the project described in the podcast above, Medschlr, aims to make medical research and the discussions about it censorship proof. Because I bitch and moan a lot about centralized medicine in an Ontario Bitcoiners group, I have been invited to participate and provide suggestions to Medschlr.

I would love to reorient my career into something useful around the administration of decentralized medicine. Possibly it's an oxy-moron to say the "administration" of decentralized medicine. But just like with Bitcoin, there will be a need for education. I recently spoke to a nurse who works at a clinic that is raided regularly by the medical mafia because they offer alternative cancer treatments. When I described patient self-custody of medical records to this nurse, she said, on the one hand it's a good idea, but she doesn't have faith that most patients are up to the task. That sounds very similar to self-custody of one's money. It takes more time, attention, dedication and education. Maybe it's a savages-only club that self-custodies its own medical records. And maybe that club will need education in medical record self custody. If there is place to develop systems of administration of decentralized medicine for savages, I am very interested.

In the meantime, since I've been invited to weigh in on Medschlr, here is where my mind goes with starting useful discussions in that one and I'd love feedback:
\- Since Medschlr creates an online, censorship-proof space for discussion of medical research, could a censorship-proof discussion be started around the harms of SV-40? I have noticed that we have extensive access to academic journal articles with a Toronto Public Library card (as the TPL has deals with a bunch of platforms that let us access scientific journal articles), but magically, the studies on SV-40 only show the abstracts and we cannot download or access the full articles. Is every important SV-40 article behind a paywall? Or could some be publicly displayed on a forum like Medschlr? And even if not, the discussions around SV-40 could cite every study discussed, show the receipts, so to speak.
\- Would Medschlr like to create a discussion on tracking health problems associated with different jab batches, incorporating information from Kevin McKernan's work and the Pfizer papers and all the studies that have come out on harms?
\- What about posting case reports on patient outcomes from Dr. William Makis' cancer protocol?
\- What about posting case reports on the application of a Makis-like protocol on other conditions that involve a Warburg shifted metabolism (like endometriosis)?
\- What about posting case reports on a quantum biology protocol of tracking every sunrise seen without obstruction of glass, sun exposure, cold exposure and DDW?

I don't know if the folks running Medschlr have an appetite for any of this. Whether they do or they don't, the fact that technologies are being developed to do this is very helpful and I'd like to get involved.

In terms of getting myself out of the eye of the storm (Canada), I have plans to head to El Salvador in the coming Fall or Winter, take a look around and see what I can do to move there. If anyone knows of any opportunities for remote work in anything related to the above, please let me know. I have a degree in Economics (i.e. Keynesian brainwashing at the outset, but on a better path now with Saifedean Ammous' work) and a Masters in Business Analytics. I'm fluent in English and French and I will be a fast learner of Español.

I'm hoping for a section where we speak/write directly about decentralized medicine.
Because it can mean many things and in writing about it and discussing, we can be clear on what we mean.
And then we can strategize on the best ways to roll out decentralized medicine into the world.

We have got to go back to the UK early next year and really do not want too so I have come up with this plan for moving to El Salvador and wondered what members thoughts are?

My plan is to buy enough MSTY and a bit on top for living expenses, I will have a small regular income as well but not enough to live on so we will use MSTY to top up. I already own BTC and will not be using this to live on (hodl).
My questions are

1.  What do members think of this strategy?As I have never used an income fund before (MSTY) to live on?
2.  I have looked online but cannot see what tax we would pay for the MSTY dividends and the small amount of income we will receive from the UK once we Become residents of El Salvador?

I already have an online business but can’t take it with me to El Salvador due to the time difference so I am hoping the above strategy can work as it’s my only hope of getting my family to El Salvador away from the hellhole UK!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355582>

Is exercise not the goal of walking dogs?

Is the actual reason to allow them to ground & get solar exposure.

So is it accurate to call it animal abuse, if a pet owner does not walk their dog or let them go outside regularly? Or feed them out of season foods?

What do pet owners think?

I've never personally owned/had a pet.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-01T22:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355583>

Some cats never go outside. They are grounding to certain extent by being on the floor BUT they are also exposed to ALL the in home nnEMF!
I think all dogs get to go out several time a day so they get sun and good grounding in the back yard away from nnEMF.
People with no back yard have to walk their dogs And if they are sitting most of the day the exercise (walking) is good for both the dog and human along with the sun. The dog will benefit from grounding out side but the human will not unless bare footed or has grounding shoes.

### AlbertD — 2025-07-01T22:48:29+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355586>

Any time I've taken my dog outside, she loves to lay on the ground and capture some sun. As Uncle Jack has pointed out, once she's had enough, she naturally goes into the shade. Should it be considered abuse not to let our pets out in the sun? In my opinion, Yes, given that all it takes is to at least open the window and let some photons into the house. Most people live with closed, heavily draped windows until night time which is a detriment not just to pets but pet owners, as we know. I also agree with Anthony on the nnEMF side of things.

### Carly18 — 2025-07-02T16:10:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355616>

A clue that my home environment wasn’t healthy was how quickly my and my dog’s health collapsed during the scamdemic.
I attributed my health issues with stress but when my dog passed due to “idiopathic epilepsy”, the ER vet suggested he may have had a brain tumor. This was within the timeline in which I had started RLT in the winter and discovered Dr Kruse in the spring. My health improved significantly with sunrises, turning off wifi unless necessary and grounding as much as possible.
I’m now pet sitting for a friend and all of her animals are out at sunrise with me, asking for breakfast an hour later and easily go to bed at the same time I do (within 3 days of being here). The older dog sometimes sleeps indoors during the hotter times of the day but everyone else is always outside while we can. The black cat is my suntanning buddy and brings me mouse treats on the deck. The dogs and I eat lots of fresh eggs laid that day. The wifi nor the tv is ever on. I prefer the birds and the peace.

They are asleep within a hour after sunset, and awake an hour or more before sunrise, so they see every sunrise, and since I get up with them at 5:30 each morning, we spend time outside together, with me trying to learn from them.

### sarahnile — 2025-07-02T11:08:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/updated-labs.31227/post-355603>

I have attached some updated labs of mine, I am aware that the laboratory did the wrong pyruvate and I have to take that test again. My symptoms for the past year have been post nasal drip, swelling submandibular glands with pain, headache. Then the odd joint pain here and there. I had had 6 rounds of antibiotics in total. starting with an unresolving UTI, Then doctors thought I had pharyngitis, sinusitus.. My health got turmed on its head. I have not done EBV and CMV PCR's and the dysbiosis test, they are more on the pricier side  I will do them if I need to. My previous labs were done in Nov/Dec 2024. My ANA, UIBC, C3 have not changed. My ECP was previously 46.6 and now it is 17.2. I am on sunrise #19. I've been grounding for 2 hours every day and implementing a kind of modified AIP / EPI PALEO diet. I have not yet tested my environment for nnEMF. My symptoms shifted after week 2 from swollen painful gland flares to upper body aches, as if I had lifted weights for the first time in a while. Post nasal drip has decreased but not gone and no sore throat but for the past 4 days I'm extremely achy all of a sudden in my upper torso, arms and hands namely. It has lifted today for the first time and I am monitoring. I am only supplementing magnesium. I must do a check on my environment soon but I have been trying to ease into it due to my husband. He is slowly coming around, starting to make jokes etc. instead of lashing out but basically he finds the modifications to light "extreme" but is so far respecting my needs on his good days. Last night he wanted to look at his phone again in bed but with his back turned to me. I went to another room to sleep but I think our room is actually the best location in the house for sleeping, away from the street and electrical box but of course I can never be sure, I must test. Anything that stands out to anyone re my labs or any more labs I should get done? I will retest lactate/pyruvate. Does anyone have any input regarding the ANA positive, DFS70 +++positive and Anti Mi-2 +positive? I've never had Anti Mi-2 done before, this is a first.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-02T11:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/updated-labs.31227/post-355604>

I gave some feedback in your other post re: your labs - however, I was thinking that the recent shift could be due to your immune system trying to recalibrate due to the circadian corrections (i.e. doing the sunrises etc). As your immune cells all have clock genes. Due to your poor light environment prior to changes being made this would have scrambled the SCN's signals and when the master clock loses it's timing immune cells also loose their timing..

**Do not hold me to this** but the shift you are experiencing may be positive as it may be part of the "recalibration" process. As the immune cells would be reorganising. Whilst this is happening they may be dumping out "old" inflammatory signals, shifting fluid and adjusting the redox balance.. The areas of complaint are linked with major lymph drainage pathways.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - would be best to give you feedback as I could be off the mark here..

Also, prioritise having your environment assessed by a Building Biologist as chronic VGCC activation (from EMF) can prime the immune system into a low-grade inflammatory state. There is a spectrum here, however this will eventually shift you towards a FE3+ state.

Cancer markers are up (CEA is down from 17.x to 12.x but the others are upper limits of normal).
I get a letter from a Dr. Carelon, MD this morning saying he in with BCBS as a consultant or something for them. The letter stated I will no longer be able to have the Signatura test thru insurance. He claims it has no real proof it does any good. Also I canceled my CT/contrast scan for late July because I think I have had enough radiation on my body for the last 6 years and requested an MRI for Oct. The MRI was also denied. Heathcare is sick care and getting worse by the day. I only use insurance for bloodwork, scans and surgeon appt co-pay. The rest of my treat is personalized out of pocket. Now the surgeon will have to fight for the MRI and hopefully win the battle. Otherwise I'm screwed.

# Thread: Clotilde's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal.31233/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clotilde — 2025-07-03T04:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal.31233/post-355647>

Hello everyone.
It has been over 2 years since I started my journey to optimal health.
Issues I started with...
Inflammation
Teeth/Gum problems
Insulin resistance
Leptin Resistance
Adrenal Fatigue
Hormonal Imbalance
Blue Light toxic
EMF toxic (my discovery)
Trouble Sleeping
Gut issues - digestion, bloating
I am blessed to have been introduced to Jack Kruse by my daughter Emmanuela. She has and is helping me understand and put into practice Jack Kruse's prescriptions/protocols and suggests reading material, podcast, blogs etc. she is brainiac speed reader who remembers and comprehends everything she reads.

My first step was taking charge of my teeth and gum issues, I first read the book "Cure Tooth Decay"and Nutrition and Physical Degeneration by Dr. Weston A. Price. Both the books were outside my comfort zone as at that time I was a vegan!. I knew that my diet choices were contributing to my health problems. I changed my diet to include eggs, raw dairy and seafood and fish. I did experience positive changes with my gums and teeth. As I followed the protocol in the book "Cure Tooth Decay. The drastic changes in my health, however, began when I followed the Leptin Prescription and began taking actions in resetting my circadian rhythm. The power of watching the sunrise every morning has amazing results, like also bringing your glucose level down as well as grounding for charging yourself with electrons which helped me with back pain. I also, started the Epi Paleo RX (I also read the book). Introducing meat has been a slowly increasing change. I made changes in my environment at home, by changing all my light bulbs to incandescent and red and black light. I was a gym rat... I cancelled my gym membership, unplugged my TV, wake-up at sunrise and sleep by 9:00 pm to 9:30 pm. I spend as much time as possible outside. I also had trouble falling asleep at night because of the blue light exposure, at home, at the gym and work! Now when I have no control over the lighting I wear blue-blocking glasses and the WiFi in my home is off I use Ethernet connection.
Now, all these changes were positive steps towards my health journey, however, the monster in my life was nnEMF!
To be continued......

### sarahnile — 2025-07-03T15:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-on-past-surgery-scars.31235/post-355663>

Hello all, I heard Dr. Kruse say on a podcast that using red light on an area where there was a surgery would make things worse. The reasons weren't elaborated on in this specific podcast, or maybe I didn't catch it, but could anyone enlighten me on that topic? Why / how does it make it worse and does it apply to all kinds of surgery, new and old? For example, would red light on an old C-section scar be non beneficial and in fact do more harm than good?

### moon77 — 2025-07-03T17:12:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-on-past-surgery-scars.31235/post-355668>

> [sarahnile said:](/goto/post?id=355663)

> Hello all, I heard Dr. Kruse say on a podcast that using red light on an area where there was a surgery would make things worse. The reasons weren't elaborated on in this specific podcast, or maybe I didn't catch it, but could anyone enlighten me on that topic? Why / how does it make it worse and does it apply to all kinds of surgery, new and old? For example, would red light on an old C-section scar be non beneficial and in fact do more harm than good?

not sure he would recommend red over the sun.

as marks uncle use to tell me , you can put your finger in the door to land the interview but if you want to get the job your hand has to touch the ground

### sarahnile — 2025-07-03T17:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-on-past-surgery-scars.31235/post-355675>

I am not saying in place of, in addition to, possibly. I'm more interested in the why behind it than finding an excuse to use red light on a scar.

### digitald3 — 2025-07-04T15:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-on-past-surgery-scars.31235/post-355720>

Look up scar reduction and Photobiomodulation in a search engine. There are studies that show benefits to using red light therapy or Photobiomodulation on scars. The downside is man made lights aren’t as good as the sun. And it’s easy to over do the effects. The rule of thumb is only use PBM every 48 hours It’s far better to get your scar in the am sun and use the sun everyday. Which means getting your belly and scar in the sun. You can use the sun daily as it’s the sun!

My circadian rhythm is good with Ra optics, but since we moved, I have failed to eliminate wifi like we did in our old house, as we must run Ethernet under our crawl space. The beautiful thing about nature is she will do whatever she needs to do to survive. Remember we are meant to be a part of nature and it sounds like you are currently living apart from nature. Disconnecting from nature will bring on disease. Your body will do what it needs to do to try to survive... when you are sleeping it is choosing to give your brain oxygen over sleep. Forcing that sleep will deprive your brain of the oxygen it needs. A bad nnEMF environment will rob your body of EZ water and oxygen... keeping you dehydrated and hypoxic. You don’t really need to be caught up to realize your health is suffering because your connection with nature is suffering. It is always Redox before you Detox. If you value your health you will prioritize redox before detox... clean up your environment and reconnect with nature.

Dr. Kruse has done some great podcasts the last few years and his Twitter thread has great info as well.

# Thread: homebody
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homebody.31239/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenniferM — 2025-07-04T03:29:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homebody.31239/post-355701>

I literally stay close to home. Haven't been to walmart in over 20 years or the grocery store. Today I was going to take my grandson to the movies (he begged me to take him to see Minecraft) but it wasn't playing any more thank goodness. I remembered how sad he was not having a bicycle in 2 years so I thought I'm getting him a bicycle today. First stop the bicycle shop, it was closed on Thursdays. So, went to walmart. Got him a bike and helmet and by the time I got to the cash register I could barely see. My eyes were pouring, my nose was running and felt headache and fatigue coming on. I mentioned it to the cashier and she said it was probably from the cut flower section close to us. Whew, I was so glad to get out of there and my grandson was glad to get a new bike.
It is disturbing being in very low emf/rf then bam there you are soaking it up and feeling like crap for hours.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-gps-in-australia-re-ldn.31241/post-355714>

Hello,
I'm helping someone out as laid out in - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dose-naltrexdone-ldn.31171/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dose-naltrexdone-ldn.31171/)

We have provided a general overview of our suggested approach to the psychiatrist, however, this person is incredibly hard to get a hold of between the telehealth appointments as his primary focus is research and administering psychedelics. She has only a telehealth catch up once every 6 months just to stay on the books (in the event that any further assistance is required). The next telehealth isn't until late Nov.

Are there any open minded GP's that would considered prescribing LDN to this person? her journal is here - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-friend.30479/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-friend.30479/)

**In summary (part of what was sent to the psych) "**the intention is not to replace or reduce Amisulpride at this stage, but to add LDN as a terrain support layer: a kind of biological insurance policy to help stabilize glial tone, reduce neuroinflammation, and support redox and myelin repair. From what I understand, LDN works by modulating microglial activity and improving endogenous opioid balance, which may help preserve emotional connection, symbolic filtering, and inner agency while continuing antipsychotic support.

LDN’s broader effects, including glial M1 to M2 rebalancing, redox protection, endorphin rebound, and immune modulation, may also support REM sleep quality, myelin resilience, and overall stress tolerance. The aim is not to duplicate what Amisulpride is doing, but to support the terrain upstream: improving glial tone, redox buffering, and symbolic filtering so that perception and emotional input can be processed without needing to rely solely on dopamine suppression.

The proposed approach is conservative: starting at 0.25 mg at night and increasing slowly every 10 to 14 days if tolerated, with a target dose between 0.75 and 2.0 mg. It would not exceed 2.0 mg in total.

I understand that LDN is not TGA approved for this use, but it is legally prescribable off label in Australia and has been used internationally in neuroimmune and psychiatric contexts, including chronic fatigue, trauma related dysregulation, mood instability, and terrain prone psychosis. It has a strong safety profile at low doses and is well tolerated in clinical practice. To my knowledge, there are no pharmacological interactions between LDN and Amisulpride at these doses.

In this case (where used appropriately) the potential benefit clearly outweighs the risk, especially given the goal is to preserve resilience and support symbolic and emotional integration while remaining on antipsychotic medication".

I just do not want the long term amisulpride (albeit low dose) to destroy this person long term. The psych was baffled as to how such a low dose has been so effective for so long.. I know why but he does not and that is the problem with centralised medicine.

If there are any GP's out there or may know of some open minded one's that would be open to consider this on it's merits, please advise or alternatively send me a DM if you want to remain private.

Thank you.
Mat

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-04T17:28:15+0300

# Thread: Clotilde's Optimal Journal continued...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clotilde — 2025-07-04T23:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/post-355757>

In the past 2 years, I did make many changes in my home environment. I also spent more time outdoors, try to go to sunrise almost every day (at times more challenging, especially when I work because of the timing). I can see the sunrise by Lake Ontario which is 20/25 minutes away. This year, I started cold thermogenesis, In order for me to fully implement the protocols, this school year 22024/2025 - I am a teacher, in November I took a sick leave. Even with my life-style changes, my work environment was contributing to many reoccurring problems such as my blood sugar being crazy high. I remember one morning it was 6.1, also I had done hormone testing which turned out to be horrible. My cortisol was extremely low. I also experienced UTI's often, I was always feeling drained, no energy so difficult to wake-up and get out of bed in the morning, feeling light headed at work and brain fog at the end of the day. I started seen an hormone doctor to help with this issue and my daughter helped me navigate all the info that Jack Kruse gives in regards to choosing a Doctor and optimal hormonal levels.
I work in a school with over 680 students and hundreds of staff members. There are WiFi receptors in every single classroom, students use there laptops for most of the day, imagine in one room 32 Chromebooks and older students with cell phones in their backpacks. The school is located very close to Toronto hence the nnEMF with all the towers etc, is insane. Of course the lighting! Super bright Fluorescent lights all over the school. After pulling myself away from this toxic environment for 6 months, I now notice how sensitive my body is to nnEMF. For example, if I would visit someone who had there WIFI on, I would get lower abdomen cramps just like when I had UTI and frequent urination. Now, when I walk into a place with WiFi or high nnEMF I can feel it in my right ear - it gets blocked and if I stay long enough I get back pain. Once I leave this environment, example I go outdoors after sometime my ear and back is back to Normal.
After 6 months, I am doing much better, one morning after my sunrise, glucose level 4.9 -the power of watching the sunrise!
One huge mistake I made, I returned to work the last week of May. I wish I never did! After working for a week, on the weekend, I had could not eat as after a few hours of having my meal, I experienced excruciating pain for hours in my intestines. I fasted for 2 days and DGL helped - stayed home for 4 days. To be continued...

### Mat.I — 2025-07-05T12:02:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/post-355788>

You need a new line of work. There is no longevity in that line of work within schools..You are getting blasted with nn-emf and light that has likely already shifted you to a FE3+ state. Remember you do not need food.to raise your blood sugar this is done by blue rich light alone (missing the red/ir component). Your new nnemf sensitivities are due to melanin degradation, and low redox (all down stream effects of the FE3+ state).. If you value your health you need to get out of there and put pen to paper to develop the exit plan..

Centralised schools = EMF soup. That's why we homeschool...

### Clotilde — 2025-07-06T18:57:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/post-355864>

Hello Mat. Thank you for your feedback! Yes, I do value my health and your reply confirms what I have started to work on... an exit plan not to return to my job and find other options. # Thread: Help with BTC

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenniferM — 2025-07-04T23:27:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-btc.31246/post-355758>

We are going to invest in our future the next few days. I really need info on the best and safest way to buy BTC. The who, what, when and where?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-05T02:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-btc.31246/post-355770>

Use the search here on the forum.
Go to youtube, lots and lots of info there. Listen to the good the bad and the ugly and let your discernment inform and equip you to make the right decision - for you. It is important you don’t just listen to ANYONE and do what they did . . . You need to own this and make the right decision for you. I did find “Crypto Casey” was easy to understand and had some good background info that helped me get my head around the basics.

Start with a very few $, buy, sell, trade get comfortable with the process before moving larger amount's.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-05T11:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-btc.31246/post-355786>

Buy from a good exchange (OTC), however move straight (immediately) to cold storage. Do not leave too much BTC on the exchange. I personally do not leave anything over $5K (previously was $10K) on the exchange unless, I need more there for a reason. Also, it is.best practice to have a "clean" laptop just for.BTC. Before, I got into BTC, I.did lots of research on.security and after the research we did a consult with a "security" expert who I knew more than and said what I was talking about was unnecessary and way over the top.. He wasn't a security expert's a-hole.. So, anyone is capable of understanding BTC and knowing what risk level.they are ok.with.. Do.your research and get comfortable with small amounts.. Do not share any personal information with anyone re: your BTC or.stotage methods..

### Mat.I — 2025-07-05T11:21:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-btc.31246/post-355787>

I posted these general notes last year that may help (always do your own research) - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/...ice-or-friendship-come-here.25436/post-338276](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-btc-thread-when-you-need-some-answers-love-advice-or-friendship-come-here.25436/post-338276)

### Daniel Renaud — 2025-07-05T22:54:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-btc.31246/post-355825>

Everything depends on your jurisdiction. If I was in a commonwealth country I would learn how to buy from a non KYC exchange. If you don't mind giving up your personal information then there are some good options. I only use Bitcoin only exchanges. If you're going to make a big buy you might want to get your cold storage setup dialed in first. In the US for KYC Bitcoin Unchained is a good partner. They helped me a lot when I rolled my IRA over into Bitcoin. As Jack said, for advice you get what you pay for.

### fouadfo1 — 2025-07-05T02:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tanning-easily-and-losing-the-tan-quickly-implications.31247/post-355772>

Trying to understand myself a bit better. I can tan really easily, never burn (middle eastern descent), but I lose my tan very quickly.

For example I would sun max on day 1, gets lots of sun, darken to what would make me look like a dark Hispanic (picture Dev Patel after a few hours in the sun), then if I stay out of the sun for 24 hours, I would literally lighten 2-3 shades. In the winter time here in the east coast USA, I will become as light as Zayn Malik (or even lighter) I have to constantly expose myself to high noon sun to maintain a tan. I’m wondering, is this my body showing extreme need for melanin so it’s being “sucked” in to do work? Any other insights?

# Thread: LA to Sweden, health rollercoaster, new to Jack's info
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/la-to-sweden-health-rollercoaster-new-to-jacks-info.31249/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### emajen — 2025-07-05T21:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/la-to-sweden-health-rollercoaster-new-to-jacks-info.31249/post-355819>

Hey folks, hey Jack, my name is Seth. I'm a 39 year old dude living in Sweden, married with two small kids. I have been on quite the health journey since I got COVID 4 years ago, and just turned a corner on Jack. Found him through a podcast he did where he tore up some slippery pharma lobbyist, can't remember his name. I thought, I really like this guy, so I started learning more.

The "TLDR": came here to connect and learn more about fixing myself, I have had various health issues which I think are explained by Jack's work.

4 years ago I got COVID, it knocked me down for a couple of weeks but I recovered. About a month later, started having strange health issues and too many symptoms to count. Bone-crushing fatigue, muscle twitches, visual issues, hair loss, PEM just to name a few. Was then diagnosed with long COVID (I have never taken the vaccine by the way, that was a hard pass). After struggling with LC for about 2 years I came across something called TMS (tension myositis syndrome) which explained a lot about what was going on with myself. I used some of the stuff I learned in TMS groups to get better again - well, mostly better.

I consider myself "recovered" from long COVID but I still don't have the same energy envelope as I did before (looking back now, I think I was already going down hill before getting COVID, just kept pushing through), and I just feel much, much older than I really am. Now, just recently, I'm having stomach/gut issues, and I am still occasionally getting symptoms from before. After listening to Jack talk about the 3 pillars (light, magnetism, water, hope I got that right) I started to understand why my body broke down in the first place, and why now. Most of my life I spent living in the sun belt (Florida, Texas and then California), and then moved to the 59th latitude where I was starved of light. I also changed my career to programming, which put me in front of a blue screen all day. I've also been wearing contact lenses since 13, with UV protection. I have unknowingly done everything the wrong way.

The good thing is that I'm stubborn as hell and have always been very driven. So I'm channeling this into changing my life so that I can have more energy to play with my boys and be a better, stronger dad, husband, and human being. I have spent a good portion of my life burning the candle at both ends, used to run 30-40km a week, lift weights, party on the weekends, and work 80 hours a week, etc. But I realize all that stuff was just grinding me down to a nub.

The tough thing is, it's harder than hell to optimize where I'm at, so I need to get smart about it. If anyone's got tips or routines they follow while living up north I'd love to hear it.

Thanks for the community and thank you Jack for all the info you put out there. You've spent a lot of your free time spreading this info and I am so appreciative of that. Eager to learn more.

P.S. I am also big on crypto, forgot to mention, and yes I hold BTC. Was a crypto guy even before Jack, so cheers to that!

### Kathy Spears — 2025-07-05T23:42:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/la-to-sweden-health-rollercoaster-new-to-jacks-info.31249/post-355828>

Hi Seth. I relate to your story. I was born and raised in California but ended up in Sweden, married to a Swede. During my 6 years there I was diagnosed with several autoimmune disorders. Eventually I moved back to the states but landed in Tennessee where the lack of sun during the long winters didn't help me at all. I was diagnosed with Juvenile Diabetes within 6 months of being back. Dr Kruse helped me to understand that my years in Sweden set me up for all the autoimmune issues I ended up with. And my move to TN didn't help at all.

It is quite possible that you, too, will not be able to fully regain your health while living at that latitude. If you don't move then at least consider embracing the cold the way the Swedes do. I used to marvel at the way they'd go in the sauna and then roll in the snow! I was always very aware that most of my Swedish friends were very healthy and hearty but they were all outdoors people, regardless of how cold it was. I would try to keep up with them but I remember feeling miserable and not understanding how they could possibly be enjoying themselves. I eventually understood that being very cold helps the body to make the light it is missing. So embrace the suck (as Jack would say) and feel the cold, be uncomfortable! And maybe take frequent trips to the Canary Islands during the long winters.

### emajen — 2025-07-06T21:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/la-to-sweden-health-rollercoaster-new-to-jacks-info.31249/post-355875>

Hey Kathy, good to hear from you! Very similar story indeed, sorry to hear you went through the autoimmune stuff. It is really, really hard to live here! The weather, the darkness, and I just don't vibe with a lot of the locals.

I would like to move back. We just moved to the country here to get away from the crime, EMF, screens, be closer to nature, lots of things. But I really want to move back to Florida, that's where most of my family is. It would be really hard to do and selling that to my wife would be really difficult, plus with the kids ...I'm in a really tough spot. When I tell my wife about all this stuff I'm learning, I don't think she takes me seriously (or thinks Im crazy, haha) so an uphill battle.

I think "embracing the cold" is what I need to focus on. I have a lake within walking distance for winter plunges. Snow is in no short supply. So I will have to start there. That, and make a mandatory trip for a month or two down south as part of my terms for living here.

Did you get depressed living here as well? I couldn't understand it but about 2 years after getting here I was stricken with such a deep, deep sense of sadness/depression, I have never been like that, and couldn't figure out what the hell was going on. Now, after understanding all this info about light, it's starting to click.

Anyway, thanks again. I hope you are finding the healing you are looking for!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-06T16:37:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solution-for-nonjabbed-blood.31251/post-355855>

The solution:

Here is a new blog and the final one on consciousness and it is a banger if you took the jab because it contains some wisdom for you to combat your own compliance. [https://patreon.com/posts/decentralized-60-129222655](https://patreon.com/posts/decentralized-60-129222655)

I have consistently stated that fixing health and or conscious ability isn’t about adding supplements or vague “detoxes.” It’s about restoring redox power to reestablish proper tissue optics, reconnecting with natural light, and rebuilding the electron flow that powers life. This optimized tissue refraction and optics so UPEs can become signal and not noise. Anything that is noise in the system alters consciousness. For example, when LNPs from mRNA vaccines enter the body, their charged nature can disrupt mtDNA redox balance, tipping it toward oxidation and the result is seen immediately in UPE spectral changes.

This creates inflammation, cellular stress, and mitochondrial dysfunction. How it happens is astounding to the central paradigm because they have no unifeid theory ot explain it. I do because I have a decentralized thesis to link it to.

I believe the solution isn’t trying to detox toxins in the way holistic functional practioners advocate. This is pseudoscientific. The body has a specific way of doing this via POMC and melanin. Instead, we need to restore redox optically so the body can manage charge properly and restore tissue optics to lower heteroplasmy. This is how life was built to operate because of the environmental changes in the GOE.
Redox works like a energetic seesaw due to the GOE experience of life during this epoch. On one side, molecules give up electrons through oxidation. On the other, they receive electrons through reduction. Light and oxygen levels define this seesaw. The result of changing tissue optics changes how light made inside of us operates (UPEs).

The body uses semiconduction abilities buried in sunlight, water, real food built by photosynthesis, and internal systems to keep the redox seesaw of electron gain and loss balanced to main healthy optical density in tissues. When LNPs introduce extra charge from a virus, bacteria, or jab, the system becomes unstable and conscious ability is altered to give you brain fog. My decentralized thesis says melanin is one of the body’s most powerful tools for restoring balance, thanks to its ability to manage electrons. It replaced melatonin, as the king of health and disease over 600 million years ago. Melanin enhances the process of endogenous light transformation by stabilizing UPE frequencies, and it was this process that drove the evolution of the leptin melanocortin pathways over 600 million years to replace the more rudimentary light pathways of the aromatic amino acids used to build melatonin signaling, the microbiome light show, and serotonin signaling pathways in the gut.

That is laid out in today Decentralized Medicine #60 blog post. It is also why taking exogenous melatonin might be one of the most unwise things one can do when your tissue optics are destroyed. Maybe the only thing more unwise was opting for the jab.

### Jacks — 2025-07-06T19:16:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-355867>

I just got mine recently and hoping to stay away from google - I deleted my google account a while ago and don't want to get another one. Mostly using it for reading Uncle Jacks blogs outside right now and taking notes, but will be taking it on a trip soon so will see how that goes. I read that you can use F-Droid/Aurora store to download some apps - but have not done so yet. There is a daylight hacker wiki community on discord which includes an android hacking channel - not sure if it is ready yet for prime time. ### rfmitigation — 2025-07-15T05:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-356339>

What different OS are you going to install?

I have a DC-1 that I run with a wired ethernet connection and would love to run linux on it, but according to the daylight hacker wiki community that isn't an option yet.

It's unfortunate that we have to use android in order to get a healthier screen, but I understand this is the first model and they needed to get it up and running. Also most people aren't familiar with linux so the demand is super low there.

Looking forward to seeing what they come up with in the future!

### rtd — 2025-08-05T19:59:03+0300

> [JacqueAwef said:](/goto/post?id=356979)

> I also have a daylight computer. I use it with a wired ethernet connection only.
>
> How are you liking yours rtd?

I like it. My current usage is for reading and researching.

I removed majority of the preloaded software and have not logged into any major network service like Google, Amazon, apple, yahoo etc.

On a different device I downloaded APKs for apps I want on the DC-1. I'm a paying Proton customer and use their VPN, email, calendar and drive. So I installed those and Firefox. I may get the Brave broswer. Also an open source epub reader. I used a usb-c cable to push the apps over. Now with my Proton drive working on it, I can use that when needed. I have a shielded hardwired Cat 5e RJ45 to USB-C dedicated for Ethernet

I will see if NotePad++ is available so I can view and write code if I want to, not compile it. More prototyping really. That means a keyboard and I'm cool with that.

I composed this message using the DC-1

> At the end of the day, my least favorite part of the DC-1 is that the operating system is android by google.
> I would also like physical hardware switches for the wifi and bluetooth, but hey beggars can't be choosers.
>
> Looking forward to some sort of linux operating system in the future.
> I may just go with another small linux computer and run an E-ink monitor.
> That's how much I don't want to use an operating system made by google.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-06T01:34:40+0300

I ordered a Dasung Paperlike 103 Eink monitor. Supposedly it can do 60hz refresh, but who knows if that's true.

I'll be seeing if I can get it to work with a Raspberry Pi that I have laying around. Nice thing with the Raspberry Pi is you can remove the wifi/bluetooth at the hardware level on the board pretty easily. Then it can't be turned on without your consent at the software level remotely.

I kind of want to crack open the DC-1 to look at the internals, but also don't want to get too greedy and damage it.
My portable AM radio makes some unique pulsing sounding EMI when held next to the DC-1 for whatever that's worth.

### hacklose — 2025-08-20T03:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358530>

> [urko said:](/goto/post?id=358506)
>
> Hello Jacque, any update on the dasung screen. I was thinking about purchasing one.

I'd be curious to see what Jacque thinks.

For what it's worth, I've tried the Daylight DC-1 and the Dasung Paperlike 103 monitor.
They each use different screen technology to achieve a grayscale "E-paper" display.

The DC-1 screen has absolutely no ghosting. The Paperlike 103 does.
The DC-1 has a visually better refresh rate than the Paperlike 103, but the Paperlike 103 really does have a surprisingly fast refresh rate for an E Ink monitor. They both advertise 60hz refresh rate.

The DC-1 uses a backlight and the Paperlike 103 uses a front light. My eyes prefer a front light.
The ghosting on the Paperlike 103 is very noticeable when watching videos.

I like that the Paperlike 103 is a monitor, so you can use a windows, mac, or linux OS to go along with it. Linux for the win!
With the DC-1 you're stuck with google's android OS, at least for now, or using an app that lets you use the DC-1 as a monitor with a windows OS.

They both have pros and cons. I end up using the DC-1 with a wired ethernet connection most of the time.
If I need a color screen, I have an older laptop that I run linux on. I use that about 2% of the time these days. I really don't find I need color very often, but everyone is different.

### Johan Lindøm — 2025-08-20T20:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358557>

Anyone got a stand and a keyboard for the DC-1, and a muse? Any insight to share? I see there is a pin connections in the back, but how to use it?

### hacklose — 2025-08-20T21:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358559>

> [Johan Lindøm said:](/goto/post?id=358557)
>
> Anyone got a stand and a keyboard for the DC-1, and a muse? Any insight to share? I see there is a pin connections in the back, but how to use it?

I just lean my DC-1 on the screen of my old laptop. I use an Anker 6-1 USB-C hub so that I can use a keyboard, mouse, and ethernet all at the same time through the DC-1's single USB-C port. It also has a pass through USB-C power connection so it can be charged without unplugging the USB-C hub from the DC-1.

I recommend charging the DC-1 when you are not using it and running it on battery power when you are using it. The electric fields are much higher when charging it vs. running it on the battery.

There are plenty of different USB-C hub options available. Just find one that supports what you want to use.
Keyboard and mouse work perfectly fine with the DC-1. An ethernet internet connection works with the bluetooth and wifi shut off in the settings. Good stuff!

### Ryan M — 2025-07-07T15:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breeched-delivery.31257/post-355911>

My wife is 37 weeks and our daughter is breeched. She has been doing all of the "flipping baby" methods we have found online to no avail. She has tried chiro, acupuncture and an ECV two days ago.

We are in Connecticut and Yale New Haven as a whole, does not like to do breeched deliveries. They will do it if you refuse a C section, but they won't let you plan induction, so you could end up with an OB with little experience delivering breeched babies.

There is one OB at UCONN that apparently teaches methods in delivering breeched and does them frequently. We are trying to get an appointment there.

Does anyone, specially an OB, have advice on the safety of delivering breeched? My wife really wants to avoid a C section, but wants to prioritize safety. It's difficult to find statistics in the safety of breeched deliveries because of the incentives to push C sections.

### JenniferM — 2025-07-07T17:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breeched-delivery.31257/post-355919>

I was in the acupuncture's office day and a pregnant lady came in so I asked her about how acupuncture will help with delivery. She told me she was there because the baby was breech and heard he had skills. Come to find out the acupuncturist righted the baby.
The acupuncturist is a 4th generation acupuncturist and other medical techniques.
Just a thought for you..

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-09T01:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breeched-delivery.31257/post-356037>

You've just said a lot about your wife in you journal. Now this explains the breech issue.
My thesis posits that the stroma in tissues is piezo and flexoelectric (collagen- and proteoglycan-rich connective tissue) and fully supports structural integrity and photo- bioelectric signaling in tissues like the dental pulp and cornea. You can read about that in the next blog on the eye and you can read about it here on the forum about enamel pitting anothe rmember asked about.

The Moderna jab: Is a big time transgenerational weaknesses in stromal integrity which likely has exacerbate these effects, compromising tissue development and the kids non movement.

Applying my thesis to breech delivery, we can consider how photobioelectric disruptions affect the uterine stroma, placental stroma, or fetal connective tissues, potentially altering fetal positioning.

Breech Delivery and Stromal Photobioelectric Effects

Breech delivery occurs in about 3–5% of term pregnancies and is influenced by maternal, fetal, and environmental factors. My thesis would explain this phenomenon through the following mechanisms:

1\. Uterine Stromal Weakness and Biomechanical Failure
Uterine Stroma Role: The uterus contains a stromal layer (endometrium and myometrium connective tissue) that supports implantation, placentation, and uterine expansion during pregnancy. This stroma, rich in collagen and proteoglycans, maintains biomechanical integrity and facilitates myometrial contractility. We no know spike protein loves the female GU tract. this is why so many jabortions and spontaneous abortions occur.

Photobioelectric Disruption via Other causes:

Lack of melanin = lack of energy redox of tissues.
High-Carb Diet: Excessive carbohydrate intake induces mitochondrial stress and inflammation, reducing ATP production in stromal cells. This weakens uterine collagen matrices, impairing the uterus’s ability to guide fetal positioning through biomechanical forces.

Blue Light (380–550 nm): Blue light exposure increases ROS, disrupting mitochondrial function in uterine stromal cells. This would impair cellular signaling and stromal remodeling, reducing the uterus’s capacity to orient the fetus head-down. It also destroys melanin and leptin signaling.

nnEMF (1G–5G): nnEMF disrupts mitochondrial electron transport and bioelectric potentials, potentially altering the stromal water structure and collagen alignment. This may weaken the uterine stroma, reducing its ability to generate directional forces for fetal rotation.

LNPs of the jabs = demolish charge carrying ability of the stroma.

Impact on Breech: A weakened uterine stroma may fail to provide the biomechanical cues (e.g., tension, contractility) needed to guide the fetus into a cephalic (head-down) position. This mirrors stromal thinning in keratoconus or enamel pitting, where structural failure leads to functional deficits.

A compromised placental stroma should be expected to alter amniotic fluid volume or placental attachment (e.g., placenta previa), both associated with breech presentation. This could limit fetal mobility, preventing spontaneous rotation to a head-down position.

Mom took jab before conception so fetus is GMO.

Fetal Stromal Weakness and Musculoskeletal Constraints = collateral jab effects

Fetal Connective Tissue Role: The fetal stroma, derived from neural crest and mesodermal cells, forms connective tissues like ligaments, tendons, and muscles. These tissues influence fetal posture and movement within the uterus.

Transgenerational Weakness: As in many other diseases in my thesis, transgenerational stromal weaknesses (e.g., from maternal exposure to nnEMF, jabs, light, or poor nutrition) should be expected to impair fetal collagen synthesis or proteoglycan stability, leading to weaker musculoskeletal structures.

Bioelectric Signaling and Fetal Orientation

Photo-Bioelectric Role: Bioelectric potentials (Becker’s current) guide cellular differentiation and tissue organization during embryogenesis. In the context of pregnancy, these potentials will influence uterine and fetal tissue polarity, directing fetal orientation.

Disruption by nnEMF: nnEMF can alter bioelectric gradients (melanin) in the uterine or fetal stroma, disrupting polarity cues that guide fetal positioning. This could confuse the fetus’s orientation, favoring a breech presentation.

Red Light Potential: Red light photobiomodulation (630–850 nm) enhances mitochondrial ATP and bioelectric signaling, potentially supporting stromal health and fetal movement. However, transgenerational stromal weaknesses may limit its efficacy.

So in the 37th week you should make you wife put her gut in the sun constantly to change the kids position naturally. It is still not late to use Nature's methods.

Uterine Innervation: The uterus is innervated by the autonomic nervous system (sympathetic and parasympathetic fibers via the pelvic plexus), established later in development (around 10–12 weeks). Disruptions in bioelectric signaling from nnEMF could impair autonomic regulation of uterine contractility, reducing spontaneous contractions that help reposition the fetus.

Impact on Breech: Altered innervation or bioelectric signaling in the uterus or fetal craniofacial structures may disrupt the coordinated movements needed for cephalic presentation, especially in late pregnancy (28–36 weeks). Risk for craniosynostosis should be high in OB and pediatrician.

Your wife's lack of melanin and the jab means she is like a woman with a genetic stromal disease. She needs to be treated as such. Studies on connective tissue disorders (e.g., Ehlers-Danlos syndrome) show increased rates of breech presentation due to weakened pelvic or uterine tissues, analogous to stromal thinning in my model.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-09T01:21:39+0300

# Thread: Shielding a Wi-Fi Router with a Faraday Bag
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### OptimalSkr — 2025-07-07T19:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355934>

I am staying in an AirBnb and there is a wifi router.

But I came prepared - I bought a Hodufy faraday bag and an ethernet cable. What I do is put the router in the faraday bag and run the ethernet cable from the router to my CPU. Now I am able to get the internet on my laptop without wifi.

However when testing with the Safe and Sound Classic 1 meter ([https://emf-protection.ca/product/safe-and-sound-classic/](https://emf-protection.ca/product/safe-and-sound-classic/)) I get a high reading when near the router, but as I move away it dramatically lowers to almost nothing at about 20 feet.

Also I can still get a wifi signal and use it on my phone.

Question to the resident EMF expert [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) ---- is this normal readings? Or is the bag not sufficient quality to block the EMF? Also my meter is older and maybe the router is still broadcasting at a frequency my meter does not measure.

Am I fooling myself with the low readings? Thoughts?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-07T19:26:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355936>

The bag will (should) help but you need a meter with numbers so you know what the reading is. Newer meter are also calibrated for 5G technology and newer routers. You could invest the Safe & Sound Pro II ($400) or the new Cornet 5G Quad Pro ($200) if you want to save some money.

In the mean time put a couple of layers of aluminum foil between you and the router. Not over it, just shield the side you are on.

With all nnEMF distance is your friend.

### OptimalSkr — 2025-07-08T00:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355963>

Thanks Anthony - Ya I figured my meter is a bit old -- the network is listed as a 5g router.

There must be some benefit to the faraday bag right? I mean my meter does show a huge drop especially with distance --- Have you tested different kinds of faraday bags to see the efficiency for different generations of signals or would it be all the same in terms of shielding..... I just assumed that it would 100% block all frequencies to zero... But I looked online and it said something like it cuts the frequencies and they die off after a certain distance etc (not sure I understand the physics of that lol)

For the aluminum foil you mean put sheets of it over the bag that already covers the router?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-08T02:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355966>

Go to [https://liveemfsafe.com/emf-101](https://liveemfsafe.com/emf-101)
Scroll down for some shielding ideas options.
Any fine metal mesh (or foil) will work (think screen door on a microwave oven).
NOT 100% but I often see 90%+ reduction. For a quick, easy, cheep fix, very doable for most.
With any nnEMF - less is better than more.
I think the screen works better if there a small distance (a few inches) between the router and the screen.
Take RF readings at least 3 feet from the source (has to do with wave length).

### OptimalSkr — 2025-07-08T15:23:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-356001>

Thanks -- I will look more intro upgrading my EMF game!

# Thread: Physics Questions - Shielding Magnetic Fields from Powerlines
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TB14 — 2025-07-07T22:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355952>

My understanding is that shielding of low-frequency AC magnetic fields is accomplished using materials such as Mu-metal that have high permeability to redirect the fields, which is in contrast to the techniques used to shield low-frequency AC electric fields and radiofrequency waves, which I believe is generally accomplished using materials that reflect or attenuate them. I am not sure I fully grasp the low-frequency AC magnetic field shielding mechanism of materials with high permeability and have some applied questions below involving three shielding scenarios hoping to increase my understanding (and also hoping this could be helpful to others as well). Answers to any of the below, even if not going through every piece of every question, would likely be super helpful.

Say you have a main utility feeder powerline. The current going through this powerline generates AC magnetic fields in continuous circles with the powerline at the center of each circle, with the fields progressively decreasing in intensity with distance from the powerline. Now say you have a house 20 feet from this powerline that is exposed to the AC magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current.

**(1) Powerline Cylinder Enclosure**: Would wrapping the portion of the powerline the current of which is giving off magnetic fields to which the house is exposed in a cylinder of high-permeability material, such as Mu-metal, eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of the house to the fields?
(A) If not:
(i) Is it because AC magnetic fields at the house would be too far from the cylinder to be drawn into/along the cylinder?
(a) If so, does this imply that magnetic fields are unlike solids/liquids/gasses in that enclosing them in a barrier (even one that has the electromagnetic property of high permeability) does not have any impact on the extent to which such fields travel through/outside the barrier?
(ii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would simply be too strong to be significantly mitigated by such a cylinder and would thus saturate the cylinder, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields escaping outside the cylinder and reaching the house?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the cylinder were thick enough, enclosing the powerline within it would indeed eliminate the exposure of the house to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
(iii) Or is such a cylinder shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in blocking the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current for some other reason?

**(2) Attic Horizontal Sheet**: If the right-hand rule for determining the direction of magnetic field lines indicates that the fields generated by the powerline's current are "entering" the house from above, would putting a horizontal sheet of high-permeability material in the attic directly above a particular room eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of that room to the fields generated by the powerline's current?
(A) If not:
(i) Is it because I'm misunderstanding the implications of the right-hand rule, and magnetic fields (or maybe just AC magnetic fields) don't, in fact, travel in a particular locational direction, per se?
(a) If so, does this imply that such an attempt at shielding would fail because the magnetic fields from the powerline current would still be entering the room not only through the walls, but also through the floor?
(ii) Or, alternatively, is it because while the magnetic fields from the powerline would be cut off from entering the room by the shielding sheet above the room, the fields would likely simply just "bulge" back into the room from the sides (through the walls, rather than through the ceiling) after traveling along the path of the sheet?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet extended far enough beyond the perimeter of the room in all directions, this "bulging" effect would be insufficient to cause the fields to reach the room, and thus that such shielding could in theory be effective?
(iii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would be too strong to significantly mitigate (even at a distance of 20 feet) and would thus saturate the sheet, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields then passing through it into the room?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were thick enough, placing it above the room would indeed eliminate the exposure of the room to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
(iv) Or is such a horizontal sheet shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in blocking the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current for some other reason?

**(3) Buried Vertical Sheet**: Would burying a vertical sheet of high-permeability material in the front yard of the house with the same width of a given room of the house, which is as tall as such room is deep, and which is buried deeply enough to intersect the magnetic field lines from the powerline that pass through such room, eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of that room to the fields generated by the powerline's current?
(A) If not:
(i) Is it because the intersection of the magnetic field lines by the vertical buried sheet occurs after the magnetic field lines have already passed through the room (from above, through the ceiling), per the right hand rule?
(a) If so, does this imply that such an attempt at shielding using a buried vertical sheet of high-permeability material could succeed with respect to shielding a home across the street from the home at issue from the fields generated by the current going through the powerline at issue, because such fields are entering that home across the street from below (through the floor), per the right-hand rule, and thus could be intercepted by a buried sheet before so entering?
(ii) Or, alternatively, is it because while the magnetic fields from the powerline would be diverted by being pulled forward away from the room by the buried shielding sheet, the fields would likely simply just "bulge" back into a path that takes them through the room by the time they entered the room?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were extended to be tall and wide enough in all directions beyond the field lines of the fields that would otherwise pass through the room, this "bulging" effect would be insufficient to cause the fields to reach the room, and thus that such shielding could in theory be effective?
(iii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would be too strong to significantly mitigate (even at significant distance from the powerline) and would thus saturate the sheet, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields that pass into the room remaining unaffected?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were thick enough, placing it in the proper location would indeed eliminate the exposure of the room to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
(iv) Or is such a buried vertical sheet shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in redirecting the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current away from the room at issue for some other reason?

### Saichi — 2025-07-07T23:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355956>

I assume you already ruled out shared neutrals in your house as a cause?

### TB14 — 2025-07-08T00:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355959>

Yes, in my specific case with the Florida house, I'm pretty sure the elevated readings in the house are caused by a buried main utility feeder powerline in the front yard. I know exactly where the powerline is and the AC magnetic field readings increase on both the TriField TF2 and NFA1000 the closer I get to it, approaching from either side. The fields are much higher in every part of the front yard than in any part of the house. They decrease in strength proportionally to distance from the powerline in every part of the house and both the front and back yards that I've measured. There's no change in the readings when I flip off the whole-house breaker at the breaker panel.

I have wondered if there's anything the utility might be able to do to limit the fields coming off the powerline if I were to take the right approach to get them to do it. Not sure if there's any possibility that any relatively minor thing they might be willing to do like tightening the neutral/ground connections at the transformers near my house might improve the situation.

Even if not, I'm semi-optimistic that something can be done about it in terms of shielding, though. Just need to figure out the physics of it first to determine what would even work before trying to design a cost-effective plan.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-08T00:35:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355961>

What are the gauss readings in the front vs back of the house?

### TB14 — 2025-07-08T00:49:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355962>

It seems to depend significantly on the season, the time of day, and the weather that particular day, presumably since that largely dictates neighborhood power consumption. Today (relatively mild summer day) around 5:45 p.m.:
\- Directly over the powerline: ~7mG
\- Front door of the house (~20 feet away from the powerline): ~1.5mG
\- Back door of the house (~40 feet away from the powerline): ~1.0mG
\- Back yard twenty feet from the back door (~60 feet away from the powerline): ~0.75mG

### Saichi — 2025-07-08T02:09:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355965>

NFA1000 doesn't have a single axis reading option?

I just noticed G-iron flex has high corrosion resistance although I doubt it was intended for outdoor use in ground. Curious whether you'll be able to get a permit to shield the power line length either way, and how much of your legal expertise and time would require.

Building biologist would tell you to sell the house, but I'm all for extreme measures to mitigate.
