# Thread: Dali's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dalis-optimal-journal.31291/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dcintra — 2025-07-12T00:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dalis-optimal-journal.31291/post-356194>

My Optimal Journal

I’m grateful to have found Dr. Kruse through Michael Yon, and Nicole Shanahan’s Council of the Canceled; following is my medical history.

I am a 47 year-old woman born in Lakeland, Florida of Cuban and Spanish ancestry. I lived in Lakeland until college at UF in Gainesville, Florida, and near Atlanta since 2003. I worked in third-party logistics until 2008, married, and am a SAHM and homeschooler of two healthy children, 15 and 11. My main concern is the effects of one Moderna and one Johnson & Johnson Covid vaccine taken in spring 2021. I believe but cannot prove I have had three miscarriages or failures to implant since then, and tested high in Covid spike antibodies recently.

Otherwise a lifelong concern is tiredness and less cognitive performance and emotional regulation than I would like. I have experienced depression and anxiety as long as I can remember, as well as procrastination and indecision. I have difficulty planning and sometimes staying on task. I can write, read, and enjoy complicated text, but it’s challenging to settle my mind to do any of them. I have medicated with television, internet, and podcasts, but have not taken psychotropic meds in years. I love music and have nearly perfect pitch perception. Currently I see a branspotting therapist and use polyvagal stimulation music. I have a happy marriage to a supportive husband, and wonderful children.

I was overweight after puberty, lost some weight in college, and was able to lose weight after my second pregnancy with Pilates and portion control. But after the vaccines and attempts to conceive, I gained 20 pounds. In the past year I have tried to lose weight with a carnivore diet, and later the Leptin Rx, but gained instead. I have been chronically low in vitamin D, which I discovered with my second pregnancy.

I’ve had myopia since 5th grade, wore RGP contacts for years, couldn’t wear them by 2019. Astigmatism. Dx Meibomian gland dysfunction. RGP are no longer uncomfortable but I rarely wear them.

Lifelong upper respiratory congestion, mostly tolerated, unmedicated. Positional nasal congestion, alternates sides. Improves with sun.

In my early 20s, I took tetracycline for months and Accutane briefly for acne. I developed a patch of dermatitis behind my neck in college and another patch on my groin in my late 20s. I have some melasma on my forehead. I am treating these with sun exposure.

Working with a functional diagnostic nutritionist for upper respiratory issues with my son, I discovered in 2019 our whole family had elevated mold mycotoxins as well as parasites and bacterial infections. We followed various protocols. For the past two years we have worked with a naturopathic physician and have done IV and oral glutathione, as well as binders to detox. I have now dropped detox and all supplements except Neprinol and Boluoke. I take ivermectin.

Having found Dr. Kruse in fall of 2024, I started prioritizing sun and raised my vitamin D to 86, and raised my DHA eating salmon and other seafood twice a week. This month I listened to the February 2019 Q&A on 5G and ALS, which helped me finally accept the importance of sunrise. I rebooted the Leptin Rx and for the first time in years, am losing weight and am able to get into ketosis. My blood glucose is testing lower and my ketones, higher.

Following is a family history.

Mother: Born and raised in Guantanamo, Cuba. Gave birth to my brother, had a miscarriage, stillbirth (or irregular adoption) in Madrid, another miscarriage in Florida, then gave birth to me. I was born with jaundice. Hypothyroidism in her 40s, allergies and hypertension, and significant overweight. Obsessive about health, constant doctor visits, experiences much musculo-skeletal pain. Hammer toe surgery. Knee and shoulder surgery. Heart attacks. Quadruple bypass surgery. Aneurism of the pulmonary artery (?). Neuropathy. Arthritis. Chronic circadian mismatch (sleeps 2 a.m. to 10 a.m.). Florid PTSD/histrionic personality/anxiety, is declining cognitively in her early 80s. 2 Moderna vaccines.

Father: Born and raised in central rural Cuba. Carpenter, later steel fitter/welder upon moving to Florida. Snored since childhood. Duodenal ulcer. Tonsillectomy/uvulectomy/soft palate reduction to correct snoring. Type II diabetes. Good eyes. Hearing loss. Has had prostate cancer for 10 years. Now 91. In cognitive decline. 2 Moderna vaccines.

Brother: Born in Cuba. Forceps assisted birth, blow to head in playground accident. Myopia. Many car accidents, one with a broken neck, halo apparatus. Florid grandiosity and insomnia. Chronic circadian mismatch. Irritable. Observed narcolepsy. Morbid obesity. Edema. Heart attacks. Died of congestive heart failure at 53.

Maternal Grandmother: Born and raised in Guantánamo, Cuba. Lived in Madrid, Gitmo, Central Florida. Deformative arthritis. Arteriosclerosis and total dementia in her 70s. Melanoma in the eye; tumor grew outward. Died subsequent to hemorrhage.

Maternal Grandfather: Born and raised in Cuba, worked at Gitmo for 33 years as a heavy machine operator and tool room attendant. Smoker. Reconstruction of the nose and lower mandible due to carcinoma, 40+ operations, all care at Bethesda Naval Hospital. Glaucoma. Pacemaker. Tracheotomy. Stomach feeding tube. Died at 80 subsequent to hemorrhage from a melanoma of the esophagus.

Maternal Aunt: Developed schizophrenia and type II diabetes. Lived at Gitmo. Died in her 70s. Two healthy children and one born with two stomachs and hydrocephalus.

Maternal Uncle: Medical history unknown, currently in cognitive decline. Cuban Revolution and Counterrevolution, political prisoner for 22 years, lived in Venezuela and now Nicaragua.

Paternal Grandfather: Medical history unknown. Died in his 70s(?) by suicide.

Paternal Grandmother: Medical history unknown. Developed dementia.

Paternal Aunts and Uncles: 3 with type II diabetes, 1 blind, one deceased with lupus, one deceased with cancer, one deceased from complications of Parkinson’s. The uncle with Parkinson’s had a healthy son and one profoundly retarded daughter with a large red mark covering half of her face.

Husband: From Paris. IT professional. Childhood appendectomy. Vitiligo. Pleurisy. Kidney stone. Possible colitis. Tiredness. Epididymitis. Tested gluten sensitivity, resolved. 2 Moderna vaccines. Tested low in spike antibodies.

Son: (15) I had ovarian cysts early in his pregnancy; they resolved. Induced labor, Cervadil. Chronic upper respiratory congestion. Tonsillectomy/adenoidectomy/turbinate reduction, ineffective. Parasites, C. difficile, H. pylori, resolved. Stopped vaccines in 2020. No vision correction.

Daughter: (11) Severe constipation at 3, tested severe gluten sensitivity, parasites, resolved. Chronic upper respiratory congestion. Stopped vaccines in 2020. No vision correction.

Well, I had my consultation with Dr. Kruse this morning. He was surprised at my spike protein number, which I didn't disclose above: 17,822 as of March 2025. I must get it below 10,000 due to high risk of turbocancer. I should check my spike protein levels quarterly, if not monthly. I'll continue ivermectin at 0.6 mg/kg, two weeks on, one week off.

Dr. Kruse believes the environment in Atlanta is driving spike protein production since my immune system is working well, given my vitamin D is good, 86, and I achieved that without supplements. I must assume the mRNA intercalated into my genome unless proven otherwise. He recommended sun, grounding, and highly recommended moving out of Atlanta to one of the black beaches of El Salvador, or one of the calderas, or another place that tests significantly lower in nnEMF than Atlanta, which apparently is as bad or worse than Nashville.

I'm absorbing his recommendations and ordering deuterium-depleted water today. I have sel de Guérande to add to my water per his suggestion.

My neighbor and I had a long chat. Her numbers are similar and she's on the same page healthwise.

Dr. Kruse helped me see where my money would be best spent. I'm supposed to find out about an institute in New Mexico that teaches nnEMF building biology, and/or purchase tools to test my environment on my own.

I forgot to ask him about IV methylene blue and IV vitamin C.

At any rate our session was very much to the point and serious.

# Thread: Taliesins Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taliesins-optimal-journal.31295/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taliesin — 2025-07-12T22:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taliesins-optimal-journal.31295/post-356219>

Hi! My name is Taliesin. Here are my quick stats:
**1a.** I was born in Cambridge, Massachusetts.
**1b.** I grew up for most of my life in Ellensburg, WA.
**1c.** I have spent the last 4 years in Los Angeles/San Diego, California, for College/Masters.
**2\.** I am 23 years old.
**3a.** Mother is a first-generation American (Maternal Health: Myopia, Midlife Excessive Hair Shedding).
**3b.** Grandmother immigrated from the Netherlands; the family on that side was in the Netherlands for many generations.
**3c.** Grandmother died of ALS at age 46.
**3d.** Great-grandmother lived into her 90s and had glaucoma.
**4\.** I was diagnosed with keratoconus in 2021(?). I saw Dr. Kristina Voss for it again at the University of Southern California Keck School of Medicine in 2023. At that time, she said that I did not need the cross-linkage surgery yet, but would need to monitor it in case I did need it in the future. I have not had an appointment since then, but will soon.
**5\.** For my whole life, I have had convergence insufficiency, which led to frequent headaches, especially when reading, and occasionally double vision when my eyes were exhausted. When I had double vision, I could force my eyes to focus, but it was difficult and uncomfortable. This condition has improved, but has not completely subsided.
**6\.** I had Cubital Tunnel Syndrome since 2021, and had Cubital Tunnel Release Surgery for it at the University of Southern California Keck School of Medicine in 2023. Since the surgery, it has improved, but still acts up when I work out, type for long periods, or play guitar for long periods. It has never been 100% back to normal since the injury occurred. I always have slightly lower dexterity and feeling in my pinky and ring fingers. This is very impactful because I am a musician.
**7a.** I used to be barefoot 24/7, but for the past year, I haven't been barefoot at all.
**7b.** I have glasses, but I only wear them for driving.
**7c.** I have a pretty strict sleeping routine of going to bed at 8:30 PM and waking up at 5:00 AM, and I recently began using a Melatonin supplement for sleep.
**8\.** I have generally high levels of anxiety 24/7, which I have been working on for many years through behavioral and mindfulness techniques.

# Thread: Incidental Pineal Cyst

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jill Dee — 2025-07-13T00:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incidental-pineal-cyst.31296/post-356220>

Hello,
New to the Journal… but have been becoming Savage myself for a little while… Thx for sharing your work and all that demands of you in your life. ;-)
I did not find “Pineal Cyst” in search box or previous discussions.

Q.
Incidental Pineal Cyst.
60yr old healthy female
Not calcification, Zero symptoms. 1.5cm found MRI w/o contrast one yr ago 7/2024 in a series of ‘prevention” medical testing done w “Fountain Life” out of FL. All testing was very/good otherwise.

UV morning sunrise added, and full savage-to-be soon at 13N.
How best to monitor? (Rejected the 1yr follow up recommendation MRI w contrast)
Uncle Jack Input greatly appreciated.
Thank You

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-13T00:45:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incidental-pineal-cyst.31296/post-356224>

This history suggests a benign condition. Your recent addition of UV morning sunrise exposure and plans for a full-spectrum light regimen at 13°N latitude align with my thesis’s focus on natural light as a restorative force.

Understanding the Pineal Cyst Context

Pineal cysts are common, with prevalence ranging from 1.5% to 23% in healthy adults on high-resolution MRI, often discovered incidentally. For a 1.5 cm cyst without symptoms, calcification, or atypical features (e.g., solid components, nodularity), research indicates a benign course, with stability observed in most cases over long-term follow-up (e.g., 6 months to 14 years). The lack of hydrocephalus, Parinaud syndrome, or other neurological signs further supports this. The pineal gland’s role in melatonin production and its sensitivity to light, as per my thesis, suggest that its function could be influenced by environmental light exposure, though the cyst itself is unlikely to alter this unless it grows significantly.

### Jill Dee — 2025-07-13T02:43:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incidental-pineal-cyst.31296/post-356229>

Boom!
Thanks so much,
\-keep Rockin’ it…

# Thread: Consciousness Blogs Questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consciousness-blogs-questions.31302/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nick-Rolser — 2025-07-14T15:35:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consciousness-blogs-questions.31302/post-356275>

As a psychology student, I’ve been very interested reading through the latest blogs on consciousness. I was already familiar with Penose and Hameroff’s papers, and after reading Becker’s book it struck me as obvious that the crystalline lattice structure of microtubules probably enabled them to act like semiconductors for the DC current. However, reading through the blogs has helped paint a clearer picture to see how mtDNA, DDW, melanin, light, and oxygen all work in tandem to produce consciousness. Nevertheless, I do have a few questions:

1\. Are Becker’s DC currents and Popp/Van Wijk’s UPE’s the same thing (I.e, “photonic current”) , or is it better to think of them as a coupled system?
2\. Jack says that the high information content of the narrow UPE spectra collapses the wave function via “measurement-like events”. Is this a different process than Penrose’s objective reduction? Is it more akin to a subjective collapse?
3\. Hameroff speculates that the microtubule associated proteins (MAPs) “tune” themselves. Am I correct in assuming that the mitochondrial electric fields is likely to be orchestrating that?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-14T20:09:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consciousness-blogs-questions.31302/post-356308>

1\. No Tissues have their own optical density which determines their quantum abilities.
2\. yes. Penrose does not understand how scale change light's abilities and that is on display with his theory
3\. Nothing tunes itself. Light, solitons, and acoustic phonons are needed.

### Nick-Rolser — 2025-09-17T21:31:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consciousness-blogs-questions.31302/post-359653>

I have another question somewhat related to consciousness that I figured I'd add to this thread:

I was thinking about the common occurrence of drug-induced psychosis from psychedelics such as LSD or psilocybn as a result of poor redox potential and destruction of neuromelanin in the substantia nigra/VTA from nnEMF paired with increased UPE emission. The way I'm picturing it intuitively is as though there isn't enough of a "screen" (melanin) to capture the light from the projector (UPEs) and that this elevates dopamine levels in the nigrostriatal pathway.

Second, and perhaps more importantly: I've been trying to fact check the idea that melanin can degrade into dopamine and finding it incredibly difficult. If someone could explain the mechanism by which this happens or point me to a blog that does I'd be very grateful. Is it some lesser-known biophysical mechanism involved? Because the mainstream biochemistry paradigm seems to suggest no such physiological pathway exists.

>  www.england.nhs.uk

### [UK concludes biggest pandemic simulation with virus targeting children](https://www.disclose.tv/id/38675lannj/)

Breaking news from around the world.

 www.disclose.tv

### UK concludes biggest pandemic simulation with virus targeting children​

SUMMARY
This autumn, the UK conducted Exercise Pegasus, the country’s biggest pandemic simulation in nearly a decade and its first Tier 1 national emergency exercise since Covid-19. Concluding last month, it involved ministers, all devolved nations, COBRA activation and every major government department.Participants faced a fictional novel strain of enterovirus D68 (EV-D68) that originated on the imaginary southeast Asian island of Musiyana after a food festival.

Unlike Covid-19, the simulated virus primarily killed infants, children and teenagers, causing respiratory failure, brain swelling and occasional paralysis. The three-phase drill began in September with regional spread in Asia, progressed in October to a WHO-declared pandemic with school closures, hospital strain and street protests over social distancing, then escalated in November to national lockdown, non-essential business shutdowns, travel bans and fears of food shortages from potential pig herd infection.

Sir Peter Horby, Director of the Pandemic Sciences Institute at Oxford University said:

> “The choice of an enterovirus is a good choice because it is a real possibility with real risks but is different from what we have seen before,”

Ministers tested both containment and mitigation decisions, including whether early travel restrictions could keep the virus out of Britain. The exercise followed Covid Inquiry findings that pre-2020 plans focused only on coping with high deaths rather than prevention, and that school closures “brought ordinary childhood to a halt”.

Lessons from Pegasus will be published next year.

# Thread: Laurens Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-optimal-journal.31320/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3

### laufolan — 2025-07-16T18:21:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-optimal-journal.31320/post-356422>

Optimal Journal

I joined in May and am grateful to find all this info. I was diagnosed with type LADA Type 1.5 diabetes LADA in February with A1C of 7.0 in january. I also have rosacea and leaky gut. Before fiding you, I went raw vegan for 6 weeks lowered my antibodies and got my c peptide up from 1.08 to 1.44 from feb to march – but htey dropped and increased after travel, lost too much weight and lost my period (for 73 days). I still produce insulin but since going carnivore keto my c petptide has dropped to .6. I got my A1C from 7.1 in February to 5.8 as of last week. My antibodies are going down. ( type 1 and throglobulin)

Had Lasik eye surgery when I was 25 and bunion surgery when I was 23. I have the MTFR mutation and 4 vitamin D mutations – if this is relevant. I live in Massachusetts 42 degrees N on in a coastal town Marshfield and grew up two towns over. I am 35 years old. I had eating disorders in college for 1.5 years , on birth control from 18-31, and had irregular periods. My period has been regular since coming off birth control, except two months where I shifted my diet and lost weight due to going Raw Vegan in february before I found your work. I went from (125-113 now back at 120 with a goal of 128 - my cycle has been back for 2 months) I am new and quickly realized I have been doing major things wrong for the past 35 years. I was vegetarian for the past 8 years 3-4 of those I was vegan- eating whole foods and tons of carbs. I would go on fruit weeks sometimes while living in MA and ate smoothies everyday for 5+ years. I also was a night owl my since I was little up until finding you – I worked in restaurants from age 13-35. I wore contacts from age 13-25. Growing up consistently had strep, mono in college, and have the EBV antibodies currently. Recent testing showed negative though. I haven’t gotten any vaccinations since high school or middle school whatever was required to attend – except one Hepitatis B when I was traveling to India in 2018(didn't finish the sequence). I was a c section, formula, and grew up eating tons of carbs. I am irish/English/dainish/ Scottish heritage – but have been surprisingly tanning well. Body is stuck in sympathetic state - nervous system is fried.

I had a meeting with Dr Jack at the end of May and he told me to get a building biologist and low and behold my partner and I built a microwave that we have been living in for the past 3.5 years. The readings were obnoxiously high, with 10 devices that were off the charts – but fortunately I have removed all of the devices and am able to get the house to be below RF of 10 while sleeping. Still have some work to do with this but progressing. I got a 2025 Mercedes in September that was also a microwave and I have given it up. As well as stepped away from waitressing and the studio I worked at due to the environment and the effects on my body – all changes I started making in may/June. The studio I worked at teaching hot yoga my feet and sometimes hands and tounge would go numb only when I taught there.

I am sitting in the sun for 4-8 hours a day and spending lots of time in the woods currently and putting all my intention and attention to healing and reversing. It was a struggle for me to wake up at sunrise as I spent the last 17 years going to bed between 12-3am and waking up 6-11am. The first 2 weeks I was out a half hour after sunrise, then a few days out at sunrise but falling asleep outside, and now in week 3 up and not falling asleep. My energy has been extremely low. I hope this is an adjustment period. I am eating keto/carnivore with seafood, wild venison/boar, tallow/lard and salt only since the beginning of June.. I think the changes I have already made will show big improvements, and am planning to head to El Salvador & Nicaruaga for the winter months – thinking late November through January/Feb. I am drinking 50 oz deuterium depleted water daily and an additional 20 oz of plain . I have started with your work from the begginning and moving forward - it is easier for me to comprhend this way- but have not been reading anything new yet other than the recents pow wows

I got tested for heart rate variability and bolt score – and they were bad – my nervous system is fried and stuck in a sympathetic state. Im thinking nature and light will ease this

Maternal History

Mom – Bipolar/depression diagnosis but no longer and off all meds, alcoholic started drinking at age 9 – sober for 1.5 years, disordered eating, sexual/physical abuse starting at age 3, has had 12 surgeries ( spine, 6 knees, wrist, 2 bunions, teeth) , osteoporosis

Grandmother – Type 2 diabetes, alcoholic, cancer all over dies in late 70’s

Grandfather – Tyoe 2 diabetes Survied Lung cancer (smoker 50 years) cataracts, alcoholic, died cancer at 92

Paternal History

Dad – Gut issues, alcoholic 1.5 years sober – doctors say hes in great health but definitely has IBS or something

Grandmother – Louie Body Syndrom died

Grandfather – Type 1 diabetes died in 1969 when my dad was 10 from diabetes

**QUESTIONS FOR JACK:**

I would love to know the best suggested place in El Savador to heal for my particular case – I am a person who is very good at being alone and love the jungle and forest and trees – I like the beach too, just love to be in nature – I like people, but have no problem being alone and am very low maitenance- the most important thing to me is being in the most optimal environment to heal. Would the ideal location be on the volcano?

I read on the forum LADA might not want to eat eggs – what are the parameters for this? I eat duck eggs – I have had food sensitivity tests and I am not reactive to them – I will be shifting to more epi paleo over the next few weeks – but still hesitant to introduce more veggies as I was very reactive to them – when does the carnivore become detrimental?

I have been depleted of energy lately – I don’t know if it is the transition of waking up so early, lack of carbs, or something else – My whole body is tiredDo you have any suggestions to add to what I am already doing?

Is the c peptide of .6 due to eating no carbs? It is a drastic drop in my opinion from 1.08 and since I have been able to get it up in march its strange to me

Is there a way to focus redox in a particular area or does it only improve with the whole body practices. My chest gets red but heat rashes – is this a shitty redox area if it heat rashes?

An old video stated to not add salt in water – is this accurate as it seems newer stuff praises salt especially with 5g or is it – more salt on food not in water

I have been doing CT in the ocean and face dunks at the moment as I was under the impression autimmmune and my stressed out nervous system it would not be beneficial . I have a cold plunge but have been avoiding because of this. Is this true – should I not CT ovulation to mesntrul cycle?

### laufolan — 2025-07-22T18:45:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-optimal-journal.31320/post-356784>

Just read the lasik blog -i got lasik 10 years ago is this something that can not be supported? I understand the scar is there but can the sun now help heal the damage already done?

### laufolan — 2025-08-16T01:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-optimal-journal.31320/post-358322>

I have very low leptin - 1.1 and can see how that is such a root in my situations - sticking to the leptin rx as well eat high fat as I had eating disorders in the past -- I feel I have to refeed myself, I had such strict diet regimen and cleansing protocols the past few years to "heal" I see how it could have just made stressors worse - it's wild - I deprived myself of so much for so long light& proper nutrition, water, sleep it's time to nourish it all back in

### [360: Paraguay: Economic Backbones Of A Rising Nation - The Expat Money Show - With Mikkel Thorup](https://expatmoneyshow.com/episodes/360-paraguay-economic-backbones-of-a-rising-nation/)

If you’ve been looking for stability, opportunity, and sovereignty in one place, you need to start paying serious attention to Paraguay. With food independence, energy abundance, and a banking system that quietly outperforms many better-known jurisdictions, Paraguay is becoming a go-to...

### Mr.AJ — 2025-07-16T19:00:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paraguay.31321/post-356425>

What merit is there to offshore banking now that BTC exists? Like A) what's the point of keeping assets in fiat that will lose value and B) you still run the risk of banks closing and money gone.

### Michalis — 2025-07-16T21:00:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paraguay.31321/post-356434>

Having a base in Paraguay is not a bad idea if you can afford to go there and sort out the residency. Banks won't close down overnight and the system is moving to stablecoins. Play all fields if you can..

### Patrick Newhouse — 2025-07-17T01:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paraguay.31321/post-356457>

The residency is a relatively easy process if you have the right people representing you. It probably won't stay easy for long though!! Countries are definitely going to make the process more difficult as demand for travel documents increases.

Also, a residency there gives you access to the Mercosur agreement countries, which is very similar to the way a European passport gives you access to the entire EU but in South America.

# Thread: Skin and water thermometers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 0.66eV Eva — 2025-07-16T19:19:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356427>

Hi,

To start face dunking, I bought a Etekcity Lasergrip 1080 Laser Thermometer Digital Infrared Thermometer Temperature Gun for Kitchen Cooking BBQ Grill and Bath Water, -58℉~1130℉ (-50℃～610℃). I saw some people on this forum bought similar products and use them to test their skin temperature and the water temperature.

But after reading the directions, I doubt I can use that for the skin safely. I may keep it to test water temperature or return it and buy a simpler, non infrared thermometer for the face dunk and cold plunge.

I'd love to receive some suggestions from people here for a good quality skin thermometer.

Thank you!

### Woodstock — 2025-07-18T17:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356549>

> [Daphnée said:](/goto/post?id=356427)
>
> Hi,
>
> To start face dunking, I bought a Etekcity Lasergrip 1080 Laser Thermometer Digital Infrared Thermometer Temperature Gun for Kitchen Cooking BBQ Grill and Bath Water, -58℉~1130℉ (-50℃～610℃). I saw some people on this forum bought similar products and use them to test their skin temperature and the water temperature.
>
> But after reading the directions, I doubt I can use that for the skin safely. I may keep it to test water temperature or return it and buy a simpler, non infrared thermometer for the face dunk and cold plunge.
>
> I'd love to receive some suggestions from people here for a good quality skin thermometer.
>
> Thank you!

I don’t have a good suggestion because I only tested the water and not skin when I tried face dunking. I didn’t think I was overdoing it. But I ended up getting a sinus infection from face dunking. I am sure the bacteria was already in my sinus. But the face dunking possibly messed with my immune system. I was afraid to cold plunge after that but I ended up trying it in El Salvador and it was great. If 55 degrees is too cold, I still found benefit at higher temps and then gradually lower or add more ice after 5 minutes. If you feel really uncomfortable just get out and warm up. Just keep at it and you will eventually be able to go longer and colder. 55 is plenty cold for me though! Otherwise my joints in hands and feet ache.

### 0.66eV Eva — 2025-07-20T01:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356624>

I realised I have been too credulous about the warnings in the directions!!! It makes me understand how much brainfog I have. I receive way more infrared from my IR-red panel than from this thermometer. And this thermometer works great! Yesterday was my 2nd day and at one time my forehead went to 54.3°F. I do many very short consecutive dunks during a few minutes (3-5min) because my forehead hurts from the cold...from dehydration (I'm sure) and possibly still too much omega-6.

I had not thought about the possibility of sinus infection. OUch! I'm glad that you enjoy it now. Are you still in El Salvador? So you are in the cold plunges phase of the CT protocol?

Thank you [@Woodstock](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/32840/) !!

### Woodstock — 2025-07-20T16:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356659>

> [Daphnée said:](/goto/post?id=356624)
>
> I realised I have been too credulous about the warnings in the directions!!! It makes me understand how much brainfog I have. I receive way more infrared from my IR-red panel than from this thermometer. And this thermometer works great! Yesterday was my 2nd day and at one time my forehead went to 54.3°F. I do many very short consecutive dunks during a few minutes (3-5min) because my forehead hurts from the cold...from dehydration (I'm sure) and possibly still too much omega-6.
>
> I had not thought about the possibility of sinus infection. OUch! I'm glad that you enjoy it now. Are you still in El Salvador? So you are in the cold plunges phase of the CT protocol?
>
> Thank you [@Woodstock](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/32840/) !!

I am back in Boulder for a few months. I only did a week of face dunks last fall before getting sick and didn’t want to continue that method. I did my first cold plunge in El Salvador in March. It felt great in the heat. I dunk in to the top of my neck but I don’t put my head in the cold water. I have been increasing the time I stay in at my own pace. I think it’s good to tune into your own body and what it’s telling you as we are all different. The instructions are a general guide. I think when I found a longer blog on CT, it explained a lot of different methods and I realized there are many ways to do it! In the book Epi-Paleo it says to check your VO2 Max before doing CT. I asked a bunch of mitos and no one had ever gotten the test done. So….I had been afraid to do it but then realized that no one else was checking this. The danger is when people go too cold for too long. I’ve seen people push it and it was kind of scary to watch. I suggest slow and steady wins the race. I would just get a cold plunge off of Amazon and get started based on my experience. No need to go below 55 degrees in my opinion but everyone is different.

### Kayasem1 — 2026-02-27T21:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-367769>

> [Daphnée said:](/goto/post?id=356624)
>
> I realised I have been too credulous about the warnings in the directions!!! It makes me understand how much brainfog I have. I receive way more infrared from my IR-red panel than from this thermometer. And this thermometer works great! Yesterday was my 2nd day and at one time my forehead went to 54.3°F. I do many very short consecutive dunks during a few minutes (3-5min) because my forehead hurts from the cold...from dehydration (I'm sure) and possibly still too much omega-6.
>
> I had not thought about the possibility of sinus infection. OUch! I'm glad that you enjoy it now. Are you still in El Salvador? So you are in the cold plunges phase of the CT protocol?
>
> Thank you [@Woodstock](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/32840/) !!

How have the face dunks been helping you?

### Woodstock — 2026-03-01T04:17:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-367834>

I don't ever face dunk because I don't want another sinus infection. my husband has a chronic one for 30 years. I think I'm too vulnerable. i haven't done a cold plunge in a while. i think stretching out in a full tub would be better because my knees usually hurt after a cold plunge that is vertical. i don't know if I should be doing them or not. but when in a very hot climate like El Salvador it's certainly much easier. and is invigorating! I took a break because I'm not really sure if all the bacterial infections like Lyme, Bartonella, EBV, Step, Staph, etc may be migrating during the plunges. makes my joints hurt more. maybe switching from hot tub to cold plunge would be a good thing for me? I don't know and I left Colorado and have no plans to return for more than a couple weeks at a time to purge more items and see my family briefly but not long enough to make my vitamin D plummet. i will have to be more diligent about testing before and after if I go back. but I'm at least going as far as Fiji in a couple months to sail and dive. see the world. or at least some cool underwater reefs that are still alive! before it's too late and the evil people destroy it all. El Salvador is a great place to get SCUBA certified with La Libertad diving here in Atami/Palmarcito area. He does it one on one. great for challenged brains. never thought I could do SCUBA. I am breaking through all my barriers! unstoppable!

# Thread: I NEED to leave the UK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-to-leave-the-uk.31324/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Luke Jeal — 2025-07-16T20:16:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-to-leave-the-uk.31324/post-356432>

Hi everyone,

After listening to June and Julys pow wow webinars, it has been the punch in the mouth I have needed to start executing my exit from the UK.

I am a Chiropractor in the UK and I have a wife and 2 children, who are 1 and 3. I am happy to leave the UK as I can see what is happening whilst my wife is a bit more cautious about leaving. I would like to move to Central America where I can continue practising as a Chiropractor. My wife is a nurse but is currently looking after the children full time.

I am looking for some advice and tips on moving. I have so far looked into Costa Rica, Panama, Nicaragua and El Salvador but it does seem confusing on where would be best to go.

If anyone can help or give me some advice that would be great. I would like to visit some countries first before moving so hoping to go to Central America in the next few months and try and make a decision from there.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Madeleine's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mightygumshoe — 2025-07-16T21:16:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-356439>

I joined in early May 2025, after reading Dr. Becker's The Body Electric, and half of Light in Shaping Life by Van Wyck (had to put it down for later, kind of deep). A friend introduced me to Dr. Kruse via some YTube videos and later the Danny Jones 4-hour podcast that to say was enlightening falls short. And I was hooked. My main motivation for joining was twofold: 1) find out more about my own body despite being healthy (I take no meds at all ...on the cusp of 63...except lots of Vit D and occasional calcium tablets). After starting red light and seeing big improvements where I had no expectations...had to join the tribe; and 2) get answers for my daughter's health which has been an issue since birth. I raised her alone, as hubsters died young. She is severely gluten and corn intolerant (autoimmune, not allergy), hypotonic, mold and chemical sensitive, and has hypermobile joints and sensitive nerves. Yet something must have gone right...she is a corporate account manager, an engineer, a brilliant musician (composes on piano), a great artist, and gifted with a phenomenal memory recall -- which I credit to math and music. I thank God for her every day!

She tested negative for Celiac in 9th grade but we had already given up gluten for months and weren't going back to eating it for 3 months prior to a test. So am not surprised. She was falling asleep standing up when she ate gluten-laden products; and gained 15 pounds. Also it caused severe mental fog, joint pain etc, and sometimes rashes. Hard in the teen years. And back then she was still eating corn and had a few symptoms intestinally not shared with me. And a lot of joint inflammation every time she ate corn -- she already suffered from loose joints. Piece by piece we started figuring some things out....took years, 15 of them.

I read that Celiac/gluten intolerance often presents at hormone changes (puberty, menopause). Anyhow, fast forward, kiddo made it through college with no issue (commuted locally) and just avoided wheat/barley/rye etc... and eventually corn, then everything was WONDERFUL for about 3 years; then she landed a corporate job, with a technology company in the automotive space (driverless cars, 5g, 6g and more....) and had to return to work most days and sit in an open floor plan with 5g phones and non-native EMF everywhere. And popcorn and wheat everywhere at lunch.... We do know she is sensitive to EMF! Cellphone pain when placed near ear etc. Leg pain with a laptop near thighs....after return to work, some symptoms in the gut returned, along with other new things. For the record we still do not know if she is a Celiac or not...and if gluten exposure or 5g/EMF or both are the problems at work. But I keep telling her I think we can heal your gut. At college wheat and corn were in the lunchroom and on the chairs and she never got sick. Never.

Nobody in our bloodline either side has ever had Celiac though I became severely gluten intolerant (joint pain and massive headaches, no gut issues) after delivering her.... and I did occasionally suffer headaches after drinking a beer or two... if not light beer, which of course I now believe was a gluten sensitivity. But not Celiac. When I went through menopause, I started having joint pain and severe headaches if I ingested wheat and brain fog too; and after I gave it all up (except corn), everything disappeared. I have permission to share the above.

And this is why I am here.

We are finding some answers for my daughter -- and I thank Dr. Kruse for answering my question about body pain during airplane flights (another issue my daughter has) in the latest July 2025 PowWow -- now I can deep dive on it. As can she. No, I had never heard of collagen vascular disease...NEVER. I did research in the blogs but was using other terms....hypotonia, airplane joint pain, etc. FYI, I am/was an electrical engineer who worked with many sensors ... and semiconductors etc for 15 years before switching careers...and I do know what flexo, piezo, pyroelectrics, even ferroelectrics are...that said, how it all ties into the human body and its environment...on that front I am still a newbie despite reading Becker's book and I plan to keep reading. THANK YOU from the bottom of my heart. PS, yes we live in a crappy place, Michigan, with Whitless as governor. Hard to leave family yet I am seriously considering a move south, where I can have land, a garden, more sun, etc. I worked so hard as a single mom (DIY) to get my house the way I like it...put in my own wood floors, molded my own pavers from concrete etc....and I back up to state land with deer, and woodchucks, and fox, and turkey etc....so it is hard to consider leaving...but health is wealth. I get it.

Since April/May of this year, cannot remember right now, I started going outside for the sunrise, getting sun midday, and continued to walk outside here in the evening (Michigan). The only months I don't walk outside are December and January and the beginning of February. But the AM routine is new. As is no sunglasses. Anyhow, I took Dr. Kruse's advice, and started using red light at night (not 7 days a week...but 4 or 5) and wear blue light blocking reading glasses and regular lens glasses for television at night which I rarely watch -- started going to the library again for paper books and revitalized a very old original Kindle from way back (black and white) so I can read on that device too. With these changes, light weightlifting, and now biking and swimming, and limiting caffeine to two morning cups before 11am...I started to and have continued to sleep through the night -- at least 7 hours. Sometimes more. NOT FOR 35 years had I been able to do this!!!

And there have been other changes. Years ago I had something taken off my nose, not cancer but was told precancerous. Well it was always a crusty dry spot forever afterward and I was told to stay out of the sun. Now that I have ditched the sunglasses and the sunscreen (I am very fair haired/light skin toned), I tanned up nicely over time this year, and the rough patch on my nose is completely gone. Was there for 25 years! Completely gone! Smooth skin! People keep telling me I look younger. For the record, I'm on the cusp of 63. I take no meds, except 10,000 units of Vit D in the winter daily, and 5k every other day in the summer. I eat steak once a week, 4 ounces and chicken once a week, the rest is seafood -- shrimp, salmon etc. and I feel awesome. I feel very blessed to have found this group and thank my friend for directing me here. God bless you all. Dr. Kruse if you read this, know you are such a blessing!!! I appreciate you as do countless others.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-07-26T22:15:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-357012>

Short update -- For last three to four months (some things added later), I have been
1) **Seeing morning sun 7 days a week**; also afternoon and evening. Am outside probably 2 to 4 hours a day, t-shirt or tanktop, bathing suit, etc.

2) **Wearing 99.9% bluelight blocking glasses** -- a) readers for computer work; b) straight lenses for nighttime tv/movie watching. Mainly a book (paper) reader but sometimes tablet use.

3) **Red light box used 4x a week sometimes more**. Shooting for 6x. Usually for 20 minutes at a time up to one hour. On face, back of head, feet, sometimes entire upper body. I rotate even during one session. Have two red bulbs in house for nighttime. Yes, I still use regular lights at times too.

4) **Eating shrimp and salmon more during the week** (2-3x, sometimes 4) and less red meat (2x a week); I still eat organic Amish chicken and the bone broth from it. I use butter / olive oil / avocado oils. The idea of an oyster...can't do it. Texture issue for me. Gag.

5) **Dropped Vit D Supplements for now**. Getting enough sun.

6) **Earthing using a mat grounded outside \[ground change is new\], run into my house via window but in a "dead zone" electrically speaking (tested).

RESULT**
1) **I sleep through the night** -- **every night** now, 8 to 9 hours (despite pet interruptions for a 4am feeding...right back to sleep); was 4 at start, then 6, then seven to 7.5 hours and occasionally 8. I also wake up VERY rested. No shiners under my eyes.

2) **I lost 8 or 9 pounds over the last two months** **without even trying.**

Side note -- we have a backyard pool hidden (don't tell our HOA); anyhow in Michigan temps have been getting colder at night. I heat it with a solar heater I made (copper tubing under glass painted black) and a small solar pond pump. (6x10 foot 33" pool).....here at end of July temps are dipping at night. So pool is cooling. I like my pool 85....but am still getting in and swimming at 79....and treading water. I'm a wuss, but it's a start. Don't laugh. Will read more of the CT blog...at least I'm taking action and doing something.

Peace.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-08-08T17:25:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-357826>

The main reason I joined this forum is because of my daughter's health issues. To be frank, she kind of gave up on talking to anyone with MD after their name. From this site, and Dr. Kruse's response on the last Gold forum, I know she has a collagen vascular disease. She also has gut issues. Despite testing negative for celiac (we had already given up all gluten \[wheat/barley/rye\] but had not yet given up corn, she was told she was probably celiac or gluten intolerant. To test it, she would need to eat wheat again for 3 months then have her intestines biopsied. She said no. So, after that we took matters into our own hands.

After reading posts here, and blogs and doing my own research on vascular issues, a leaky gut, etc... I have encouraged my daughter to follow some of Dr. Kruse's protocols and by golly she actually listened.

For the past 3 weeks she has seen the sun every morning -- no exceptions, barefoot outdoors before work. No glasses. For at least 10 minutes.
1) She gets outside later in the day, in a tank top when she works from home and eats dinner outside here in Michigan when she can, or walks before sunset. No glasses during the walk.
2) She purchased blue light blocking glasses for computer work at her office; and for nighttime tv.
3) No wifi at night.
4) She earths outside every day as much as possible. We'll see what happens in winter -- maybe she can earth on the garage floor a bit. She feels joint pain at first when she earths (outdoor), which later subsides. And said her feet turn a little pink, then it all subsides. I feel nothing.
5) This one is the gamechanger. DEUTERIUM DEPLETED WATER -- 5 and 10ppm bottles (glass - she reacts to PET) from Litewater, diluted to 80ppm ---- her GUT is SO MUCH BETTER. WAY BETTER. She committed to drinking it for at least 60 days and will probably drink it for one whole year. As a gift I gave her three deliveries for her birthday, the rest is on her.

Side notes --

I've been reading Pollack's 4th Phase of Water and am starting to grasp some of the science that is in the blogs a bit better. Next, I will go back and read Light in Shaping Life which I abandoned 5 chapters in because it was just too deep at the time and my mind wandered.

Don't know where to put this article about taurine that I recently read online so I am putting it here. It mentions Deuterium, EZ water, taurine etc. [Taurine prevents Mitochondrial Dysfunction and protects Mitochondria from ROS and Deuterium Toxicity](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00726-024-03440-3) (January, 2025)

Dr. Kruse, I read your Epi-Paleo RX book this past weekend and put a nice review in for you on Amazon. Amazing read. I devoured it early Saturday, then went back and reread it with a highlighter. Thank you. I also ordered two of the Paleo books mentioned/recommended in your book. The Leaky Gut portion of the book was of most interest. I shared the Leaky Gut Rx for autoimmunity prevention (bone broth with coconut oil etc) with family. We don't use Coconut oil...time for a change. My daughter is now looking at getting B12, folate and glutathione tests -- and looking for B12 supplements (she cannot have corn or corn derivatives -- so takes none, but does eat a LOT of green leafy salads, veggies etc -- still doubtful is enough); have heard there are topical B12 but she is looking for sublingual/pill form. That book is a must read.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-08-11T20:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-357996>

> [Axholler1 said:](/goto/post?id=357950)
>
> When u say Litewater 10ppm diluted to 80ppm how do you achieve this dilution? How much total in a day?

We used this site. Our tap water is filtered. We assumed about 150 ppm. Michigan. We have both 5 and 10 ppm litewater at home. With 5 ppm it is about a 60/40 mix of tap (filtered) to ddw bottle water that is 5ppm. The litewater site I think under faq somewhere has dilutions as well, for 80, 100 etc ppm, a chart. How much you want to make in final concentration is in this calculation. My daughter was drinking 1/2 liter, now 3/4 to a liter a day. She adds a little sea salt to it (couple pinches) [https://www.nourishmeorganics.com.au/pages/deuterium-dilution-calculator](https://www.nourishmeorganics.com.au/pages/deuterium-dilution-calculator)

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-10-13T23:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-360981>

Have not been on here much lately. Thought would share my daughter’s health is better. Also though we had lowered electric field in her bedroom etc by moving things around, her bed is now back up against the wall, so I added a 5’x10 or 12’ piece of metal screen over a wall that has electrical wires running behind it, and used an ESD (electroststatic discharge) cable kit with “snaps” to attach it to an outlet ground cover screw….and…what was low at side of bed away from wall 200mv/m sometimes up to 5v/m but at wall 50 v/m and even more…..is now zero. Measured with trifield meter. My daughter for last three nights is sleeping incredibly hard. She is earthing after work until bed (in dead zone in my home and outdoors) and will increase it to all night. But the screen has helped. She is EHS. Since starting DDW at 100 ppm 3/4 liter a day, with pinch of sea salt, her gut, mood, wellbeing etc much improved. Been three months drinking that. She chases that with low DDW food. She is working on getting a WFH job so she can relocate to water and more sun. For now, we make do. Blessings. Owe a lot to you all. She will add her art to the wall but for now does not care that it is ugly looking because it works. Next up we swap out four light bulbs with incandescents. Other common lights used at night are all red light except kitchen. Michigan electric rates are high. But this swap should also help, where I read, work out, etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-22T17:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-361407>

Taurine has a very dark side that food gurus never speak on. Yes, it promotes bile flow and digestion… But it also binds to bile and forms taurocholate; a bile acid that feeds toxic bacteria. → More taurocholate = more H₂S (mitochondrial inhibitor) → More endotoxin → More Candida overgrowth and more SIBO risks If taurine makes you feel worse… this is why.

It’s not that taurine is bad, but in the wrong gut environment due to poor light choices on your skin, it can backfire hard. Fix your skin clocks to fix you gut problems before you flood it with bile.

Was there for 25 years! Completely gone! Smooth skin! People keep telling me I look younger. For the record, I'm on the cusp of 63. I take no meds, except 10,000 units of Vit D in the winter daily, and 5k every other day in the summer. I eat steak once a week, 4 ounces and chicken once a week, the rest is seafood -- shrimp, salmon etc. and I feel awesome. I feel very blessed to have found this group and thank my friend for directing me here. God bless you all. Dr. Kruse if you read this, know you are such a blessing!!! I appreciate you as do countless others.

Did Jack tell you to specifically use the red light device at night? I thought I read somewhere on here to use it in the morning.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-10-27T02:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-361654>

> [Kel said:](/goto/post?id=361652)
>
> Did Jack tell you to specifically use the red light device at night? I thought I read somewhere on here to use it in the morning.

Morning is better probably. In summer I did not use at all, because was outside a lot. But at night, I like to put at back of head behind the top of my head when I lay on my back, about two or three hours before bed. It quiets my brain. A lot. In 8 minutes. Only use it 15 or 20. I meditate and say prayers. Fall is here in Michigan, so I will probably start doing it again. We use red lights and yellow incandescents in house after sunset. I cannot express how great I feel.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-11-03T17:50:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-362064>

Ok, going to share a data point. It is just one data point with hopefully many more to follow.

Since March of this year I got my daughter to take some of Dr. Kruse's advice. She gradually added things in and MAN OH MAN have we seen improvement. She eats gluten/corn free. We were told was Celiac from a blood test years ago, but said no to gut biopsy and truthfully I never believed 100% that she was Celiac \[just my own mom gut saying that based on what I saw\]....but any exposure to wheat/corn etc makes her very sick. We are a grain free home.

At work they have popcorn days -- she usually WFH those days and still gets sick from it floating around desks the next day. But this time she stayed at work for meetings and DID NOT GET SICK AT ALL. AT ALL!!!!! FIRST TIME EVER ...as in 12 maybe 16 years...I lost count. One data point. I hope for more. We are lucky too in that where she sits at work apparently is a low RF/E-field zone. She took meter to work and measured. And they turn off overhead lights.

Since March 2025 gradually added these things...

1) Morning sun, no glasses.
2) Walk in sun, no glasses in afternoon (lunch) and again in evening before sunset - getting harder here in Michigan with time change. But making effort and trying to drive home with windows down. We will open windows that face east to get morning sun if need be in our home in the dead of winter.
3) Blue light blocker overlays for work and at home indoors and in evening.
4) Red light therapy pad and box (we like Bestqool but that is just us, not selling anything). She uses it every day. I use it sometimes. More in winter, like now.
5) Red lights in our house at night. Blue light blockers worn with any screen on at night.
6) In Sept/Oct added a few incandescents -- for night time book reading before bed.
7) Earthing - outdoor in summer; also we can earth in our basement/garage if wet concrete floor in winter (VERY DEAD ZONE THERE) and in one place on main floor of house. With #8 below seems more dead spaces even in bedrooms. I still walk barefoot outside in 37-38 deg weather in here Michigan and it no longer bothers me. My daughter...not doing that.
8) Added metal (window) screen 5'x25' roll from Home Depot cut to size, around my daughter's office and bedroom walls upstairs at my home; thumbtacks hold it in place; grounded with ESD clips. e-Field went to zero ~1 foot from wall. No RF. No e-field. Did same in my bedroom. Put on backside of my office wifi router to protect me in my home office too. My daughter started sleeping really hard at night and waking up refreshed.
\- I bought wall filters for dirty electricity too. And saw difference in outlet measurements before/after -- 85-90 went to 26. Or 31.
\- My kitchen was over 135 on outlet meter. Dropped to 31 with a Stetzer filter in outlet. Some other outlet circuits were already low.
\- I am no longer getting headaches...hmm.

\- My daughter is EHS -- cell phone to ear hurts. Laptop on thighs "burns" -- she can feel electrical wiring overhead on a walk and will actually run from it. I always wonder if this is why she can feel thunderstorms on a sunny day when they are 100 miles away and nowhere in sight.

9) Back in June.... Added DDW 10 ppm water diluted to 100 ppm with pinch of sea salt (mineral salt she tried has class action for mold contamination - once we got her off that and went back to good ole sea salt, big change). Been drinking DDW, 1 liter a day. **NO GUT ISSUES SINCE SHE STARTED DRINKING DDW** - with the sea salt. She is like a NEW person.

It all adds up and helps but for me **DDW and the screen in her room and the filters HUGE DIFFERENCE**. Sunshine matters too. She earths in shower (copper pipes) and wherever else she can.

One data point. I hope there are more. She is a completely different person. Reminds me so much of my late husband who was the funniest man I ever knew. Best present I could ever give myself -- and my daughter. She is getting her health back, and looking for a position that enables her to live in the sun, in a warmer climate. For now, I will take what I can get. People don't understand when I tell them what you eat and what you do can affect you mentally and physically; it all goes together. Environment is a big deal. You can work with it, or against it. Fix what you can. THANK YOU.

Next up -- she will be eating shrimp three times a week like I do. I actually feel like we are turning a corner here. I feel better too.

God bless.

And a shout out to my dear friend Julie who is not on this forum who sent me videos about Dr. Kruse back in December/January of this year.

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mightygumshoe — 2025-07-16T22:22:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-might-heal-autoimmune-diseases.31327/post-356444>

[https://www.scientificamerican.com/...that-sunlight-might-heal-autoimmune-diseases/](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/surprising-ways-that-sunlight-might-heal-autoimmune-diseases/)
1) Talks about MS and benefits of UV light therapy
2) "The new studies offer tantalizing hints that UV therapy might also work for other autoimmune diseases such as type 1 diabetes, rheumatoid arthritis, Crohn’s disease and colitis."
3) Discusses Vit D
4) "Hart says. 'UV light calms inflammation in the skin. But it also then calms inflammation in the central nervous system. It’ll calm inflammation in the pancreas and the gut. So I think it’s not fully realized the potential it has to be a controller of body homeostasis.'"

# Thread: Nicaragua?
