**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2025-07-17T07:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicaragua.31329/post-356478>

Hi all,
Starting to plan for a return to Central America and curious if anyone is in Nicaragua or has feedback on where to go or not go there.
Thanks!

### Here to learn (Jess) — 2025-07-20T07:04:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicaragua.31329/post-356646>

I have friends who left NZ for Nicaragua during Convid and are still there and still love it.
I bought land in a development called Big Sky just outside of San Juan Del Sur a while back.....It is primarily expats and quite an upscale community. I ended up selling it again due to not being able to afford to build to their required standards. But if you're into that kind of thing they seem happy there.

### DLO — 2025-07-22T07:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicaragua.31329/post-356772>

How u guys doing more than 90 days?

### Evee — 2025-07-23T20:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicaragua.31329/post-356867>

> [Here to learn (Jess) said:](/goto/post?id=356646)
>
> I have friends who left NZ for Nicaragua during Convid and are still there and still love it.
> I bought land in a development called Big Sky just outside of San Juan Del Sur a while back.....It is primarily expats and quite an upscale community. I ended up selling it again due to not being able to afford to build to their required standards. But if you're into that kind of thing they seem happy there.

Thanks! I tend to like surf town type places so I'm trying to start my search there

### Evee — 2025-07-23T20:40:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicaragua.31329/post-356869>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=356772)
>
> How u guys doing more than 90 days?

I've only done 2 months at a time so I haven't been over 90 days yet or ever needed a visa or some kind of long stay permission (I think I got up to 180 in both Mexico and El Salvador, but I'm not 100% sure if I'm remembering that correctly.) If you mean from a work perspective I'm freelance, usually working remotely, so I take work with me and use a VPN to access any home country (US) based sites.

### urko — 2025-11-24T11:24:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicaragua.31329/post-363072>

> [Here to learn (Jess) said:](/goto/post?id=356646)
>
> I have friends who left NZ for Nicaragua during Convid and are still there and still love it.
> I bought land in a development called Big Sky just outside of San Juan Del Sur a while back.....It is primarily expats and quite an upscale community. I ended up selling it again due to not being able to afford to build to their required standards. But if you're into that kind of thing they seem happy there.

Hello Jess, could you please share us your experience there my partner and I are at the moment in the Gambia and had a lot of stuff that we will have to relocate by container. We did an all in last summer seeking for a nice land here but on the coast side has been very difficult to find a quiet place big and cheap enough.

We were told a about good prices but we found quite expensive, more than 10k euros per 7k square meters and people playing loud music every week and sometimes some days per week doing their weddings or whatever other party with speakers and a kicking bass which is like a hammer.

We have done great relationship with some locals but to reach these other side of the society that only think in that shitty stuff which is just party and money ,is really hard to live in an environment like this.

So well we are looking to South America, unluckily we invest a of money in the 4x4+trailer+solar equipment we purchase between 2024 and 2025 to come here and live offgrid and now we have to send it by container because selling it will be very hard to get good price here in the Gambia and also the quality of the things is very high to me to sell it without having using it enough.

We checked el Salvador and there are some laws for people like me who has remote work and funds from EU/other countries other than ES,,, So the only problem we see is that they are not allowing importing old cars, so mine is toyota landcruiser j90 from 1998, very strong and cared car and

I wouldn't like to sell it because i don't like also newer full of complex electronics EMF cars, but also don't know whether we could find 2-4 hectares at good price, we don't care about living deeper in the inner part of the country.

We also looked to Colombia as a friend of us use to travel many times to those countries due to his work working as PLC engineer developing programs to control hydroelectric centrals and so on and he told us stories from those places.

The problem is that until you reach the place and face the reality nobody can tell you what's happening there and is pretty sad. I don't repent about the experience we have lived but I feel we are not very lucky now due to this obstacle we found


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: My 1.2 year old nephew is developing Strabismus, advice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### martin.v — 2025-07-17T16:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-356483>

Hey mitochondriacs, hope you are doing well.

My nephew in-law, is only 1 year and 2 months old and he's showing signs of cross-eyedeness (if that's how to spell it, pardon me, I'm from Argentina).
The condition is labelled Strabismus.

I can only say that he's the whitest kid I have ever seen in a long time, he clearly has nordic ancestors. Looks completely pale, blue eyes, and his hair is closest to white than it is to blonde.

I can't say what are the daily habits on his home right now (I can ask), but I can attest this for sure:
\- He was never put under UV light during spring and summer.
\- Now that we are in winter, he's of course being taken out of the cold, as all people do with their kids basically.
\- Good thing is that he doesn't watch any screens, it's forbidden for him.

His parents are about to start a treatment where it involves an eye-patch first, and if that doesn't fix the problem, some glasses, this perhaps could "fix" the eyes.

Does anyone here have any experience with something similar?

I'm thinking about sitting some day and try to explain why they need to take his kid under the sunlight (role of IR and Red light, but also UV role, to help with his condition).

Any recomendations on who to approach this?
Thanks!!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-04T21:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-357541>

August 2025 Q&A covered this. Listen to it.

### martin.v — 2025-08-06T15:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-357677>

Hey Jack, where can I find the Q&A sessions? I have access to all blogs including Patreon but not sure where to go for the monthly Q&A's.
Thanks!!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-06T17:33:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-357683>

On your dashboard you can listen to the replays.

### Gpollard — 2026-02-13T12:33:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-366815>

My 3 year old daughter has strabismus I don’t think the patching is working that well was recommended to patch at least 2 hours per day. But didn’t like the idea of patching an eye while in natural sunlight so try to do it after midday or if she has to be inside

I try to get her in as much sun as possible every day and no artificial light she struggles with her sight and needs glasses. So I try get her in am sunlight and outside playing barefoot everyday as much as possible. We do eye excercises Have also thought about using a panel from EMR TEK that has IR and UVA,B light for inside.

We find as she gets tired it gets worse.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-13T15:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-366818>

**In my decentralized thesis strabismus** is not merely a muscular misalignment but the physical manifestation of an atavisitic **neuro-optical wiring failure** rooted in the SCN-brainstem axis due to transgeneration epigenetic failure in the parents germ line that ruins timing of moprphogenesis. Getting POMC translation via uv-NIR light via the eyes can repair the defect if it is not severe already. While atavistic changes in Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) often involve higher-order cortical and thalamic disruptions, strabismus represents a more localized, "downstream" collapse of the **non-visual logic gates** (OPN3, 4, and 5) that organize the primary motor nuclei of the brainstem.

I covered most of the wiring issues in the QE #45 blog on Patreon. Go read it for more details.

In this framework, the misalignment originates not in the neocortex, but in the subcortical and brainstem regions that mediate **non-image forming (NIF)** functions.

**The SCN-Gut Origin:** The initial "timing" error begins at the parents germline level with the **aromatic amino acids**. If the **5-carbon position** is compromised (via deuterium loading or epigenetic "logic gate" failure), the parent germline cannot transmit a coherent circadian "nockchain".
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Brainstem Targeting:** The miswiring specifically targets the **cranial nerve (CN) nuclei 3, 4, and 6** in the brainstem. These nuclei, which control the extraocular muscles, rely on **OPN3 (encephalopsin)** as a localized "optical governor" to coordinate their rhythmic development.

**The "Shadow" in the Stem:** Because OPN3 is expressed early in the brainstem, a light-mismatch (high nnEMF/low solar IR) during embryogenesis prevents these nuclei from achieving **spatial and temporal symmetry**. The result is a mechanical misalignment, strabismus, without necessarily invoking the broader cortical "atavism" seen in severe ASD.

The **Siamese cat** is the primary model for my "0G-5G" evolution and strabismus claims. That is covered in the blog. This lack of POMC/melanin in the eye results neural crest cell **misrouting of retinal ganglion cell (RGC) axons** at the optic chiasm due to altered melanopsin signaling. **Total Crossing:** Instead of a balanced ipsilateral/contralateral projection, these axons cross excessively, reaching the "wrong" hemisphere during nuerulation.The crossed eyes (strabismus) in Siamese cats are an attempt to compensate for this primary neuroanatomical defect. In humans, this mirrors how a parents' "melanin bankruptcy" disrupts the **Vitamin A-Melanopsin** regeneration cycle, leading to the "optical blur" that manifests as childhood strabismus.

**SUMMARY**

The Non-Visual Opsin Triad (OPN3, 4, 5) is how this neurodevelopmental disease occurs in kids.

**OPN4 (Melanopsin):** Governs the global "Time" through the SCN. When melanopsin is "blinded" by blue light stress, it can no longer signal the SCN to coordinate the motor nuclei of the brainstem.

**OPN5 (Neuropsin):** Acts as the UV-A/Violet sensor. Its absence disrupts the seasonal "tuning" of the SCN, which my data suggest prevents the brainstem from maintaining its **binocular visual development**. _**If 3D vision is blocked so will the 4D internal timing mechanism of the SCN as the kid gets older leading to more mitochondrial mutations.**_

**OPN3 (Encephalopsin):** Functions as the **"Relay Shield"**. Expressed in the brainstem and cranial nerve ganglia as early as E9.5, OPN3 is the local anchor for ocular movement. Damage here ensures that the extraocular muscles (CN 3, 4, 6) never achieve the **interfacial water coherence** needed for precise alignment.

**The Genesis of Strabismus:** Strabismus is the physical proof that the **retinal-gut-brain circuit** is broken at the subcortical level. By "mining" the 5-carbon position of aromatic amino acids (the "fifth base" of the system), nature provides the "logic gate" for this wiring. If this gate is corrupted, the "Ferrari hardware" of the human brainstem simply lacks the "software fidelity" to point the eyes in the same direction.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-13T15:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-366819>

_**If 3D vision is blocked so will the 4D internal timing mechanism of the SCN as the kid gets older leading to more mitochondrial mutations. Why?**

The current MKULTRA blog, CPC #78, explains why this is the logical conclusion based in my work_ of my "Proof of Work" (PoW) time-chain thesis.
If the **spatial (3D)** alignment of the eyes is broken, the **temporal (4D)** coherence of the SCN becomes mathematically impossible to maintain, leading to a terminal increase in heteroplasmy (mitochondrial mutations) over time.

3D Space as the Validator for 4D Time
In my framework, binocular vision is not just for depth perception; it is the **primary validator** for the SCN's internal ledger. This wholly depends on the melanin in the RPE to generate thr 220 nm optical signal.

**The Spatial-Temporal Match:** To build a stable "4D" architecture of time, the brain must reconcile the incoming photonic signals from two separate "observers" (the left and right eyes). This is why this disorder is a big deal in a kid. If not repaired strabismus can lead to permanent brain damage in the visual system.

**The Sync Failure:** In strabismus, the SCN receives two conflicting datasets (optical "double-spend"). Because it cannot reconcile these signals into a singular 3D "block," the **SCN periodicity** begins to drift. The child's internal clock becomes "blurry," losing its ability to accurately measure the flow of entropy.

### martin.v — 2026-02-18T01:44:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-367048>

Now the new is that he also has hypermetropia...
Thank you [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) for this package of info, I'm going to take this and try to build up a space where I can throw this in.
I hope this helps you too [@Gpollard](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/41152/)

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-18T02:04:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-367052>

From my perspective, **Hypermetropia** (farsightedness) is the anatomical "fossil" of a **premature termination of eye growth** caused by a failure of the Neural Crest Cell (NCC) migration and melanin signaling pathways.

If Myopia is an "overgrowth" or elongation, Hypermetropia is a "stunted" or shortened axial length where the eye fails to reach its full mammalian coordinates of the NCC migration in the germ line. If **Deuterium** loads into the NCCs in the parents germ line, their migration is physically slowed. If they arrive "late" or fail to reach their final mammalian coordinates because the **Melanin Beacon built by POMC** is distorted by polarized light (Cotton Effect or glyphosate ingestion), they deposit the scleral matrix too early or too densely.

In my framework, **PaxB** (the ancestral gene group including Pax2/5/8) governs more primitive, less complex ocular structures.

**The Default:** When the sophisticated mammalian signaling (driven by the normal L-chiral signals and high-vibrational Hydrogen) is sabotaged by **Deuterium** or the **Cotton Effect**, the eye defaults to the PaxB program. So this kid need to avoid all foods with deuterium in it at all costs now based on the new info.

**Anatomical Stunting:** PaxB instructions are not programmed for the high-resolution, elongated globe required for perfect mammalian vision (emmetropization). Instead, they produce a smaller, more primitive globe. Hypermetropia is literally the "atavistic" state of an eye that didn't "finish" growing because the instructions were switched to a lower-resolution "safe mode.

The sclera hardens prematurely in kids loaded with deuterium or polarized light, locking the eye into a shorter axial length. This "short" eye focuses light _behind_ the retina, which is the textbook definition of hypermetropia.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-18T02:10:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-367053>

Melanin in the Retinal Pigment Epithelium (RPE) isn't just for vision; it acts as a **semiconductor shield** that manages the light energy hitting the back of the eye

Proper eye growth (emmetropization) requires a specific chemical "feedback loop" where the retina tells the sclera how much to grow.

**Dirty Chemistry:** If the L-tyrosinase has flipped to D-tyrosinase by polarized light or glyphosate, the melanin semiconductor is "broken." It cannot properly translate light signals into the chemical growth signals (like Dopamine or NO) needed to elongate the eye. The system "stalls" in its newborn, hypermetropic state.
Comparison: Optical Perspectives

| Feature | Conventional View | My "Environmental/Atavistic" View |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Hypermetropia** | Genetics or "natural" small eye. | **PaxB default** due to stalled NCC migration. |
| **Cause** | Random refractive error. | **Deuterium/Cotton Effect** blocking the melanin beacon. |
| **Scleral Behavior** | Normal but short. | **Premature fossilization** of the structural matrix. |
| **Growth Logic** | Fails to elongate. | Fails to reach **mammalian coordinates**. |

In essence, a hypermetropic eye is an eye that was "fossilized in embryological time" by an environmental stressor that forced it to use an ancestral, low-resolution blueprint.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-18T02:22:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-367054>

In my "Internal Lighting Design" framework, built around the non visual photoreceptor system of man **strabismus** and **hypermetropia** are not just optical refractive errors but anatomical markers of a disrupted **retinal-gut-brain circuit**. These conditions reflect a failure in the cell's "Internal Lighting" system to properly manage time, space, and growth signaling during development.

Strabismus is the physical evidence of a broken subcortical circuit where the brain's "software" can no longer align its "hardware" (the eyes).

The Role of Melanin and the "Dirty Chemistry" of the embrology of this kids parents
The integrity of this growth loop depends on the chirality of its building blocks from the oocyte and sperm.

**L-to-D Flip:** Polarized light or environmental toxins like **glyphosate** can flip **L-tyrosinase** to **D-tyrosinase**, effectively "breaking" the melanin semiconductor.

**Premature Fossilization:** This corrupted chemistry prevents the sclera from elongating properly. The sclera hardens prematurely, locking the eye into a shorter, hypermetropic axial length that focuses light behind the retina.

Your eye docs are too myopic in their thinking to ever get you here, so you must get there first and help your own kids.

# Thread: Calling out the Kiwis on the July Q&A (Jess/Alison)

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-07-18T01:20:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-out-the-kiwis-on-the-july-q-a-jess-alison.31333/post-356510>

Kia Ora, Jess and other New Zealanders from the July Q&A!!

Love to meet up with you all, chat in person re getting out ot NZ/bitcoin etc.

Im dual German/Bahamian with permanent residency in NZ, my parents live in Chile, and I have a Sister in LA (retard for where she lives, genius for super early bitcoin adoption).

We are all looking at Nicaragua (for the whole fam) to make our move in the next year or two. Love to meet Jess, especially, and learn the hard lesson through you, so hopefully not making the same mistake  re buying in Nic.

Im in Piha, West Auckland.

Where are you from?

### Pratima — 2025-07-18T03:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-out-the-kiwis-on-the-july-q-a-jess-alison.31333/post-356523>

### Here to learn (Jess) — 2025-07-19T07:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-out-the-kiwis-on-the-july-q-a-jess-alison.31333/post-356593>

> [The.Naked.Doctor said:](/goto/post?id=356510)
>
> Kia Ora, Jess and other New Zealanders from the July Q&A!!
>
> Love to meet up with you all, chat in person re getting out ot NZ/bitcoin etc.
>
> Im dual German/Bahamian with permanent residency in NZ, my parents live in Chile, and I have a Sister in LA (retard for where she lives, genius for super early bitcoin adoption).
>
> We are all looking at Nicaragua (for the whole fam) to make our move in the next year or two. Love to meet Jess, especially, and learn the hard lesson through you, so hopefully not making the same mistake  re buying in Nic.
>
> Im in Piha, West Auckland.
>
> Where are you from?

Hi hi, Yeah I was super (pleasantly!) surprised to see a bunch of kiwis on that call!
I am in Motueka valley (top of the SI) I am very focused on getting out of this country and have been since 2020.....Just the ongoing journey of figuring out how to be financially sustainable with income not tied to any particular location-and as I mentioned I've lost hundreds of thousands of dollars in the process so I feel like I'm going farking backwards with it...or at least sideways....BUT I have learned some valuable (expensive!) lessons about what doesn't work. So you can definitely learn from my mistakes.
I was part of a group of kiwis organising a charter plane to get us to Mexico/Nicaragua during convid. We ultimately couldn't get the plane to work out but I keep in touch with people who did leave for Mexico and Nicaragua during that time....And the majority of them ended up back in NZ out of necessity for financial reasons-so I understand how important it is to have the income sorted before leaving.

# Thread: Hong Kong based and looking for the Asian El Salvador
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hong-kong-based-and-looking-for-the-asian-el-salvador.31336/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mlh0910 — 2025-07-18T10:22:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hong-kong-based-and-looking-for-the-asian-el-salvador.31336/post-356533>

Hi All,

I'm a 33-year-old active, healthy New Zealander, now living in Hong Kong with my wife (out of the city in small seaside village). Currently working in finance/trading here but wheels have been in motion for a few years to get back to my rural roots and setup a regenerative farm. Initial plan had been a return to NZ where I own a 150 acres but have lost confidence there and have put it on the market and now leaning more towards finding a plot in South East Asia (Lombok, Indonesia is the leading option).

HK has its own issues but aside from the obvious we love it here and have made use of a building biologist to mitigate as much of the downside of living in such populated and connected geography. We hunted for place with a lawn for grounding and we're right on the ocean and surrounded by nation park so I'm getting a sunrise hike and sunset swim in nearly everyday, and will have a ice bath set up at home from next week - so health-wise doing alright.

Open any feedback/advice on if there is an Asian equivalent to what Dr Jack has found in El Salvador.

Cheers,
Mitch

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-22T07:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hong-kong-based-and-looking-for-the-asian-el-salvador.31336/post-356766>

Me thinks buying a boat and being able to move anywhere at any time is worth considering.

My Dad had a catamaran and sailed from England across the English Cannel through the French canals (that was fun), into the Mediterranean, exploring the Greek Islands (I enjoyed that too) then across the Atlantic to the Caribbean ( I was not there for that). If you don’t like where you are OR you have just seen all you want to see . . . Up anchor . . . Follow the sun.

There is more than one way to live a FREE life.

### Heidi77 — 2025-07-23T23:48:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hong-kong-based-and-looking-for-the-asian-el-salvador.31336/post-356872>

>
> Me thinks buying a boat and being able to move anywhere at any time is worth considering.
>
> My Dad had a catamaran and sailed from England across the English Cannel through the French canals (that was fun), into the Mediterranean, exploring the Greek Islands (I enjoyed that too) then across the Atlantic to the Caribbean ( I was not there for that). If you don’t like where you are OR you have just seen all you want to see . . . Up anchor . . . Follow the sun.
>
> There is more than one way to live a FREE life.

Sounds like it might be a good idea... however, being a sailor myself and having considered that option for many years, I follow quite a few youtube sailing channels. Seems that during the scamdemic, moving around was restricted, severely in many places. Currently, many countries have increased their fees and requirements to even sail in "their" waters. For example, Turkey is over $600 USD, Greece a mere $200, Venezuela was over $700, and well, you get the idea. Not blowing the idea apart, as I still think it is a great one, but I do see that the PTB have even started to come down on those who choose to use the wind as their freedom option.

Hi East Asian here having worked in HK and South East Asia alot, I think a couple a white guys on some Indonesian island you're going to have to deal with security issues.

Unless you have a trusted community there, I'd avoid Indonesia. If it's not the locals that want to rob you, it will be the government itself.

Of course looking Asian/javanese lowers risks, and speaking the the language Bahasa Indonesia lowers risks more...

East Taiwan would be safer, but you risk the Chinese Communist party rolling in someday.

East Malaysia is safe. Sabah, and has live volcano. Bribing is common but they mostly leave you alone.

I personally have some bias against Philippines..but my exposure has mainly been big cities and doing business there... Cities are shit holley and government and people in them corrupt.

Since you work in HK I assume you have some coin saved up... Why not just south NZ?

I mean BTC clearly is the arc when the money flood comes.

But it has to be important to have some sort of circular economy around you that accepts btc right? Because if not you're left having to go BTC -> fiat you are still trapped in this system they're building.

BTC + Jacks' new bluetooth app seem like the antidote to this problem.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-19T03:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worse-than-cbdc-just-approved-outside-of-constitution.31340/post-356577>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=356564)

I don’t get it . . .
Every transaction you have ever made with a credit or debit card (or check) was trackable.
They know where you were, when and what you purchased.
How is this any different?
So what if I use a Walmart digital coin to shop at Walmart or an Amazon coin at Amazon.
We were all enslaved years ago.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-07-19T17:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worse-than-cbdc-just-approved-outside-of-constitution.31340/post-356609>

> They know where you were, when and what you purchased.

You know how Blackrock is able to tell companies how woke they must be and regulate their carbon emissions simply by being the largest shareholder?

By allowing private entities to issue currency (direct violation of the constitution) they can now do those types of things to the individual.

Even with the loss of privacy via cards, I don't think they quite have that level of control built in yet. But I think it's coming with this.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-19T21:06:06+0300

Went to use it at a store and it was rejected. Called the bank, had to replace it. (I always carry 3x cards).
They can cut you off any time they want.

What do you think a store “gift card” is? A form of money issued by a company.
To buy/use a Walmart coin/token you will have to give them USD or another token/coin . . . What is the difference?

# Thread: Sue's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sues-optimal-journal.31342/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SueLeBlanc — 2025-07-20T00:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sues-optimal-journal.31342/post-356623>

Just discovered Dr. Kruse and his work a couple of months ago. Very excited about the journey.
My mother and grandmother both Sicilian. My father Swedish/English. Grandmother had no health issues. Choked on a grapefruit at about age 60. My mother passed at age 83 with lung cancer and never smoked. Dad did smoke and passed at 83 for Alzheimer's.

I'm age 64. Raised in Boston area. Lived there till age 26. Then 6 years in Atlanta area. Back to Boston for 2 year internship. Moved to Arizona for 10 years. 1998 experienced my first emergency c-section. 2nd in 2000. This is where the fun begins. Also diagnosed with vitiligo and Hashimoto's.
2005 moved to Colorado. Majority of the time over 7,000ft. 2015 had to stop working as a chiropractor due to partial paralysis from Lyme and mold exposure. Moved to a new build but was clueless about the Airforce Academy a few miles away. Also very close to I-25. Have been here for 8 years and heavily treated for Lyme and mold. Mold counts went down but still not knowing from day to day if I can drive due to severe lightheadedness. Vitiligo has worsened over the years. I am able to walk 4 miles most days.
Since we found Dr. Kruse, we have taken drastic steps to change our lives and get on his Rx. We have been staying at our rural camp for the last 5 weeks in order to get the sunrise and about 5 hours of sun a day. We are 60 miles away from the nearest military base. We have measured near zero EMF (except when we turn on Starlink). Next step is to ck the low frequency EMF and turn everything off at night. We are at 5800 feet now and I have gotten rid of the CPAP and oxygen.
For the last 6 months I have been dealing with severe back and leg pain that wakes me up at night. Just got an MRI and it looks like severe Deg. disc disease. L5/s1. It has not been reviewed by an MD yet. It's a lot worse at night. I continue to walk most days anyway. I would like to schedule a consult soon. We will be upgrading to family membership this weekend. My husband is already gold.

# Thread: Looking for a punch in the mouth for my son... (Jess's Optimal journal)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Here to learn (Jess) — 2025-07-20T05:02:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-356640>

Alright, I'm new here and finding my way around...
I am a 47 year old solo mum from New Zealand.
I have been all in on Jack's work for the last couple of years. I must've listened to hundreds of hours of podcasts (some of them 10 x before I can feel I have got my head halfway around what he is saying) Learning as much as I can and plan to spend the rest of my life doing so! I believe there is nothing more worthwhile to be learning!

I upped my membership to Gold recently, despite really not being able to afford it as I am starting from scratch financially here, having lost my life's savings learning some hard lessons while tryna get us out of NZ during Convid.

I am here right now due to my sense of urgency around needing to do everything possible to help my son.

My son: Born in 2008. Breech, C-section, jaundiced. Super clingy baby-I had to carry him everywhere as I couldn't put him down or he would cry. Never slept well, would wake up screaming from every nap. Diagnosed with (ALL) Leukaemia at age 1.5. Went through 3+ years of chemo, high-dose steroids, antibiotics, countless anaesthetics/lumbar punctures, x-rays etc etc....

...He is now 17 and living a completely counter-circadian lifestyle-much to my dismay-and despite my best efforts to convey to him the risk he is taking. He just rolls his eyes at me and says his lifestyle is normal and I am off the deep end crazy for making such a big deal about it.
He is not dumb, he is actually a smart kid...but neurodivergent to some degree and has the autistic trait of PDA-the demand avoidance/ oppositional defiance thing...So that, coupled with him being the age he is, I currently can't get him to do anything at all unless he sees the value in it/feels he has chosen it for himself....hence why I am here hoping Uncle Jack can provide him with a come to Jesus moment, a punch in the mouth as he says!....I really need to get some buy-in from him in terms of his health. I need to somehow make more real to him the consequences of his lifestyle choices. Because he sure as hell isn't gonna listen to me.

He does school online so he is on a screen during the day, and he is a gamer so he is on screen at night too. I enforce 3 nights a week off from the gaming and he gets off at 10.30pm on school nights but god knows what time he gets off on the weekends. I am in bed by 8.30 so I really don't know, but I can tell you it is 1.30 Sunday afternoon and he hasn't gotten up yet which is normal for weekends.

There is no way in hell I could get him outside to see the sunrise with me....I try to get him to spend some time outside but he will sit for 5 minutes outside (wearing a freaking hoodie) He is ocd about dirt so he doesn't like his feet touching the ground....He doesn't like the sun. If he gets hot he gets a headache which lasts the rest of the day so he avoids the sun. He doesn't like the beach. He is the whitest half-Samoan you will ever see. He doesn't do any exercise so he is skinny, has no strength or muscle mass and he only wants to eat carbs. I am very focused on eating a local, seasonal diet, so at least while he is still living with me I can have some control over what food I have here for him to eat. I can now get him to eat red meat once a week, eggs once a week, chicken once a week and fish a couple of times a week-and this is massive progress which I am really happy with!...left to his own devices he would only eat bread, chips and pizza.

It is a form of torture for me to see him this way. He just has no enthusiasm for anything apart from gaming. I believe he has burnt out his dopamine system-amongst other things I am sure, and he just has no spark of life, but will say he is fine and it is normal. It breaks my heart.

People suggest things like turning his power off, cutting his internet, or kicking him out etc....but I believe it is better to continue trying to get some buy-in from him in terms of his health....I am very aware he will move out at some point relatively soon anyway and then I will have lost my opportunity to positively impact him in any way.

I am hatching a plan to try to get him to agree to come away with me for a month (Ideally to El Salvador...but probably more realistically to Samoa as it is half the cost from NZ.) ...Samoa is latitude 15ish South...which is similar to El Salvador to the North anyway so I hope it will be good enough. We are 40 something South latitude here so it has got to be better than that.
With the caveat that he will see the sunrise every day for a month, spend more time outside during the day-even in the shade, and have no screen at night....I am going for a melanin renovation, circadian realignment, hard screen detox thing.....IF I can SOMEHOW get him to agree to this plan....I just hope and fucking pray that he will feel better ENOUGH from doing this, that he will see the value in it and will want to continue with at least some of it moving forwards in his life.

I am gonna have to slave for the next 4 months to save to pay for this but I am willing to do whatever it takes at this point. I just need to get him to agree to it.
I cannot just stand by and watch him heading for a slow-motion car crash of a bad health outcome after fighting to keep him alive all this time....Especially not now I am very aware that it was my life/light choices that caused him to be born with such a high rate of heteroplasmy. (A decade of nighttime shift work and an ex-vegan), his Dad was also a nighttime shift worker.

I freaking WISH I could afford a consult so Uncle Jack could speak to him directly for half an hour but I just can't afford it....Maybe could afford 5 minutes. If only there was a 5 minute option!

### Here to learn (Jess) — 2025-07-20T07:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-356647>

> [Quintavious the 3rd said:](/goto/post?id=356643)
>
> If a child doesn't listen to his own mother, then he sure as hell won't listen to some random, shirtless guy on the internet more than halfway across the world named Jack Kruse who ain't his daddy, uncle, your husband or boyfriend.
>
>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/)
>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/)

He has heard it from me a million times.
Also I am clearly not a Neurosurgeon or anything like that. I am learning as much as I can but I have no professional clinical experience.... I am just a parent here doing everything I can trying to get help for her kid.

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-07-20T09:47:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-356648>

(edit - posted before reading links above!)
I know it's hard when you realise that your actions have harmed your children and you want to fix it, but i can honestly say that your fears about his health will push him away and he will go in the opposite direction just because he can. 17 and a gamer is hard. My son is now 22, was a hardened gamer very late nights, at least his job at the time had him outside during the day, but he struggled because he was going to bed late and having to get up at 8 in the morning, and basically work was boring and couldn't wait to get home and back to the pc.

For a child any attention is good attention in an odd way so your nagging and fussing is feeding some part of him with a conflicting part that shouts in his head, for you to just shut up and leave him alone. I know it's difficult but could I gently suggest you back off and just don't say anything, no rolling of eyes, pointed words, slammed doors, banged cups nothing passive agressive !! hahah really hard coming from someone who was excellent at that for years!!

He may say why aren't you nagging me anymore, and you just say he is old enough now to start being responsible for his body and health, and up to him, (AND STOP THERE) no you will learn, nag nag. And let him realise. It's never too late he has years to work out that all that shit is harming him, but he has to do it on his own terms for himself, not for you.

We had the advantage my son loved to play the guitar and piano so that go him away from tech for a bit.
Our son now gets up early (not quite sunrise) but wthin an hour which is vast improvement, he bought his own blue block glasses, he turns his machine off at 10 now and is in bed and asleep pretty quick. He recently swopped all of his evening shifts for morning ones. He has started asking questions a couple of years ago (because we didn't do any covid nonsense) , and whilst all his friends think we are bat shit crazy, he is realising that we have done soo much research into this and are healthy compared to everyone elses parents and people generally our age, so he can see the connection. I know hear him telling his friends about the dangers of blue light etc, so there is hope.
17 is barely adult and front rational brain is nowhere near developed to really appreciate your new found wisdom, unfortunately. Sending you lots of calming thoughts.
(edit - ok so he is still a minor, still in your house, pull the plug. ) sending you more courage!
Good luck.

### Jessnet3 — 2025-07-21T03:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-356706>

> [Here to learn (Jess) said:](/goto/post?id=356640)
>
> Alright, I'm new here and finding my way around...
> I am a 47 year old solo mum from New Zealand.
> I have been all in on Jack's work for the last couple of years. I must've listened to hundreds of hours of podcasts (some of them 10 x before I can feel I have got my head halfway around what he is saying) Learning as much as I can and plan to spend the rest of my life doing so! I believe there is nothing more worthwhile to be learning!
>
> I upped my membership to Gold recently, despite really not being able to afford it as I am starting from scratch financially here, having lost my life's savings learning some hard lessons while tryna get us out of NZ during Convid.
>
> I am here right now due to my sense of urgency around needing to do everything possible to help my son.
>
> My son: Born in 2008. Breech, C-section, jaundiced. Super clingy baby-I had to carry him everywhere as I couldn't put him down or he would cry. Never slept well, would wake up screaming from every nap. Diagnosed with (ALL) Leukaemia at age 1.5. Went through 3+ years of chemo, high-dose steroids, antibiotics, countless anaesthetics/lumbar punctures, x-rays etc etc....
>
> ...He is now 17 and living a completely counter-circadian lifestyle-much to my dismay-and despite my best efforts to convey to him the risk he is taking. He just rolls his eyes at me and says his lifestyle is normal and I am off the deep end crazy for making such a big deal about it.
> He is not dumb, he is actually a smart kid...but neurodivergent to some degree and has the autistic trait of PDA-the demand avoidance/ oppositional defiance thing...So that, coupled with him being the age he is, I currently can't get him to do anything at all unless he sees the value in it/feels he has chosen it for himself....hence why I am here hoping Uncle Jack can provide him with a come to Jesus moment, a punch in the mouth as he says!....I really need to get some buy-in from him in terms of his health. I need to somehow make more real to him the consequences of his lifestyle choices. Because he sure as hell isn't gonna listen to me.
>
> He does school online so he is on a screen during the day, and he is a gamer so he is on screen at night too. I enforce 3 nights a week off from the gaming and he gets off at 10.30pm on school nights but god knows what time he gets off on the weekends. I am in bed by 8.30 so I really don't know, but I can tell you it is 1.30 Sunday afternoon and he hasn't gotten up yet which is normal for weekends.
>
> There is no way in hell I could get him outside to see the sunrise with me....I try to get him to spend some time outside but he will sit for 5 minutes outside (wearing a freaking hoodie) He is ocd about dirt so he doesn't like his feet touching the ground....He doesn't like the sun. If he gets hot he gets a headache which lasts the rest of the day so he avoids the sun. He doesn't like the beach. He is the whitest half-Samoan you will ever see. He doesn't do any exercise so he is skinny, has no strength or muscle mass and he only wants to eat carbs. I am very focused on eating a local, seasonal diet, so at least while he is still living with me I can have some control over what food I have here for him to eat. I can now get him to eat red meat once a week, eggs once a week, chicken once a week and fish a couple of times a week-and this is massive progress which I am really happy with!...left to his own devices he would only eat bread, chips and pizza.
>
> It is a form of torture for me to see him this way. He just has no enthusiasm for anything apart from gaming. I believe he has burnt out his dopamine system-amongst other things I am sure, and he just has no spark of life, but will say he is fine and it is normal. It breaks my heart.
>
> People suggest things like turning his power off, cutting his internet, or kicking him out etc....but I believe it is better to continue trying to get some buy-in from him in terms of his health....I am very aware he will move out at some point relatively soon anyway and then I will have lost my opportunity to positively impact him in any way.
>
> I am hatching a plan to try to get him to agree to come away with me for a month (Ideally to El Salvador...but probably more realistically to Samoa as it is half the cost from NZ.) ...Samoa is latitude 15ish South...which is similar to El Salvador to the North anyway so I hope it will be good enough. We are 40 something South latitude here so it has got to be better than that.
> With the caveat that he will see the sunrise every day for a month, spend more time outside during the day-even in the shade, and have no screen at night....I am going for a melanin renovation, circadian realignment, hard screen detox thing.....IF I can SOMEHOW get him to agree to this plan....I just hope and fucking pray that he will feel better ENOUGH from doing this, that he will see the value in it and will want to continue with at least some of it moving forwards in his life.
>
> I am gonna have to slave for the next 4 months to save to pay for this but I am willing to do whatever it takes at this point. I just need to get him to agree to it.
> I cannot just stand by and watch him heading for a slow-motion car crash of a bad health outcome after fighting to keep him alive all this time....Especially not now I am very aware that it was my life/light choices that caused him to be born with such a high rate of heteroplasmy. (A decade of nighttime shift work and an ex-vegan), his Dad was also a nighttime shift worker.
>
> I freaking WISH I could afford a consult so Uncle Jack could speak to him directly for half an hour but I just can't afford it....Maybe could afford 5 minutes. If only there was a 5 minute option!

He’s still a minor - you set the rules. If he must be on a computer for school, have him do it outdoors on a daylight computer. Pull the plug on the gaming or at the very least put more stringent parameters around it. Don’t expect others (like Jack Kruse) to do what you must do yourself.

### Jessnet3 — 2025-07-30T17:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-357204>

> I was hoping hearing it from someone like Jack who has a wealth of knowledge and experience in the consequences of bad light choices, could make it more real to him. That's all.

You are, though. You’re expecting someone else to convince him of the importance of these choices when you are his parent. Why does he not trust your guidance here? You said he is a gamer and plays well into the night, which is made permissible under your roof. Setting more stringent parameters is important to his health and is your duty. If he leaves, he will learn the hard way - but sometimes that is exactly what people need to change. “Tough love” is difficult, but it’s an ultimate love - it’s harder than just bending to what our children immediately desire.

### Jessnet3 — 2025-07-31T06:17:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-357260>

I do understand that you’ve been through a long road with his health, and I have compassion for you doing this alone. That said, I’ve seen firsthand in my own family how softening too much for kids in that state, especially smart, oppositional ones, can backfire long-term. My brother was similar: on the spectrum, resistant to authority, gamer. My parents bent over backwards, sacrificing a lot with limited means to make life easy on him and avoid conflict, fearing that the alternative would be worse. But when he finally had to face the world on his own, he was completely unequipped. No resilience. No structure. He still struggles immensely today at nearly 40 years of age.

For your son, setting boundaries maybe looks like setting screen curfews on the weekends too, and pairing that with a new obligation such as a part-time job, community volunteering, music lessons - anything that demands daylight exposure and a bit of responsibility. Have him listen to a podcast or two with Jack in the car to plant the seed. But ultimately, you are the most important guide in his life. You can emphasize that these changes are part of an ongoing two-way dialogue you wish to have with him on his health.

### MTA321 — 2025-07-31T15:48:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-357276>

Think of it this way... by doing nothing you are still complicit in making his health outcomes worse. Set boundaries and stick to them. Start with blue blocking glasses at night for gaming. fairly simple rules, if he doesn't wear them he doesn't play. Assuming you take care of all his financial needs, he will soon realize that sticking to your rules is a better alternative than leaving the house and not being able to afford food much less time to play video games; so don't let that fear override your instincts.

Not sure how those claims can be made based on my quick skim of the actual study.
Some quick notes
1\. In vitro only (i.e. cell dish) not people
2\. Cells were GFP tagged spike RBD frags (not exposed to virus or vax).
3\. Numbers refer to percentage of GFP + cells left afterr transfection (not evidence of actual spike degradtion)
4\. Paper states its unknown whether allicin affects transcriptiopn or translation & where full length spike is affected at all.
5\. primary human splenic fibroblasts are stromal cells not immune cells.
6\. Unknown how they can draw the IVM conclusion...
I would say the majority of above is marketing hyperbole..

How would you even maintain the 50uM concentration based on the oral dose? 24hr exposure bath that lacked appropriate controls IMO

# Thread: JVs Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John Vieceli — 2025-07-20T19:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-356683>

John Vieceli, recently turned 62.

**Grandmother:** Died of a heart attack. I think she was in her early 60s. Overweight but not obese. Active. Polish decent. Loved gardening, composting, making fermented foods.

**Mother:** Died of lung cancer at 50. Smoker since teens. Discovered the tumor late which had metastasized to her brain. Doctors gave her 6 months but she lasted 5 more years.

**JV early years:** According to the baby book my mother kept nothing was unusual at birth other than I was breach. Growing up I was very active. Outside all the time playing sports. I developed asthma in middle school. Attacks would only come around fall every year. We didn't have money so when the attacks came I just laid down at home and didn't move to conserve energy. As I grew older, they attacks were not as bad, and then, when I went away to college they disappeared. In high school. I also start wearing glasses during this period.

**20s-40s:** Get a degree in graphic design. Still active mostly playing baseball and softball. No major injuries or sickness. Had allergies to pollen mostly. Computers become heavily used in graphic design and I become an early adopter. Days are long, sometimes 15 hour days working under blue light in front of a computer. In my 40s I partner with two friends and create a branding agency whose cliental is athletes, sports organizations and musicians. Still working late hours but now stress has doubled.

**Early 50s:** Realizing that I was killing myself, I break off and start my own business where I can pick and choose clients as well as how much I want to work. Up until this point, I haven't had any major injuries or illnesses. May have had the flu but I rarely got sick. No flu shots or vaccines. I avoid taking anything even aspirin. Diet has always been meat, fish, veggies. I get married and move from Chicago suburbs to Park City, Utah. I become super active, mountain biking, snow boarding, hiking. Have two kids and decide to move back to Illinois. First instance of glaucoma shows up. Pressure in one eye is slightly elevated. We would watch this for the next 10 years. Before we move I develop this rash behind my knees and a little rash on the elbow crease in my left arm. I think it's due to moving (dust) and stress of moving back. Rashes disappear when we get back to IL.

**Mid-50s:** I start doing Crossfit. Blood work starts showing some numbers that are out of range. Vitamin D low (taken in Jan though) C-reactive protein is high, LDL and HDL is high, TSH is high, TPO high, Testosterone low. Hypothyroidism runs in the family on my grandfathers side. Going through a difficult divorce during this period. Glaucoma still rising. All during this period I am feeling strong and vibrant.

**60s:** Glaucoma gets to 24. Doc recommends a laser surgery to open duct. We do it but doesn't work. Puts me on eye drops. I read the side affects and reluctantly start taking them. 6 months later new check up shows pressure down to 23. Vieceli stumbles on to Jack's 2017 Vermont Youtube video. I watch as mush jack as I can get. Convinced I can cure this based on Chantel's father's results, I stop all drops and never miss a sunrise rise or sunset, use blue light glasses after sunset and eat more fish. Next check up, doctor raves about efficacy of medication, LOL. Pressure now down to 11. I join Optimal Health.

**8 months ago:** Jack, I had a 30-minute 1-on-1 with you back in early Jan. Seeing the success I had with the glaucoma I wanted to attack this thyroid issue (and talk a little bitcoin ) The protocol you suggested was to get the EMR-tek panel and use it until spring, then get as much sun as I could and test my Vitamin D to get a baseline before anything else. Is this a good time to get Vitamin D tested or wait until mid-August? Is there a specific Vitamin D panel I need? I was hoping to schedule another 1-on-1 with you after the test to discuss next steps.

**2 weeks ago**: Had a rough period of work. Not terribly long hours but stressful. Sitting and standing at desk working. I developed some stiffness in my lower back. Also noticed a small rough patch right side of neck right above hairline. Went to the chiro but tight back has persisted. Last week it was hard to squat down and pick something up. Super tight in my pelvis and goin area. Can't put on my socks or shoes because I can't cross my legs. If I lay on the floor and try to lift my leg I can barely get it 6 inches off the ground. Getting into the car, I need to lift my leg to get in. After a 5-minute ride in the car, when I get out it takes about 2 -3 seconds to stand up straight. Hamstrings are super tight especially right at the glutes. After the couple of seconds everything is normal. Walking no pain. Walking up stairs though isn't painful just no power. Joints are sore. Started in my hips but couple of days ago it's in my shoulders too. Uncomfortable to raise my arms over my head. Feels like I did a heavy workout the day before. Some fatigue. Was concerned so went to a see a doc. All he did was refer me to a back and pelvis specialist and prescribed Naproxen and Cyclobenzaprine. Not taking them.

Jack wasn't sure what my next steps should be? I definitely would like to do another 1-on-1 with you just wasn't sure if I needed to do anything else (labs) before. Thanks for reading my journal!

### John Vieceli — 2025-08-14T18:50:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-358235>

On July 31st, I went to see a neurologist at Northwestern. He did a series of tests physical tests, ie pushing against this force, dull and sharp needles on areas to see if I could feel the difference and a tuning fork to determine when the vibrations stopped. Everything was fine except my my power. I arm and leg muscles are weak. He order quite a list of labs. Here is the list of labs which have come back as normal.

Aldolase, CK, Hmg-Coa Reductase Antibody, Serum, Myomarker 3 Plus Profile, Myositis Specific 11 Antibody Panel, TSH, Acetylcholine Recep Binding, Acetylcholine Recep Blocking, Acetylcholine Recep Modulat, Hemoglobin A1c, Vitamin B12/Folate Panel, HIV Antigen/Antibody,Reflex Confirmation, Anca Screen With Mpo/Pr3, Reflex Anca Titer (CDH/DCH/KH/VWH/NWR), Ganglioside Gm1 Antibodies IgG/IgM, Celiac Disease Panel, Axonal Neuropathy, Autoimmune/Paraneoplastic Evaluation, Serum, Rheumatoid Factor, Protein, Total, Albumin

And here is the tests that came back out of range...

**Immunofixation, Serum (Nmh/Lfh)**
IgG = 1,840 Range = 610-1,616 mg/dL
Lambda = 403 Range = 90-210 mg/dL
Kappa/Lambda Ratio = 0.6. Range=1.4-2.7 Ratio

**Protein Electrophoresis, Serum**
Alpha 2 Protein = 1,840 Normal Range = 610-1,616 mg/dL
Lambda = 403 Normal Range = 90-210 mg/dL
Kappa/Lambda Ratio = 0.6 403 Normal Range = 1.4-2.7 Ratio

**CBC With Differential**
Neutrophils = 79 Normal Range: 34.0 - 73.0%
Lymphocytes = 10.2 Normal Range: 15.0 - 50.0%
Absolute Lymphocytes = 0.8 Normal Range: 1.0 - 4.0 10ˆ3/µL

**Ana, Reflex To Autoimmune Disease Panel**
Anti-Nuclear Antibody Value: Positive Normal value: Negative
ANA PATTERN Value: Speckled Normal value: (none)

**Sedimentation Rate = 99**
Normal range: 3 - 10 mm/Hour

**C-Reactive Protein = 49.9**
Normal range: 0.0 - 10.0 mg/L

**Protein Electrophoresis Path Review, Serum**
Value: An accentuated alpha 2 band observed. A restricted band seen in the gamma region.
Immunofixation, Path Review Serum (NMH)

On Tuesday I was referred to a Hematologist at the Cancer center. He ordered more blood work and I am going a 24 hr urine collection. In terms of my condition I would say nothing drastic has changed but each day I feel that I have a diminished range of motion. More to come.

### John Vieceli — 2025-08-25T17:38:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-358712>

Update: The urine test and other tests came back and the docs characterized them as unremarkable although they are still detecting abnormal protein in the blood. The Hematologist and Neurologists agree better defining this on an EMG is the critical test to determine if this is related to the IgG Lambda or something else entirely.

Last Friday I had an EMG test. Everything was normal. Docs still can't figure out what's going on. Next up is a MRI Brain with/without contrast and Cervical Spine with/without contrast.

### John Vieceli — 2025-08-28T20:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-358866>

Doctors are now advising a bone marrow and muscle biopsy. Even though the EMG was normal they still believe it amyloidosis. Docs still want the MRIs just the biopsies first.

# Thread: Mike T/s Optimal Health Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-t-s-optimal-health-journal.31352/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### michaeltatarjr — 2025-07-21T20:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-t-s-optimal-health-journal.31352/post-356749>

Hey all. Here's my optimal journal in outline format...

About me:
47 yrs old
Learned about Jack and his 'tribe' about a year ago.
Recently bumped up to a gold membership. I'm deliberately keeping a somewhat low profile, as I 'read the room' learn, and observe for a bit.
Started with the benefits of sunlight and cold plunges.
Took to his info voraciously as I had 'cured' myself from disease states 10 years ago, and had an 'idea' that what he was saying was possible.
1\. The disease state: Unknown name. More like a set of symptoms I just called "The fuzzies"
Symptoms (restless leg, numbness in right leg. Involuntary muscle movements in right hand, etc. )
3 doctors , an MRI and many many kinds of tests later...
No fixes from standardized healthcare. "We don't know..."
A personal trainer I knew had mentioned something about a great applied kinesiologist. "If it's structural he'll fix it"
He did fix it. Was better in about 2 months
Part of the fix was light therapy, as well as stretching, chiropractic manipulation, etc.

Four goals while I'm here:
Learn as much as I can
Get to be as 'superhuman' as possible (essentially optimizing my health for jiu jitsu and in general...)
Help son as much as I can
Has moderate dyslexia and dysgraphia
Has alpha-gal
Help my wife as much as I can
Has Hashimotos
More or less in remission

Me:
Currently trying to be 'superhuman'
Want to radically improve my cardio for brazilian jiu jitsu.
Hoping to push cold plunges colder and longer. Typically plunge 2-3 days a week. Two short 5-10 mins and one long.
Can do a 48 minute cold plunge in 48 degree water, totally submerged up to my neck.
Lately been doing the ice pack method outside. Pack my torso in 10-50 lbs of ice for 30-60 minutes. I could probably go longer but I get bored.
Looking to steepen the gradient here.
Get lots of sun!
See the sun come up, almost without fail. Sunrise is TINA in my household.
Out there when it's cloudy
Out unless it's raining really hard
5+ hours a day on the weekend
Bought a convertible - Top is down coming and going to work unless it's raining or below 25 degrees.
Ran a grounding mat to our patio (which is raised) so I can ground, drink coffee, and watch the sun come up. We do our morning bible study there and is a great way to start the day.
Bought a magnetico
More broadly, I experiment each month with different things
Need to bring blood testing/number tracking into the mix.
Currently working on getting our home to be a haven from nnEMFs. It's a big job and I just started!

Wife
During my wife's pregnancy with our youngest, she was diagnosed with Hashimoto's.
Did not tolerate the levothyroxin, etc. so had to do a lot of emergency studying to try to help her.
Speculated that there was a better way, as many others seems to just 'live with it' without much incident.
It's more or less in remission, though we've not tested in quite awhile.
How we 'cured'
diet changes really helped (No nightshades, no dairy)
Plenty of sleep/revamping circadian rhythms (indirectly)
And some over the counter supplements (Thyrothrophin and Drenamin, both from Standard Process)
Sunlight is TINA! She is out there with me in the morning every morning.
The last time she tested, her T antibodies tests were all just a little above normal, down from 100x too high. The doctor asked what she had done. She told the Dr. Dr was was silent a moment and said "I don't know why any of that is helping you. Keep it up."
She works from home in front of a window
We get nearly an hour of morning sun each day.
She also gets 30 minutes outside everyday around solar noon. It seems the 'blast' of UV is really good for her.
On the weekends we get more sun as well.
Often 90 minutes or more of evening sun and grounding
This fall we plan on getting her a convertible as well.

Son
9 yr old
Alpha-gal
Dyslexia
Joins us in the morning
Revamping his diet. He's really into carbs, but has been a good sport about changing things.
He even did a cold plunge with me recently!

Three questions
1\. Has anyone on here increased there cardio with cold plunging, etc.?
2\. How do I help my son with his dyslexia and alpha gal?
3\. Any insight on further reversing her Hashimoto's?

I've taken this amazing client through my consciousness experiential Soul Rebirth program, and I'm gifting him a consult with Dr. Kruse this Friday, July 25th.

He had with our time together one of the top three experiences of his entire life - after marrying his beloved and the birth of his twin boys. He experienced the presence of all of his ancestors, including the ones who had ended their life early. Pure peace was his intention, and it was exactly what he received. He now has an anchor point, and is excited for what's to come next.

Including selling the home, travelling to El Salvador for a few months and figuring things out. And we want to optimize everything else.

He is likely coming for one more multi day session with his wife in late August early September, where we'll take them on a breath journey and beyond, and have access to multiple modalities including live blood analysis, EBOO, Hyperbaric O2 chambers, etc. along with consciousness expansion and being outside of the city and on the earth grounding and early morning sunlight. # Thread: Stephanie's Health Pilgrimage
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-22T22:29:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-356796>

I am a nature lover, classical pianist, and nature/landscape photographer trying to heal from pharmaceutical damage primarily due to a benzodiazepine taken in my 20s -- but I also took long-term antibiotics, Accutane, and birth control pills in my early teens and 20s that I think primed me to be easily damaged later -- and worsened my central nervous system. I did not take any covid vaccines, as I knew well enough about big pharma by that time. I am vehemently anti drug in general: never drank or smoked in my whole life: a teetotaler, had straight edge stickers on my car as a teen.

I have forsworn virtually all pharma for 10 years, except for Advil occasionally, but I don't even like taking that. However, menstrual cycle pain and side effects can be so horrible that I take a few Advil during that time. My goal is to eradicate Advil as well. I cannot tolerate supplements, though, or herbal medications so it's difficult to find an alternative. The benzodiazepines caused sensitivities to everything (except Advil) and now I can basically only use food and nature as medication.

Every day, I ground, swim in the ocean, walk the beaches and forests on my wonderful peninsula -- so I am in nature often. I eat a paleo based diet with the highest quality food and water that I can find. I also don't currently work as I am focusing on my health. I have perfect lab tests and have seen every specialist imaginable, even Environmental Illness doctors to see what I can do to get better. Though, I realized most of them are incompetent quacks so I gave up on doctors awhile ago -- but will occasionally use my conventional doctor for lab tests that I want done.

My goal is to eliminate any need for technology-based or government reliant living, maybe even live in an off grid, eco-village at some point. Modern living really isn't for me: I had to learn the hard way how destructive it is. I want to heal my central nervous system damage and am willing to do whatever it takes to accomplish this. I can't go to El Salvadore at the moment because my chemical sensitivities are so severe that I can't sit next to a bunch of people wearing cologne and perfume in the airports or on an airplane. All traveling must be done in my car and I am not even sure I could handle any hotels or air bnbs right now. That is the main thing I suffer from, though the sensitivities are getting progressively less severe as I heal, but I still have to avoid lots of places, people, and things because of the flares I get when trapped around chemicals.

### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-26T23:19:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357015>

When I was at my worst with my health about 6 years ago, I couldn't work and neither could my husband because he had to help me. We moved to a rental house on the marshes of Cape Cod where we had a perfect view of sunset. I saw one of the most beautiful wintery, snowy, fiery sunsets of my life there, and I took photos of it, shared it on my Instagram (CapeCodGirl4Life). The post became a big hit which I wasn't expecting. I then became addicted to photographing sunset photos, and I went to different beaches and locations every night, unfailingly, hunting the perfect sunset shot. I would post them on Instagram and my account began to grow fast, and I was featured on many hubs and magazines, etc. Photography then became a big part of my life where I would shoot moon scenes, full moon especially -- chase northern lights, shoot comets, etc.

The main point to sharing all of this is to remind myself that I saw exponential and rapid healing during this time period. I knew that getting out in nature and creating art was helping me, and I thought perhaps there was also something about watching sunset that was healing me. Now I know for sure, after studying Jack's work, that this is surely what happened. I remember that watching the northern lights also healed me almost instantaneously. I felt the power from that, especially last October when the lights were so strong that they happened from sunset until morning hours, visible to the naked eye.

Jack is right: Nature's lights are where it's at. All of health is connected to this.

### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-26T23:24:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357016>

I took this photo in June of the Hydrangeas and the best sunset Ive seen thusfar this Summer. This is how the healing happens: these colors, hues, frequencies -- and of course, the beauty.

I did not take any covid vaccines, as I knew well enough about big pharma by that time. I am vehemently anti drug in general: never drank or smoked in my whole life: a teetotaler, had straight edge stickers on my car as a teen.
>
> I have forsworn virtually all pharma for 10 years, except for Advil occasionally, but I don't even like taking that. However, menstrual cycle pain and side effects can be so horrible that I take a few Advil during that time. My goal is to eradicate Advil as well. I cannot tolerate supplements, though, or herbal medications so it's difficult to find an alternative. The benzodiazepines caused sensitivities to everything (except Advil) and now I can basically only use food and nature as medication.
>
> Every day, I ground, swim in the ocean, walk the beaches and forests on my wonderful peninsula -- so I am in nature often. I eat a paleo based diet with the highest quality food and water that I can find. I also don't currently work as I am focusing on my health. I have perfect lab tests and have seen every specialist imaginable, even Environmental Illness doctors to see what I can do to get better. Though, I realized most of them are incompetent quacks so I gave up on doctors awhile ago -- but will occasionally use my conventional doctor for lab tests that I want done.
>

Modern living really isn't for me: I had to learn the hard way how destructive it is. I want to heal my central nervous system damage and am willing to do whatever it takes to accomplish this. That is the main thing I suffer from, though the sensitivities are getting progressively less severe as I heal, but I still have to avoid lots of places, people, and things because of the flares I get when trapped around chemicals.

Hi Stephanie,

I react to everything also but not in an anaphylaxis way so it's not life threatening.

I find it interesting that you had long term antibiotics and accutane, this is also a part of my history. I have issues with water too as you mentioned on the webinar, I drink purified water only now or I react.

The chemical sensitivities are an absolute pain in the ass, I have to dodge then like the plague but can only imagine how hard it would be if they caused life threatening reactions.

Did your issues start suddenly overnight or was it a gradual decline?

Have you had your environment checked for EMF?

### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-27T16:33:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357033>

> [jaedyn said:](/goto/post?id=357030)
>
> Hi Stephanie,
>
> I react to everything also but not in an anaphylaxis way so it's not life threatening.
>
> I find it interesting that you had long term antibiotics and accutane, this is also a part of my history. I have issues with water too as you mentioned on the webinar, I drink purified water only now or I react.
>
> The chemical sensitivities are an absolute pain in the ass, I have to dodge then like the plague but can only imagine how hard it would be if they caused life threatening reactions.
>
> Did your issues start suddenly overnight or was it a gradual decline?
>
> Have you had your environment checked for EMF?

I wouldn't call mine anaphylaxis either, but depending on how severe the reaction is, it can last minutes, hours, days, or months -- and it will increase my sensitivities during that time to things. My symptoms are similar to many others who have central nervous system damage from pharma.

When I react to something, I immediately feel this weakness spread through my body, then I get neuropathy, sometimes I can feel as if I've been stung by a jellyfish, and then have neurological heaviness in muscles/neuro muscular problems -- trouble swallowing and other things. I could also describe the reactions as severe fibromyalgia symptoms, too.

What are your reactions like and how has it prevented you from doing things in life?

I'm getting better gradually but I had a major set back from taking a methylated B12 vitamin (204,000 times the daily value of B12), that was way too strong and it caused damage to me all over again and I'm trying to heal from that. That happened last year.

About the water, I drink mountain valley and Fiji. I'm scared of trying to deterium depleted water because anytime I try something strange or different, I don't know what the reaction is going to be. I even reacted to apple cider vinegar severely last year, which also makes me sound like Jordan Peterson because he has a reaction to that too and everyone called him crazy for it. He talks about it on Joe Rogan. I attribute his reaction to his benzo injury.

So I think because you and I took Accutane, we were screwed for life. It then primed us for being sensitive to everything. You were taking 200,000 IUs of synthetic vitamin A. I took it for months and then I developed scabs on my arms so I had to stop taking it immediately. I also took doxycycline for about 6 months to clear my skin, too. But then I started getting strange side effects from that, so had to stop it. What happened with you?

I would say my chemical sensitivities began pretty rapidly while taking benzodiazepines in my early 20s. How about you?

I have checked my environment with a safe and sound pro. Even though I live .2 miles from a cell phone tower, that wasn't the worst culprit. It was my AC which I have put faraday material over now, my cell phone, and wi Fi which I got rid of and now am hard wired in my apartment. Also my husband's gaming headphones with blue tooth were bad, too. I've turned off all signals in here.

Crazy what the CNS can do to someone, I've never been the same since it ramped up, much like yourself. Mine can also last upto weeks but unsure if it's ever persisted into months, at least obviously anyways as it's just a sea of symptoms most of the time.

I had to restrict down to eating only meat for years as everything else would cause a reaction. I can only eat a handful of foods now aswell, it triggers intense brain fog, fatigue, dizziness, mood issues and the list goes on and on. Unable to socialise anymore, smells instantly trigger symptoms and I lose all brain energy to socialise very quickly. Pretty much just in survival mode every day just to maintain full time work with no physical/neuro energy left over for anything else. My optimal Journal has all of this much more detailed if it's of interest.

Did the b12 trigger the CNS again and made you more reactive to everything in general or?

I react to apple cider vinegar aswell, not crazy at all. I'd imagine the DDW would be a very high quality product and would be less likely to cause issues,but I understand the body when so reactive doesn't care about that and can reject it. If you could get your hands on a single bottle and microdose it, it may give a lot of benefit as Jack mentioned on the webinar. DDW is on my to do list.

I took accutane for around 3 months I think and noticed depressive symptoms but this was when I was 22yrs old, after reading all the terrible things about it i stopped. I was on minocylcine for years due to cystic acne and had many rounds of antibiotics for tonsillitis and other things. I also had venlafaxine after I became suddenly ill and that absolutely ruined my libido and caused quite severe numbness and anhedonia. The minocycline/accutane possibly could be implicated in some of the intracranial issues I've had.

Mine started over night with all other symptoms, literally went out with friends one night and woke up to never be the same, that was 6 years ago.

Your AC had wifi capabilities? The SS pro only checks for RF, what about magnetic and electric fields? These can cause hypersensitivity aswell.

Jack said I had a brownout that night it all went haywire and to test a better environment out in far north Queensland for 4 weeks.
