I definitely want to read your journal. Where do I find it? I am new here -- do you have a link to it? I am relieved to know someone else here who has MCS. I am actually worried that my condition sounds pretty peculiar and crazy to others: I usually experience lots of gaslighting over it, even from family and especially from doctors. People think that MCS is a psychological condition when it most DEFINITELY isnt.

Let me know if you do decide to try the DDW. Is there a specific brand that you want to get? I would be curious to see if you feel anything after drinking it, or even if it helps.

To answer your question about the B12: I was doing amazingly well and exponentionally getting better after tapering off the benzodiazepine. But then I took the B12 a few years later, which was stupid of me because it was on a list of things not to take if you are benzo injured (I just thought maybe I was recovered enough to take supplements, but I guess not). I instantly felt as if I had brain damage and I only took a small piece of the B12: I had nightmarish symptoms, relentlessly: both physical and mental; even muscle convulsive episodes in the middle of the night for months. Then it turned into full blown life threatening MCS (I had chemical sensitivities before but not to a debilitating degree). It has been over a year and I am still damaged but am much better through sun, grounding, nature, diet, and avoiding ALL chemical exposures. I had to stop teaching piano because I could smell everyone that walked into my apartment. Now I am able to tolerate being around lots people as long as we are outside though. I can hang out with someone indoors as long as they have absolutely no fragrances on them or in their clothes.

So I cant take supplements, like, EVER AGAIN, ha. If I can't get my medicine from food and nature, then I don't even know what to say. I am now actually sensitive to radiation and can't get Xrays anymore. I tried to get bite wing xrays but even just a couple caused neuropathy and full body weakness. I also had a severe reaction to an MRI so cant go near those either. Also, when I go to the dentist, I can't do novacaine at all or even carbocaine, so all dental procedures must be done with no numbing whatsoever. Fortunately I found a dentist who is skilled at doing this and I felt no pain when he reconstructed a tooth for me a few months ago.

Yes my AC had wifi and though it claimed you could turn it off, the signal was still there bouncing around my entire house. It had the highest EMF readings of everything and I couldn't believe that I had used it for so long before I realized what was happening after using the safe and sound pro.

Oh, by the way, you should always keep activated charcoal on hand. That has saved my life on so many occasions, especially if I have consumed something that disagrees with me. It instantly adsorbs toxins in the body. It would actually stop episodic reactions in many cases. I think everyone needs to keep this on hand: I take it in my purse with me everywhere. Even if you had food poisoning, it can save your life.

I think if you click on my profile you should be able to find my posts, or go into the optimal Journal section on the main thread page and scroll through until my journal shows up.

I'm sorry to hear you've been gaslighted over your MCS, it's happened to me aswell but luckily only a few people here and there. It's hard if your family and friends do it, but if your doctors are you definitely haven't found the right one yet. Definitely not crazy, there's heaps of chronically ill people that suffer from it!

I think lite water is meant to be pretty decent! I'm able to drink Evian water, it's not super low deuterium but still less than average bottled water if that's worth trying?

Honestly I think our bodies are on such high alert knowing the damage that's been caused previously by pharma, that anything that resembles it even slightly will trigger all the crazy symptoms. There could have been an excipient in the benzo you had originally that was also in the b12 and triggered you. I've had to try a stupid amount of compounding pharmacies and excipients to be able to take cortisone and LDN. It's like my body even knows where it's been made and will react to the same compounding pharmacy.

Also very sensitive to EMF, if I go near a live switchboard, wifi, microwave or anything that is radiating I can instantly feel it, pretty insane. I feel zonked after any xrays ir MRIs aswell. EMF opens up the BBB, I guess we used to have more of a buffer but now our bodies are on high alert/don't have enough redox to stop the symptoms arising.

Thankyou I've tried it a few times but also react to it so I've had to ditch it. Do you notice any tingles or any odd symptoms when playing piano? I'm noticing it more now with electronic devices or phones.

### VictorianGirl — 2025-08-06T20:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357691>

I just read through your journal. I posted a reply about something because it stood out and was wondering why you are consuming any steroids at all with the condition that we have. I am also surprised your taking LDN because even that is no good for people with MCS. When was the last time you were pharmaceutical free?

Yeah I don't feel good around ANY manmade technology which is why I don't feel safe getting on an airplane and going to El Salvador (thats the only thing holding me back: I would have to rent an RV and drive down). I think the radiation would cause horrible side effects. When I went on a vacation to NH, I also reacted to driving up the White Mountains: I was having symptoms like I couldn't breathe and muscle weakness as I drove higher in elevation. That's another reason I am scared of airplanes. I haven't been on one in 6 years.

I have had trouble holding my cell phone and typing on my laptop: it seems to rev me up. Reading on screens also causes blepharitis of the eyes and eyelid swelling. I basically can't go near anything manmade anymore.

As for the digital piano: I don't particularly feel anything when playing that and my EMF reader wasnt really high around it, but it still could produce dirty electricity. I live an apartment though and wouldn't be able to play an acoustic instrument because of how loud they are. I don't really have a choice but to use a digital piano for piano lessons and practicing.

# Thread: What are geopathic stress lines? Hartmann grid, curry lines
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-geopathic-stress-lines-hartmann-grid-curry-lines.31364/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-07-23T00:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-geopathic-stress-lines-hartmann-grid-curry-lines.31364/post-356801>

I see online some information about "geopathic stress lines," and claims that when such lines intersect in the Earth, it's bad for you.

I was trying to dig deeper to see what scientific underpinning it has, but I couldn't find any details about whether they're talking about magnetic anomalies, AC fields, or otherwise.

Is this a real phenomenon? If so, what exactly are they talking about?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Nathan Siles Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-siles-optimal-journal.31365/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nate Siles — 2025-07-23T00:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-siles-optimal-journal.31365/post-356804>

Hi Jack,
You've said clearly over the past year that glycolytic Warburg metabolism in the fovea and testes is a protective design—normal, not pathological. These immune privileged areas use glucose metabolism to shield against magnetically controlled free radicals and to avoid unnecessary proliferation.

But in my case, those exact areas—despite that built-in protection—were the first to go. Retinoblastoma at 8 months old, with tumors destroying my central vision almost immediately after detection. Then in 2015, non-seminoma testicular cancer—something far from conventional.

Lately I’ve been trying to zero in on what the environmental trigger or combination of factors might have been—especially in a 1992 world. What specific mitochondrial toxin or photonic mismatch could have bypassed that design in the retina? And again, in 2015, what got through in the testes?

It keeps raising the same question: if these areas are metabolically protected by design, why were they the first to break down in me? Is it that their sensitivity makes them early detectors of severe environmental mismatch? That would make sense in my case—but most others don’t follow that pattern.

So what made me different? What allowed those defenses to fail? I’m trying to understand what this says about the relationship between light, magnetism, mitochondrial control, and tissue-specific vulnerability.

Would love to hear your thoughts.
Nathan


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Non Mesh Hernia Surgery at Shouldice Hospital
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FDDio — 2025-07-23T01:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/post-356806>

For anyone considering Hernia Surgery, I highly recommend looking into Shouldice Hernia Hospital in Toronto. They are globally recognized for their non-mesh hernia surgeries, meaning no foreign mesh is placed in your body. The hospital itself is more like a resort, offering nice amenities and good food for a pseudo-centralized facility over a typical 3 nt stay. The main building, originally a historic estate residence converted into a private hospital in 1953, benefits from being an older structure with minimal EMF levels (less than 1 mG) in the semi-private (2-person) patient rooms. Additionally, there were no ceiling lights in the rooms, just a single fluorescent light bulb behind the bed that was easily dealt with.

The expansive grounds provide lush grass for earthing and numerous benches to enjoy the morning sunrise and evening sunset, though unfortunately, patients aren't allowed outside on the day of surgery (tho I tried!).

Surprisingly, while at the hospital, I had many insightful conversations with fellow patients who were well-versed in the drawbacks of centralized medicine and government policies. It was incredibly rewarding, especially after being thanked for introducing some to Uncle Jack. It was refreshing to find like-minded individuals and engage in meaningful discussions, which can be rare in our everyday lives.

### PIAZ — 2025-07-23T01:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/post-356809>

Thank you for that. My husband will soon have hernia surgery, and Doc is saying: "mesh." Being easier for him to "put his head in the sand" I am researching, but not many options. I will send him the direction of Shouldice. Very timely, and appreciated.

### FDDio — 2025-07-23T01:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/post-356810>

You are most welcome. Everyone I spoke to at the Shouldice was very pleased they went there and the common saying was _"if you have to get a Hernia surgery, this is the place to be."_ However, keep in mind they have fabulous results (and have the data to back it) but that is because they only take fit individuals. If anyone is overweight, they tell you how many pounds you need to loose before they will operate on you. I was one of the fortunate ones that did not have to lose weight, but I had other (past) issues that they were extremely cautious about (since they don't have regular hospital emerge room facilities on the premise).

They website is pretty comprehensive but if you/your husband have any questions after browsing through it, feel free to ask away. I am 6 days post op and not any more pain or discomfort, tho I'm still avoiding lifting anything that's somewhat heavy.

# Thread: Should I just give up?

### SpiderMonkeyXYZ — 2025-07-23T02:26:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-just-give-up.31367/post-356812>

Hello all. Love all the learning from Jack’s posts, but the more I do the more I feel like its already too late for me. I got LASIK more than 20 years ago, and I get the round 1 and 2 of the moderna jab. Im in the sun as much as possible, I limit screen time and wear blue light blocking sunglasses in the evening. But Jack’s messaging leaves me to think it’s too late. The damage is already done despite feeling fine, I’m 42 fit and healthy feeling but I just dont know anymore.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-23T02:29:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-just-give-up.31367/post-356813>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-just-give-up.31367/post-356814>

But Jack’s messaging leaves me to think it’s too late. The damage is already done despite feeling fine, I’m 42 fit and healthy feeling but I just dont know anymore.

### digitald3 — 2025-07-23T15:42:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-just-give-up.31367/post-356846>

Don't EVER EVER give up. That is a loser's game. Don't play into it. And if there is that nasty little voice in your head that is saying bad things to you, tell it go way and shut up. Tune it out. That's the devil talkin' and he's a bad dude. You're here. You're reading and doing and learning. DO NOT EVER GIVE UP. Every day is an opportunity to do something new. I had Lasik too (no side effects...that I know of ...but they could still be there....) Just like my favorite Christmas movie, Santa Claus is Coming to Town...."put one foot in front of the other...."  Each day is a blank page to start afresh. Embrace it.

# Thread: I'm trying to understand this consciousness/MKULTRA thing

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356848>

Thought of like this in essence, maybe not in technicality:

_There is a "quota" of day and night that need to be had. Unless you separate them with sun, they blend into each other.
If losing consciousness naturally is the increase of DC currents, then you would have more DC during the day, and less during the night with artificial lighting = Dampening your literal consciousness_

**_If the lack of light(sun) increases these DC currents and eventually starts sleep, then what happens if we can see without the sun. So LED lights keep you awake/stimulated, but like a zombie, and its actually completely dark. And there is no difference between night and day anymore. And the average person actually has near 24 hours of darkness per day?_**

So we are talking to literal-fucking-sleepwalking people. House windows alter the suns spectra to LED-like, same with car windows, same with chemtrails/shit in the sky, same with sunscreen and sunglasses. What else?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-23T16:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356852>

Without full spectrum solar power, your MT and myelin atrophy, and you de-evolve. Simple.

### JenniferM — 2025-07-23T18:35:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356860>

Not to nose in or anything but my brother sleep walks and talks. When in jail or prison, the cell mates they pair up with always ask for a different cell...LOL...He's been known to walk to the gas station, sometimes in clothes and sometimes in underwear, grab something and talk a minute leaving with the item and as usual they'd hit me up to pay. He's done it his whole life.

### riksa — 2025-07-23T19:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356861>

### JenniferM — 2025-07-23T19:43:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356863>

# Thread: Dr Kruse and Gym Bros
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-and-gym-bros.31370/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Base_Strength — 2025-07-23T19:32:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-and-gym-bros.31370/post-356862>

Dr Kruse, I’ve been following your posts slamming “gym bros” and I totally get where you’re coming from...indoor artificial environments, junk light, hypertrophy obsession, endless output, Bluetooth tech, and total disconnect from circadian biology.

That said, as a barbell strength coach, I’m curious where you’d place the kind of training I do with clients. It’s definitely not bodybuilding or aesthetics-based. I don’t coach powerlifters either.

My focus is helping regular people get stronger using foundational human movement patterns: squat, deadlift, over head press, and bench press performed with low reps, long rest, and a linear progression model. Getting stronger each week as measured by the weight on the barbell.

No music. No tech. No chronic cardio. Just short, focused strength training in a garage with the door open. I go out of the garage in between sets and get direct sunlight and ground and encourage my clients to do the same.

I would argue that strength is the final way we interact with our environment. My goal is to help people double their strength in 3 months and triple it in 6, which I’ve seen drastically improve quality of life. Get in the sun and be strong!

So I’m wondering if this approach still falls into the “gym bro” category from your perspective? What I see in the gym are people who are chronic exercisers but not strength training.

Appreciate everything you share. It’s shaped how I think about health in a big way.

### DLO — 2025-07-24T08:13:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-and-gym-bros.31370/post-356889>

How many times have you heard Jack mention movenat?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### bjones824 — 2025-07-23T19:46:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-test-results.31371/post-356865>

just did a full blood panel for first time in many years...wondering if anyone has advice about elevated levels of TGAB? Apparently you should have no Thyroglobulin Antibodies (TgAb) but I have a level of 117 IU/ML...reading the research is a bit concerning as this could indicate anything from thyroid cancer to various autoimmune diseases...I am still waiting for the other results, but one encouraging result is that my Thyroid Peroxidase Antibodies (TPO) is low at only 2.
I eat a seemingly perfect diet, get plenty of the sun exposure, exercise, etc...so not sure what I may be doing wrong or if I should be concerned. Any insights are most welcomed.

# Thread: Clotilde's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal.31372/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clotilde — 2025-07-23T20:40:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal.31372/post-356868>

I off work on summer holidays until September with working plans to possibly not return in September. I was in Mexico on the 7th of July for 10 days. I spent my time mostly outdoors at the beach. I watched the sunrise at the ocean front on what looked like to me to be volcanic rocks. I definitely feel the positive responses within my body now that I am back. For example on my way to Mexico, during the flight as usual the nnEMF sensitivity effected my bladder (trips to the toilet every 30 minutes) feeling pressure and urgency to use the washroom. Also, my left leg felt like it had a pinch nerve. Sometime after I landed and I was outdoors it all stopped. I also had a lot of sunshine to recover from jet lag. I was overjoyed on my trip back home not to experiences those symptoms on the plain. My sensitive around WIFi also seems to have decreased.
As part of my health journey I would like to visit El Salvador. Possibly at the end of August. How is the weather at that time? I will post some questions on the All About El Salvador as I would need an AirB&B to stay at.

# Thread: New Update for Members of This Website.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-update-for-members-of-this-website.31374/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-24T02:36:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-update-for-members-of-this-website.31374/post-356879>

Next Q&A, you're here about a new option we are going to start offering people for jumping without wings when you come to ES to visit, to figure out what you want to do, or rent for a period of time to fit your plan.

The first option is a turnkey option for those who want a luxury transition. I have purchased a luxury apartment that includes cleaning, security, and the option for car/truck rental. It will also include the opportunity to spend one night a month having dinner with me, and we can discuss whatever you'd like. The Apartment is ideal for three single people. It also includes parking, if needed. You can also rent the entire space for a family of four. It will include many extras, like use at my Country Club as well. The price per month will be disclosed to those who put their name on a waiting list. We had already offered this to people after the last Q&A, and three single females jumped at the initial promotion. They got a price no one else will get. We wanted to see how people would react, and so far, the interest is brisk.

As a result, I am offering another opportunity. I purchased a very large property in Costa Del Sol for those who are in a wheelchair for disability reasons. The compound will accommodate 16-18 people. This area is all private and will have private access. It will have maid service, car service if needed, and a large saltwater pool. You will have access to the estuary in this area if you are handicapped, and you will have access to the open ocean and beach. The property is currently being renovated and offers many options for mitos. I anticipate it will be a mitocompound, and I am building it to foster a sense of community for my tribe as they come to see how ES works for them.

The third option will not be available until January 2028. I have purchased three apartments in a new luxury apartment community that will be open for visitors to explore and determine how ES will work for their lives. The apartments are one-bedroom, two-bedroom, and three-bedroom. The amenities will also be superior.

The fourth option is to partner with me on a real estate deal in Atami to build a mito community. You will yolo in and I will provide land and plans and builders. You will share in profits.

If you are interested, you must be a verified member of this website. If you are not, do not even bother asking because the answer is NO.

I will start a waitlist, and we will proceed from there. August Q&A will have more on this. If you post in this thread bullshit, I will delete it. This is for serious people.

### Lisaillames — 2025-07-29T20:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-update-for-members-of-this-website.31374/post-357143>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=356879)
>
> Next Q&A, you're here about a new option we are going to start offering people for jumping without wings when you come to ES to visit, to figure out what you want to do, or rent for a period of time to fit your plan.
>
> The first option is a turnkey option for those who want a luxury transition. I have purchased a luxury apartment that includes cleaning, security, and the option for car/truck rental. It will also include the opportunity to spend one night a month having dinner with me, and we can discuss whatever you'd like. The Apartment is ideal for three single people. It also includes parking, if needed. You can also rent the entire space for a family of four. It will include many extras, like use at my Country Club as well. The price per month will be disclosed to those who put their name on a waiting list. We had already offered this to people after the last Q&A, and three single females jumped at the initial promotion. They got a price no one else will get. We wanted to see how people would react, and so far, the interest is brisk.
>
> As a result, I am offering another opportunity. I purchased a very large property in Costa Del Sol for those who are in a wheelchair for disability reasons. The compound will accommodate 16-18 people. This area is all private and will have private access. It will have maid service, car service if needed, and a large saltwater pool. You will have access to the estuary in this area if you are handicapped, and you will have access to the open ocean and beach. The property is currently being renovated and offers many options for mitos. I anticipate it will be a mitocompound, and I am building it to foster a sense of community for my tribe as they come to see how ES works for them.
>
> The third option will not be available until January 2028. I have purchased three apartments in a new luxury apartment community that will be open for visitors to explore and determine how ES will work for their lives. The apartments are one-bedroom, two-bedroom, and three-bedroom. The amenities will also be superior.
>
> The fourth option is to partner with me on a real estate deal in Atami to build a mito community. You will yolo in and I will provide land and plans and builders. You will share in profits.
>
> If you are interested, you must be a verified member of this website. If you are not, do not even bother asking because the answer is NO.
>
> I will start a waitlist, and we will proceed from there. August Q&A will have more on this. If you post in this thread bullshit, I will delete it. This is for serious people.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-07-24T03:59:31+0300

### [HQ | https://www.facebook.com/reel/1077348947878866 | Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/share/r/1BCLk4YFKH/)

https://www.facebook.com/reel/1077348947878866

 www.facebook.com

The end is nigh. They are coming for us

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-07-24T12:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tighter-controls-in-the-uk-coming-january-1st-2026.31375/post-356890>

From 1 January 2026, crypto service providers and crypto exchanges will begin collecting data on users' activities and reporting transaction details to HMRC for UK residents. Users will be required to provide service providers with the information requested.

This also

From 1st January 2026, crypto service providers (aka platforms) will have to collect personal data on their users or face fines of up to £300 per user. They will then have to report each transaction for that user to HMRC. This will mean HMRC will know who you are and what your crypto transactions are

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-07-24T12:19:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tighter-controls-in-the-uk-coming-january-1st-2026.31375/post-356891>

# Thread: Salt of the Earth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### roxhupp — 2025-07-24T23:32:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/post-356918>

Hello fellow warriors,
My name is Roxanne , I'm 54 and seeking enlightenment & truth.

I'm new here since June and catching up listening to all Q&A from december 2024 to now. Been following Uncle Jack for 1-2 years and applying some knowledge but knew something was missing so I'm jumping in with both feet.

Was procrastinating on starting my journal because there seems to be so much I want to share but decided to just take one bite at a time and get this moving forward. Also because I don't like sitting at computer (even with my blue blockers), I'm blessed to have time freedom to just be and prefer to be outside most of the day and have yet to get a daylight computer.

I joined for health freedom but have been going down rabbit hole of BTC so I can keep enjoying that time freedom. And of course this has sidetracked me as I want to move faster than I probably should at this point. Until I get trustable guidance on best way to rollover our 401K, ROTH & traditional IRA for which I'm still too young to start withdrawing. After hearing July Q&A , I think I should wait regarding Unchained capitol but want to make changes ASAP. Most are with Fidelity and wondering if I trade into BTC EFT is better then nothing at this point until I can figure out how to self store. Having a hard time swallowing this orange pill but determined to get it down.

I'm a brain warrior seeking optimal brain function and right now I have a difficult time comprehending and understanding things. Many of the blogs are like a 747 over my head and although it is hard to comprehend, what I like is the overall simplicity of sunrise to sunset. That resonates with me and I can easily align with it & follow my gut instinct on these truths. Even as a young teenager coming from a wealthy family, I'd offer to paint the fence outside just to be outside taking in the sun but needed to be active/productive and not just sit there and tan while my father hires contractors to do work I volunteered to do.

Growing up with money made me see things in a different light. I did not take it for granted and actually didn't value money because I could see that money didn't make my family happy. I love to give back and be of service and that's what makes my heart happy.

Like Uncle Jack mentioned about the wisdom learnt from talking with old age folks' is to live life with no regrets. And I have always been intuitive in that sense.

Grateful for all that has flown in my direction but never saved money, just lived in the moments and traveled the world with our kids, all over Europe & Japan. We pride ourselves with no debts except for mortgage , never lived beyond our means and only traveled if we could pay cash up front.

Now seeing how the economy is doing what we have is minimal and not up to my standards for being able to give back like I've always thought I would. Poor planning on my part but now awake to better potential future.

Recently an empty nester with no regrets as we have set up and paid for our children education and they can now be independant. Starting their lives with no debts. Now it's MY TIME to THRIVE.

For me it's always been about making people feel good and giving back. I've been told I was like the SALT OF THE EARTH because I love to season people with knowledge that I've learnt especially when it came to finding truth about health. (I will post my health history and journey in another optimal journal thread.)

I want to keep seasoning people but have lost my drive, passion & purpose. Still have a passion for traveling but now need to find a purpose aligned with that so I can reclaim my zest for life and keep serving.

Jesus sprinkles us like salt into the world **to prevent the erosion of kingdom values, to prevent the decay of society, to fight against the tide of inhumanity and oppression**. This is my mission.

Grateful to have found like minded savages that follow similar journeys. Like musketeers we must be ONE for ALL & ALL for ONE. xoxo

### PIAZ — 2025-07-25T17:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/post-356956>

Hi again, Roxanne - Not sure how to privately message you... so here goes - take my opinions with a grain of salt  as I'm new (and yet wildly enthusiastic) to BTC. Vast galaxies to learn here. I used Unchained, on-boarding was idiot proof, and yet, just before you leap I would check out the other BTC company recommended often here (Swan) as I believe their fees are lower across the board. SWAN will offer a free consultation (I used Zoom) and uses Equity Trust as their IRA custodian, and Unchained uses Fortis bank. Just wire the money to them; that part couldn't be easier.... ESPECIALLY now, I would proceed slowly and methodically, take a few days to learn the lingo and basics. I was gung-ho to get it done but that was before, as you say, July 19. There was a special 1/2 price, one week only fee for onboarding with Unchained when I involved myself. Many, many folks here are considerably more knowledgable than I am, still in BTC Kindergarten myself. Tell me how to talk with you in person and I'll call you if you want. Not sure why I'm PIAZ here, it's PIA, haha

# Thread: Washington DC - Mitochondriac safe haven coming available
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/washington-dc-mitochondriac-safe-haven-coming-available.31380/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Katie P — 2025-07-25T01:26:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/washington-dc-mitochondriac-safe-haven-coming-available.31380/post-356927>

Not necessarily looking to meet up, but just wanted to share a rare gem for anyone still weathering the DC swamp. I'm moving out at the end of the summer, and my apartment which is hands down one of the best in the city, is about to hit the rental market.

This place isn’t for everyone. But if you're a DC mitochondriac, this might just be your sanctuary.

Two years ago, I toured over 30 spots before finding this one. I’m no professional building biologist, but I brought my Trifield to every showing, and this was the cleanest I found. Bonus: it’s the _only_ apartment I know of where you can sunbathe _completely naked_ year-round in the middle of Capitol Hill and not get arrested. Yes, really, naked sun bathing in our nation's capital, pretty empowering. This apartment is unique and has a privacy fenced patio and no other building can see it where it is located while the sun makes it apearance all day long.

The insulation? Trash. Which is perfect if you're into cold and heat shock proteins. I run almost entirely without heat or AC. Not just because I’m cheap, but because I know the benefits.

It’s also an older building, so the windows have original glass. There is no modern UV-filtered nonsense distorting your light environment. Natural light floods in.

If you're stuck and trying to do good work here in DC and want a space that supports high dopamine and real biology, this is your spot. Would love to see it go to someone who gets it.

I'm not the landlord, I don't own the property, and my lease is up, so not trying to get out of any contract, but I was just thinking it would be a shame if I couldn't pass this haven onto someone else trying to heal who is otherwise stuck in a toxic environment.

# Thread: What cars are healthiest?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyhoffman — 2025-07-26T19:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/post-357009>

Hey everybody,

I’m currently in the market for a new car, and I’m overwhelmed by the options available. There are so many new cars on the market, each with its own unique features and technology. Hybrid engines, full electrical vehicles, new interior display monitors, Wi-Fi/Bluetooth technology and so on.

I’m particularly interested in the possibility of living out of my car for short periods. I’ve considered a 2025 Toyota 4Runner or a 2025 Land Cruiser, both of which have hybrid engines. However, I’m concerned about the potential emissions from hybrids, especially when the car is running. I’m worried that I might be exposed to unwanted EMF while I’m sleeping in the car at night / while driving during the day.

Ideally, I would just be sleeping in the car at night with it off, but I also work and would be driving in the vehicle throughout the day. I’m not sure if I should be concerned about the EMFs from hybrids, or if there’s another way to minimize my exposure.

I’ve read some preliminary research that suggests hybrids don’t produce significantly more EMFs than standard cars, but I’m not sure if that’s accurate. I’m also aware that even non-hybrid cars have a significant EMF problem, so I’m not sure if I’m overthinking it.

I’m open to any advice or guidance. I’m willing to use an EMF meter to test the cars I’m considering, but I need some help understanding what to look for. I also have the EMFSAFE home testing kit, so I’m not afraid to use that as well.

Thanks.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-07-26T23:43:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/post-357018>

New vehicles are terrible, avoid them like the plague. Vehicles act like a faraday cage, so avoidance of EMR inside the vehicle is extremely important.

Older gasoline vehicles without bluetooth, wifi, rf cruise control, gps, keyless entry, etc. are a much better and much cheaper option.

The best are old diesel vehicles with mechanical injection pumps, although I realize hardly anyone wants to play that game.
You can remove the alternator and charge the vehicles battery(s) with a solar panel(s) on the roof. As diesel engines don't rely on spark ignition and older models had mechanical injection pumps, the majority of the electrical load is from starting the engine, and once started, the engine needs no electrical power to keep it running.

Probably not the answer you're looking for, but hope this helps!

### DLO — 2025-07-27T18:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/post-357036>

 rumble.com

### Mr.AJ — 2026-01-06T18:59:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/post-364863>

I've been looking a bit as well. I think you have 2 possible options:

1) buy an old car without all of the electronics (Prob <2010?). Issues will be finding parts.

2) buy a new car, print out the schematics and find a very good mechanic who can subtract the superfluous. Automotive makers are making it tougher to do this, but you'd be surprised how crafty some mechanics are.

### JenniferM — 2026-01-12T21:09:52+0200

### TB14 — 2026-01-12T22:48:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/post-365223>

I drive a 2023 Honda Civic and the measurements I've taken with the NFA1000 (magnetic and electric fields from 5 Hz to 1 MHz) don't seem much elevated from baseline. RF measurements from 200 MHz to 8GHz on Safe & Sound Pro II also didn't really seem to register anything. These findings surprised me, though, because it's a very tech-heavy vehicle, and I imagine there are likely some negative effects of that. I worry especially about the electronic pressure detection components in the seat for the seatbelt detector, which I assume are constantly on and in very close proximity to your body. Those unfortunately go way back, tho - might have to get something pretty old or have them removed to avoid it.

>
> OK . . . If you have a RF meter . . . Leave your phone at home . . .
> Tell us what make, model, year your car is . . . Go for a drive . . . What is the RF reading?
> Include where you live . . . AND where you are driving.

I’m in El Salvador for the winter, but once we return home I’ll share exact measurements. That said, we chose a 2024 Land Rover Defender because it consistently gave us the best readings with our meter - both while driving and when parked. Nothing else we tested came close. Readings were comparable to the ambient levels we see in our 12+ year old Tahoe and even our 1982 Defender. You can shut off Bluetooth and Wi-Fi and it is truly “off,” unlike many other makes (Ford, for example) that continue to emit RF in the background regardless of settings. Like every other modern vehicle it does have eSIM, but unlike other makes it is not continuously broadcasting.

We intentionally selected a baseline configuration without many bells and whistles. Defenders aren’t especially tech-heavy to begin with, but because of that choice, I can’t speak to higher-trim or more optioned models.

### TB14 — 2026-01-14T00:24:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/post-365285>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=365254)
>
> What is the actual RF reading in the car with no cell phone on?
> The conversation is about how RF toxic various cars are.
> People wan to know what are low EMF cars.

No additional RF above ambient baseline detectable by Safe & Sound Pro II (200 MHz - 8 GHz). Or by Safe & Sound mmWave (~20 GHz - 40 GHz). Could be some above 40 GHz - I believe Honda says the car is equipped to emit that - but I have no way of knowing currently since I haven't found a meter that measures that. Have considered building my own but haven't gotten around to seriously looking into it yet.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-01-14T20:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-cars-are-healthiest.31383/post-365327>

Has anyone been able to effectively shield a newer vehicle where you can not turn off wifi/bluetooth radios?

I got one of my cars down to 00 uW/m2 by disabling wifi, but BT you can not turn off. Other car is newer and worse.

### aunty shelly — 2025-07-27T02:09:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-resonance-magnetic-analyzer.31384/post-357025>

The human body is the aggregates of a large number of cells which are in continuous growth, development, differentiation, regeneration and apoptosis, and the cells constantly self-renew through its own division. 25 million cells are divided at one second in an adult’s body, and the body's blood cells constantly renew at the rate of about 100 million per minute. In the process of cell division and growth, those charged bodies of atomic nucleuses constituting atoms as the basic unit of cell and the electrons outside the nucleus are in constant high-speed movement and changing, thereby constantly emitting electromagnetic waves.

The electromagnetic wave signals emitted by the human body represent the specific state of the human body, and the emitted electromagnetic wave signals are different under the different conditions of the human body, such as health, sub-health, disease, etc. If we can determine these specific electromagnetic wave signals, we can determine the status of the body's life.

Quantum medicine considers that the most fundamental reason of falling sick is that the spin of electrons outside the atomic nucleus and the orbit change, thereby causing the change of atoms constituting a material, the change of small biomolecules, the change of big biomolecules, the change of all the cells and finally the change of organs. Because the electron is a charged body, when the spin of electrons outside the atomic nucleus and the orbit change, the electromagnetic wave emitted by the atoms will change. The energy o f the electromagnetic wave changes caused by the changes of the human body’s diseases and physical changes in the nutritional status is extremely weak and usually is only nano gauss to microgauss. The frequency and energy of the weak magnetic field of hair determined directly or by holding a sensor by hand compare with the resonance spectra of standard quantum of diseases and nutrition indicators set in the instrument after the frequency and energy are amplified by the instrument and processed by the computer, and then the corresponding quantum value being from negative to positive is output. The size of the quantum value indicates the nature and extent of the disease and the nutrition levels. Finally, the test results are resolved by clinicians. For example, cancer cells are different from normal cells, and the electromagnetic waves emitted by cancer cells are also different from the electromagnetic waves emitted by normal cells. Quantum resonance testing tumor is to send the standard wave of cancer cells to the specimen. If there are cancer cells in the human body, resonance will occur, and the instrument will detect the signal. The more the number of cancer cells is, the more intense the signal is, and the quantum value tends to the negative value. If there are no cancer cells, resonance will do not occur, and the quantum value tends to the positive value. It's similar to the principle of listening to broadcasting from the radio. There are many radio waves in the air. If you want to listen to some designated broadcasting, you can transfer the radio to the corresponding frequency, at this moment, resonance occurs, so that you can listen to this broadcasting. Quantum resonance uses this principle for testing.

This is what it says in the software, comments appreciated

# Thread: Cold Plunge/Exposure for someone of Middle Eastern Descent

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### fouadfo1 — 2025-07-28T21:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-plunge-exposure-for-someone-of-middle-eastern-descent.31391/post-357112>

Hello everyone, I needed some advice. So long story short, I was born and raised in the US. Have been living around the 42nd latitude essentially all of my life in the north east.

My parents are both of Yemeni descent. Their parents as well were both of Yemeni descent. Additionally, according to my dad, there was no race mixing for at least several generations prior to his parents as well. Generally speaking, the Yemeni people do not often race mix, even with the surrounding Arab ethnicities for reasons beyond the scope of this post. Essentially, my genetics should be fairly homogeneous.

So to my question, will someone with my genetic makeup benefit from cold plunging/cold exposure as a means to compensate for the loss of sun especially during the winter time? I recall Dr Kruse mentioning that certain people may not benefit from cold exposure because their genetics are not really wired or evolved to utilize or benefit from such practices. I understand that ideally, I should be where I belong , but I am working hard to ensure that within my 5 year plan I will be at a more agreeable latitude, but for now I have to do the best that I can given my life’s chess board.

Thank you all in advance!

# Thread: Zoom Alternative
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zoom-alternative.31392/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiohackersTO — 2025-07-29T01:40:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zoom-alternative.31392/post-357120>

I started listening to the latest Pow Wow recording.

Regarding the part about using Zoom and being listened to... have you considered using Jitsi Meet?

Does team Kruse have a private server? If so, you can run Jitsi Meet (which is open source) from it.
Jitsi does not require any download for invitees who attend meetings from a computer, though it does require a download for attending by phone. If you attend a Jitsi meeting from your computer you can just attend through your browser.

I'm not pretending any tech is bulletproof against spying, but if you use Jitsi on your own server, it at least makes team evil work a little harder to spy on you than when you use the same centralized platform the entire world uses.

# Thread: Brain Warrior
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-warrior.31393/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### roxhupp — 2025-07-29T04:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-warrior.31393/post-357121>

My optimal health journal,

Haplo I1a1

54 year old french Canadian grew up in Ottawa, Ontario latitude 45, moved to Southeastern Iowa on Mississippi River latitude 41 in 1998 on work visa to work in cardiopulmonary department.
I was always overweight yet very active. Figure skating, snowmobiling, alpin skiing in winter and swimming, camping in summer time.
Always had hard time studying in college. I did best if I could follow lecture/teaching with visual notes and had to write notes to retain information. Had swimmers hears and often difficulty comprehending what I heard but I pushed through because I had great drive to excel. Health was my passion so I became a Registered Respiratory Therapist RRT. Cross-trained into cardiology and obtained my credential as Registered Diagnostic Cardiac Sonographer in 2005. This is a success story because of my learning disability.
\-1997 had gallbladder removed. Which I now know has affected my digestion and gut health. SInce 2019 learnt of ox bile & digestive enzymes with meals to offset this.

\-2002-2004 C-sections 2 beautiful healthy baby girls now 20 & 22. Had gestational diabetes for 1st daughter but not with second. Reached an all time high weight of 285lbs with 5'2 height....

\--2005 put on SSRI for general anxiety. Then remembered advice from my elders...life is to short and kids grow up fast so I decided to stop working full time to be more present with my kids. And stopped using antidepressants. Preferred to apply principle thinking and take stressor away instead of using drugs.

\-2007 Gastric bypass laparoscopic and back at work in 7 days. I feel no pain and heal very easily. Felt like this was my only option to be fully active with my kids after years of unsuccessful yo-yo dieting. Had no regrets for 15 years until now knowing what I know. Been on B12 injetions ever since.

\-2010 Tympanoplasty of my left ear. which also had replacement of tiny bones of the middle ear (ossicles)...now I don't hear as well unless I put pressure behing ear to connect does bones. And this creates dissociation when in groups. I was once an extrovert and now have become an introvert, which is almost by choice because I have low tolerance for stupidity especially since Covid. Feel best when I'm secluded.

\-2015 diagnosed with OCD, then ADHD, then seasonal depression (which I knew deep down was BS since being canadian I love the outdoors) many drugs were tested and made me feel worse and again had deep instinct that they didn't know how to diagnose, they never even did any test... all was subjective not objective. So did some research and found Dr Daniel Amen book "Change your brain , Change your LIfe".
PS my father was on lithium diagnosed with bi-polar and I wanted to rule that out !
Maybe midlife crisis but I opted to take money out of 401K to get brain scans done intead of buying a red corvette. I became a BRAIN WARRIOR on a mission.
Findings of scans done at AMEN CLINIC in NYC. (see resting and concentration scans below). Wondering if this is the white matter Uncle Jack talks about.
\-Mild hypoperfusion of the anterior temporal lobes and right inferior orbital prefontal cortex.
\-Severely increased perfusion to the thalamus in both studies.
\-Moderate increase perfusion to basal ganglia bilaterally during concentration.
\-Mild hypoperfusion of the prefontal pole, occipital lobe and dorsal parietal cortex
\-MIld scalloping seen on both studies , more pronounced at rest.
\-Decreased internal cerebellar tracer activities seen at rest.
\-Increased activity in posterior cingulate cortex -finding correlates with superior intelligence and may protect from development of Alzheimers. ( Dr said only 10% of population have this increased white matter marking and he references ALbert Einstein as an example...LOL It did make me feel better. But explained my drive and curiosity to know and understand more even with my learning impairment and dysfunctional cognition. (Started on EPA/DHA supplements 3:2 ratio, Vit D3 with K2, SAMe, L-Tyrosine, Phosphatidyl Serine and Hyperzine A. ) and work on gut health, thyroid and lymes.

\-2016 AT the age of 45 had heart attack. 40% blockage of my LAD no stents needed. Figured it was probably due to all dental work and root canals I had done.
CAC not done to monitor so no prior to compare but had one done in 2023 and my score was 86. Hoping I'm in process of reversing damage done by working on my glucocalyx. Using Cardio Elite from Quick Silver Scientific labs.

\-2017 went back to school to study functional medicine, received my certification as traditional naturopathic doctor in 2020. I wanted to be in control of my health and my family, instead of being guided by others. During this time I found out I had hashimotos, lymes, mold toxicity, high arsenic . Never went for my board certification because I dropped out of program IIOM (International Institute of Original Medicine) never did my dissertation and wasn't aligned with what they wanted me to do as far as nutritional aspect with RBTI (Reams Biological Theory of Ionization). They follow seventh advent beliefs, I did not. I had started following Dr Georgia Edes from Ancestral Way of Eating and that resonated true to my soul. Started carnivore in 2019. Felt better doing that then all the oxylates from food, glyphosate etc.

\-2019 started focusing on my environment. Irradiate all mold in house, removed carpets, air doctor, water filtration system and now only drink distilled water structured with sea salt from France. and Use Quinton isotonic plasma water ( from my understanding it is the closest structure to amniotic fluid) at bedtime for calm parasympathetic system and use Quinton hypertonic in AM for busy days. This was part of my regiment to balance my adrenal glands since my adrenal burnout. Also had estrogen dominance so started on BHRT. I do regular coffee enemas, Sauna, EMR-TEK red light therapy, PEMF with SOTA and Silver Pulser inspired by Robert O Beckers work. ALso started having eye vision issues since doing on-line classes too much nnEMF.

\-2023 Ovarian cyst found on right Ovary. NO JABS EVER. But my husband did. I know about shedding but too late he did without my knowledge.
At removal pathology report found mucinous adenocarcinoma. I started high dose melatonin , B17, ivermectin, Transfer Factor (in PDR) . PETS scan came back clear and no other markers but it was too late they had already removed appendix during full hysterectomy, becasue they said that was usual origin of this type of cell but report was negative...High stress followed my cortisol went up, AIC went up so started working more directly on leptin /insulin resistance.
Leptin was 15.9 now 4.2,
Adiponectin was 19 now 14,
A1C was 6.1 now 5.5,
fasting glucose was 100 now 84,
hsCRP was 40 now 0.49,
cholesterol was 266 now 208,
LDL was 171 now 117,
triglyceride was 128 now 88,
HDL was 62 now 77,
APO B 120
homocysteine was 14 now 6.7,
fibrinogen still high at 441,
Vit D was 66 now 86...I'm always outdoors with good tan.
Thyroid panel TSH =2.4, FT3=2.7, TT3=97, RT3=8, FT4=1.9, TT4=6.2 TPO=0.9. on Levothyronine T4/Liothyronine T3 of 8 mcg/10 mcg
IgF1 =203
and all sex hormones within range pregnenolone 98, DHEA 106, SHBG 68 on 40mg progesterone, 0.1ml estradiol and high optimal testosterone not supplemented at this time. (Good libido)

\- 2024 Cognition started getting worse. eg...during a conversation someone mentioned and asparagus and my mind would stop to try and figure out what was an asparagus and I'd miss full conversation...this was scary that my brain could not compute an image or recognize what an asparagus was. Did some more testing and found Walsh protocol. I had copper toxicity Copper was 159 now 85, Ceruloplasmine was 39 now 21, Zinc plasma was 90 now 127, whole blood histamine was 39, so I seemed to be overmethylator probably from too many supplements. Stopped almost all supplements and retested histamine which was even lower at 36....which meant I am one of the 8% of actual over methylator. excessive activity of dopamine, norepinephrine and serotonin . therefore SAMe, st John's worth and methionine is not good fit for me. No wonder paxil, zoloft, prozac never worked well on me. Grateful I followed my gut instincts on those. SO now I started demethylating with folic acid, niacinamide, 4.5mg LDN (low dose naltraxone), N1O1, methylene blue low dose when doing IRT.
BRAIN WARRIOR BECAME A MITOCHONDRIAC

\-2025 Current issue needing iron transfusion with venofer , even while eating red meat daily and supplementing with 60mg chewable ferrosequel iron for better absorbtion due to gastric bypass. I cannot seem to find answer to=
low ferritin 4.4 now 49,
iron 17 now 87
%iron saturation was 4 ,
TIBC was 445 now 517,
Hgb 13.1 now 16.1,
Hct 45 now 51,
RBC was 5.7 now 6.3,
WBC was 7.1 now 5.4, ??? infection,
MCV 81, MCH 25 RDW 23, platelets 368
Vitamin A = 62.

I know I've left stuff out , my brain is like mush right now. Need to unplug off blue light!!!

Presently on magnesium, zinc, Creatine, hydrogen tablets , B12 injections, inositol, MCT oil , transfer factor, niacinamide, folic acid, dyhydroberberine, hemeplex iron, melatonin, CBD, MB, digestive enzymes with ox bile with meals , Serrapeptase and binders for heavy metals using CellCore a carbon technology company. And membrane mend from quicksilver scientific.
I want simple Vitality and aiming for less is more .

Have been looking at sunrise since June 2023. Have not been daily for about a year but now resuming and being consistent. Done some occasional CT but now following protocol for 1 week. After reading Épi-paleo RX book.

Started with the Quilt and reset my eating time frame . Since being carnivore I’m fully satiated and only eat once or twice a day per my instinct and often make myself eat to keep up with iron need .

I think this is a myelin issue not melanin. Again this comes from what I heard on podcast but have not read blogs associated with this. Part of my learning disability
Still trying to comprehend all the science of Fe2 vs Fe3.

I believe I need to start reading demyelination blogs.
Any feedback would be greatly appreciated. Hard to focus on where to start with 15 years of blogs.

Your Brain Warrior Roxanne

Been trying to catch up with the great information. I am often in and out of pool during the day and have done cold showers in evenings now started doing cold packs on torso a few hours before bed. I do have amber & red light bulbs throughout the house in evening & blue blocking glasses. I usually liked to read before bed but with these low light it's straining my eyes. Unfortunately this lead me to watch Tv instead for distraction and unwind my mind from overthinking. I know this isn't optimal but so far it isn't affecting my sleep. 11pm seems to be best time for me to go to bed. If I go to bed between 9-10pm , I am up multiple times and restless.

\-olive oil

# Thread: Hello, new member
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-member.31400/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lisaillames — 2025-07-29T22:10:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-member.31400/post-357152>

Hello Geeks,
I’m a non geek here to learn, make changes in my health and life and pass on the wisdom to others!
I am 60 years of age, obese, recently gained 40lbs. in less than two years and I want my mitochondria to be healthier and to live a healthier life.
symptoms: fibromyalgia, insomnia, anxiety, migraines, chronic fatigue, pain, shingles outbreaks, sciatica, stiffness in my fingers, brain fog, IBS issues, lack of appetite.
medical history:
Age 60 dx with obesity, chronic fatigue and pain. Age 51 dx IBS and acid reflux. Age 50 dx with fibromyalgia, fatty liver. Age 48 dx migraine, tinnitus, neuropathy in hands and feet, blood clot in the right atrium of the heart due to meta port, pre-diabetes. Age 47 dx breast cancer triple negative BRCA1gene. Age 45 dx interstitial cystitis. Age 30 dx with endometriosis, uterine fibrosis, asthma and allergies put on prednisone, inhalers and antibiotics for about 10 years. Age 21 in 3 car accidents within 3 month period suffered whiplash. Teenage years seasonal allergies, rosacea, acne, heavy bleeding and clots during menstruation. Also, exposed to second hand smoke.
Surgery: age 19 abortion, age 26 removed ovarian cysts, age 30 dx endometriosis, age 47 double mastectomy, age 49 bilateral oophorectomy, age 59 two brain aneurysms.
No covid vaccine
Substance use: no drugs, no alcohol use, never smoked
food allergies: shell fish, eggs
allergies to medications: NSAIDS, acetaminophen, MRI and CAT scan contras, idodine, latex, clorox, penicillin, oxycodin,
current medication: bilanta 90mg, valtrex 3000 mg, naltrexone 4.5mg, amlodipine 5 mg, methocarbamol 500mg, allergic, atorvastatin 40mg, magnesium complex, melatonin 10mg.
supplements: D3 5000mcg, k2 180mcg, zinc100mg
family history:
mother passed away age 54 from metastatic breast cancer
father passed age 29 brain hemorrhage from a car accident
family history on mother’s side: breast and ovarian cancer, diabetes, seizure, bi-polar
After reading many articles of Jack’s I am up watching the sun rise and sunsets, daily swimming in my new salt water pool, outside daily for hours, but I burn in the sun and get shingles outbreaks and migraine, I’m building up my heat tolerance, the tan stays on my body but leaves my face quickly. Wear blue blockers for electronic use, also I have limited my use on electronics, after sunset amber lights only in home. Any info greatly appreciated!!!

### JacqueAwef — 2025-07-30T17:38:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-member.31400/post-357205>

Great job getting more sunlight and it's fantastic that you did not get the covid shots.

Have you bought any electrical meters to measure for RF/microwaves, AC and magnetic fields, dirty electricity?
Minimizing EMF in your environment is extremely important, much more so than diet or supplementation.

If you still use a cellphone, you will need to reduce your use of it to very few minutes per day.
Anything that uses wifi or bluetooth has to go.

Blue blockers are a band aid for electronics use. I recommend getting a screen that doesn't emit blue light at all.
For example, the daylight computer DC-1, or anything that uses an Eink screen.

The focus needs to be on safe sunlight exposure in a low EMF environment.
Hope this helps!

### JacqueAwef — 2025-07-30T22:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-member.31400/post-357227>

Some of the most common RF meter manufacturers that I see people use are Cornet, Trifield, and Safe and Sound.
Cornet and Trifield have low sampling rates compared to Safe and Sound and miss a lot of what is actually going on.

Highly recommend the Safe and Sound Pro II as one of your first purchases. It's not cheap, but you get what you pay for. Remember that this meter will only measure radio frequency(RF)/microwaves.

You will need other meters to measure AC electric and AC magnetic fields.
Gigahertz Solutions and Alpha Labs are great manufacturers.

A lot of people will not want to deal with this stuff on their own and hire building biologists to do these measurements for them. That is always an option. I prefer DIY.

# Thread: UVB in the Shade
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-07-30T00:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357158>

**tl;dr** Has anybody else measured UVB using a spectrometer? What are your absolute irradiance numbers and comparison between in shade vs. in direct sunlight?

**Long form:**

I was curious about the irradiance of UVB from sunlight in direct sunlight vs. in the shade, so I bought a spectrometer that can measure into the UV range - Hopoocolor OHSP-350UV.

In the shade, there is:
\- IR (up to 850 nm): 1512 uW/cm2
\- UVC: 11 uW/cm2
\- UVB: 33 uW/cm2
\- UVA: 633 uW/cm2
\- blue: 1030 uW/cm2.

In direct sunlight, there is:
\- IR (up to 850 nm): 8430 uW/cm2
\- UVC: 0 uW/cm2
\- UVB: 11 uW/cm2
\- UVA: 1826 uW/cm2
\- blue: 7191 uW/cm2

Three things don't make sense to me:
1\. UVC is nonzero in the shade. Must be instrument measurement error?
2\. UVB is higher in the shade than in direct sunlight. This makes no sense. Light frequencies can only become more and more red-shifted as they bounce off things and lose energy, no?
3\. Why is the UVB level in direct sunlight so low? The UV index here was 9-10 today when I took these measurements.

I see that UV index is defined as UVI=40×Eery (when Eery is in W/m2)
I also see that [a paper](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14993940/) calculates UVB\_{280-315} = 7.55xEery.
So that comes out to UVB\_{280-315} (in uW/cm2) = 18.9xUVI.

UVI = 10 should result in 180+ uW/cm2.
11 or 33 uW/cm2 would only be explained by 0-2 UVI.

Is my instrument broken or is somehow there some geo-engineering blocking a lot of UV light? I will test again later today when it's lower UV to see what the irradiance levels are.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-30T08:07:27+0300

### Mat.I — 2025-07-30T08:09:19+0300

### Mat.I — 2025-07-30T08:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357185>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=357160)

### Mat.I — 2025-07-30T08:27:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357187>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=357185)
>
> Is it a clear day or cloudy?

Based on your UVB empirical formula 18.9 x 8 = 151.2 uW/cm2 (clear day)

2 hours post solar noon say 50% drop, still ~ 75 uW/cm2

However, thin cloud cover could drop ~ say 30 to 50% and thick due to the short wavelength would/could be around say 70% to 90% which gets close to what was measured.. re: shade vs direct difference - the sensor must be perpendicular to sun (to avoid cosine error). Most devices (including my solar meter have cosine correction), however, would be minimal for UVB given the directional nature..

### AlbertW — 2025-07-31T00:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357232>

I think the issue is likely software overcorrection for the UV range numbers.

By default, the spectrometer measures from 230nm to 850nm. I went into the software settings and changed it to measure only 230-400nm and got reasonable numbers. At 1 hour past solar noon, I read 280 uW/cm2 UVB, which is excellent. 280/18.9 = 14-15 UVI, which doesn't match with what the weather sites tell me, which is peaking at 10, but at least this value is in the right neighborhood and not off by an order of magnitude.

I asked ChatGPT to offer an explanation for this, as I know little to nothing about spectrometers. It suggested that the irradiance for 400 nm+ is so extreme in direct sunlight that UV could look almost like noise. The spectrometer integrates over some amount of time T to compute irradiance, and likely applies software corrections to account for non-UV sensors having some "light spillover" into the UV sensor buckets. Correction of such buckets when irradiance is orders of magnitude larger for the non-UV buckets could make it hard to distinguish between the "real" UV values and the spillover values. Too much noise not enough signal.

I'm glad to see high irradiance for UVB, but of course, like [@Mat.I](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26512/) and [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) mentioned elsewhere, this tells us nothing about the "quality" of these rays to ensure that they are coherent enough for our bodies to synthesize Vitamin D.

The UVC is still totally off...but at least UVB looks more real?

### AlbertW — 2025-07-31T00:10:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357233>

I also took a few measurements in the shade (concrete roof with open sides, not tree shade), and UVB generally registered 5-15% of direct sunlight.

If 15%, does that mean if I sit in the shade for 6x as much time, I could synthesize enough Vitamin D? I would think I can possibly syntehsize even more, as I'd assume in extreme sunlight, Vitamin D synthesis may slow down (non-linear)?

**Does anybody know how N minutes of I UVB irradiance compares to 5N minutes of I/5 UVB irradiance in terms of Vitamin D synthesis?**

In some light tree shade, I also measured around 10-20% of the irradiance of direct sunlight.

The sun shade number seems to agree with what's found in the literature. Here's a study of Sacramento, CA sunlight under tree shade. They measured 17%: [https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/36ec/91f47b119825069d4c7d70bd3b2f3b113b81.pdf](https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/36ec/91f47b119825069d4c7d70bd3b2f3b113b81.pdf)

Experimental evidence is presented for a new implementation of supersolidity in a driven-dissipative, non-equilibrium context realized in a photonic-crystal waveguide, demonstrating the breaking of translational symmetry with exceptionally low losses.

 www.nature.com

Most of us in this forum already understand that light isn’t just energy but also an information carrier that shapes biological structure, behavior, and coherence at the quantum level. This experiment is a powerful confirmation of that idea. It demonstrates that light can self organize into a supersolid (simultaneously ordered and fluid), which strongly supports the idea that biology is quantum mechanical at its core, and light is its master programmer.

Modern human birth practices, particularly for jaundiced children, recreate GOE-era stressors, driving an atavistic state that increases disease risk: Neonatal jaundice, exacerbated by blue light therapy and the vitamin K shot, generates ROS/UPEs akin to porphyrin-mediated stress during the GOE, impairing OXPHOS, raising heteroplasmy, and forcing a Warburg-like shift to glycolysis, as seen in hypermetabolic states (Picard et al., 2024). This lowers mtDNA redox power (ΔΨm), slowing IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz) and neural oscillations (40 Hz to 20–30 Hz), disrupting myelination (Agan et al., 2025), and causing sensory overload, mirroring autism phenotypes (1/27 births).

Reduced DC electric currents (per Becker) and photo-bioelectric power impair neural development, collagen piezoelectricity (leading to EDS), and autonomic function (POTS), while altered hemoglobin (+3 Hb, fetal Hb retention) exacerbates mtDNA hypoxia, increasing vulnerability to modern mitochondrial toxins (blue light, nnEMF, seed oils). UV light and melanin play a critical role in restoring mitochondrial function in jaundiced children, counteracting the atavistic stressors reminiscent of the GOE:

Jaundiced neonates, with impaired heme synthesis and immature oxygen sensors (neuropsin, mTOR), exhibit a disrupted TCA cycle topology, as UV-A light (380 nm) regulates POMC and melanin production to chelate toxins and support OXPHOS, reducing the Warburg-like state induced by blue light therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019; Tan et al., 2023).

This light-driven oxygen sensing, evolved post-GOE, is mirrored in COVID-19, where SARS-CoV-2 attacks hemoglobin’s porphyrin, exacerbating hypoxia and ROS, akin to jaundice (Wenzhong & Li, 2020; Al-Kindi et al., 2021). Diagnostic tools like UPE measurement, showing increased emissions from bilirubin-induced ROS (Van Wijk et al., 2008), and mtDNA oscillation analysis, revealing a drop from 100 Hz to 20–50 Hz due to desynchronized cristae (Kurz et al., 2010), can confirm mitochondrial stress, guiding interventions like UV-A exposure and melanin supplementation to mitigate autism risk and systemic effects like EDS and dysautonomia. Rh-negative babies, more prone to jaundice due to Rh incompatibility, face amplified mitochondrial stress from RhoGAM, exacerbating the GOE-like atavistic state at birth: RhoGAM’s immune activation induces low-grade inflammation, increasing ROS and mtDNA heteroplasmy, while hemolysis elevates bilirubin and free heme, generating additional ROS/UPEs under blue light therapy, further impairing OXPHOS and forcing a Warburg-like metabolic shift (Picard et al., 2014; Kruse, LinkedIn).

This synergistic stress slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizes cristae, and damages oligodendrocyte mitochondria, thinning myelin and contributing to sensory overload, autism risk, and systemic effects like EDS and dysautonomia (Agan et al., 2025). The Vitamin K shot, often mislabeled as a vaccine, introduces an additional mitochondrial stressor in jaundiced newborns, as its package insert explicitly lists jaundice as a side effect due to hemolysis, particularly in vulnerable neonates: Hemolysis from the shot increases bilirubin and free heme, generating ROS/UPEs under blue light therapy, further damaging mtDNA, raising heteroplasmy, and impairing OXPHOS, which reinforces the Warburg-like metabolic shift and slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizing cristae and contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and autism risk (Agan et al., 2025).

Archimedes' principle and maternal light environment further exacerbate mitochondrial stress in jaundiced babies, influencing birth timing and long-term health outcomes: Fatter babies, often from sunlight-deficient mothers with gestational diabetes, exhibit negative buoyancy due to increased fat mass, delaying pelvic engagement and prolonging gestation, which increases hemolysis, bilirubin levels, and jaundice risk, while maternal blue light exposure disrupts EZ water and accelerates ubiquitination rates, amplifying mtDNA stress and heteroplasmy transgenerationally via blood UPE signaling (Sohns et al., 2004; Wallace, 2010). This circadian mismatch, reflected in the mother’s SCN clock speed and impaired DHA levels, slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizes cristae, and contributes to autism, obesity, and skin cancer risks, underscoring the need for sunlight exposure to restore mitochondrial function and buoyancy dynamics, mitigating these GOE-like stressors (Oláh et al., 2013; Agan et al., 2025).

The evolutionary divergence of Rh-negative blood types, lacking the RhD antigen, reflects a trade-off that increases jaundice risk and mitochondrial stress in modern newborns, amplifying the GOE-like atavistic state: Rh- babies, more prone to hemolysis from Rh incompatibility, face heightened bilirubin and ROS/UPE production, exacerbated by RhoGAM, the Vitamin K shot, and blue light therapy, which impair OXPHOS, raise heteroplasmy, and slow IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and risks of autism, EDS, and dysautonomia (Agan et al., 2025; Flegr et al., 2015). The Rh- mutation, likely selected for pathogen resistance or hypoxic adaptation, does not involve copper-based blood, a common myth, but underscores the need for careful management of environmental stressors like blue light, advocating for sunlight exposure to mitigate these effects (Ferguson et al., 2019). The decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis is reinforced by red blood cells donating electrons to methylene blue-mediated reduction of methemoglobin compartmentalized in liposomes, enhancing the hemiiflusome’s role in mitochondrial function and oxygen delivery in jaundiced neonates:

This electron transfer, amplified by red/NIR light (660–700 nm), reduces MetHb to functional hemoglobin, stabilizing heme metabolism, restoring TCA cycle oxygen utilization, and increasing IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), countering the Warburg shift and ROS/UPE production from blue light therapy (Yang et al., 2014; Sohns et al., 2004). In Rh-negative babies, where hemolysis and RhoGAM exacerbate mitochondrial stress, this process mitigates mtDNA hypoxia, supports myelination, and boosts bioelectric currents, reducing autism, EDS, and dysautonomia risks (Agan et al., 2025). The hemiiflusome, a light-sensitive hub for proton flux and heme synthesis, evolves from GOE-era adaptations, with UPEs from MB reduction serving as a diagnostic marker to guide decentralized interventions like sunlight exposure (Ferguson et al., 2019). The confirmed existence of hemiflusomes, newly identified recycling hubs within mitochondria, and the role of thanatotranscriptomic genes enrich the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, linking light-driven bioenergetics to neonatal health:

Hemiflusomes, integrating proton flux, heme synthesis, and TCA cycle activity, suppress thanatotranscriptomic gene expression during the day to manage photonic/metabolic chaos and minimize UPE over-emission, while at night, low-level expression complements melatonin’s repair, reflecting an evolutionary adaptation to diurnal cycles (Ferreira et al., 2018; Nunnari et al., 2019). In jaundiced neonates, hemiflusome dysfunction from blue light therapy and hemolysis (e.g., RhoGAM, Vitamin K shot) impairs IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), increases ROS/UPEs, and activates thanatotranscriptomic genes, exacerbating autism and EDS risks, whereas melanin and red/NIR light (e.g., via methylene blue) restore hemiiflusome function, supporting myelination and bioelectric currents (Yang et al., 2014; Agan et al., 2025).

The origin of carbon through the rare triple-alpha process in collapsing stars, where three helium nuclei collide to form a threshold where chaos meets meaning, underpins the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, reflecting life’s asymmetric choice: Carbon’s unique ability to bend light into structure (via melanin and porphyrins), hold spin without collapse (enabling quantum coherence in mitochondria), and shape time into memory (through DNA and epigenetic light signals) drives hemiflusome function, TCA cycle regulation, and thanatotranscriptomic suppression, countering the ROS/UPE chaos from blue light therapy in jaundiced neonates (Nunnari et al., 2019; Van Wijk et al., 2008). This stellar legacy, fine-tuned by the Hoyle state, enhances red/NIR light’s role in restoring IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), supporting myelination and bioelectric currents, and mitigating autism and EDS risks in Rh-negative babies stressed by RhoGAM and hemolysis (Agan et al., 2025; Ferguson et al., 2019). Sunlight exposure, rich in UV-A/B and red/NIR, offers a decentralized solution by upregulating POMC and melanin to chelate toxins, restore CCO/VDR functionon the IMM, and support mitochondrial health, mitigating the long-term consequences of blue light therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019; Oláh et al., 2013).

“In Rh-negative babies, where hemolysis and RhoGAM exacerbate mitochondrial stress, \[…\]”
You are getting it wrong when you are claiming that RH-negative babies are the ones effected by any of this. This is not how it works. Rhogam is given to the MOTHERS that are Rh-negative when their babies are RH+…. the babies that are RH+ birthed to RH- mothers are the ones that are affected. I’m surprised you didn’t get this right because there is a real lack of understanding of this topic across the board which leads to Rh- women being forced to get the Rhogam shot.

# Thread: Resistant husband got vertigo
