### sarahnile — 2025-08-01T00:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resistant-husband-got-vertigo.31414/post-357306>

My husband suddenly woke up with vertigo. It was in both ears and it was extremely traumatic for him. He's more open now, on the bright side. No more resistance to red light at night etc. I had to take care of him for about a week and it kind of messed up my routine...

In the mean time my lactate and pyruvate tests came back, can anyone interpret them?

**Keto acids, in serum**
The patient's keto acid profile has not been interpreted as indicative of a metabolic disease. It is recommended that the results be evaluated together with the patient's clinical condition and other laboratory findings, and that clinical and laboratory follow-up be carried out.

-   Pyruvic acid: 5.00 mg/L (Reference: <50 mg/L)
-   Keto-glutaric acid: <1.00 mg/L (Reference: <9 mg/L)
-   Acetoacetic acid: 16.00 mg/L (Reference: <20 mg/L)

**Lactate, plasma:** 7.6 mg/dL
Reference range: 4.5 – 19.8 mg/dL

# Thread: Joy's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-optimal-journal.31416/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### joyfreedom — 2025-08-01T03:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-optimal-journal.31416/post-357314>

I am relatively new to the Forum and Jacks teachings. I have made changes with greeting the sunrise and sunset, eating more like a great white, reducing the EMF in our home. I am 63 living in New Zealand, I know its not the greatest place for sovereignty and sun. I have started collecting my bitcoin.
We lived in the Gold Coast, Australia for 20 years where my health improved greatly. Now realizing living on the beach and being in the sun was one of the main reasons.
We returned back to NZ around the crazy time of covid, I new instinctively that the jab was wrong on so many levels. I have been working towards to taking my power back from centralized medicine as I had been immersed in it in my younger years. I have never had a mammograms' or smear test. Sadly I did vax my two children.
We have two children and four grandchildren. One grand daughter has Noonan Syndrome, on the lower end of the spectrum thankfully.

I was a very unwell child with pneumonia (collapsed lung) removal of tonsils. Boils and carbuncles. We lived on a farm, which seemed a healthy lifestyle.
When I started menstruating's I formed a ovarian cyst that twisted and became gangrene. From that point on I had problems with more cysts and overshadowed my life from 12 - 40
My mother and grandmother lived to their mid 80's, Mum died from leukemia. Grandmother and mother English, my father is Scotland / English
I'm the youngest of four. My oldest sister and middle sister both died from multiple myeloma, ages 63 and 65. My brother took the jab and it damaged his heart and now he has prostate cancer which he is treating outside the medical system.
The health issues I have now since being back in NZ are tinnitus, shortness of breath when doing any form of cardio. This starting from breathing in the dust from the plant deadly nightshade, it has gotten a lot better but will catch me out at times. I would so love to resolve this issue and my tinnitus. My energy levels are ok now however my recovery time from physical exertion is longer than it should be.
Looking forward to learning more and connecting with over savages

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-01T17:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-optimal-journal.31416/post-357333>

One of the best investments you can make is buying a Safe and Sound Pro II.
Then you can start measuring and minimizing your RF/microwave exposure.

I don't recommend using the popular Cornet or Trifield meters for measuring RF/microwaves as they have a low sampling rate. They are cheaper for a reason.

You will also want a meter that can measure AC electric and AC magnetic fields. A lot of options do both in one unit.
I believe Gigahertz Solutions makes some of the best units, but other people have their opinions as well.

# Thread: Heat exposure & cataract

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AntonisK — 2025-08-01T14:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357322>

In Dr. Becker's book Cross Currents he says that " chronic exposure to heat produce cataracts. Therefore, the lens of the eye appeared to be more susceptible to heat than any other part of the body".
Of course, Dr. Becker refers to microwave radiation, but heat is heat after all, no matter the source.
So, my question is this: should we worry about heat (via infrared radiation) exposure regarding eyes during midday sun, especially in the Summer and/or sunny days?
Should this mean we shouldn't stare at the suns' direction after sunrise?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-01T15:39:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357327>

He is correct. This is why we have tears to cool the eye.

### AntonisK — 2025-08-01T21:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357343>

Dry is is related to blue screen technology and as the eye dries, Carnot's theorem kicks in and the heat sink for semiconduction decreases and this is why dry eye and cataracts are linked. Becker showed in Cross Currents that heat exposure causes cataract formation. Excessive chronic blue light exposure causes dry eye by disrupting hydration signals and vitamin A metabolism. Reduced water production at CCO alters the heat sink (per semiconduction principles) leading to heat buildup. Heat exposure, as noted by Becker, contributes directly to cataract formation.

The creeping blindness of cataract formation reflects a broader metaphor in modern life: modern comforts (e.g., technology abuse, centralized medicine) suppress the discomfort of poor thinking; poor thinking and poor outcomes should lead to fostering wisdom and identity. Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Melanin, Melatonin, and Vitamin D Deficiency: Hypothyroidism reduces Coulomb charge in the skin and lens due to low melanin, melatonin, an an altered VDR, acting in unison to alter how Gauss law can be used and this impairs charge collection in cells leading to disease. This process opacifies the lens, causing cataracts.

Stars require darkness to shine, just as human biology needs light-dark cycles for optimal function. An opaque lens diminishes this balance, reducing the relative power of light to drive epigenetic programming and cellular evolution. Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. This heat stress, combined with a diminished heat sink (e.g., in the retina and lens), heightens cataract risk because it alters the production of high signal UPEs in the eyes and central retinal pathways leading to more diseases you never saw coming.

# Thread: Improving search/discoverability of Jack's content + forum
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### AlbertW — 2025-08-01T19:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/post-357340>

There is a ton of excellent information here: in Jack's blogs, in the webinars, in forum posts from all the members.

However, it takes a lot of time to ramp up, connect the dots, and find what you are looking for. I know that there is Search functionality on the forum and Search functionality on the blogs, but they are very crude tools.

I think that AI could help in discoverability of Jack's content. Recently, I've been doing a deep dive into Vitamin D synthesis, and I manually searched for Vitamin D posts on the forum, searched for Vitamin D blog posts, etc. I then copied bits and pieces of Jack's content into ChatGPT and repeatedly iterated with the LLM to ask critical questions that didn't make sense to me.

Of course AI cannot replace reading the blogs and forum posts directly, but they can aid a lot in even identifying the right blogs/forum posts to read. For example, as a beginner, if I read something about Vitamin D, it'd be nice to easily find posts on the forum about other people with similar Fitzpatrick skin type, haplogroup, etc. surface. If I'm reading 1 of Jack's blogs, and it has some side note about eczema, it would be great if I can see how that ties into other blog posts.

Does anybody else feel this way? Or am I just not using the existing tools well enough? It just seems like there is so much knowledge here and there is a missed opportunity to aid the reader in finding the right dots and connecting them faster.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/post-357469>

I've been doing the learning by subjects for months now, and I realize that's not the best approach for me
I was trying to be efficient and learn about specific topics and diseases but, although I've learned a lot, I totally missed some previous foundational info, and that makes some of the newly learned info irrelevant or unusable because parts of the foundation is still flawed/left unquestioned
So I started to go back and follow the chronology of the posts, as much as possible,
this will need time

The analogy that comes to mind about my process with this information is that it's like renovating a house in Costa Rica; you redo the floor, change the tiles, fix the damaged gypsum, windows, fix the electric system, repaint inside/out, hang the picture frames.... then dry season comes, and the clay soil cracks and opens everywhere, opening gaps between the base of the walls and the floor, stressing the poorly done foundation that you never even questioned in the first place

### AlbertW — 2025-08-03T21:47:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/post-357477>

Yes, good analogy.

For me, I’ve realized that I can’t do bottoms-up or top-down in isolation. Learning the material by reading only quantum mechanics yields no intuition about how it’s relevant to disease. Learning disease first also provides not enough of the foundation, as you say.

I may end up agreeing that reading the blogs in chronological order may be best as it more closely reflects the investigator’s (Jack) learning process combining bottoms-up and top-down knowledge accumulation. It allows the reader a chance to see if they can make the same connections that Jack did during his learning journey.

Having some AI tooling would be amazing for this, to aid in recall of previously-learned subjects and connecting to future related topics, if desired.

### AlbertD — 2025-08-13T17:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/post-358126>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=357477)
>
> Yes, good analogy.
>
> For me, I’ve realized that I can’t do bottoms-up or top-down in isolation. Learning the material by reading only quantum mechanics yields no intuition about how it’s relevant to disease. Learning disease first also provides not enough of the foundation, as you say.
>
> I may end up agreeing that reading the blogs in chronological order may be best as it more closely reflects the investigator’s (Jack) learning process combining bottoms-up and top-down knowledge accumulation. It allows the reader a chance to see if they can make the same connections that Jack did during his learning journey.
>
> Having some AI tooling would be amazing for this, to aid in recall of previously-learned subjects and connecting to future related topics, if desired.

1 (Why QM). Learn quantum mechanics first. Because quantum mechanics are the basic rules of the subatomic/atomic world. Then you transition to branches of physics of relatively larger scales (solid state physics - structure/properties of crystalline materials) or condensed matter physics (atomic interactions of solid and liquids).

Chess Analogy: Once you learn all the basic rules of chess, you can learn the basic strategies/puzzles/openings. And you build on that with more advanced strategies, etc. I brute forced a QM textbook last month. The last time I took a math course was over a decade ago in high school. I never took linear algebra/differential equations. AI helped a ton. I still cannot really derive most of the equations outlined in the book or solve many of problems without going back and forth with the solutions line by line, but the goal was to learn the basic concepts.

Me learning QM: I got some pieces of how it ties to disease because learning QM explains for example what a magnetic field is at the subatomic level or how sleeping on a magnetico relates to the Zeeman effect. But ultimately, the basic concepts/connection to diseases will be more familiar when I get to more advanced physics topics. QM is the major stepping stone, because these concepts are quite different from any other scientific field. But my intuition is the others are much more palatable because we're building on what we already learned in QM and it is not from the ground up. I think learning QM is the hardest, but it is also the most important.

2(Why QM + use AI). With your background, generating the right AI prompts will expedite your learning of foreign topics. (I have no background in physics when I started this journey). ChatGPT/Perplexity are incredible information retrieval applications. But it has no sense of creativity and I doubt that it will make ties in the way that Jack could. I mean most humans can't. Also, centralized medicine can be extremely controversial. Two highly touted studies from prestigious journals conducted by researchers from highly prestigious institutions can yield completely opposing results, while for quantum physics that isn't really possible.

3 (Reading blogs). Reading the blogs will show you some of the connections. My takeaway from reading a little over 400 blogs is that there is too many missing pieces to this puzzle. I could read all of them (almost 760 total now?) and still miss the connections/rationale behind them. Because I don't know solid state physics. I don't know non-linear optics. I don't understand quantum coherence, quantum optics, quantum magnetism, etc. Many of the excerpts in the blogs comes from the books by Becker/Mae-wan Ho/Ling. They leave breadcrumbs of the missing scientific disciplines that are overlooked in the medical community... Reading the blogs just taught me how little I really know and hints at why all these seemingly unrelated disciplines all tie into this story. If I were you, I would still read the blogs (in chronologic order ideally). In all likelihood, I have to re-read the blogs once I've learned all this physics. I despised physics in school because I didn't know it was important. Now I do.

I have been unable to clear up inflammation of the skin on my nose (rosacea?) that occurs when I have dairy (cheese or milk), and is a more recent effect I didn’t previously have.

My vitamin D level is 82 without supplements.

I sleep in essentially zero emf and have been following the ways of nature and quantum principles for a couple years now.

When I go in the sun with the rosacea on my nose I get a sunburn and peeling. I never get burned anywhere else on my body of course.

This started a couple months after moving to France 9 months ago. I have been traveling and still experience it in other places.

Any ideas or similar experiences? It’s maddening not being able to get any sun if I have any milk. Of course I can cut out milk… but my last vice is a couple Cafe Lattes most days. It just isn’t as good with butter.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-03T11:13:12+0300

Also you should not be drinking coffee at that latitude it will further mismatch you.

Also, if you cannot stop the coffee easy - you are likley using it as an energy crutch which gets to my first point of potential underlying terrain issues (low redox) + DA signalling issues.

### Kendall Toerner — 2025-08-05T11:35:46+0300

### hacklose — 2025-09-26T18:59:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-emf-in-a-wall.31426/post-360067>

> Unplugging everything in the room helped some.
>
> Even with everything unplugged though, max reading is 300 uw/m2. Mostly in the 60-125 range. Just trying to get it under 10 bc I'm really sensitive to it when sleeping.

Do you have a way to measure the electric field and magnetic field? Or is the only meter you have the Safe and Sound Pro II?
The Safe and Sound Pro II is only measuring RF.

There is a small chance that someone had a cordless phone system installed in the walls of the house. I've seen that once before.

Use the max reset button on the Pro II often and see if you can get to the exact location of the RF.
Remember that the Safe and Sound Pro II's areas of highest sensitivity are out the front and the back if the meter is held upright.
If you hold the meter like this <||>.

Hope this helps.

# Thread: parents of kids who have had reactions to bee stings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kel — 2025-08-04T05:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357504>

My daughter has been stung 5 times by bees. She is 9 years old. The first two times there was no reaction other than some swelling. The third time it was on her thumb and she had an anaphylactic reaction and had to be injected with epinephrin at hospital. The fourth time she was fine again with just some swelling. And then the 5th time she had the anaphylactic reaction and went to hopspital again.
We have epipens and are now prepared if this happens again. What I am wondering is why she sometimes reacts so extremely and other times is fine.
I asked Dr Jack and he said to build immune system with grounding and more sun, etc. We are doing this.
Just curious if anyone has any personal insights or ideas on what else we can do as well. And also am curious if anyone has kids who had anaphylactic reactions that they then grew out of.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-04T16:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357522>

### Kel — 2025-08-09T03:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357839>

Dr Jack mainstream doctors are telling me that if she has the anaphylactic reaction again I should use the epipen but then head straight to the hospital. Would you generally agree with that or does the epipen stop the reaction usually? I ofcourse want to do all I can if this happens again, but dont trust the hospital to not give her stuff she doesnt need.. Also just trying to evaluate how we continue to live our life in a semi-rural area. We go on lots of surf trips to pretty isolated places. Would Benadryl be ok to take along with the epipen, to help combat the puffy eyes and rash we experienced last time? Or should the two medicines not be mixed? Through redox is it possible to grow out of the reaction?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-09T05:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357846>

Precautions are wise, but if you want the immune reaction to vanish built the kids redox power with the sun and it happen again.

### Kel — 2025-08-09T15:28:43+0300

# Thread: EZ Water and tunneling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ez-water-and-tunneling.31433/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### johnq77 — 2025-08-05T02:18:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ez-water-and-tunneling.31433/post-357555>

There is a lot of abstract concepts to think about when studying Quantum Biology, mostly in the realm of biophysics and quantum particles. I am trying to wrap my head around the lattice structure of EZ water and it's implications on moving information around the cell and entire the human body. Organized water in the cell and through out the body will interact with hydrophilic surfaces, building charge separation, and creating little batteries. But does the structure also allow for tunneling of protons and electrons, far and near? If something needs to happen simultaneously in different regions of the body, are these processes connected through EZ water and tunneling? Would love to hear peoples thoughts on this. Thank you.

# Thread: High estradiol concern
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-estradiol-concern.31439/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Asael Enriquez — 2025-08-05T22:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-estradiol-concern.31439/post-357654>

Hi,

Here are my results for a male hormone panel test done a couple months back. Estradiol seems to be quite elevated at 56 pg/ml as seen on the attached document.
I've been reading that high estrogen levels can promote yeast overgrowth in the digestive tract and can make it extremely difficult to overcome regardless of diet or supplements.

As far as strategies to improve redox, I have been able to reduce my sleeping environment to under 5 microwatts per meter squared and ELF down to .80-100mV at my new home.

I have not been consistent with being out for sunrise and blocking blue light at night due to my work schedule and personal life situations. However, now that I am back on track, I will diligently be sure to follow the above actions moving forward. My candida symptoms have reduced quite drastically since greatly reducing nnEMF exposure at night, yet I still suspect an overgrowth based on symptoms that still linger.

Can elevated estradiol be a result of excessive blue light at night and not waking up with sunrise? Or is it the yeast overgrowth itself that is driving the elevated estradiol? If so, what is the mechanism behind it.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-06T03:48:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-estradiol-concern.31439/post-357665>

High E2 = no sun.

# Thread: Boy Mom in KC > to ES or GT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boy-mom-in-kc-to-es-or-gt.31446/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### joyfullyfefe — 2025-08-06T19:29:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boy-mom-in-kc-to-es-or-gt.31446/post-357689>

Hello all,

I'm a 34 yo SAHM, my husband and I have 4 sons (5, 3, 2, newborn). I am originally from SE Houston, but my husband is from KC, Kansas and this is where we live currently.

I am mainly here because I have not come across someone that was as like minded as we are. We did not vaccinate our children, my husband was not vaccinated, and we did not comply during the lockdowns (we didn't even wear masks/my job didn't know what to do with me and ended up putting me on short term disability for 6 months just to keep me out of the way). We were still impacted by it, as my son was born the day of the first lockdown (we did not have a hospital birth). My family was unable to visit us and it really took a toll on my post partum experience. With that said, we would be foolish to believe something like this will not be repeated within our lifetime, and most certainly our childrens. We were already a family that spent a ton of time outside, but we were not catching sunrises. Since I learned about this major necessity I started doing it right away in Feb (even in the snow) of last year and never looked back (my husband and children began to follow in my footsteps not long after).

My paradigm shift was not in a full podcast, but a podcast clip, Jack said something to the tune in regard to Ray Peat and Weston Price's work, that what they missed entirely was that these tribal groups were eating their food in the sun. It was like I had an immediate epiphany that what I had been sensing about these influencers was right, I just didn't have the missing and obvious piece, which was the sun.

We have been planning to move to El Salvador or Guatemala since last year, before learning about Dr. Jack. I found Dr. Jack through an influencer who I no longer follow. In fact, I was ripped off by her in the new year of 2024. I took a major step back and unfollowed several people and really kept my social media silent for a year. I decided to start listening to Kruse's podcast as I kept being drawn to them. I was hesitant to invest any money into anyone else because I had been ripped off by that influencer. Fast forward to when Kruse defended Dr. Alexis earlier this year, when he did that, I felt again a parallel in our beliefs. I had also came to the realization that influencers pushing certain diets, supplements, and "lifestyles' was stealing precious time and energy. Due to this I decided Alexis and Jack were both worth my time and I am very grateful especially now with everything being a complete disaster with the current administration and feeling more urgency to get moving on our plans to move down south.

### MTA321 — 2025-08-06T21:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boy-mom-in-kc-to-es-or-gt.31446/post-357693>

Welcome. I remember when I had my epiphany listening to Uncle Jack. It was a really weird moment and really shattered my paradigm of reality regarding health and life. The aspect of light being a primary driver of all things seems like common sense in hindsight. The second realization I had was: if I completely shit the bed on this topic, what else am I currently blind to?

Cheers to more dopamine.

### AlbertW — 2025-11-25T22:37:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quartz-car-windows.31448/post-363182>

I just measured my 2025 RAV4 and 2025 RX350 in a very RF quiet environment. Turns out there is still some RF that is emitted by (what I think is) the DCM.

I get 50-300 uW/m2 at body level.
Much better than the 10,000+ uW/m2 in Tesla but not 0. Time to do some hacking around to remove it.

Does anybody know of modern cars that actually allow ALL RF to be removed without prying apart panels in the car and pulling fuses?

### knees4bukele — 2025-11-26T07:33:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quartz-car-windows.31448/post-363207>

The best solution is to buy an older car without any of this BS in the first place.

### Gian — 2025-11-26T19:47:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quartz-car-windows.31448/post-363228>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=363182)
>
>  I just measured my 2025 RAV4 and 2025 RX350 in a very RF quiet environment. Turns out there is still some RF that is emitted by (what I think is) the DCM.
>
> I get 50-300 uW/m2 at body level.
> Much better than the 10,000+ uW/m2 in Tesla but not 0. Time to do some hacking around to remove it.
>
> Does anybody know of modern cars that actually allow ALL RF to be removed without prying apart panels in the car and pulling fuses?

I have a 2010 Toyota Tundra with the stock, non-nav stereo. When the stereo was completely off I was still getting RF spikes over 8,000 uW/m2. I assumed it was due to the Bluetooth pinging (even when the stereo was off). I asked a local car audio shop if they could remove the Bluetooth antenna and at first they said yes, but they never made it happen so I replaced the stock stereo with a good quality older unit that did not come equipped with Bluetooth and I am no longer getting RF emitting from my stereo

>
> Does anybody know of modern cars that actually allow ALL RF to be removed without prying apart panels in the car and pulling fuses?

Modern cars all have over the air updates. Did you not watch the video i put up on the chick with the toyota?

# Thread: PREDATOR VS PREY IN FINANCE.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/predator-vs-prey-in-finance.31463/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-09T16:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/predator-vs-prey-in-finance.31463/post-357881>

Lessons from Harvard: Do not be well educated, it could make you broke (Harvard) or kill you (Harvard COVID policies).

"Harvard Business School taught the Strategy case study. This is perhaps the greatest business school in the United States, the best and the brightest. The professor calls to say, 'We studied the Strategy story, and we'd like you to come in and talk to the class. The students are very excited!'

Mind you, this is after we'd adopted a Bitcoin strategy and after it had worked. Here’s what happened… I joined the class on Zoom and shared our story. I told them the company was at the end of the line.

The enterprise was worth $600 million, and the market cap was ~$5 billion.

We tried unsuccessfully to grow the business for over a decade. We were at our wits' end. The only alternative was to sell the company: corporate death. Finally, we adopted a Bitcoin standard. The stock price quadrupled, employees made millions, and shareholders made billions. Our strategy was good for Bitcoin, the company, and our customers.

A few months later, our market cap was over $30 billion.

The professor said ‘OK, you've heard the CEO. What do you guys think? Would you do it?’

He then took a survey.
First student: 'I don't know, seems kind of risky.'
Second student: 'It seems like an inappropriate use of shareholder funds.'
Third student: 'This isn't normally done.'
Fourth student: 'It kind of violates convention in corporate finance.'
Fifth student: 'We really can't see any major company doing it.'

I swear I watched 10 students, and every single one of them said, 'No, we would never do this.' The takeaway? The world's full of conventional wisdom, conventional thinkers, and conventional institutions. You can give them the answer. You can even take the risk for them and prove it works. They will just stare at you and not do it.

The one conclusion I’ve reached is that you can't predict who will get it. After all, this is a financial revolution. But I'm happy to live in a world where adoption grows from 1% to 2%, then 4%, 8%, 16%, and beyond. At that point, Bitcoin won't be a $2 trillion asset class, it'll be a $100 trillion asset class. By the time your financial adviser says it’s OK to buy Bitcoin, it’ll cost $1 million.

When they say it’s a good idea, it’ll be $10 million.

Everybody gets Bitcoin at the price they deserve."

The lesson of Predator vs Prey i son display for the Savages to learn from.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-30T18:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/predator-vs-prey-in-finance.31463/post-366120>

Back then a business dealt directly with the homeowner to build a home. In 1913 the bankers came up with a new mechanism to destroy the Sears direct business model of home buying. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. # Thread: BioHacking BiPolar
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31465/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jojoplachta — 2025-08-09T19:29:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31465/post-357900>

I apologize in advance for the length of this. I'm old. (67) I'm BiPolar and probably Narcissistic (in the Greek mythology sense)

Family:

**Mother**: Outdoorsy DairyFarm kid in rural Nova Scotia, Canada Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full. She was red/blonde, very fair with white eyelashes, brows, blue eyes. She had regrown a fingertip that had been chopped mid nail at age 7 She was a Teacher so had summers off and pushed us to play outdoors til dark, took us to the beach on the regular and we often stayed all day, She got freckles, I got very tanned She had a small jaw and crooked crowded tooth placement She had her first (male)child who died at 6-9 months. I was the second born Inherited father's extremely dark eyes, he had black; I got reddish brown hair, olive skin, perfectly straight teeth Third (female)child is very blonde/fair like mother She became anorexic around 6yo, Bulimic as a teenager Mother had Gallbladder removed at 32 and was killed ina train/car crash at 34 (I was 12, sister 10) My Workaholic father was traumatized and family life became very difficult

**Maternal Grandmother:**

Dairy FarmHer, in Rural Nova Scotia (3rd generation) Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full Dark hair Dark eyes, Fair skin Got cataracts in 80's, debilitating arthritis in hands in her 60's, Had all teeth out and full dentures in her 40's, lived to be 95 with very bright mind til the end

**Father**: born to Polish immigrants very much traumatized by being sent to Canada to escape the starvation by Stalin during Holodomor, dairy farmer in Alberta Traumatized by abusive parents His father was angry and mother an extreme depressive and religious fanatic all her life. Left home at 16 Joined airforce at 18 He had olive skin, dark eyes, black hair, perfect teeth His only dental work was removal of a broken molar in his 70's I remember him being fun loving as a young child but extremely Depressed and/or Ragey after my Mothers death and finding out she was adulterous, he became a financially functioning alcoholic, but with violent rages, emotional and physical abuse and extreme mood swings

**Me**:

In utero and from birth until 3 1/2, I lived on a Dairy Farm in rural NovaScotia (11 miles from the AirForceBase) a vaginal birth in hospital, straight onto carnation instant milk/dextrin maltose formula and eventually raw cows milk, cream and lots of raw butter Cared for by my Maternal grandmother from 2 mos old when Mother went back to teaching.

At age 3 1/2 we moved to Trenton Ontario airforce base (literally 200 yards from the airplane runway living in a mobile home) Other than school, I was outdoors in nature from dawn til dusk on weekends and summer. Father took me snowmobiling in winter until my feet and fingers were near frostbitten. I was a behaviour problem to my mother, had sleep schedule resistance, very active, moody and strong willed, had frequent bronchitis, recurring tonsillitis and chronically chapped lips My adult teeth grew in straight but had 4-6 molars filled with amalgam by age 14 Wisdom teeth impacted and painful, removed at 18, 8 more fillings by 21

We had a colour tv as soon as available and I watched it sitting close every evening and Saturday mornings. I had trouble sleeping, and was sound sensitive and slept in the living room so I could be alone and have the radio on all night

At 10 we moved to 5 miles away from the airbase. I ran wild in nature,a horse kick at 12 broke my kneecap, had 3 surgeries by 16, I manipulated maximum Demerol and used it for emotional self medication, started smoking, drinking and marijuana at 11 Started menstruating at 12

At 13 my mother was killed in a car by a train which exposed her extramarital affair to my Father who was Traumatized My sister and I were moved in with Fathers "business partner" who years later I realized was his mistress. I was made responsible for 10 yo sister and mistresses 7, 4 and 6 month old sons. I developed chronic constipation, depression and clinical dry skin began Had 5 more Knee surgeries by age 19 Physical and emotional abuse from alcoholic depressed father. Lived in extreme anger and seething hatred Joined a religious cult and started experiencing intermittent mania

At 15 sent to psychiatrist by a school guidance councillor for major mood swings and religious fanaticism No parent was involved Diagnosed manic depressive Offered lithium which I declined in favour of keeping the "highs"

Forgot about it and went on with my "mercurial" life

At 18 Abandoned by "mistress turned stepmother" and her 3 children. I was dropped off at University, disowned by father and put on Student welfare/and arranged Government loans and worked restaurant overnight shifts to finish a Bachelor of Arts degree. Quit marijuana in first year because I had bad paranoia on it and a psychotic event with hashish Drank alcohol and smoked cigarettes heavily

At 20 Became sexually involved with a married man 21 years my senior w 4 kids After 4 years of a stressful affair. I lost a ton of weight, became severely hypoglycemic fainting all over the place Solved it with hospital low glycemic diet and lifetime of avoiding (or trying to) sugar and for a long time caffeine Married man moved in and we had a mostly On but also dramatically and stressfully Off Commonlaw relationship that lasted 21 years

At age 21 had emergency Pilonoidal cyst removal surgery Suspected hypothyroidism but testing was deemed normal Still constipated, very dry skin, low basal body temp to this day, low blood pressure but thyroid levels low normal to normal

At 24 I moved to Toronto, became a Realtor Lots of parties w alcohol some cocaine, still smoked cigarettes and lots of money and depressive periods Had a giant brick of a car phone in 1985 and cell phone ever since

At 30 moved to NYC w same older man/commonlaw husband Joined a Mystical School and began doing spiritual work and meditation seeking relief from depression and angry rages mixed with manic partying Quit smoking and for months at a time and for about 5 years kept sober, sugar and caffeine free

Worked at a NYC nightclub Used ecstasy excessively recreationally and was living in the night Sleeping every other night from 5am-noonish Eventually I began using 1/4 tab ecstasy every other day for depression Manic phases started to become extreme irritability Started gaining weight Used Jenny Craig to lose 25lbs gained back immediately

1998 Husband returned to Canada

In the year 2000 (age 42) Did Atkins Diet at Atkins Centre of Complementary Medicine NYC Diagnosed Hypothyroid and started Natural Dessicated Thyroid Had amalgam fillings changed by Huggins Protocol and Chelation Therapy w dmsa/vit c IV, started on Bioidentical progesterone Tested thyroid releasing hormone because thyroid was intermittently normal and low and couldn't lose the weight

In December 2001 after 911 and major depression with suicidal ideation, Dr Atkins sent me to Psychopharmacologist where I got diagnosis of Bipolar (rapid cycling) ->Started Lamotragine 25 mg and Quetiapine (after a few sleep meds) For the first time I could focus enough to read without major difficulty Obsessively read about BiPolar trying to prove I wasn't and how to not take meds for life

Realized that sleep was key so I forced 10-6 sleep cycle using ambien Became intuitively aware that NYC was making me sick

In 2005 Age 47 moved back to Toronto. HATED it Went back into Real Estate Gained 35 lbs, at times gaining rapidly (a lb/day) for no reason Also had days with hypersensitive sense of smell Constant low grade depression, low energy, constipation, dizzy spells and brain fog Saw a PhD Biochemist Nutritionist who advised 1200 mg magnesium, 10,000 iu d3, Evening Primrise Oil, Citicholine, Inositol amongst other supplements that I took for years

Still taking Bioidentical progesterone, I added testosterone, Dhea, Oxytocin (for awhile), injectable HGH (for awhile) Added estrogen eventually

2009 Consulted with Amen Clinic Found a psychiatrist to order a Spect Scan (results below) hoping to manage the BP with nutraceuticals instead of meds Confirmed BiPolar Diagnosis and also PTSD OCD ADD Kept taking Lamotragine added Cymbalta (for depression and chronic pain)changed Quetiapene to Trazodone

On the personal front I met current husband whose DIL had just had his/our baby grandson We bonded and I provided full time childcare weekdays

In 2010 my mostly estranged Father died Engaged in a stressful estate settlement for 2 years becoming estranged from sister in the process (for two years after)

Married in 2011 Was mostly euthymic for a couple years Husband Retired from 37 years policing to fulfill my "travel in an Airstream" dream In 2012 I lost 70 lbs using Bernstein Weight Loss program injecting b12 and b6 every other day, on 900 calories locarb Lo fat subsistence protein and was pretty happy for a few years. Quit drinking for a year and a half.

I had a basal carcinoma removed from my nose (pretty much the only place I'd used sunscreen)I had also used sunglasses but hardly ever. Planning to rent my house and travel long term, we found out the foundation had turned to sand Managed a long, stressful and expensive Reno replacing basement under the house Started finding the stress of Real estate too much and having cycles of depression, irritability and rage Self medicating with alcohol didn't help. I semiretired to provide fulltime daycare for baby grandson until he was 4 Embraced my role as grandmother and enjoyed it

2017 Husband took a job he really wanted and I bought us a Beach House on Lake Erie and bnb'ed our Toronto home. Husband diagnosed with colon cancer 3 months after starting/moving. To "save his life", I obsessively researched the internet and fixated on Morley Robbins' magnesium group desperate to cure cancer with nutrition! I followed the Root Cause Protocol for 5 years, in that group I found Dr Jack. I quit the 10k iu vit d Id been taking for 3 years. I maxed beach living, sunsets and sunrises, and lived rural with very low nnemf Depression persisted and intermittent rage

2018 Had an accident in the water where several tendons/ligaments in both knees landed me in a wheel chair unable to strengthen one without reinjuring Eventually found a rehab program at hospital and devoted to achieving physical fitness through Aquafit and strength classes knowing I'd eventually need knee replacement

2020 Covid STRESS believing the pandemic threat for first few months Got COVID/or a flu? Test was negative and saw the public health incongruencies and then the WEF "plan" Avoided the vax and stopped complying! I gave up RealEstate completely.

Realized Jack was "the real thing" somewhere around that time and was living a mostly outside life tanned, gardened, canned and preserved food for the "expected food shortages" and did Aquafit

Was diagnosed with High Ocular pressure and monitored for glaucoma

Started 5 year taper off Cymbalta thinking it might be responsible for the rages. Realized a few years in it also might've been causing ocular pressure

Kept up Aquafit which had become the only exercise routine to ever give me a neurotransmitter high

Marriage started falling apart Caught him online cheating during pandemic

2022 We separated. He moved back to Toronto and after 10 months I sold beach house, I moved back in with him planning to divorce but doing couples therapy and having lots of episodic rages My knees were both painful with Osteoarthritis-but I was tanned, had darkened my separate bedroom, wore blueblockers and installed screen softwares, wifi timer, increased use of airplane mode and pointed my OCD at DIY Jack Kruse but living in Depression and Social Isolation except for my 3 grandchildren and daughter-in-law who were (and still are) enmeshed in a nasty and stressful (for all of us) divorce.

Jan 23 Had left knee replacement

May 23 Blown away by Jack's Tetragrammaton/Huberman podcast Resumed obsessive tanning, sunrise and outdoor Jack podcasts everyday

June 23 Fell and broke right ankle and severely tore right rotator cuff (bicep longhead severed, subscapularis and supraspinatus with major tears) Had double malleolus pin and plate repair surgery Wheelchair bound 100% for 2 months and mostly for 6 months Suffered extreme neglectful abandonment by husband Rehabbed by sitting in open sliding glass doors until I could drag myself downstairs to ground in sun in yard in bikini, read Leptin Rx and Jack's blogs and X posts Gained another 25lbs on top of the 50 extra I was already carrying

January '24 Had rotator cuff repair surgery Months of painful sleeplessness and physio rehab and massage and chiropractic which still continue as compensatory muscle and tendon stresses erupted

While Opthamologist was monitoring for Glaucoma and because I was experiencing blurred vision, Found retinopathy and optical Nerve Damage in left eye by MRI (Results below)

Still in Psychotherapy for extreme stress w/husband Started CT in October 24 and other principals from Jacks Water Muse X post and Vagus Nerve Rx and reduced Cortisol significantly Depression still extreme but rage returned to cycles of manageable irritability

Dec 24 Osteopathy starts bringing relief to shoulder pain and compensatory (I guess) right sacroilliac pain

Started Methylene Blue at .5mg First dose was awesome euphoria and energizing Gradually increased to 1.5 mg. Eventually found Jacks warnings about messing with it and it appeared to be causing rage at 1.5 mg Stopped it

Regular CT and (it's summer in Canada)has decreased most of the rages

Did 7 years back taxes and refinanced to pay off 100k in taxes and took out 100 in equity from my house to buy bitcoin

Jan25 Saw something Jack said about not taking MB unless you see sunrise Since I was, I started it again and it definitely precipitated rage at only 1mg

Started having mild anxiety attacks Started tapering off the 50 mgTrazodone and adding CBD/CBN instead for sleep and chronic pain (I get some relief from PEA)I think the cbd has carried over into a calmer demeanor during the day too

Now I'm squirrelling away bitcoin, researching moving to El Salvador, (although I don't feel physically able to move most of the time) decluttering in hopes of moving, and bingeing the writings and podcasts of Dr Jack Kruse

Current dissatisfactions:

FAT, LOW ENERGY No motivation, NO JOY I crave carbs (although I've been really disciplined for several months now and seem to have successfully switched my eating window to between 8am and 3pm-4 with blood sugar more stabilized than it's been in 40ish years) Still Depressed (Dopamine must be low cause sometimes I don't even have motivation to even comply with my KrusePHD lessons and can't get myself to Aquafit class)
Any waves of euthymic mood are few and far between and Mania is always irritability Predominant mood is irritable and detached or depressed and detached. I'm pretty socially isolated and in a constant battle against sedentary inertia and until recently chronic pain. I have a drawer full of exit plan.

I've been in almost constant pain for many months and just a couple weeks ago seemingly unrelated to anything, the pain decreased by 80% (my guess is it's BP related and I'm grateful it's over but fear it's a new part of a cycle called Depression hurts) Muscle and tendon tears seem to keep happening, most recently my previously good shoulder without any conscious injury or strain and the odd time I get a weird seizing or cramping from the shin below my knee replacement, and eye twitches and above one side of my mouth (Since reading the demyelination series of blogs I wonder if that's why)

I just increased my thyroid meds (bloodwork showed low normal)(wondering if I should switch out NDT to Levothyroxine cause I read somewhere it can help depression in BP patients) And be had this itching if the skin on my shoulders and now arms for the last year and a half that slowly gets worse and doesn't really respond to any lotion, oil, except slightly I think to hypochlorous acid spray and castor oil? Not life threatening but super annoying and an indication of? I dunno

**Current Maintenance:**

AO Chiropractor adjusts c2 which rotates biweekly

Regular Chiro every two weeks

Osteopath once a week

Natural Dessicated Thyroid 60mg

Lamotragine (25mg) Trazodone (tapering off-9.5 mg currently)

Bioidentical hormones: prog, test, bi-est, Dhea, pregnenalone.

PEA for pain (3-4x400/day)

80/40 mg Cbd/Cbn for **sleep** (get 1.5-2 hrs deep sleep, average 1-1:45 REM 7-8 total)

I never miss Sunrise with grounding, am still out there for uva and added breath hold meditation last week Am tanning in spring summer and fall and have the doors open most of the time until deep winter, I do CT (but like 15-20 minutes at 50°) Blue blockers an hour before bed (which is before sunset now and as soon as sunsets in winter)

I have Iris on my laptop and The BlueLight Diet filters on my phone, a DaylightCo tablet for reading Jack, Sunroof open when driving, Sliding glass doors open in house, Wifi off at night

I eat epiPaleo in season (75% compliance) and have been locarb for 45 years (with bipolar discipline)

Drink 1 cup of coffee in am

Self medicate w 2-3 hard seltzer daily

Marriage has started to have glimmers of supportive rather than just stressful It's hard to be decisive about ending a (second)marriage when MY brain is BP

**2009 SPECT results**:

mild decreased perfusion to the left parotid lobe, small perfusion defects high convexity, right infraorbital frontal, occipital lobe midline, left medial temporal lobe, left anteroinferior cerebellum and bilateral infratemporal lobes as well as scattered hyperfusion throughout the cortex and marked hyperfusion appreciated in the thalamus,, right basal ganglia and left frontal lobe anteriorly as well as the posterior cingulate gyrus with mild-moderate hyperfusion to the anterior cingulate gyrus and marked hypofusion to the cerebellum

**NeuroOpthomology Report**:

Followup for mild left optic neuropathy: Mitochondrial genomic sequencing was normal. Testing for heavy metals was normal as well (mild elevated Thallium)

Evidence of ubtle left optic neuropathy with relative afferent pupillary defect, mild temporal pallor of the left optic disc which corresponded to thinning and pericapillary OCT.

Upon re-examination and pending MRI results, we would assume that she has had mild left non-arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy in the past

**2024-MRI**:

Tecnique: Multisequence multiplanar MRI imaging of the orbits with contrast

Diffusion imaging does not demonstrate any evidence of acute or subacute parenchymal brain ischemia.

The calibre of the optic nerves is symmetric. The optic chiasm is also symmetric in appearance. There is no abnormal signal or evidence of compressive lesion, the cavernous sinuses are clear. The orbital spaces are clear.

**Thoughts/Questions for my consult:**
Wondering if I should switch to lithium? Or Lithium orotate?

If I should take the "miracle" Ivermectin? The expensive but likely helpful DDW?

I do regular Blood donation? Wonder if it really is good for removing excess iron as per Morley Robbins?

I have to take Magnesium or I literally will not shit for more than a week, soooo its a solution but is it also a problem?

I also wonder if my cco is just permanently broken? Like, even when you sit in sunrise can you still remain Warburg shifted?

Do I need a Magnetico?

**Paid for a consult with Jack! Finally pulled the trigger! Can hardly wait but also bracing for a punch in the mouth I haven't considered.**

Thanks for reading. The search for balance continues.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31465/post-358157>

**UVA-mTOR Interplay and Spectra:** UVA absorption via OPN5/SIRT1 boosts NAD+, activating AMPK to inhibit mTOR, favoring photo repair like mitochondrial biogenesis. Outputs include IR-A emissions (600–1000 nm) from ATP/AMP ratios and clock genes, promoting coherent UPE for DNA repair. Melanin (from tyrosine/phenylalanine) protects DNA by ROS quenching, while melatonin (95% mitochondrial, from tryptophan) switches metabolism seasonally. Interconnections involve quantum mechanisms like electron transfer. Sunscreen, sunglasses, nnEMF, bluelight, and shitty cities/states/countries block all of UV light = THOSE WHO STAY SICK WITH THIS DISEASE [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978)

# Thread: ME/CFS, Chronic Migraine, Fibro, Chronic neck and back pain, Sleep disorder.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/me-cfs-chronic-migraine-fibro-chronic-neck-and-back-pain-sleep-disorder.31467/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dee Dee — 2025-08-10T04:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/me-cfs-chronic-migraine-fibro-chronic-neck-and-back-pain-sleep-disorder.31467/post-357920>

**Hi all,

I have CFS (after virus on the 6th Cranial nerve) 2009.**
I had **double vision for 3 months and severe headache.**
They said it would be permanent but my sight recovered.

I was very ill and 16 years later I still have fatigue, chronic migraine and eye symptoms.
I've been having **Botox** for years for the headaches- it's really helped so much- I couldn't get out of bed.
I was a single mum to 4 and doing my degrees and so very ill.

Thinking of changing from Botox to injections- due to cost.
**The injections are monoclonal antibodies-** I don't like the sound of that but I need to research.
**Any opinions on it?**
I have trialled no Botox and **all the sun exposure etc hasn't changed the headaches or neck pain.**
Also Fibro, chronic neck, back pain and sleep disorder (originally not enough REM but now Apnea).
I've put off having a neck fusion for years as I don't want more metal in me or more surgeries.
However, I am in alot of pain- it radiates round the front of my neck and face, eyes and my across my upper spine.
My surgeon has really helped me previously with leg function and he is the only one Id trust to do it (he didn't do the back fusion).

Been following Uncle Jack for years and I share it with open minded people.

Lots sun exposure, stopped sunglasses years ago, do all the blocking blue light strategies and have ethernet (which I barely use). Lots grounding.

**Thinking of getting deuterium depleted water but not sure what ratio I need when adding it to water?

Also emr-tek light to top up red light exposure (winter here).**

We're also getting ready to turn the electricity off at night.

I haven't noticed any difference in my sleep when I stay in a different location.

My son says I should go to ES for a month to heal.
I'm reading healing stories now.

Australia is very totalitarian. Jabbed people are having many strange issues and dying.

A 28 yo guy I met had a heart attack after the first one, and pancreatic cancer after the second.
Treatment helped then it came back. Not looking good.

Thank you
Donna

Best wishes to all here - my tribe

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:26:54+0300

# Thread: Sleeping in nature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Timefighter — 2025-08-10T12:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357928>

Hello, I tried to sleep in nature for the first time these past two days and it didn't work out well.

Im in Galicia (Spain) right now and Im staying in a very small village who has pretty nice beaches. As there are not too many people here and the beach is empty at night I decided to try and sleep there.

I buried my chins and my feet underneath the sand and covered my body with a beach towel but the cold here is outrageous and even more during night time. There are also plenty of pill bugs that were constantly sifting through the sand trying to reach and bite my skin. I wasn't able to get a minute of sleep. The time I Iasted the most was only until 3 AM when I went back to sleep at the apartment.

I've been willing to do this for a long time and I will keep trying in other places. I would like to know if any of you have try it, and if so, what advice would you give me.

Thanks for your time

### eve001 — 2025-08-10T13:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357930>

You need some sheltering from the wind and bugs.
Try a small tent or bivy?

Also, get an EMF meter so that you can pick the best spot to try.
The new Cornet is an affordable one. (200€)
Or at least check a map indicating where you will find cell towers.
Cell towers disrupt sleep.

New swiss study on 5G and deep sleep: [https://www.sciencedirect.com/scien...CXYL82glmWYHVNCdaQ\_aem\_KLmQ3Uecjtixua6jMYCgQg](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S105381192500343X?via%3Dihub&fbclid=IwY2xjawMFRQlleHRuA2FlbQIxMABicmlkETFPME9IMUg4cmdmSldRQVdxAR7F6zAbtCxBBkV-cPMuxad1nD1nsFK8i7SkQtuel18rCXYL82glmWYHVNCdaQ_aem_KLmQ3Uecjtixua6jMYCgQg)

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-10T15:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357934>

You need a tent or yurt made of natural fibers to keep grounding connection but avoid bugs.

Bring a meter with you and test bc you'll be surprised.

### Timefighter — 2025-08-11T10:43:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357967>

Thank you very much for your answers.
I was already planning on buying the Cornet and I will start looking up for tents that allow grounding.

### eve001 — 2025-08-11T14:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357971>

> [Timefighter said:](/goto/post?id=357967)
>
> Thank you very much for your answers.
> I was already planning on buying the Cornet and I will start looking up for tents that allow grounding.

Cool, if you find any such tent I' d love to know about it. I tried one of synthetic material and don't like it. Not sure why exactly -apart from the smell. I put a grounding sheet in it and connected it outside - but that was in humid clay ground.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-11T16:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357975>

One thing you can consider is buying a tent made of whatever fabric and cutting out most of the bottom and covering it with a blanket made of animal fur (I believe REI sells some nice ones that aren't too pricy), or just buy a bottomless tent and you can use whatever blankets/insulation you want.

It is tough to find a tent made of natural fibers.

# Thread: BioHacking BiPolar
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31472/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jojoplachta — 2025-08-10T15:32:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31472/post-357931>

I apologize in advance for the length of this. I'm old. I'm BiPolar and probably Narcissistic (in the Greek mythology sense)

Family:

**Mother**: Outdoorsy DairyFarm kid in rural Nova Scotia, Canada Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full. She was red/blonde, very fair with white eyelashes, brows, blue eyes. She had regrown a fingertip that had been chopped mid nail at age 7 She was a Teacher so had summers off and pushed us to play outdoors til dark, took us to the beach on the regular and we often stayed all day, She got freckles, I got very tanned She had a small jaw and crooked crowded tooth placement She had her first (male)child who died at 6-9 months. I was the second born Inherited father's extremely dark eyes, he had black; I got reddish brown hair, olive skin, perfectly straight teeth Third (female)child is very blonde/fair like mother She became anorexic around 6yo, Bulimic as a teenager Mother had Gallbladder removed at 32 and was killed ina train/car crash at 34 (I was 12, sister 10) My Workaholic father was traumatized and family life became very difficult

**Maternal Grandmother**:

Dairy FarmHer, in Rural Nova Scotia (3rd generation) Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full Dark hair Dark eyes, Fair skin Got cataracts in 80's, debilitating arthritis in hands in her 60's, Had all teeth out and full dentures in her 40's, lived to be 95 with very bright mind til the end

**Father**: born to Polish immigrants very much traumatized by being sent to Canada to escape the starvation by Stalin during Holodomor, dairy farmer in Alberta Traumatized by abusive parents His father was angry and mother an extreme depressive and religious fanatic all her life. Left home at 16 Joined airforce at 18 He had olive skin, dark eyes, black hair, perfect teeth His only dental work was removal of a broken molar in his 70's I remember him being fun loving as a young child but extremely Depressed and/or Ragey after my Mothers death and finding out she was adulterous, he became a financially functioning alcoholic, but with violent rages, emotional and physical abuse and extreme mood swings

**Me**:

In utero and from birth until 3 1/2, I lived on a Dairy Farm in rural NovaScotia (11 miles from the AirForceBase) a vaginal birth in hospital, straight onto carnation instant milk/dextrin maltose formula and eventually raw cows milk, cream and lots of raw butter Cared for by my Maternal grandmother from 2 mos old when Mother went back to teaching.

At age 3 1/2 we moved to Trenton Ontario airforce base (literally 200 yards from the airplane runway living in a mobile home) Other than school, I was outdoors in nature from dawn til dusk on weekends and summer. Father took me snowmobiling in winter until my feet and fingers were near frostbitten. I was a behaviour problem to my mother, had sleep schedule resistance, very active, moody and strong willed, had frequent bronchitis, recurring tonsillitis and chronically chapped lips My adult teeth grew in straight but had 4-6 molars filled with amalgam by age 14 Wisdom teeth impacted and painful, removed at 18, 8 more fillings by 21

We had a colour tv as soon as available and I watched it sitting close every evening and Saturday mornings. I had trouble sleeping, and was sound sensitive and slept in the living room so I could be alone and have the radio on all night

At 10 we moved to 5 miles away from the airbase. I ran wild in nature,a horse kick at 12 broke my kneecap, had 3 surgeries by 16, I manipulated maximum Demerol and used it for emotional self medication, started smoking, drinking and marijuana at 11 Started menstruating at 12

At 13 my mother was killed in a car by a train which exposed her extramarital affair to my Father who was Traumatized My sister and I were moved in with Fathers "business partner" who years later I realized was his mistress. I was made responsible for 10 yo sister and mistresses 7, 4 and 6 month old sons. I developed chronic constipation, depression and clinical dry skin began Had 5 more Knee surgeries by age 19 Physical and emotional abuse from alcoholic depressed father. Lived in extreme anger and seething hatred Joined a religious cult and started experiencing intermittent mania

At 15 sent to psychiatrist by a school guidance councillor for major mood swings and religious fanaticism No parent was involved Diagnosed manic depressive Offered lithium which I declined in favour of keeping the "highs"

Forgot about it and went on with my "mercurial" life

At 18 Abandoned by "mistress turned stepmother" and her 3 children. I was dropped off at University, disowned by father and put on Student welfare/and arranged Government loans and worked restaurant overnight shifts to finish a Bachelor of Arts degree. Quit marijuana in first year because I had bad paranoia on it and a psychotic event with hashish Drank alcohol and smoked cigarettes heavily

At 20 Became torridly involved with a married man 21 years my senior w 4 kids After 4 years of a stressful affair, I lost a ton of weight, became severely hypoglycemic, was fainting all over the place Solved it with hospital low glycemic diet and lifetime of avoiding (or trying to) sugar and for a long time caffeine Married man moved in and we had a mostly On but also dramatically and stressfully Off Commonlaw relationship that lasted 21 years

At age 21 had emergency Pilonoidal cyst removal surgery
Suspected hypothyroidism but testing was deemed low normal Still constipated, very dry skin, low basal body temp to this day, low blood pressure

At 24 I moved to Toronto, became a Realtor Lots of parties w alcohol some cocaine, still smoked cigarettes and lots of money and depressive periods Had a giant brick of a car phone in 1985 and cell phone ever since

At 30 moved to NYC w same older man/commonlaw husband Joined a Mystical School and began doing spiritual work and meditation seeking relief from depression and angry rages mixed with manic partying Quit smoking and for months at a time and for about 5 years kept sober, sugar and caffeine free

Worked at a NYC nightclub Used ecstasy excessively recreationally and was living in the night Sleeping every other night Eventually using 1/4 tab ecstasy every other day for depression Manic phases started to become extreme irritability Started gaining weight Used Jenny Craig to lose 25lbs gained back immediately

1998 Husband returned to Canada

In the year 2000 (age 42) Did Atkins Diet at Atkins Centre of Complementary Medicine NYC Diagnosed Hypothyroid and started Natural Dessicated Thyroid Had amalgam fillings changed by Huggins Protocol and Chelation Therapy w dmsa/vit c IV, started on Bioidentical progesterone Tested thyroid releasing hormone because thyroid was intermittently normal and low and couldn't lose the weight

In December 2001 after 911 (lived 30 streets from ground zero and watched the 2nd plane hit) had major depression with suicidal ideation, Dr Atkins sent me to Psychopharmacologist where I got diagnosis of Bipolar (rapid cycling) ->Started Lamotragine 25 mg and Quetiapine (after a few sleep meds) For the first time I could focus enough to read without major difficulty Obsessively read about BiPolar trying to prove I wasn't and how to not take meds for life

Realized that sleep was key so I forced 10-6 sleep cycle using ambien Became intuitively aware that NYC was making me sick

In 2005 Age 47 moved back to Toronto. HATED it Went back into Real Estate Gained 35 lbs, at times gaining rapidly (a lb/day) for no reason Also had days with hypersensitive sense of smell Constant low grade depression, low energy, constipation, dizzy spells and brain fog Saw a PhD Biochemist Nutritionist who advised 1200 mg magnesium, 10,000 iu d3, Evening Primrise Oil, Citicholine, Inositol amongst other supplements that I took for years

Still taking Bioidentical progesterone, I added testosterone, Dhea, Oxytocin (for awhile), injectable HGH (for awhile) Added estrogen eventually

2009 Consulted with Amen Clinic Found a psychiatrist to order a Spect Scan (results below) hoping to manage the BP with nutraceuticals instead of meds Confirmed BiPolar Diagnosis and also PTSD OCD ADD Kept taking Lamotragine added Cymbalta (for depression and chronic pain)changed Quetiapene to Trazodone

On the personal front I met current husband whose DIL had just had his/our baby grandson We bonded and I provided full time childcare weekdays

In 2010 my mostly estranged Father died Engaged in a stressful estate settlement for 2 years becoming estranged from sister in the process (for two years after)

Married in 2011 Was mostly euthymic for a couple years Husband Retired from 37 years policing to fulfill my "travel in an Airstream" dream In 2012 I lost 70 lbs using Bernstein Weight Loss program injecting b12 and b6 every other day, on 900 calories locarb Lo fat subsistence protein and was pretty happy for a few years. Quit drinking for a year and a half.

I had a basal carcinoma removed from my nose (pretty much the only place I'd used sunscreen)I had also used sunglasses but hardly ever. Planning to rent my house and travel long term, we found out the foundation had turned to sand Managed a long, stressful and expensive Reno replacing basement under the house Started finding the stress of Real estate too much and having cycles of depression, irritability and rage Self medicating with alcohol didn't help. I semiretired to provide fulltime daycare for baby grandson until he was 4 Embraced my role as grandmother and enjoyed it

2017 Husband took a job he really wanted and I bought us a Beach House on Lake Erie and bnb'ed our Toronto home. Husband diagnosed with colon cancer 3 months after starting/moving. To "save his life", I obsessively researched the internet and fixated on Morley Robbins' magnesium group desperate to cure cancer with nutrition! I followed the Root Cause Protocol for 5 years, in that group I found Dr Jack. I quit the 10k iu vit d Id been taking for 3 years. I maxed beach living, sunsets and sunrises, and lived rural with very low nnemf Depression persisted and intermittent rage

2018 Had an accident in the water where several tendons/ligaments in both knees landed me in a wheel chair unable to strengthen one without reinjuring Eventually found a rehab program at hospital and devoted to achieving physical fitness through Aquafit and strength classes knowing I'd eventually need knee replacement

2020 Covid STRESS believing the pandemic threat for first few months Got COVID/or a flu? Test was negative and saw the public health incongruencies and then the WEF "plan" Avoided the vax and stopped complying! I gave up RealEstate completely.

Realized Jack was "the real thing" somewhere around that time and was living a mostly outside life tanned, gardened, canned and preserved food for the "expected food shortages" and did Aquafit

Was diagnosed with High Ocular pressure and monitored for glaucoma

Started 5 year taper off Cymbalta thinking it might be responsible for the rages. Realized a few years in it also might've been causing ocular pressure

Kept up Aquafit which had become the only exercise routine to ever give me a neurotransmitter high

Marriage started falling apart Caught him online cheating during pandemic

2022 We separated. He moved back to Toronto and after 10 months I sold beach house, I moved back in with him planning to divorce but doing couples therapy and having lots of episodic rages My knees were both painful with Osteoarthritis-but I was tanned, had darkened my separate bedroom, wore blueblockers and installed screen softwares, wifi timer, increased use of airplane mode and pointed my OCD at DIY Jack Kruse but living in Depression and Social Isolation except for my 3 grandchildren and daughter-in-law who were (and still are) enmeshed in a nasty and stressful (for all of us) divorce.

Jan 23 Had left knee replacement

May 23 Blown away by Jack's Tetragrammaton/Huberman podcast Resumed obsessive tanning, sunrise and outdoor Jack podcasts everyday

June 23 Fell and broke right ankle and severely tore right rotator cuff (bicep longhead severed, subscapularis and supraspinatus with major tears) Had double malleolus pin and plate repair surgery Wheelchair bound 100% for 2 months and mostly for 6 months Suffered extreme neglectful abandonment by husband Rehabbed by sitting in open sliding glass doors until I could drag myself downstairs to ground in sun in yard in bikini, read Leptin Rx and Jack's blogs and X posts Gained another 25lbs on top of the 50 extra I was already carrying

January '24 Had rotator cuff repair surgery Months of painful sleeplessness and physio rehab and massage and chiropractic which still continue as compensatory muscle and tendon stresses erupted

While Opthamologist was monitoring for Glaucoma and because I was experiencing blurred vision, Found retinopathy and optical Nerve Damage in left eye by MRI (Results below)

Still in Psychotherapy for extreme stress w/husband Started CT in October 24 and other principals from Jacks Water Muse X post and Vagus Nerve Rx and reduced Cortisol significantly Depression still extreme but rage returned to cycles of manageable irritability

Dec 24 Osteopathy starts bringing relief to shoulder pain and compensatory (I guess) right sacroilliac pain

Started Methylene Blue at .5mg First dose was awesome euphoria and energizing Gradually increased to 1.5 mg. Eventually found Jacks warnings about messing with it and it appeared to be causing rage at 1.5 mg Stopped it

Regular CT has decreased most of the rages

Did 7 years back taxes and refinanced to pay off 100k in taxes and took out 100 in equity from my house to buy bitcoin

Jan25 Saw something Jack said about not taking MB unless you see sunrise Since I was, I started it again and it definitely precipitated rage at only 1mg

Started having mild anxiety attacks Started tapering off the 50 mgTrazodone and adding CBD/CBN instead for sleep and chronic pain (I get some relief from PEA)I think the cbd has carried over into a calmer demeanor during the day too

Now I'm squirreling away bitcoin, researching El Salvador, (although I don't feel physically able to move most of the time)decluttering in hopes of moving, and bingeing Jack Kruse

**Current dissatisfactions**:

FAT, LOW ENERGY No motivation, NO JOY I crave carbs (although I've been really disciplined for several months now and seem to have successfully switched my eating window to between 8am and 3pm-4 with blood sugar more stabilized than it's been in 40ish years) Still Depressed (Dopamine must be low cause sometimes I don't even have motivation to even comply with my KrusePHD lessons and can't get myself to Aquafit class)Any waves of euthymic mood are few and far between and Mania is always irritability Predominant mood is irritable and detached or depressed and detached. I'm pretty socially isolated and in a constant battle against sedentary inertia. I have a drawer full of exit plan.

I've been in almost constant pain for many months and just a couple weeks ago seemingly unrelated to anything, the pain decreased by 80% (my guess is it's BP related and I'm grateful it's over but fear it's a new part of a cycle called Depression hurts) Muscle and tendon tears seem to keep happening, most recently my previously good shoulder without any conscious injury or strain and the odd time I get a weird seizing or cramping from the shin below my knee replacement, and eye twitches and above one side of my mouth (Since reading the demyelination series of blogs I wonder if that's why)

I just increased my thyroid meds (bloodwork showed low normal)(wondering if I should switch out NDT to Levothyroxine cause I read somewhere it can help depression in BP patients) And have had this itching of the skin on my shoulders and now arms for the last year and a half that slowly gets worse and doesn't really respond to any lotion, oil, except slightly I think to hypochlorous acid spray and castor oil? Not life threatening but super annoying and an indication of? I dunno

**Current Maintenance:**

AO Chiropractor adjusts c2 which rotates biweekly

Regular Chiro every two weeks

Osteopath once a week

Natural Dessicated Thyroid 60mg

Lamotragine (25mg) Trazodone (tapering off-9.5 mg currently)

Bioidentical hormones: prog, test, bi-est, Dhea, pregnenalone.

PEA for pain (3-4x400/day)

80/40 mg Cbd/Cqbn for sleep (get 1.5-2 hrs deep m, average 1:45 REM 7-9 total)

I never miss Sunrise with grounding, am still out there for uva and just added breath hold meditation last week Am tanning in spring summer and fall and have the doors open most of the time until deep winter, I do CT (but like 15-20 minutes at 50°-55°) Blue blockers an hour before bed (which is before sunset now and as soon as sunsets in winter)

I have Iris on my laptop and The BlueLight Diet filters on my phone, a DaylightCo tablet for reading Jack, Sunroof open when driving, Sliding glass doors open in house, Wifi off at night

I eat epiPaleo in season (75% compliance) and have been locarb for 45 years (with bipolar discipline)

Drink 1 cup of coffee in am

Self medicate w 2-3 hard seltzer daily

Marriage has started to have glimmers of supportive rather than just stressful It's hard to be decisive about ending a (second)marriage when MY brain is BP

**2009 SPECT results:**

mild decreased perfusion to the left parotid lobe, small perfusion defects high convexity, right infraorbital frontal, occipital lobe midline, left medial temporal lobe, left anteroinferior cerebellum and bilateral infratemporal lobes as well as scattered hyperfusion throughout the cortex and marked hyperfusion appreciated in the thalamus,, right basal ganglia and left frontal lobe anteriorly as well as the posterior cingulate gyrus with mild-moderate hyperfusion to the anterior cingulate gyrus and marked hypofusion to the cerebellum

**NeuroOpthomology Report**:

Followup for mild left optic neuropathy: Mitochondrial genomic sequencing was normal. Testing for heavy metals was normal as well (mild elevated Thallium)

Evidence of subtle left optic neuropathy with relative afferent pupillary defect, mild temporal pallor of the left optic disc which corresponded to thinning and pericapillary OCT.

Upon re-examination and pending MRI results, we would assume that she has had mild left non-arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy in the past

**2024-MRI:**

Tecnique: Multisequence multiplanar MRI imaging of the orbits with contrast

Diffusion imaging does not demonstrate any evidence of acute or subacute parenchymal brain ischemia.

The calibre of the optic nerves is symmetric. The optic chiasm is also symmetric in appearance. There is no abnormal signal or evidence of compressive lesion, the cavernous sinuses are clear. The orbital spaces are clear.

**Wondering** if I should switch to lithium? Or Lithium orotate?

If I should take the "miracle" Ivermectin? The expensive but likely helpful DDW? Get a magnetico ?

I do regular Blood donation. Wonder if it really is good for removing excess iron as per Morley Robbins?

I have to take Magnesium or I literally will not shit for more than a week, soooo its a solution but is it also a problem?

I also wonder if my cco is just permanently broken? Like, even when you sit in sunrise can you still be Warburg shifted?

Paid for a consult with Jack! Yay! Finally pulled the trigger! Can hardly wait but also bracing for a punch in the mouth I haven't considered.

Thanks for reading. The search for balance continues.

### Jojoplachta — 2025-11-08T20:42:22+0200
