### Jojoplachta — 2026-03-07T21:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31472/post-368357>

I've been so stuck since I got back from ES! Feeling like I'm too old and sore and low energy (dopamine) to make the change I know intuitively I need to to slow down the physical and mental deterioration all my reading of Jack's thesis has helped me diagnosis. My current bipolar swing is between planning to uproot and move and preparing to curl up and die here DOH! Spring in Toronto is slowly rolling in...resolving to capitalize on the slight increase of dopamine to ACT!

### Jojoplachta — 2026-03-07T21:32:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31472/post-368359>

Posting this reply from Jack to a new member Sara so I can read it over and over
[Jack Kruse said:](https://forum.jackkruse.com/goto/post?id=368264)
Change is excruciatingly difficult for low agency people because their biological system lacks dopamine creation in their frontal lobes due to a lack of sun. A low dopamine frontal lobe consistently overestimates the value of what they presently have while simultaneously underestimating the value of what they may gain by giving that up now. They do not understand opportunity cost in anything. Unless you are prepared to give up something valuable to you right now, you will never be able to change at all. You’ll remain forever in control of the things you cannot give up. Low dopamine = low agency and it binds us to this reality and keeps us unskilled in communicating with ourselves or nature. To become a decentralized thinker requires solar dopamine optimization.

[View attachment 34509](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/34509/)

This perspective on **agency and dopamine** as a function of light is a powerful lens for understanding why breaking the cycle of night shifts and metabolic disease feels impossible.
The image above is, Edgar Albert Guest’s poem "It Couldn't Be Done", is the anthem for the **High Agency** individual. But as I point out, "buckling in with a bit of a grin" requires the neurochemical fuel to do so. Sunlight provides that juice. Your spouse's pilot light is likely out as your is.

**1\. The Dopamine "Valuation" Error**
In a low-dopamine state (often driven by that "broken retinal clock"), the frontal lobe's **reward-prediction error** system glitches.

**Status Quo Bias:** When dopamine is low, the brain perceives the "effort" of change as a survival threat. It clings to the known (even if painful, like the night shift or chronic GERD) because it lacks the "chemical optimism" to visualize a better future state.

**Opportunity Cost Blindness:** You cannot calculate what you are losing (health, longevity, vision) because your brain is stuck in a low-power, "emergency" mode. It prioritizes the immediate paycheck or the comfort of habit over the long-term gain of solar-syncing.

**2\. Low Agency as a Biological Trap**
If the **RPE-SCN-Frontal Lobe** pathway isn't being "charged" by sunlight, the individual becomes biologically reactive rather than proactive.

**Communication Breakdown:** You mentioned being "unskilled in communicating with ourselves." This is because internal communication requires **GABAergic tone** (to quiet the noise) and **Dopaminergic drive** (to signal importance). Without light, the signal-to-noise ratio in the brain collapses.

**The Control Paradox:** We think we are "in control" of our routines, but as your text suggests, we are actually **controlled by the things we refuse to give up.** In a diabetic/night-shift context, this is often the artificial environment that provides a sense of false security while slowly draining the biological battery.

**3\. Decentralized Thinking = Solar Autonomy**
To become a "decentralized thinker" means moving away from a system that dictates your light/dark cycles (like a hospital night shift) and returning to the **source**.

**Solar Optimization:** Sunlight isn't just for Vitamin D; it's the specific stimulus that manufactures the dopamine needed for agency.

**The "Sacrifice":** Real change always requires giving up something valuable (perhaps the current work schedule or modern comforts) to reclaim the biological ability to **choose**.

The "thousands who tell you it cannot be done" are often those stuck in the same low-dopamine, low-agency loop. They are projecting their own biological limitations onto your potential.

# Thread: Magnetic Fields From Excess Ground Current
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JDMurray — 2025-08-11T01:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-357957>

Have any of you heard of a way to resolve magnetic fields from what seems to be excess ground current in the neighborhood- a field with no obvious direct source, no utility lines, just permeates when the heat of summer is greatest? A building biologist we spoke to mentioned large farms directing it with copper lines but was very vague. We are in a single family home so it may be impractical or impossible with a small lot.

Measures mid afternoon until night 1.5 to 2.5 mG, varies by summer day, around .5-.8mG when summer passes. We are using an Alpha Labs UHS2. Turning off the home breaker doesn't drop it one bit, repeated several times with the same results. Not from water pipes, those are in the front and measure no current on them. Doesn't diminish much from side to side (.2-.4mG at times maybe) or in back yard. We tried digging along the side of the house just to see if we found anything and found nothing. Power lines are buried along the front by the road and drop off within a few feet, but this picks up midway to the backyard and just stays. The Texas 811 line for utilities came out and marked all lines and showed maps with nothing running where we are measuring. When the power went out to the whole neighborhood the readings were at .2mG so I think this being excess ground current makes sense at this point. The question is, is there anything practical that can be done to mitigate this?

### Rusty — 2025-08-31T08:18:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-358940>

I doubt there's much you can do. The only fix is if the utility improves the neutral return system.

Meanwhile, Find quiet zones & use them for bedrooms/ long stay spaces.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-31T13:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-358944>

> [JDMurray said:](/goto/post?id=357957)
>
> Have any of you heard of a way to resolve magnetic fields from what seems to be excess ground current in the neighborhood- a field with no obvious direct source, no utility lines, just permeates when the heat of summer is greatest? A building biologist we spoke to mentioned large farms directing it with copper lines but was very vague. We are in a single family home so it may be impractical or impossible with a small lot.
>
> Measures mid afternoon until night 1.5 to 2.5 mG, varies by summer day, around .5-.8mG when summer passes. We are using an Alpha Labs UHS2. Turning off the home breaker doesn't drop it one bit, repeated several times with the same results. Not from water pipes, those are in the front and measure no current on them. Doesn't diminish much from side to side (.2-.4mG at times maybe) or in back yard. We tried digging along the side of the house just to see if we found anything and found nothing. Power lines are buried along the front by the road and drop off within a few feet, but this picks up midway to the backyard and just stays. The Texas 811 line for utilities came out and marked all lines and showed maps with nothing running where we are measuring. When the power went out to the whole neighborhood the readings were at .2mG so I think this being excess ground current makes sense at this point. The question is, is there anything practical that can be done to mitigate this?

You need to demonstrate objectionable current is present under the NEC that way the utility provider must do something about it.. Have you checked current on.your grounding electrode? Also have you done a remote earth test e.g. N to E voltage at service vs remote temporary earth electrode to N?

### JDMurray — 2025-09-04T21:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-359137>

Thanks for the replies, I will check for current on the grounding electrode and the service vs remote N to E voltage.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-15T00:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-podcasts-from-uncle-jack.31475/post-358250>

The podcast is live:

We all paint the picture of our lives with our actions and our perspectives, yet few of us realize we are the artist. What is important in life is life is how we choose to perceive the canvas; being happy doesn't mean that everything is perfect. It means that I have decided to look beyond the imperfections. People with the right perspective see opportunity everywhere, and they see problems as possibilities.

We see the rainbow not just the storm. We see the potential in people. People in life are like gold. We must dig through a lot of dirt in order to experience their magic. When we find gold we must value it; look at the present moment with them as a gift, and stop using the future with them to escape the present. We need to marvel at the gifts we have now and everyday. We see the big picture in life. We realize the more we lift and light up others the more depth of field our painting takes on. The more we do this the more people are drawn to see how "we paint" our canvas.

We are here to value life and the "right perspective" will often change everything.......a narrow perspective limits your vision of what is possible. It puts your mind in a box that is hard to escape. In life, we are always in the middle of someone's perspective, so we have to always stay in the shadow of our eternal promises, so we that we are at the head and not the foot of their perspectives.

This is where the magic happens with others. As long as our perspective deals with what is beneficial and uplifting for others, there's really no problem at all. We can live in harmony with nature and with ourselves. Make these moments important, vital, and worth living.

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 17

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-11T16:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-357979>

So apparently Google is making it much more difficult for Graphene OS to implement the new Android updates onto their OS. Particularly if you use a Pixel device.

Is anyone on here tech savvy enough to know whether using Graphene OS is still the play or if there's an alternative custom OS that could be better now?

OR, if Pixel is still the best phone to run Graphene on or if an alternative is now better?

Here's a few YT videos on the topic:

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-11T19:08:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-357991>

If you have to use a cell phone, I recommend the Mudita Kompakt. It has an Eink screen that looks great in the sun and offline+ mode which cuts the cell modem and microphones at the hardware level and also cuts the camera, bluetooth, and wifi at the software level.

Privacy when using any cell phone is an illusion.

### hank — 2025-08-11T19:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-357992>

From my understanding, Pixel is the only phone Graphene will work with. I have an 8 pro with Graphene and havent noticed any issues so far with the recent update, version 16, Aug 5.
If you are on twitter, follow Graphene, they are quite active there with the latest info

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T01:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358006>

Why would you want to use Google's Pixel hardware?
You get a blue lit screen and only software level cut offs for the modem, wifi, and bluetooth no matter what OS you install.

Anything that is cut off at the software level can be remotely turned on/off without your knowledge.

### DLO — 2025-08-12T02:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358008>

> [JacqueAwef said:](/goto/post?id=357991)
>
> If you have to use a cell phone, I recommend the Mudita Kompakt. It has an Eink screen that looks great in the sun and offline+ mode which cuts the cell modem and microphones at the hardware level and also cuts the camera, bluetooth, and wifi at the software level.
>
> Privacy when using any cell phone is an illusion.

I wouldn't waste money on an e-ink phone then again the only time i am using the phone is when i'm outside or i have blueblockers on if i'm inside, but thats just me. Privacy concerns I can understand esp since btc is pushed so hard here. The best wallet with no tracking i've found is token pocket but as with any update it could change.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T02:37:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358009>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=358008)
>
> I wouldn't waste money on an e-ink phone then again the only time i am using the phone is when i'm outside or i have blueblockers on if i'm inside, but thats just me. Privacy concerns I can understand esp since btc is pushed so hard here. The best wallet with no tracking i've found is token pocket but as with any update it could change.

I can't see your logic in how the features I listed would be a waste of money.

Offline+ mode offers you better privacy with physical hardware power cut offs for the cell modem and microphones. Even if the phone is hacked, they can't turn those back on at the software level. The eink screen allows you to not wear blue blockers which are a band aid. Looking at a blue lit screen with blue blockers on still exposes you to artificial light, just not blue artificial light.

### DLO — 2025-08-12T03:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358010>

Not a band aid at all when 80% of the time i'm indoors using my phone is at night. You are still going to need a light source when using e-ink in the dark.
My old xcover pro has a battery that comes out if i want privacy. If you could show me the difference with your fancy offline+ vs airplane mode you might have a case.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T03:16:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358012>

Is that how you access the internet at night or just for communicating with people through text or calls?
If it's just for calls, do you have a landline or voip ethernet option so you can avoid the cell phone?

Candle light works well enough for eink. I also realize most people won't want to resort to using candles.

Are you recommending they use the same cell phone that you have vs. the one that I recommended?
The Google Pixels that Graphene OS works on don't have a removable battery or hardware cutoffs.

### DLO — 2025-08-12T03:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358013>

Calls are done through laptop only hardwired.
I'm not really browsing at night with the phone it's just more so to pick the mp3 i want to play and thats about it.
I"m not recommending anything other that e-ink phone is a waste of money for me.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T06:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358020>

Anthony S is looking for recommendations on whether to use Graphene OS on a Pixel or if there is another OS that is preferred. I chimed in that the hardware is important and recommended to not use the Pixel and use the Mudita Kompakt instead. I don't like cell phones, but for people that want to use one, I think this option is better than most. Just my opinion of course.

What would you recommend for him?

### Michalis — 2025-08-12T12:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358027>

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T18:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358042>

Here's the study that compared a few different android OS including /e/OS.

[https://www.scss.tcd.ie/Doug.Leith/Android\_privacy\_report.pdf](https://www.scss.tcd.ie/Doug.Leith/Android_privacy_report.pdf)

Graphene or Calyx was not studied by them.

They mention, "In summary, we find that /e/OS collects essentially no data and in that sense is by far the most private of the android OS variants studied."
"Essentially no data" Take that with a grain of salt.

Any government can collect whatever data they want about you. Corportations will bend over in a second to give them this data so they can continue doing business. They do not care about you or your privacy at the end of the day when pushed.

As much as I love open source. Open source operating systems only make it easier for hackers to access your data as the person who wants to hack the code has free access to the code.

Using any of these cell phones is a security, privacy, and extreme health risk.

### pfedorow — 2025-08-12T18:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358045>

I think the flicker is too high on pixels. Been searching for alternative.

> [JacqueAwef said:](/goto/post?id=358042)
>
> As much as I love open source. Open source operating systems only make it easier for hackers to access your data as the person who wants to hack the code has free access to the code.
>
> Using any of these cell phones is a security, privacy, and extreme health risk.

That is completely not true , using a major operating system is the bigger risk. Hackers will spend more time attacking them as security holes are published many months sometimes years before a fix is implemented.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T19:06:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358046>

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=358045)
>
> I think the flicker is too high on pixels. Been searching for alternative.
>
> That is completely not true , using a major operating system is the bigger risk. Hackers will spend more time attacking them as security holes are published many months sometimes years before a fix is implemented.

Eink is the way to go for screens these days. They are definitely not perfect in terms of user experience, but if anything, it gets you off the phone/computer and doing other things.

The Daylight DC-1 also has an interesting screen technology, but unfortunately uses an Android OS with google apps.

Yes, closed source and open source OS can be hacked.
Using any of these cell phones is a security, privacy, and extreme health risk.

### hacklose — 2025-08-19T02:49:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358451>

I have a Mudita Kompakt and really like the offline+ mode which actually disconnects the power going to the modem and mics. The eink screen is nice too. I leave it in offline+ mode almost all of the time.

For texting, I slide the offline+ mode switch up so I can receive texts. Then after they come in, I slide the switch down, putting the phone back on offline+ mode. I type all of the text, then slide up the offline+ mode switch, hit send on all the pretyped text, wait for it to send, then put the switch down again. This way I only expose myself to a few seconds of radiation.

It's not a perfect phone and the keyboard is slow for texting. I barely use it, mostly use a landline, so it doesn't really bother me much.

### DLO — 2025-08-22T02:55:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358605>

New to the whole Pixel thing was looking at the above phone and wondering whats the point of them offering updates till 2030 or whatever date if google is cracking down?

### hacklose — 2025-08-22T20:10:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358628>

Using google hardware in the first place doesn't even make sense.

# Thread: Introduction / Observations of a nascent Mitochondriac
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-observations-of-a-nascent-mitochondriac.31480/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JasonAppel — 2025-08-12T01:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-observations-of-a-nascent-mitochondriac.31480/post-358005>

Hi Everyone,

I'm Jason. I'm a new member here. I've been following Uncle Jack on podcasts for awhile and joined with the Patreon membership a few months ago to read the blogs. I jumped into last months Q&A which I found valuable and informative with questions about my outsized histamine responses to mosquitos and "allergens"

I'm still getting a sense of this forum, the enormity of which gives the effect of drinking from a firehose. Nevertheless I'd like to post some personal observations here as a journal of sorts which hopefully is A) appropriate for this forum and B) valuable to other members.

Observation 1. Inability to maintain the tan

Of late, I'm paying very close attention to the degree to which my skin is getting and maintaining a tan. This past Saturday I spent all day out in the sun without any need for shade and noticeably increased my tan. Yesterday was spent outside for a good part of the day as I went to a local Bitcoin BBQ meetup but I certainly got a lot less sun. As an aside, I never wear sunglasses or sunscreen and have seen the sunrise almost every single day in the past 12 months. Yesterday and today, involved a few hours of work which I do on the computer while either outside or on the screened porch. I'm less tan than I was Saturday even before getting a ton of sun and believe this is probably not good. I'm ignorant as to the decentralized wisdom on this topic but generally gather that my body is pulling in whatever melanin was cultivated.
I'd like to ask, if this is indicative of some health issue (poor redox, excessive blue light, excessive nnEMF) and if so what is the ideal move considering I'm spending my days outside sans-sunglasses and sans-sunscreen (SSaSSy) seeing the sunrise every AM and doing little to no light at night and generally going to sleep soon after dark.

How’s your nnEMF situation?
I’m no expert but have heard Jack on several Q&A’s talk about nnEMF causing the tan to disappear.

# Thread: HELLO..

### sunissol — 2025-08-12T12:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.31481/post-358028>

Hello everyone, I'm a little late since I managed to join this forum.

But now I'm finally happy with everything you share here. It takes me a little longer because it's not my language (I'm Spanish). But I'm getting by, unlearning and learning again.

I truly believe that this site is a gem.

That's why I can't let another day go by without giving infinite thanks to everyone, especially the creator and director of the orchestra.

I'm looking forward to visiting and experiencing the energy of El Salvador. Next November-December would be good.

I have a curious anecdote related to El Salvador. My mother passed away a year and a half ago at the age of 101, at home, without hospitals. Her last six months of life were quite sweet, and this had a lot to do with the kind and gentle care of a Salvadoran woman who emigrated here a few years ago. This woman gave her peace and was a breath of fresh air for her every morning. For that reason alone, El Salvador gives me good vibes. She's the first person from El Salvador I've met here.

Well, that's all for now. I'm still soaking up all this wisdom.

Hi, Everyone
I recently had a consultation with jack and he stressed that I have to get to El savador.

I live in florida and the plane flight to El Savador is only 3 hours but I have real concerns about flying for a few reasons.
1\. I have severe insomnia that is really easily influenced and I believe my insomnia started due to radation exposure when I was in the hospital. So anything concerning radation totally freaks me out.
2\. Just diagnosed with RA and not certain how my body will react to flying.
3\. I have an undiagnosed circulatory problem and im concerned about blood clots.

My husband and I have been looking into driving there and I just wanted to know if anyone else has driven to El Savador. Ive tried looking into other methods like cruises, ferrys,etc with no luck.

Sorry - I have not driven - but you may want to consider the following responses to your questions.

1) Jack had a recent blog on protection from radiation when flying which you should be able to access at Silver level: [https://optimalklubs.com/cpc-71-gold-yellow-as-a-pomc-radiation-shield-during-aiplane-travel/](https://optimalklubs.com/cpc-71-gold-yellow-as-a-pomc-radiation-shield-during-aiplane-travel/)
2) Read the jetlag RX and hack as many things as you can: [https://jackkruse.com/jet-lag-rx-human-flight-biohacking/](https://jackkruse.com/jet-lag-rx-human-flight-biohacking/)
3) I always wear DVT socks when flying to help with circulation in legs and I always get an aisle seat so I can get up and move around frequently. I drink lots of water and perhaps add some salt to retain water to counteract the dehydration of air travel. I eat seafood beforehand. A 3-hour flight is really not that long and once you are there you should be able to recover well. The drive will take you a very long time and you have to pass through several countries.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-13T17:19:16+0300

I don't know. If I had to guess I would say it's more about avoiding blue light at the wrong times.

So I'm thinking about it wrong somewhere, hence the original question.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:02:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heme-protein-damage-and-renovation.31485/post-358139>

**STRINGING THE LESSONS ON THE FORUM:**

The K-T (Cretaceous-Paleogene) extinction 66 million years ago, from an asteroid impact in the Yucatan (Factor X), caused prolonged darkness, global cooling, and initial hypoxia from dust-blocked photosynthesis. UV light was blocked.

Non-avian dinosaurs and eutherian mammals faced maladaptive HIF-1 surges under stress, leading to energy depletion and extinction. Mammals survived via small size, nocturnal habits, endothermy, and flexible diets, thriving in low-light niches during a "nocturnal bottleneck."Post-K-T, sunlight's return spurred algal blooms, surging atmospheric oxygen and supporting higher metabolic rates.

This "mitochondrial world" favored heme protein adaptations, like efficient hemoglobin for oxygen affinity in endotherms. What are the absorption spectra of most heme proteins and why might you think this is the case?

**Heme Proteins and POMC Gene Connection:** Heme, tryptophan-containing for oxygen handling (e.g., cytochrome C in oxidative phosphorylation), linked to the POMC gene in mammals. POMC, located on human chromosome 2p23 (conserved in primates), encodes peptides like ACTH, α-MSH (for pigmentation and melanin synthesis), and β-endorphin for stress and energy regulation. UV light stimulates POMC expression and cleavage, enhancing melanin production and related functions. **_All heme proteins on the IMM are loaded with red light chromophores. CCO has 4 of them embedded in it._**

Heme influences POMC via circadian regulators Rev-Erb-alpha/beta, integrating oxygen metabolism with light-driven rhythms. Post-K-T oxygen surge amplified this, enhancing mammalian neuroendocrine adaptations for diverse environments.

# Thread: Mother and Son in need of answers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31495/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1

### american_shaolin — 2025-08-14T16:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31495/post-358226>

Hello,
My name is Josh and I've been tasked with helping a mother and son who has issues with ELF and melanin reproduction. They live in a house without any electricity and are out in the sun all day. They are also a huge follower of Jack Kruse. She asked me to relay some questions from her, my apologies if this is in the wrong spot.

Her note:
I am afraid I became hypersensitive to the lowest level of ELF. You can see those pulses only on a very sensitive Cornet meter (most of them are not). (I have a few of them)
It shows 0002.–0003., but every 2-3 hours there are pulses of 0005. . All day and all night long.
It’s not because of the powerlines, cables, or electrical devices. I think they are all over the country (Portugal), even in the middle of the woods or the ocean. Unfortunately I can feel them in my body, they are causing a lot of pain. My condition becomes worse each day. There is no material to block them and nowhere to hide. RF I can block, so no need to get into that.
My questions are:

Dr. Kruse explained that earth has been electrifying since the 1900s. Is this the cause for those pulses? Is there something to block them?

Are those pulses of ELF the same everywhere in the world? Do they change in a different altitude?

I’m doing the sun therapy in UV light, drinking Q18 with flor de sal, trying to build melanin and enhance the solar callus but it feels like the ELF pulses of 0005. are keeping knocking off the melanin and melatonin in my body.
Is this a losing battle?
I fear that those pulses of 0005., which most people are not aware of and can’t even measure, are causing autoimmune diseases and when you become sensitive to them — game over.
I hope there is a way to overcome this condition. Please help. Thank you, Gallia

# Thread: El Salvador ocean swimming?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sirena — 2025-08-14T21:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/post-358240>

Where do mito ocean swimmers actually swim out past the waves?
vs hoping in an ocean pool or swimming pool.

Atami? El Palmacito? Shalpa? San Blas?
Where?

Thanks

### LoriLee — 2025-08-14T21:15:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/post-358241>

a regular person does not belong swimming out past the waves. I have been in shalpa many times and the policy is to not go out above your mid thighs, knowing that the wave heights come and go. It can be very dangerous if you get in a tide where more of your body mass is below water versus above water. Now, having said that, I have seen some surfers, people who are very versed with ocean swimming, go out way further than I think anyone should. I dont think shalpa is the place to do that though. with it being within a cove, the riptide is much worse there. Lots of surfers at el zonte so maybe that is the place? I dont really have an answer except please be careful!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-15T02:01:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/post-358255>

Sirena your question is flat retarded.

As such, free iron is stored as ferritin, and as it rises it acts as an antenna in you and transforms energy into non coherent UPEs = heat in you which drives heat higher = where hot flashes come from.

**1\. The Loss of the Monthly "Scrub"of mass**
For decades, the female body uses the menstrual cycle to offload **excess iron** and **deuterated metabolic water**.

**The Post-Menopause Trap:** When periods stop, the "exit valve" closes. Iron begins to accumulate in the tissues.

**Melanin Failure:** If **melanin** (the body’s primary broadband absorber and chelator) is depleted or "jammed" by glyphosate or nnEMF, it can no longer "caged" this free iron.

**2\. Ferritin as a "Dirty Antenna" like biology had to deal with in the GOE. Point made clear in the blogs.**

Free iron is toxic, so the body shoves it into **ferritin** storage.

**The Antenna Effect:** Ferritin is a nanocage with a mineral core of hydrous ferric oxide. In an environment saturated with **nnEMF**, these ferritin cages act as **paramagnetic antennas**.

**Energy Transformation:** They capture ambient electromagnetic frequencies and, because the system lacks the coherent **220nm UPE signals** (due to the "seed oil" D-PUFA interference), they transform that energy into **non-coherent infrared (heat)**.

**3\. The "Hot Flash" as Mitochondrial Exhaust**

This is where the "Hot Flash" originates. It is not a "brain glitch"; it is a **localized thermal dump**.

**Heteroplasmy & Heat:** As mitochondria age and **heteroplasmy** (mutated mtDNA) increases, they become less efficient at making "cold" ATP and more prone to "leaking" heat.

**The Flash:** When the "ferritin antennas" pick up nnEMF, they trigger a sudden discharge of this non-coherent thermal energy. The body attempts to dump this heat via vasodilation and sweating from the eccrine glands talked about in CPC #79 ---> the classic hot flash. Some women get a lot og GI symptoms in menopause -----> exocrine mass dump = CPC#79 too.

**4\. The Matrix Collapse**

This rising iron/ferritin level further poisons the **TCA cycle**, leading to more deuterium incorporation into the **collagen matrix**. This creates a feedback loop:

1.  **High Iron** = More nnEMF absorption.
2.  **More Heat** = Destruction of the **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water from CCO**
3.  **Low EZ Water** = Protons can't flow, leading to more saggy collagen, saggy breasts, fat, **ligament tears** and skin aging, (the "exhaust problem").

# Thread: Hello from Hokkaido, Japan
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### salva21m — 2025-08-15T09:18:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358270>

Hello everyone, I am writing from the distant land of Hokkaido, the Northern island of Japan. Originally from Lisbon, Portugal, and living in Japan since 2011.

I found Bitcoin first and at the time I was really happy to discover the best form of money. Bitcoin also brought me into the rabbit hole of Decentralized Health and Dr. Jack Kruse's teachings. Then I learned that "time" and our "health" are the scarcest assets in the world... never missed a sunrise since since becoming aware of that.

I am looking forward to continue learning with Dr. Kruse and everyone here in the Forum. I really hope that the knowledge of quantum biology can spread around the world!

### DLO — 2025-08-15T19:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358288>

I've always wanted to visit Japan... back when the food was still good and they had to start to cut corners to keep the prices the same or else locals would protest ....

### salva21m — 2025-08-16T00:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358320>

> [HLG2025 said:](/goto/post?id=358274)
>
> hi! i moved to tokyo from the united states in the beginning of this year. i thought i was the only one living in japan on this forum. i cant wait to visit hokkaido! i havent really gone far outside of tokyo yet but planning on it very soon.
> in regards to sunrises- im having a hard time dealing with the around 4:30 am sunrises here in japan! wondering if you wake up that early?
> excited to meet someone else in japan on here!

Hi, it's nice to know there are more mitochondriacs in Japan. Hokkaido is really nice, specially during summer, close to nature here. Hope you can visit one day. Yes I do wake up that early, no exception. I go to bed at 20:30 these days... I adapt my sleeping time depending on the sunrise time.

### salva21m — 2025-08-16T00:06:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358321>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=358288)
>
> I've always wanted to visit Japan... back when the food was still good and they had to start to cut corners to keep the prices the same or else locals would protest ....

Food is still quite good here and quite cheap compared with the cost of life in other countries. It's a good deal. Hope you can visit one day!

### DLO — 2025-08-21T03:53:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358571>

I'm sure they are importing USA Rice now by the boatload.... NOT

### salva21m — 2025-08-22T04:06:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358607>

It's getting more difficult to find local food due to the influence of big supermarket chains. As I am in Hokkaido, it is easier to find salmon and other fish from here as a source of DHA. Local meat and lamb also easier to find (I have to try the lamb as it is also big on DHA). I try to avoid carbs, but still when I eat rice or potatoes, I also focus on those from the region. I think Japan is still quite a good country to find local healthy food.

### DLO — 2025-08-22T04:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358608>

Salmon would not be native to Japan how about sardines or herring?

### salva21m — 2025-08-22T04:40:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hokkaido-japan.31500/post-358609>

There are many types of Salmon native to Japan and specially Hokkaido. the region's connection to salmon relates to the Ainu, the indigenous people of Hokkaido. They have a long-standing relationship with salmon, which was a crucial part of their diet and culture. Archaeological evidence from Hokkaido shows that people have been fishing for salmon in the region for nearly 10,000 years. In addition, you also can find the Pacific Herring and Japanese sardines.

# Thread: Overcoming Caffeine Addiction

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nick-Rolser — 2025-08-15T18:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358283>

I'm looking for some advice on how to overcome my caffeine addiction. My current understanding is that I'm low dopamine and that improving my redox is the answer. The problem, it seems, is that even when I get morning sunrise and spend as much time as possible outside, avoid ALAN, etc. my eczema/rosacea is preventing me from absorbing the UV to produce the melatonin to regenerate the tissues and create the melanin to raise my dopamine levels.

I have a pretty strong will but after many failed attempts to quit cold turkey I'm thinking its time to work smarter and not harder.

Should I be putting more emphasis on seafood? I eat it probably 2-3 times a week. Or maybe survivors soup? I've done some shellfish broth but haven't done bone broth yet.

Thanks in advance.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-15T19:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358294>

You overcome this addition by fixing the redox signaling on the IMM. A CCO VDR story laid out in the current blog series.

### Nick-Rolser — 2025-08-15T23:05:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358319>

Got it. Think I'll try to up my AM light intake and get even stricter with the evening light. Might try to maintain a carnivore diet for a little while to make more DDW.

### Nick-Rolser — 2025-08-18T15:32:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358404>

Just to give an update on this for anyone who might be interested: I'm now on day 3 of no caffeine, which felt almost impossible just a few days ago. The day I posted this I got a lot of sun, walked on the beach, totally cut out tech use after about 6:00 pm (only using my daylight PC and light phone I might add), and went to bed as soon as it got dark. Woke up in the morning and spent 15 min in the sun, followed by a high fat and protein meal before heading to work.

Definitely was not at my best cognitively on day 1 but that was to be expected. I thought I felt a headache coming on towards the evening time but as soon as I went outside after work and got some sun that went away.

Some other observations I found interesting: even though I wound up eating some foods I normally wouldn't (for example a sandwich on sourdough bread that probably had some seed oils in it) I woke up with hardly any inflammation or digestive issues that tend to occur when I've eaten these things in the past. I'd heard Jack talk about Alan Frey and blue light opening the gut barrier but as someone who values empiricism that was sort of definitive proof to me that light is the major problem.

That's a very good question. I have not measured with a Gauss meter, only with a cheaper device that detects electric and magnetic fields. I didn't detect anything egregious in my room but also am aware I need a better way to measure. Nevertheless its something I have little control over at the moment. I live in an apartment complex with many routers. Our router is on the other side of the apartment but I can't shut it off at night because I live with video game addicts. On the plus side, I live in a small town of about 10,000 people.

If all goes according to plan, I'll be moving out of this place in 4-5 months and heading down to the 28th latitude. Hoping to have a lot more control over such things then.

I prefer using the Cornet ED98QPro5G and the Safe and Sound Pro II in combination.

I use the Cornet for electric and magnetic fields and use both of them to measure RF. The Safe and Sound Pro II has a higher sample rate for RF than the Cornet, so it will register some of the higher peaks you are exposed to. If you can only afford one, buy the Cornet. It's cheap and works well.

I measure body voltage with a multimeter I used in back in the day on electrical projects.

# Thread: ENVIRONMENT OR FOOD?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environment-or-food.31508/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-16T04:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environment-or-food.31508/post-358327>

Your Job Could Be Shaving Years Off Your Life – And It's Not Just in Your Genes

Why does this matter? Genetics might be your blueprint, but your environment is the architect rewriting the plans. Experts are buzzing: Forget chasing gene therapies, because real wins come from fixing what's around us. Cleaner air, better jobs, kicking bad habits? These could add years to your prime.This isn't just science; it's a wake-up call.

Your cubicle, commute,your computer terminal, or corner store might be aging you faster than Father Time. Time to rethink: What in your world needs a upgrade?

Share if this hit home because we need to spread the word and live longer!Imagine this: You've got "good genes," but your daily grind, zip code, and bad habits are secretly accelerating your biological clock faster than a ticking bomb. A groundbreaking study of nearly 500,000 people just dropped the mic on aging, and it's a game changer.

Published in Nature Medicine, this massive analysis from the UK Biobank reveals that your environment – the "exposome" of everything from air pollution to workplace stress packs a bigger punch on how you age and die than your DNA ever could.

Key bombshells: 25 environmental culprits identified: Things like socioeconomic status, smoking, and even where you live are directly tied to "proteomic aging" (fancy term for how your proteins break down over time) and higher mortality risk.
Poverty and puffs steal your youth: Low income and smoking emerged as top villains, speeding up premature aging and death MORE than genetic factors. Lifestyle trumps luck: While genes play a supporting role, modifiable exposures like pollution, social isolation, and poor sleep drive disease risks way harder. Diet? Jury's out: The study couldn't nail down reliable diet data, because food really does not matter as much as many believe but environmental factors screamed "change me now!" for longer, healthier lives.

CITE
Integrating the environmental and genetic architectures of aging and mortality M. Austin Argentieri, Junhui Huang, Chirag M. Lakhani, Chirag J. Patel Nature Medicine, volume 31, pages 1016–1025 (2025) DOI: 10.1038/s41591-024-03483-9 Published online: 19 February 2025

The Science Behind It

# Thread: mother and son in need of answers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31509/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### american_shaolin — 2025-08-16T16:11:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31509/post-358333>

Hello,
My name is Josh and I've been tasked with helping a mother and son who has issues with ELF and melanin reproduction. They live in a house without any electricity and are out in the sun all day. They are also a huge follower of Jack Kruse. She asked me to relay some questions from her, my apologies if this is in the wrong spot.

Her note:
I am afraid I became hypersensitive to the lowest level of ELF. You can see those pulses only on a very sensitive Cornet meter (most of them are not). (I have a few of them)
It shows 0002.–0003., but every 2-3 hours there are pulses of 0005. . All day and all night long.
It’s not because of the powerlines, cables, or electrical devices. I think they are all over the country (Portugal), even in the middle of the woods or the ocean. Unfortunately I can feel them in my body, they are causing a lot of pain. My condition becomes worse each day. There is no material to block them and nowhere to hide. RF I can block, so no need to get into that.
My questions are:

Dr. Kruse explained that earth has been electrifying since the 1900s. Is this the cause for those pulses? Is there something to block them?

Are those pulses of ELF the same everywhere in the world? Do they change in a different altitude?

I’m doing the sun therapy in UV light, drinking Q18 with flor de sal, trying to build melanin and enhance the solar callus but it feels like the ELF pulses of 0005. are keeping knocking off the melanin and melatonin in my body.
Is this a losing battle?
I fear that those pulses of 0005., which most people are not aware of and can’t even measure, are causing autoimmune diseases and when you become sensitive to them — game over.
I hope there is a way to overcome this condition. Please help. Thank you, Gallia

# Thread: My Optimal Journal - Chris
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-chris.31514/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris Amen — 2025-08-16T20:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-chris.31514/post-358342>

I am 73 years old, 4’11” and have been careful what I eat my whole life as I am allergic to so many things, gluten, dairy, eggs, soy, peanuts, corn etc.

I live on 18 acres in Southern Oregon on the Rogue River. I have an organic garden with 30 fruit trees and grow a huge veggie garden and eat with the seasons. And I have done this for the last 35 years, even when I lived in Ojai, Calif. I mostly cook for myself because when I go out to eat, I often get sick.

My mother had me when she was 20 years old. In fairly good health, active but when I was 2 months old, she had appendicitis and that was the end of my nursing. She is still alive at 93 years old. I have struggled with eczema since I was a child but as long as I eat cleanly it is not a problem. I walk every morning at sunrise and sunset at least a mile. I work out with weights 3 times a week for the last 25 years. I struggle with my weight and have all my life. I have been up to 165 pounds for a small gal was way too much, but I am down to 118 now but it is difficult to keep my weight down.

I also have Osteoporosis even though I work out and am active. My labs are not bad but am pretty sure I am Leptin Resistant as I am hungry all the time.

I have signed up for a consultation with Dr. Kruse and am excited for the guidance. I have a lot of labs, MRI and a CT Cardio Score which was good.

I will put more information in this journal as I go.

### Chris Amen — 2025-08-16T21:02:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-chris.31514/post-358343>

I thought I should add some additional information. Since finding Dr. Kruse’s work, I have listened to over 20 podcasts. I have just completed the Webinar series for 2012 and listen to the last 3 PowWow’s. I am printing out the blogs and reading and putting them in a binder. Dr. Kruse is such a great teacher, but I have a long way to go to understand all of this. But am working on it.

I have had an EMF specialist out to my house to evaluate and mitigate problems in my home. Luckily all of my lights are incandescent. After dark, I try not to turn on the lights and if I need to, I have blue light blocker red lenses. I work many hours on the computer a day and have for years doing volunteer work for a non-profit organization that I have worked with over 30 years. I have cut that time down in half on all of my devices and have ordered a Daylight computer. In addition, I have put the Iris app on my computers. Added a blue light blocker to my TV and only watch with the red glasses.

I am working at eating Paleo and have doubled my protein intake and have been outside as much as I can and grounding as well. OK, I think this is a good beginning.

### GeMo — 2025-08-16T22:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-chris.31514/post-358345>

# Thread: Eyeglasses that let through UVB
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyeglasses-that-let-through-uvb.31515/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-08-16T22:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyeglasses-that-let-through-uvb.31515/post-358344>

I’ve heard some people on this forum and also in real life who claim that their glasses let through UVB because it doesn’t have UV coating on it, and that some plastics let through UVB.

I did some research and I don’t understand what this magical material is.

From my research, I see that the common materials are CR-39 plastic, Trivex, polycarbonate, and high index.

CR-39 blocks all UVB and part of UVA. Trivex and polycarbonate are claimed to block all UV. Same with high index.

The UVB coatings just block more UV, as far as I understand, but it doesn’t imply that if you forgo the coatings, all UV passes through. In fact I have a pair of standard lens (1.5 index) from Eyebuydirect, which I believe are CR-39. I measured the sunlight spectrum through the glasses with a spectrometer, and sure enough, the UVB range is truncated.

What material doesn’t block UVB? I only know of quartz, and that wouldn’t be a trivial eyeglass order to make.

I found somebody on X talking about Optix L-UVT acrylic for their windows to let in UVB, but that’s not something you can buy off the shelf for your eyeglasses. [https://x.com/mrjoecomo/status/1949525952058573162](https://x.com/mrjoecomo/status/1949525952058573162)

I know the solution is to not wear glasses. That’s what I do today working on my computer outside, but it’s annoying as I’m very myopic. I tend to sit 1-2 feet away from a 32” monitor to see.

### DLO — 2025-08-17T18:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyeglasses-that-let-through-uvb.31515/post-358381>

[Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07DHK5PGL?tag=consecho10340-20&linkCode=osi&th=1&psc=1&language=en_US)

purchase and measure with spectrometer
Or use windows key + for the magnify.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/felicia%E2%80%99s-journal.31518/post-358351>

I did not take the jab, and didn’t comply with the masks in 2020. Became a student of jack’s 2 months ago. Ever since having my oldest son the day of the lockdown I was more drawn to be outside with him. I’m fortunate that we created a habit of spending time outside and grounding. It was like I became a mother and I was incredibly drawn back to nature.

I have four boys (5,4,2 and newborn). Everyone around me is struggling to get pregnant, has had a miscarriage, or has a child thats severely sick. I feel everyone around me is struggling with fertility and yet it seems like we can’t prevent ourselves from getting pregnant even when we’ve tried. This is not a complaint, but an observation.

When I learned about Jack something clicked, and I started catching sunrises last spring (2024). I can say with certainty that my post partum recovery was the easiest, in fact I didn’t deal with ANY post partum hair loss. My midwife is concerned that I’ve never had a child 2 years apart (which I guess is the appropriate window to fully recover and rebalance your hormones) and has been suggesting a vasectomy or hysterectomy but we likely will not work against nature. I’m 34 and my husband is 28.

Since my children are always with me, I have noticed that they have rarely, and I mean rarely have gotten sick since starting catching sunrises. None of them have any vaccinations. I’m looking forward to logging what our winter looks like (I usually struggle with a short amount of seasonal depression).

### summerxo — 2025-08-17T01:49:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strong-sun-causing-what-i-think-is-vitamin-c-craving.31519/post-358354>

I've been following the protocols outlined by Jack for over a year now. I've always been curious why after about 45 minutes lying out in strong sun around solar noon, I get a very strong craving.

At first I thought the craving might be for water or salt or sugar. I tried different combinations of those things, but the only thing that quenches it is if I juice some lemon or lime and drink it.

I now have a routine of having three limes (in water with salt) at the ready at that time and always feel much better almost immediately after drinking it.

I think I heard Jack mentioned on a podcast that being in the sun increases your need for vitamin c? I've just always been curious about the mechanism of what I'm experiencing...

# Thread: Zach's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zachs-optimal-journal.31522/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ZachGray — 2025-08-18T01:43:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zachs-optimal-journal.31522/post-358393>

First off, thank you Uncle Jack- I'm happy to be here.

My road to optimal begins in 2018.

I left my job in Vermont to be a volunteer track coach at the University of Georgia. I was 24 years old. Working for free was a "necessary" evil at the time to climb your way into the collegiate coaching ranks due to NCAA restrictions on coaching staff sizes. Those restrictions that have since been lifted.

In 2019 I enrolled in graduate school earning a small stipend from UGA's athletic department. Graduate work was a loophole to at least give coaches in my position some sort of income. I'm a very patient person and these decisions were made with my long-term professional goals in mind. Short-term finances were on the back burner, and to my detriment I've never been money motivated anyway. This mindset was a critical mistake.

The grad program was complete bullshit, just necessary for the stipend but as Covid derailed a timely graduation I ultimately chose to drop out in 2021 thus resigning from my position with the team. I left UGA with only a few thousand dollars to my name, depressed and unsure about what was next, but I was unvaccinated and grateful to have avoided the madness happening in other states (so I thought) despite pressure from the team & considerable pressure from my anchors (family & ex-GF). It was quite clear there was a massive PsyOp occuring and I was not going to be fooled...

**...until my financial burdens wore me down and I learned the hard way health & wealth are directly tied together.**

I ended up accepting a coaching position with the US Ski Team, which was based in the US but traveled significantly. Despite it now being the summer of 2022 and travel restrictions eroding, the jab was a requirement. I looked into faking a card but desperation ultimately made a fool out of me. I got my first jab in Athens, GA in July 2022 as a card was needed to enter US Ski & Snowboards training center in Utah.

All my principles had been thrown out at that moment, the self-betrayal felt like a greater injury than whatever the jab would potentially do to me. I got my second jab in Park City, Utah in late September before boarding a flight to Europe where fear again made a fool out of me.

Never again.

Fast forward to January 2025. I'm on a plane to Germany with a new client (I now work with a group of NFL players) and he recommended Jack's first podcast with Danny Jones. I didn't listen to that one until March, but I found your Nourish VT series and my life has changed profoundly for it.

**The Next Epoch**
Since discovering your work Jack, my focus of the past six months has been on the health side of the coin, not only for my own vitality but considering how paradigm shifting the biophysics world view is, I've had a lot of catching up to do (and still do) as it's directly related to my work & the athletes I serve.

Now that the guys are all back with their teams my energy has shifted heavily to the wealth side of the coin. I'm new to bitcoin but the road to financial sovereignty is under way and it's paved with satoshis.

BTC BTC BTC & REDOX REDOX REDOX.

That's where my head is at right now. Making my way through the blogs, I printed out about a hundred of them to give to the guys and read myself.

I currently don't have a home base, I'm writing this from Vermont. For the NFL offseason I'm in Los Angeles, but it looks like we'll start next offseason in Hawaii. Would love to eventually have a training group in Central America during the offseason. I'm planning a trip to ES this fall to scout it out. Very interested in the 1 & 2 bedroom units you mentioned on the Q&A.

**Vitamin D** as of Feb 2025: **40** (certainly much higher now, aiming for 80+ year round)
**Spike Level** as of Aug 14th: **2141**

Thanks again, and onward.

### ZachGray — 2025-08-25T18:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zachs-optimal-journal.31522/post-358715>

For further context. I got two hits of Moderna. How is progress going with getting samples of every lot?
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Prior to discovering Jacks work, I thought I had a fairly holistic perspective on fitness. The world of sport/recreation has been my life- my father is a coach, my mother is a PE teacher, and I'm a track coach/trainer by profession guided by as much as possible, first principles thinking (albeit with a poor knowledge base of physics & mathematics ironically). Fitness, as I assume most would agree here is not a certain physique, athletic feat, or hobby.

**_fitness_** by the biological definition is:
_the ability of an organism to survive & reproduce in its environment_

Pre-Uncle Jack, my fundamentals or non-negotiables of fitness were:

Base of Maslow's hierarchy (air, food, water, shelter, clothing, sex)
Needs based theory (autonomy, competency, relatedness).

In addition to those objective foundations, I include my personal barometers of health which are:

Equanimity
Curiosity, Creativity, Courage, Collaboration (Neil Turok's four C's of innovation)
The ability to do what I want with great joy (play of any sort)
Openness to new knowledge changing ones behavior/thinking (could fall under curiosity as well)

One of my goals as a coach has been to create an environment for clients/athletes that stimulates growth in all of the above areas as they would directly support whatever goals they have on their fitness journey or in their athletic career.

In addition to that, my first and foremost priority when working with anyone is **_do no harm._**
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Post-Uncle Jack, this is a period of daily iteration & revising my bedrock of fitness to integrate:

Light, Water, & Magnetism
Circadian Biology
Financial sovereignty (bitcoin)

Light for instance may very well replace many of my previous "fundamentals" given it encompasses all material things ie food, shelter, clothing, technology.

**_My current dilemma_** as a coach is that my first priority is **_do no harm._** In a post-covid world that looks a lot different given our current understanding of the short & long term impacts of the vaccines.

If an estimated 72% of the global population took the covid vaccine, that's around a 72% reduction in possible clients for a coach that is guided by the precautionary principle & do no harm _provided all that coach has to offer is an exercise prescription_. There is an unfortunate opportunity to help people navigate the new world as a GMO human and as one of those GMO humans, I'm quite invested in A. doing everything to potentially reverse it as irreversible as it appears to be & B. master the skills of living and hopefully reproducing as a GMO human. The latter begs the question of what are the risks to the next generation if their parent (s) are vaccinated if we're not already infertile?

**[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/35481/) ,** you mentioned on a Q&A you wouldn't even consider exercising unless spike levels were below 5,000. How did you land on that value? What is the range of values you're seeing in people? I'm still making my way through the covid blogs, so perhaps that is answered in one of those.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-18T04:51:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unexpected-radiation-effects.31523/post-358400>

In the summer of 1978, at a secret Soviet facility near Moscow, young physicist Anatoli Bugorski was preparing for what he believed would be a routine task. He was working on the massive U-70 proton accelerator, a machine capable of propelling particles near the speed of light. That day, a safety light warning that the accelerator was active malfunctioned, and trusting his familiarity with the lab, Bugorski leaned in to inspect a part of the machine—right into the path of a high-energy proton beam. The beam pierced the back of his head and exited near his nose, carrying an energy hundreds of times greater than a lethal dose of radiation.

Miraculously, Bugorski did not die instantly. He later described the experience in words that would become legendary: “I saw a flash brighter than a thousand suns, but there was no pain.” In the hours and days following the accident, the left side of his face swelled grotesquely, his skin peeled, and he suffered permanent consequences: complete hearing loss in his left ear, partial facial paralysis, and later, epileptic seizures. Yet he remained conscious, aware, and functioning—defying the expected limits of human survival.

Doctors were astonished, as the dose of radiation he received should have been fatal within seconds. Despite his injuries, Bugorski completed his studies, earned a doctorate, and continued his career as a physicist in the Soviet Union, silently carrying the story of a man who survived an almost unimaginable scientific accident. His experience remains one of the most extraordinary events in the history of physics, a testament to the astonishing—and sometimes inexplicable—resilience of the human body. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-18T18:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unexpected-radiation-effects.31523/post-358423>

**The Absence of Pain: Neurobiology Meets High-Energy Physics**

Pain perception requires activation of nociceptors, specialized sensory receptors responsive to thermal, mechanical, or chemical stimuli—and subsequent signaling via the peripheral nervous system (PNS) to the central nervous system (CNS). The brain parenchyma itself lacks nociceptors; pain from intracranial events (e.g., headaches) arises from stimulation of dural, vascular, or meningeal structures. In Bugorski's case, the proton beam traversed from the occipital region through the left hemisphere, exiting near the nose, likely damaging cortical and subcortical tissues without directly engaging pain-sensitive meninges or vessels in a manner that triggered immediate sensation.However, your point about pain receptors in the beam's path is valid: the trajectory could involve scalp, skull, and extracranial tissues with abundant nociceptors. Why no pain?

Proton beams in accelerators like the U-70 deposit energy via ionization along a narrow track (Bragg peak phenomenon), causing microscopic burns and cellular disruption with minimal lateral spread. This "thin track" ionization, estimated at ~1 mm diameter in Bugorski's incident, may not have activated sufficient nociceptors for conscious pain registration, especially if the velocity (near light speed) rendered the damage instantaneous.

Post-event pain (swelling, peeling skin) aligns with delayed inflammation and tissue repair, as nociceptors respond to secondary cytokines and edema. My hypothesis is that pain requires light to be "slowed down"reframes this in quantum biological terms. The proton beam, while not electromagnetic "light," delivers energy akin to high-frequency radiation (equivalent to ~10^11 eV per proton), far exceeding biological chromophores' (e.g., cytochrome c oxidase in mitochondria) capacity for absorption and processing.

In my decentralized thesis, pain manifests when energy/information is transduced slowly enough for mitochondrial UPEs (ultraweak photon emissions) to collapse wave functions in neural microtubules. If the beam's energy overwhelms this, it bypasses slowdown via refractive index or dielectric constants in tissue. As a result no "time" elapses for pain signaling, as the system's entropy (ignorance) prevents registration.

**Subjective Entropy:** Ignorance as the Fabric of Reality

Entropy, as Renato Renner articulates, measures ignorance is a lack of knowledge about microscopic details, whether particle motion, code digits, or quantum states. This idea unifies thermodynamic entropy (Boltzmann's probabilistic disorder) with informational entropy (Shannon's uncertainty), both subjective and observer-relative. My thesis echoes this idea in every concept I teach you in blogs: entropy's apparent increase (e.g., oil-water separation) depends on the observer's resolution; a superhuman thermal viewer sees microscopic disorder rise. But few of you see it. This is why you all make the mostake comparing yourself and your issues to each other and it is futile to do so. It just creates disorder in thinking. No one is stacking this lesson as far as I can tell.

In Bugorski's case, the "observer" is his biological system, chromophores and mitochondria unable to track the beam's momentum due to its extreme energy. As I have pointed out a myriad of times, if melanin (a key chromophore) is "weak" or the energy too strong, the system loses track of information (Noether's theorem), manifesting as no immediate pain or time perception.

**This subjectivity has profound implications:**

1.  _Pain and Time:_ Pain requires temporal processing, nociceptor activation (~milliseconds) to cortical integration. If entropy (ignorance) is maximal during the event, no "arrow of time" forms for pain, as entropy engraves time's direction. Bugorski's flash without pain suggests a relativistic-like compression: the beam's near-c velocity minimized interaction time, preventing entropy increase in pain pathways.
2.  _Eyes, Consciousness, Demyelination:_ All share subjective entropy. Vision (retinal chromophores) processes light information; consciousness (microtubule wave function collapse) reduces quantum uncertainty; demyelination increases neural entropy by disrupting UPE signaling, raising ignorance about photo-bioelectric states. In my thesis, these are linked by mitochondrial "information engines," where entropy taxes (heat dissipation per Landauer's limit) limit processing. Bugorski's survival implies his system adapted to high-entropy input, perhaps via Prigogine's internal compensation.
3.  _Centralized Medicine's Failure:_ Conventional models assume objective entropy (e.g., radiation dose → deterministic damage), ignoring observer subjectivity. Doctors predicted death because their "ignorance" (averaged macroscopic metrics like temperature, pressure) couldn't model the microscopic resilience. Decentralized medicine, inspired by Szilard's engine (a thought experiment linking information to work and entropy), views health as distributed entropy management, mitochondria as engines extracting order from chaos without central control. **_My 1980s realization at Charity Hospital aligns: medicine should decentralize to mimic nature's probabilistic, observer-relative entropy flows._**

Implications and the Sense of It

You all should sense the deep implications of this thread: if entropy is subjective, reality (including pain, disease) is composed of ignorance. Centralized medicine's "averages" (e.g., dose-response curves) fail because they ignore microscopic details where time manifests. In Bugorski, no pain occurred because his tissues "lost track" of the beam's momentum, entropy maximized instantly, collapsing time perception. This challenges determinism: consciousness arises from reducing entropy (ignorance) via UPEs/microtubules, but overwhelming energy (like the beam) creates "timeless" states.

Broader ramifications:

-   _Health as Entropy Management:_ Diseases like demyelination increase entropy (disrupted UPEs raise neural ignorance), explaining cognitive haze without macroscopic damage.
-   Decentralized Interventions: Prioritize light/circadian alignment to minimize entropy tax, as mitochondria can't "remember" (store information) without power.
-   _Philosophy of Science:_ As Jaynes showed in his work, maximum entropy principle makes inferences from incomplete data observer-dependent, rendering centralized models illusions. Szilard's engine papers, showed us by tying information acquisition to entropy production, it prefigures decentralized systems where agents (cells) act intelligently amid uncertainty.

In essence, Bugorski's case exemplifies how subjective entropy exposes centralized medicine's blind spots: it can't model what it doesn't "know." Decentralized views empower by embracing ignorance as opportunity for resilience.

Those are the lessons all of you keep missing.

THEY ARE MASSIVE

# Thread: Hello from the UK - some advice please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### regularjoe777 — 2025-08-19T13:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358476>

Hi everyone, first post after gaining access to the forums and coming across Jack Kruse work / podcasts only a week or so ago.

Over the last 1-2 years I've been suffering from 2-4 times a month waking in the night with severe stomach pain, eventually reaching / vomiting and pain subsiding 6 hours later. I've tried narrowing it down to particular processed foods, like crisps, alcohol and general overconsumption late in the day.
My doctor prescribed omeprazole initially, but that didn't do anything.

I've been doing a bit of extended fasting now and again and trying to be careful what I eat but fumbling in the dark really. Since coming across Jack Kruse I'm shifting my eating to morning, afternoon and avoiding consumption altogether in the evening.

I've just had an ultrasound today which has picked up an inflamed gallbladder and gallstones.
Wondering if someone would be kind enough to point me in the right direction? I definitely don't want to have my gallbladder removed!
Thanks in advance

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-19T16:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358490>

My recent work in the demyelination series and on the gut nerves provides some inight. Read them.

### regularjoe777 — 2025-08-20T20:26:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358554>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=358490)
>
> My recent work in the demyelination series and on the gut nerves provides some inight. Read them.

Hi thank you, I found the Demyelination series on your Patreon so signed up and have had a read through, along with your "Do you need a gallbladder" article.

It's an awful lot of new information, but I think I understand how I got to this point; working from home since Covid, many hours after dark in front of screens, little morning sunlight and a fair measure of gluttony over the years. I may also have exacerbated the production of the gallstones through some prolonged intermittent fasting...

Since my ultrasound and identification of dodgy gallbladder and stones I've committed to better choices - I'm keen to ensure I'm on the right path. My digestive system feels "blocked" at the moment, I don't know if that's anything down to my new found awareness of my condition, but I am eating slowly are carefully and more in line with my carcadian cycle.
I certainly seem to have lost my abnormal appetite, perhaps that is through eating more mindfully or just the state of my body right now. I know this is not a quick fix I'm just very keen to bring down the inflammation and heal.

My most recent blood test with the doc showed low potassium, so I was trying to counter this with bananas and tomato juice, but I think I need to avoid Tomatoes as they are a nightshade (as per brain gut 6 epi paleo rx post). Am I ok with banana's - which are never in season and imported to the UK. I will try to look for alternatives in the meantime.

I also wonder if any supplements are recommended e.g. L-Glutamine, Kelp, Magnesium, milk thistle, dandelion & turmeric, coconut oil.

Thank you in advance.

### regularjoe777 — 2025-08-22T00:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358599>

> [S Hudson said:](/goto/post?id=358592)
>
> Morning sunlight and getting as much sun as you can may well help with the feeling clogged up. Leaving the Uk for sunnier climates may also benefit, with the added incentive that you will no longer be in a failing country that is on the brink of either civil war or tyranny (prob b

Thanks I'm tapping in. I agree the UK is going to the dogs, like most western countries, but where to go?
I hear El Salvador is a good place to be, anywhere else that anyone could recommend?

### S Hudson — 2025-08-22T17:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358619>

> [regularjoe777 said:](/goto/post?id=358599)

# Thread: Rosacea - a chronic inflammatory skin condition?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosacea-a-chronic-inflammatory-skin-condition.31530/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ajazz — 2025-08-20T10:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosacea-a-chronic-inflammatory-skin-condition.31530/post-358537>

Hey inspiring people,

I've been wondering: _is Rosacea really a chronic inflammatory skin condition_?

That's at least the mainstream opinion…

For context: I've been dealing with rosacea for the last 5–10 years, and it's become more severe over the past couple of years.

Could it be genetic? My mom has it too.

I've noticed mine is triggered by heat/cold, alcohol, stress, and spicy foods. High-histamine foods like avocado and tomatoes also seem to cause flare-ups.

I've tried cutting out gluten, raw dairy, and sugar, but haven’t noticed much improvement.

I do my best to see the sunrise each morning and limit blue light in the evening. Still, I work in tech, so I’m in front of screens more than I'd like. I take regular breaks for walking outside and getting some sun.

I'm very curious if anyone here has insights or recommendations.

I'm even considering trying a full carnivore diet for a month to see if it makes a difference.

I'm also getting some blood work done soon — any suggestions on what I should ask to include?

### Mat.I — 2025-08-20T11:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosacea-a-chronic-inflammatory-skin-condition.31530/post-358539>

Symptom (surface level) = red flushed inflamed skin. Just a boundary expression on what is happening beneath the surface.
Cause (below the surface) poor redox potential in your mitochondria..

You already know the answer as you have made the effort to mention you still work in tech and do your best to see sunrise and limit blue light in the evening.
